7. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/module_31.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n                     \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n                     MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n                     max_entries,\n                     capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/src/module_31.txt",
    "numLines": 702,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 702
  },
  "type": "text"
}

10. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/app/project/tests/test_main.txt"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```c\n/**\n * File: tests/test_main.c\n * Project: MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub (api_microservice)\n * Purpose: High-level integration / unit tests that exercise the Order-Service\n *          happy-path and error-path logic, with special focus on the built-in\n *          read-through response-cache.  The tests are implemented with the\n *          Check framework so they can be executed by CTest / Meson / Make\n *          targets alike:\n *\n *              $ meson test         # Meson\n *              $ ctest -V           # CTest\n *              $ make check         # GNU-Make\n *\n * To keep the test self-contained we provide small, header-only *fakes* of the\n * repository and cache components that implement the same public surface as\n * the production modules.  Those fakes are wired into the service via the\n * standard dependency-injection hooks that are part of the real codebase\n * (`order_service_set_repository()`, `order_service_set_cache()`).\n *\n * IMPORTANT:\n *  \u2013 The tests may link against the real production objects when they are\n *    available on the linker path.  In that case the fakes will be discarded\n *    automatically because every symbol is annotated weak (`__attribute__((weak))`).\n *  \u2013 When the production objects are *not* present, the linker will fall back\n *    to the implementations in this file so the test binary is still\n *    compile-time and run-time complete.\n */\n\n#define _GNU_SOURCE /* strdup() on glibc */\n#include <check.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public headers from the main codebase\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#include \"order_service.h\"      /* service fa\u00e7ade */\n#include \"order_repository.h\"   /* repository interface */\n#include \"response_cache.h\"     /* LRU / TTL cache interface */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Compile-time configuration\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef TEST_DB_CAPACITY\n#  define TEST_DB_CAPACITY   16  /* in-memory \u201cdatabase\u201d size                */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef TEST_CACHE_CAPACITY\n#  define TEST_CACHE_CAPACITY 8  /* in-memory \u201ccache\u201d size                   */\n#endif\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Typedefs / simple model mirrors\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef struct\n{\n    char    id[40];     /* UUID4 textual representation                     */\n    char    sku[40];\n    uint32_t quantity;\n} order_t;\n\n/* Production code usually provides this enum; duplicate for the stub build */\n#ifndef ORDER_ERR_OK\ntypedef enum\n{\n    ORDER_ERR_OK = 0,\n    ORDER_ERR_NOT_FOUND,\n    ORDER_ERR_BACKEND,\n    ORDER_ERR_INVALID_ARG\n} order_err_e;\n#endif /* ORDER_ERR_OK */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Reference in-memory \u201cdatabase\u201d (fake repository)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* A naive array-backed store \u2013 good enough for tests                     */\nstatic order_t      g_db[TEST_DB_CAPACITY];\nstatic size_t       g_db_size = 0;\n\n/* Simple counter for interaction verification                            */\nstatic uint32_t     g_repo_get_invocations  = 0;\n\n/* Insert helper                                                           */\nstatic void\ndb_insert(const order_t *src)\n{\n    if (g_db_size >= TEST_DB_CAPACITY)\n        ck_abort_msg(\"Fake DB capacity exhausted \u2013 increase TEST_DB_CAPACITY.\");\n\n    g_db[g_db_size++] = *src;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Response-cache fake\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef struct\n{\n    char    id[40];\n    order_t ord;\n    time_t  ts_insert;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\nstatic cache_entry_t g_cache[TEST_CACHE_CAPACITY];\nstatic size_t        g_cache_size = 0;\nstatic uint32_t      g_cache_hit  = 0;\nstatic uint32_t      g_cache_miss = 0;\n\n/* Time-To-Live constraint (seconds). */\n#ifndef TEST_CACHE_TTL_SEC\n#  define TEST_CACHE_TTL_SEC  3600\n#endif\n\n/* Fake implementations \u2013 marked weak so real objects override them\n * automatically when they exist in the final link set. */\n__attribute__((weak))\norder_err_e\nresponse_cache_get(const char *id, order_t *out)\n{\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache_size; ++i)\n    {\n        if (strcmp(g_cache[i].id, id) == 0)\n        {\n            /* Expired? */\n            if ((now - g_cache[i].ts_insert) > TEST_CACHE_TTL_SEC)\n            {\n                /* Evict */\n                memmove(&g_cache[i],\n                        &g_cache[i + 1],\n                        (g_cache_size - i - 1) * sizeof(cache_entry_t));\n                --g_cache_size;\n                ++g_cache_miss;\n                return ORDER_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n            }\n\n            *out = g_cache[i].ord;\n            ++g_cache_hit;\n            return ORDER_ERR_OK;\n        }\n    }\n    ++g_cache_miss;\n    return ORDER_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n}\n\n__attribute__((weak))\norder_err_e\nresponse_cache_put(const order_t *ord)\n{\n    if (g_cache_size >= TEST_CACHE_CAPACITY)\n    {\n        /* Very small, simple eviction \u2013 throw away the oldest element */\n        memmove(&g_cache[0], &g_cache[1], (g_cache_size - 1)*sizeof(cache_entry_t));\n        --g_cache_size;\n    }\n\n    cache_entry_t *slot   = &g_cache[g_cache_size++];\n    strcpy(slot->id, ord->id);\n    slot->ord       = *ord;\n    slot->ts_insert = time(NULL);\n\n    return ORDER_ERR_OK;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Fake repository implementing order_repository_i\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic order_err_e\nfake_repo_get_by_id(const char *id, order_t *out)\n{\n    ++g_repo_get_invocations;\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_db_size; ++i)\n    {\n        if (strcmp(g_db[i].id, id) == 0)\n        {\n            *out = g_db[i];\n            return ORDER_ERR_OK;\n        }\n    }\n    return ORDER_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n}\n\nstatic order_err_e\nfake_repo_save(const order_t *in)\n{\n    db_insert(in);\n    return ORDER_ERR_OK;\n}\n\nstatic order_repository_i g_fake_repo = {\n    .get_by_id = fake_repo_get_by_id,\n    .save      = fake_repo_save,\n};\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Helper functions / fixtures\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void\nsetup(void)\n{\n    /* Reset in-memory structures */\n    memset(g_db,    0, sizeof(g_db));\n    memset(g_cache, 0, sizeof(g_cache));\n    g_db_size             = 0;\n    g_cache_size          = 0;\n    g_repo_get_invocations = 0;\n    g_cache_hit            = 0;\n    g_cache_miss           = 0;\n\n    /* Inject our fakes into the service under test */\n    order_service_set_repository(&g_fake_repo);\n    /* The cache uses global C-linkage symbols; nothing to configure. */\n}\n\nstatic void\nteardown(void)\n{\n    /* Nothing to do for now */\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Test cases\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Verify that a cache hit bypasses the repository and returns immediately */\nSTART_TEST(test_cache_hit_short_circuits_repository)\n{\n    /* Arrange ------------------------------------------------------------ */\n    order_t ord = {\n        .id       = \"ORDER-12345-CACHE-HIT\",\n        .sku      = \"SKU-FOO-BAR\",\n        .quantity = 42\n    };\n\n    /* Put object directly into cache (simulate previous access). */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(response_cache_put(&ord), ORDER_ERR_OK);\n\n    /* Act ---------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    order_t out = {0};\n    order_err_e rc = order_service_get_order(ord.id, &out);\n\n    /* Assert ------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(rc, ORDER_ERR_OK);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_repo_get_invocations, 0);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_cache_hit, 1);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(out.quantity, 42);\n    ck_assert_str_eq(out.sku, ord.sku);\n}\nEND_TEST\n\n/* Verify that a cache miss fetches from the repository and populates cache */\nSTART_TEST(test_cache_miss_triggers_repo_and_backfills_cache)\n{\n    /* Arrange ------------------------------------------------------------ */\n    order_t ord = {\n        .id       = \"ORDER-12345-CACHE-MISS\",\n        .sku      = \"SKU-BAZ-QUX\",\n        .quantity = 99\n    };\n    db_insert(&ord);\n\n    /* Act ---------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    order_t out = {0};\n    order_err_e rc = order_service_get_order(ord.id, &out);\n\n    /* Assert part 1: repository path taken -------------------------------- */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(rc, ORDER_ERR_OK);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_repo_get_invocations, 1);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(out.quantity, 99);\n\n    /* Act again ----------------------------------------------------------- */\n    order_t out2 = {0};\n    rc = order_service_get_order(ord.id, &out2);\n\n    /* Assert part 2: second access is served from cache ------------------- */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(rc, ORDER_ERR_OK);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_repo_get_invocations, 1); /* unchanged */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_cache_hit, 1);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(out2.quantity, 99);\n}\nEND_TEST\n\n/* Verify that error propagation works on \u201corder not found\u201d */\nSTART_TEST(test_order_not_found_error_propagation)\n{\n    /* Arrange ------------------------------------------------------------ */\n    const char *unknown_id = \"ORDER-DOES-NOT-EXIST\";\n    order_t dummy          = {0};\n\n    /* Act ---------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    order_err_e rc = order_service_get_order(unknown_id, &dummy);\n\n    /* Assert ------------------------------------------------------------- */\n    ck_assert_int_eq(rc, ORDER_ERR_NOT_FOUND);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_repo_get_invocations, 1);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_cache_hit, 0);\n    ck_assert_int_eq(g_cache_miss, 1);\n}\nEND_TEST\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Test-suite bootstrap\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic Suite *\norder_service_suite(void)\n{\n    Suite *s     = suite_create(\"order_service\");\n    TCase *tc    = tcase_create(\"core\");\n\n    tcase_add_checked_fixture(tc, setup, teardown);\n\n    tcase_add_test(tc, test_cache_hit_short_circuits_repository);\n    tcase_add_test(tc, test_cache_miss_triggers_repo_and_backfills_cache);\n    tcase_add_test(tc, test_order_not_found_error_propagation);\n\n    suite_add_tcase(s, tc);\n    return s;\n}\n\nint\nmain(void)\n{\n    int     failed;\n    Suite  *s  = order_service_suite();\n    SRunner *sr = srunner_create(s);\n\n    srunner_run_all(sr, CK_ENV);  /* CK_ENV = use env-vars for verbosity    */\n    failed = srunner_ntests_failed(sr);\n    srunner_free(sr);\n\n    return (failed == 0) ? EXIT_SUCCESS : EXIT_FAILURE;\n}\n```\n\n",
    "filePath": "/app/project/tests/test_main.txt",
    "numLines": 342,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 342
  },
  "type": "text"
}

12. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Centralized Logging Module\n *\n * File: src/logging.h\n *\n * Description:\n *   Unified logging API for the entire MercuryMonolith codebase.\n *   Provides configurable log levels, output destination, and\n *   automatic file/line injection via convenience macros.\n */\n\n#ifndef LOGGING_H\n#define LOGGING_H\n\n#include <stdarg.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Log Levels\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG,\n    LOG_LEVEL_INFO,\n    LOG_LEVEL_WARN,\n    LOG_LEVEL_ERROR\n} LogLevel;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API Functions\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * log_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes the logging subsystem. Must be called once at application\n * startup before any logging calls.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level    - minimum log level to output (messages below this are discarded)\n *   log_file - path to log file, or NULL to log to stderr\n */\nvoid log_init(LogLevel level, const char* log_file);\n\n/**\n * log_message\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Core logging function. Typically called via convenience macros.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level - severity level of this message\n *   file  - source file name (usually __FILE__)\n *   line  - source line number (usually __LINE__)\n *   fmt   - printf-style format string\n *   ...   - format arguments\n */\nvoid log_message(LogLevel level, const char* file, int line, const char* fmt, ...);\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Convenience Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_WARN, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* LOGGING_H */\n",
  "file_path": "/app/project/src/logging.h"
}
Output
"Error: EACCES: permission denied, open '/app/project/src/logging.h'"

{
  "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Centralized Logging Module\n *\n * File: src/logging.h\n *\n * Description:\n *   Unified logging API for the entire MercuryMonolith codebase.\n *   Provides configurable log levels, output destination, and\n *   automatic file/line injection via convenience macros.\n */\n\n#ifndef LOGGING_H\n#define LOGGING_H\n\n#include <stdarg.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Log Levels\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG,\n    LOG_LEVEL_INFO,\n    LOG_LEVEL_WARN,\n    LOG_LEVEL_ERROR\n} LogLevel;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API Functions\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * log_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes the logging subsystem. Must be called once at application\n * startup before any logging calls.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level    - minimum log level to output (messages below this are discarded)\n *   log_file - path to log file, or NULL to log to stderr\n */\nvoid log_init(LogLevel level, const char* log_file);\n\n/**\n * log_message\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Core logging function. Typically called via convenience macros.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level - severity level of this message\n *   file  - source file name (usually __FILE__)\n *   line  - source line number (usually __LINE__)\n *   fmt   - printf-style format string\n *   ...   - format arguments\n */\nvoid log_message(LogLevel level, const char* file, int line, const char* fmt, ...);\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Convenience Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_WARN, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* LOGGING_H */\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/logging.h"
}

  "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Centralized Logging Module\n *\n * File: src/logging.h\n *\n * Description:\n *   Unified logging API for the entire MercuryMonolith codebase.\n *   Provides configurable log levels, output destination, and\n *   automatic file/line injection via convenience macros.\n */\n\n#ifndef LOGGING_H\n#define LOGGING_H\n\n#include <stdarg.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Log Levels\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG,\n    LOG_LEVEL_INFO,\n    LOG_LEVEL_WARN,\n    LOG_LEVEL_ERROR\n} LogLevel;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API Functions\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * log_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes the logging subsystem. Must be called once at application\n * startup before any logging calls.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level    - minimum log level to output (messages below this are discarded)\n *   log_file - path to log file, or NULL to log to stderr\n */\nvoid log_init(LogLevel level, const char* log_file);\n\n/**\n * log_message\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Core logging function. Typically called via convenience macros.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   level - severity level of this message\n *   file  - source file name (usually __FILE__)\n *   line  - source line number (usually __LINE__)\n *   fmt   - printf-style format string\n *   ...   - format arguments\n */\nvoid log_message(LogLevel level, const char* file, int line, const char* fmt, ...);\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Convenience Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  log_message(LOG_LEVEL_WARN, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) log_message(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, __FILE__, __LINE__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* LOGGING_H */\n",

20. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Centralized Logging Module - Implementation\n *\n * File: src/logging.c\n *\n * Description:\n *   Implementation of the unified logging API. Handles log level\n *   filtering, output destination management, and formatted output.\n */\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Module State\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic LogLevel g_min_level = LOG_LEVEL_INFO;\nstatic FILE*    g_log_file  = NULL;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Level to String Mapping\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic const char* level_to_string(LogLevel level)\n{\n    switch (level) {\n        case LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG: return \"DEBUG\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_INFO:  return \"INFO\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_WARN:  return \"WARN\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_ERROR: return \"ERROR\";\n        default:              return \"UNKNOWN\";\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API Implementation\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nvoid log_init(LogLevel level, const char* log_file)\n{\n    g_min_level = level;\n\n    /* Close any previously opened file */\n    if (g_log_file && g_log_file != stderr) {\n        fclose(g_log_file);\n        g_log_file = NULL;\n    }\n\n    if (log_file != NULL) {\n        g_log_file = fopen(log_file, \"a\");\n        if (g_log_file == NULL) {\n            /* Fallback to stderr if file open fails */\n            g_log_file = stderr;\n        }\n    } else {\n        g_log_file = stderr;\n    }\n}\n\nvoid log_message(LogLevel level, const char* file, int line, const char* fmt, ...)\n{\n    /* Filter by minimum log level */\n    if (level < g_min_level) {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Use stderr as default if log_init hasn't been called */\n    FILE* out = g_log_file ? g_log_file : stderr;\n\n    /* Print log prefix: [LEVEL] (file:line) */\n    fprintf(out, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \", level_to_string(level), file, line);\n\n    /* Print the formatted message */\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf(out, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    /* Add newline */\n    fprintf(out, \"\\n\");\n\n    /* Flush to ensure immediate output */\n    fflush(out);\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/logging.c"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Centralized Logging Module - Implementation\n *\n * File: src/logging.c\n *\n * Description:\n *   Implementation of the unified logging API. Handles log level\n *   filtering, output destination management, and formatted output.\n */\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <string.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Module State\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic LogLevel g_min_level = LOG_LEVEL_INFO;\nstatic FILE*    g_log_file  = NULL;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Level to String Mapping\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic const char* level_to_string(LogLevel level)\n{\n    switch (level) {\n        case LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG: return \"DEBUG\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_INFO:  return \"INFO\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_WARN:  return \"WARN\";\n        case LOG_LEVEL_ERROR: return \"ERROR\";\n        default:              return \"UNKNOWN\";\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API Implementation\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nvoid log_init(LogLevel level, const char* log_file)\n{\n    g_min_level = level;\n\n    /* Close any previously opened file */\n    if (g_log_file && g_log_file != stderr) {\n        fclose(g_log_file);\n        g_log_file = NULL;\n    }\n\n    if (log_file != NULL) {\n        g_log_file = fopen(log_file, \"a\");\n        if (g_log_file == NULL) {\n            /* Fallback to stderr if file open fails */\n            g_log_file = stderr;\n        }\n    } else {\n        g_log_file = stderr;\n    }\n}\n\nvoid log_message(LogLevel level, const char* file, int line, const char* fmt, ...)\n{\n    /* Filter by minimum log level */\n    if (level < g_min_level) {\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Use stderr as default if log_init hasn't been called */\n    FILE* out = g_log_file ? g_log_file : stderr;\n\n    /* Print log prefix: [LEVEL] (file:line) */\n    fprintf(out, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \", level_to_string(level), file, line);\n\n    /* Print the formatted message */\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf(out, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    /* Add newline */\n    fprintf(out, \"\\n\");\n\n    /* Flush to ensure immediate output */\n    fflush(out);\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/logging.c",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

    "content": "        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",

{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 702
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "new_string": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"",
  "old_string": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n                     \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n                     MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n                     max_entries,\n                     capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",
