  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
  "newString": "/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * APPLICATION ENTRY POINT\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * mercury_hub_main\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.\n * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.\n */\nint mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)\n{\n    (void)argc;\n    (void)argv;\n\n    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */\n    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");\n\n    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST",
  "oldString": "/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n                     \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n                     MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n                     max_entries,\n                     capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

        "+ * mercury_hub_main",
        "+ * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "+ * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.",
        "+ * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.",
        "+ */",
        "+int mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)",
        "+{",
        "+    (void)argc;",
        "+    (void)argv;",
        "+",
        "+    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */",
        "+    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);",
        "+",
        "+    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");",
        "+",
        "+    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
  "newString": "    LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n             \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n             MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n             max_entries,\n             capacity_bytes >> 10);",
  "oldString": "    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n                     \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n                     MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n                     max_entries,\n                     capacity_bytes >> 10);",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n                     \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n                     MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n                     max_entries,\n                     capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * APPLICATION ENTRY POINT\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * mercury_hub_main\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.\n * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.\n */\nint mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)\n{\n    (void)argc;\n    (void)argv;\n\n    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */\n    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");\n\n    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",

  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
  "newString": "    LOG_DEBUG(\"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);",

  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n             \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n             MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n             max_entries,\n             capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * APPLICATION ENTRY POINT\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * mercury_hub_main\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.\n * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.\n */\nint mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)\n{\n    (void)argc;\n    (void)argv;\n\n    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */\n    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");\n\n    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",

  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_31.txt",
