  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n             \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n             MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n             max_entries,\n             capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    LOG_DEBUG(\"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_log(LOG_INFO, \"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * APPLICATION ENTRY POINT\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * mercury_hub_main\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.\n * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.\n */\nint mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)\n{\n    (void)argc;\n    (void)argv;\n\n    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */\n    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");\n\n    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",

  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 31  \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache supporting\n *   TTL, size limits, and LRU eviction.  It is intended to sit in front\n *   of service & repository layers to short-circuit identical requests\n *   (e.g.   GET /catalog/items?page=2).\n *\n *   Metrics and structured logging are integrated to satisfy the\n *   observability requirements of on-premise deployments.\n *\n * File path:\n *   src/module_31.txt          (compiled as .c)\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread\n *\n * Public API (see function comments for details):\n *   mm_cache_init\n *   mm_cache_destroy\n *   mm_cache_put\n *   mm_cache_get\n *   mm_cache_invalidate\n *   mm_cache_purge\n *   mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logger shim\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n/*  In production builds <mm_logger.h> is available globally.                 */\n/*  We provide a fallback to keep this unit self-contained for unit tests.    */\ntypedef enum { LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR } mm_log_level_t;\n\nstatic inline void mm_log(mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    const char *lvl_str = (lvl == LOG_DEBUG) ? \"DEBUG\" :\n                          (lvl == LOG_INFO)  ? \"INFO\"  :\n                          (lvl == LOG_WARN)  ? \"WARN\"  : \"ERROR\";\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] \", lvl_str);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & Macros\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION  \"31.3.0\"   /* Semantic versioning       */\n\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE  2048u    /* Must be power-of-two      */\n#define MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL           1u       /* Seconds                   */\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN        1024u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE   128u\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG          128u\n\n#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect(!!(x), 0)\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data Types\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char              *key;\n    unsigned char     *payload;\n    size_t             payload_len;\n\n    char              *content_type;        /* MIME type (optional)  */\n    char              *etag;                /* Strong/Weak ETag      */\n\n    time_t             expiry_ts;           /* Epoch time            */\n    uint64_t           last_access;         /* Monotonic counter     */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev, *next;     /* LRU list              */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t   capacity_bytes;   /* Max RAM in bytes                    */\n    size_t   max_entries;      /* Cap on element count                */\n\n    /* Internal state */\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   tbl_size;         /* Hash table size (power of two)      */\n    mm_cache_entry_t **table;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;/* MRU                                 */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;/* LRU                                 */\n\n    /* Concurrency control */\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* Publicly exported metrics snapshot */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    size_t   entry_count;\n    size_t   current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len);\nstatic int      mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size);\nstatic void     mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes);\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * API IMPLEMENTATION\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_init\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Initializes a response cache instance.\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   c               \u2013 pointer to an allocated mm_cache_t\n *   max_entries     \u2013 upper bound on number of cache records\n *   capacity_bytes  \u2013 max memory usage for payloads + metadata\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno-compatible error code otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t *c,\n                  size_t max_entries,\n                  size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!c || max_entries == 0 || capacity_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n\n    c->max_entries    = max_entries;\n    c->capacity_bytes = capacity_bytes;\n\n    /* Initial hash table size: next power-of-two >= max_entries * 2          */\n    size_t tbl_size = MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TBL_SIZE;\n    while (tbl_size < (max_entries << 1)) tbl_size <<= 1;\n\n    if (mm_cache_grow_table(c, tbl_size) != 0)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0)\n        return errno;\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized (ver=%s, max_entries=%zu, \"\n             \"capacity=%zu KiB)\",\n             MM_CACHE_MODULE_VERSION,\n             max_entries,\n             capacity_bytes >> 10);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Frees all memory associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->table);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    memset(c, 0, sizeof(*c));\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Stores a response in the cache.\n *\n * The cache takes ownership of the payload data (it copies it internally).\n *\n * Arguments:\n *   key             \u2013 request hash key  (string)\n *   payload         \u2013 response body\n *   payload_len     \u2013 size of response body\n *   content_type    \u2013 MIME type (may be NULL)\n *   etag            \u2013 optional ETag header\n *   ttl_seconds     \u2013 time-to-live for this cache record\n *\n * Return:\n *   0 on success, errno code on failure\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const unsigned char *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 const char *content_type,\n                 const char *etag,\n                 uint32_t ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEYLEN)\n        return ENAMETOOLONG;\n\n    if (ttl_seconds < MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL)\n        ttl_seconds = MM_CACHE_MIN_TTL;\n\n    /* Pre-allocate memory before acquiring write lock to minimize lock time */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    new_entry->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_entry->key) { free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n\n    new_entry->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!new_entry->payload) { free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(new_entry->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    new_entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    if (content_type) {\n        if (strlen(content_type) > MM_CACHE_MAX_CONTENTTYPE) {\n            free(new_entry->payload); free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry);\n            return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->content_type = strdup(content_type);\n        if (!new_entry->content_type) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    if (etag) {\n        if (strlen(etag) > MM_CACHE_MAX_ETAG) {\n            free(new_entry->content_type); free(new_entry->payload);\n            free(new_entry->key); free(new_entry); return ENAMETOOLONG;\n        }\n        new_entry->etag = strdup(etag);\n        if (!new_entry->etag) goto oom;\n    }\n\n    new_entry->expiry_ts   = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n    new_entry->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n\n    /* Lock and insert */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* Evict expired + ensure capacity before insertion */\n    mm_cache_evict_if_needed(c, payload_len);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = c->table[idx];\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace existing entry */\n        c->current_bytes -= existing->payload_len;\n        free(existing->payload);\n        existing->payload       = new_entry->payload;\n        existing->payload_len   = new_entry->payload_len;\n        existing->expiry_ts     = new_entry->expiry_ts;\n        existing->last_access   = new_entry->last_access;\n\n        free(existing->content_type);\n        existing->content_type  = new_entry->content_type;\n\n        free(existing->etag);\n        existing->etag          = new_entry->etag;\n\n        /* Touch */\n        mm_cache_lru_touch(c, existing);\n\n        free(new_entry->key); /* key identical */\n        free(new_entry);      /* container freed */\n    } else {\n        /* Insert brand new entry */\n        c->table[idx] = new_entry;\n        c->entry_count++;\n        c->current_bytes += payload_len;\n\n        /* Add to LRU head */\n        new_entry->next = c->lru_head;\n        if (c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head->prev = new_entry;\n        c->lru_head = new_entry;\n        if (!c->lru_tail)\n            c->lru_tail = new_entry;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n\noom:\n    free(new_entry->etag);\n    free(new_entry->content_type);\n    free(new_entry->payload);\n    free(new_entry->key);\n    free(new_entry);\n    return ENOMEM;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Retrieves a cached response. Ownership of *payload_out is transferred to\n * the caller (caller must free). content_type_out & etag_out point to\n * internal strings \u2013 do not free, DO copy if retained beyond processing.\n *\n * Return:\n *   1  \u2013 cache HIT  (payload populated)\n *   0  \u2013 cache MISS\n *  <0  \u2013 negative errno on internal failure\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **payload_out,\n                 size_t *payload_len_out,\n                 const char **content_type_out,\n                 const char **etag_out,\n                 uint32_t *age_out)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n\n    /* Fast path: MISS */\n    if (!e) {\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiration */\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    if (e->expiry_ts < now) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to remove expired entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        /* ensure another thread hasn't removed it */\n        if (c->table[idx] == e) {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            /* Hash slot removal */\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n            c->entry_count--;\n            c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n            /* Rehash cluster (Robin Hood deletion not necessary for small table) */\n            size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[j]) {\n                mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n                c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n                size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                while (c->table[new_idx])\n                    new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n                c->table[new_idx] = move;\n                j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            }\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n\n        c->misses++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* HIT */\n    e->last_access = (uint64_t)clock();\n    c->hits++;\n\n    /* Copy payload for caller */\n    unsigned char *payload_dup = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!payload_dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_dup, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n\n    *payload_out      = payload_dup;\n    *payload_len_out  = e->payload_len;\n    if (content_type_out) *content_type_out = e->content_type;\n    if (etag_out)        *etag_out          = e->etag;\n    if (age_out)         *age_out           = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ts - now));\n\n    /* Defer LRU touch after releasing read lock to cut contention.          */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_lru_touch(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes a single entry from the cache (e.g. after POST/PUT/DELETE).\n */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    uint64_t hash = mm_fnv1a_hash(key, strlen(key));\n    size_t idx = hash & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    while (c->table[idx] && strcmp(c->table[idx]->key, key) != 0)\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[idx];\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_head) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->table[idx] = NULL;\n    c->entry_count--;\n    c->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n    /* Rehash cluster */\n    size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    while (c->table[j]) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n        c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n        size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[new_idx])\n            new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n        j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n    }\n\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->content_type);\n    free(e->etag);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    LOG_DEBUG(\"Cache invalidated: %s\", key);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Removes every entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n        if (e) {\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->content_type);\n            free(e->etag);\n            free(e);\n        }\n        c->table[i] = NULL;\n    }\n\n    c->entry_count   = 0;\n    c->current_bytes = 0;\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    LOG_INFO(\"Cache fully purged\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_metrics_snapshot\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Captures a read-only snapshot of metrics for Prometheus scrape thread.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics_snapshot(const mm_cache_t *c,\n                               mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n\n    out->hits          = c->hits;\n    out->misses        = c->misses;\n    out->evictions     = c->evictions;\n    out->entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    out->current_bytes = c->current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * INTERNAL HELPERS\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Fowler-Noll-Vo hash */\nstatic uint64_t mm_fnv1a_hash(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t h = 14695981039346656037ULL; /* offset basis */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= (uint64_t)p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ULL;            /* FNV prime    */\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\n/* Resize hash table (called under write lock) */\nstatic int mm_cache_grow_table(mm_cache_t *c, size_t new_size)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t **new_tbl = calloc(new_size, sizeof(*new_tbl));\n    if (!new_tbl)\n        return ENOMEM;\n\n    if (c->table) {\n        for (size_t i = 0; i < c->tbl_size; ++i) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->table[i];\n            if (!e) continue;\n\n            size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key, strlen(e->key)) & (new_size - 1);\n            while (new_tbl[idx])\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (new_size - 1);\n            new_tbl[idx] = e;\n        }\n        free(c->table);\n    }\n    c->table    = new_tbl;\n    c->tbl_size = new_size;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Move entry to head (MRU) */\nstatic void mm_cache_lru_touch(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e == c->lru_head) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (e == c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict until under capacity constraints */\nstatic void mm_cache_evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c, size_t add_bytes)\n{\n    while ( (c->entry_count >= c->max_entries) ||\n            (c->current_bytes + add_bytes > c->capacity_bytes) )\n    {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        /* Detach from list */\n        if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (victim == c->lru_head)\n            c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        /* Remove from hash table */\n        size_t idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[idx] && c->table[idx] != victim)\n            idx = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        if (c->table[idx] == victim)\n            c->table[idx] = NULL;\n\n        /* Rehash cluster to fill holes */\n        size_t j = (idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        while (c->table[j]) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *move = c->table[j];\n            c->table[j] = NULL;\n\n            size_t new_idx = mm_fnv1a_hash(move->key, strlen(move->key)) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            while (c->table[new_idx])\n                new_idx = (new_idx + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n            c->table[new_idx] = move;\n\n            j = (j + 1) & (c->tbl_size - 1);\n        }\n\n        c->entry_count--;\n        c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;\n        c->evictions++;\n\n        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim->content_type);\n        free(victim->etag);\n        free(victim);\n\n        if (c->entry_count == 0)\n            break;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * APPLICATION ENTRY POINT\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * mercury_hub_main\n * ----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Main entry point for the MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub application.\n * Initializes the logging subsystem and any other global resources.\n */\nint mercury_hub_main(int argc, char **argv)\n{\n    (void)argc;\n    (void)argv;\n\n    /* Initialize the centralized logging system */\n    log_init(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, NULL);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub starting up\");\n\n    /* Application initialization and main loop would go here */\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * UNIT TEST (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t cache;\n    assert(mm_cache_init(&cache, 128, 1024 * 1024) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"GET /hello\";\n    const char *body = \"world\";\n    assert(mm_cache_put(&cache, key, (const unsigned char *)body,\n                        strlen(body) + 1, \"text/plain\", \"\\\"abc\\\"\", 10) == 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n    const char *ctype;\n    const char *etag;\n    int rc = mm_cache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len, &ctype, &etag, NULL);\n    assert(rc == 1);\n    assert(len == strlen(body) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp((char *)out, body) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    puts(\"All cache unit tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         c->current_bytes -= victim->payload_len;",
        "         c->evictions++;",
        " ",
        "-        mm_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);",
        "+        LOG_DEBUG(\"Cache evicted key=%s (payload=%zuB)\", victim->key, victim->payload_len);",
        " ",
        "         free(victim->key);",
        "         free(victim->payload);"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 656,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 656
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

32. Grep @ -
Input
{
  "-n": true,
  "output_mode": "content",
  "path": "/workspace/project/src",
  "pattern": "fprintf\\(stderr|printf\\("
}
Output
{
  "content": "project/src/module_12.txt:515:    fprintf(out,\nproject/src/module_12.txt:548:        snprintf(key, sizeof(key), \"k%d\", i);\nproject/src/module_12.txt:549:        snprintf(val, sizeof(val), \"v%d\", i);\nproject/src/module_57.txt:510:        printf(\"HIT %s (%zu bytes, %s, %d)\\n\", key1, len, ctype, code);\nproject/src/module_57.txt:518:    printf(\"After expire: %s\\n\", {
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_51.txt",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * -------------------------------------------\n * File:    src/module_51.txt          (C source)\n * Module:  Rate-Limiting Service (token-bucket)\n *\n * Overview\n * --------\n *     A thread-safe, per-caller token-bucket rate-limiter that can be\n *     embedded directly in the HTTP / GraphQL gateway layer.  Fast\n *     (O(1) look-ups via uthash), lock-efficient (per-bucket mutex) and\n *     hot-reloadable at runtime.\n *\n * Key Features\n * ------------\n *  - Constant-time look-ups using uthash (included below for portability)\n *  - Fine-grained locking (global RW-lock for bucket map, mutex per bucket)\n *  - Adaptive refill using monotonic clock\n *  - Live reconfiguration (max_tokens / refill_rate) without downtime\n *  - Prometheus-style metric hooks (stubbed)\n *  - Structured logging hooks (stubbed)\n *\n * Build\n * -----\n *     # gcc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -O2 -c src/module_51.txt -o rate_limiter.o\n *\n * Notes\n * -----\n *   1. This file intentionally carries a `.txt` extension because the\n *      challenge instructions require it.  Treat it as a `.c` file.\n *   2. External dependencies (logger/metrics) are stubbed to compile\n *      stand-alone.  Wire them to your actual infra in production.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L     /* clock_gettime, strdup       */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging stubs (replace with real logger.h)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef LOG_INFO\n#   define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Metrics stubs (replace with real metrics.h)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic inline void metrics_inc(const char *metric_name)\n{\n    (void)metric_name; /* no-op */\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash single-header implementation (trimmed for string keys)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Begin uthash.h (https://github.com/troydhanson/uthash)  */\n#define UT_HASH_VERSION 2.3.0\n/* Content trimmed: only the minimal subset required for string keyed hash */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)   malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    void *prev;                    /* previous element in app order      */\n    void *next;                    /* next element in app order          */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_prev;/* previous hh in bucket order        */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next;/* next hh in bucket order            */\n    const void *key;               /* pointer to the key                */\n    unsigned keylen;               /* key length in bytes               */\n    unsigned hashv;                /* result of hash function           */\n} UT_hash_handle;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n    unsigned count;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket *buckets;\n    unsigned num_buckets, log2_num_buckets;\n    unsigned num_items;\n    unsigned ideal_chain_maxlen;\n    unsigned upper_bound;\n    struct UT_hash_handle *tail; /* tail hh in app order */\n} UT_hash_table;\n\n/* murmurhash3 */\nstatic inline unsigned _uthash_murmur(const void *key, unsigned keylen)\n{",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_51.txt",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 578
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * -------------------------------------------\n * File:    src/module_51.txt          (C source)\n * Module:  Rate-Limiting Service (token-bucket)\n *\n * Overview\n * --------\n *     A thread-safe, per-caller token-bucket rate-limiter that can be\n *     embedded directly in the HTTP / GraphQL gateway layer.  Fast\n *     (O(1) look-ups via uthash), lock-efficient (per-bucket mutex) and\n *     hot-reloadable at runtime.\n *\n * Key Features\n * ------------\n *  - Constant-time look-ups using uthash (included below for portability)\n *  - Fine-grained locking (global RW-lock for bucket map, mutex per bucket)\n *  - Adaptive refill using monotonic clock\n *  - Live reconfiguration (max_tokens / refill_rate) without downtime\n *  - Prometheus-style metric hooks (stubbed)\n *  - Structured logging hooks (stubbed)\n *\n * Build\n * -----\n *     # gcc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -O2 -c src/module_51.txt -o rate_limiter.o\n *\n * Notes\n * -----\n *   1. This file intentionally carries a `.txt` extension because the\n *      challenge instructions require it.  Treat it as a `.c` file.\n *   2. External dependencies (logger/metrics) are stubbed to compile\n *      stand-alone.  Wire them to your actual infra in production.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L     /* clock_gettime, strdup       */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging stubs (replace with real logger.h)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef LOG_INFO\n#   define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Metrics stubs (replace with real metrics.h)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic inline void metrics_inc(const char *metric_name)\n{\n    (void)metric_name; /* no-op */\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash single-header implementation (trimmed for string keys)\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Begin uthash.h (https://github.com/troydhanson/uthash)  */\n#define UT_HASH_VERSION 2.3.0\n/* Content trimmed: only the minimal subset required for string keyed hash */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)   malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    void *prev;                    /* previous element in app order      */\n    void *next;                    /* next element in app order          */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_prev;/* previous hh in bucket order        */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next;/* next hh in bucket order            */\n    const void *key;               /* pointer to the key                */\n    unsigned keylen;               /* key length in bytes               */\n    unsigned hashv;                /* result of hash function           */\n} UT_hash_handle;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n    unsigned count;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket *buckets;\n    unsigned num_buckets, log2_num_buckets;\n    unsigned num_items;\n    unsigned ideal_chain_maxlen;\n    unsigned upper_bound;\n    struct UT_hash_handle *tail; /* tail hh in app order */\n} UT_hash_table;\n\n/* murmurhash3 */\nstatic inline unsigned _uthash_murmur(const void *key, unsigned keylen)\n{\n    const uint8_t *data = (const uint8_t *)key;\n    const uint32_t c1 = 0xcc9e2d51;\n    const uint32_t c2 = 0x1b873593;\n    uint32_t h1 = 0x811c9dc5; /* FNV offset basis */\n    const int nblocks = keylen / 4;\n    const uint32_t *blocks = (const uint32_t *)(data + nblocks * 4);\n    int i;\n    for (i = -nblocks; i; i++) {\n        uint32_t k1 = blocks[i];\n        k1 *= c1; k1 = (k1 << 15) | (k1 >> 17); k1 *= c2;\n        h1 ^= k1;\n        h1 = (h1 << 13) | (h1 >> 19);\n        h1 = h1*5 + 0xe6546b64;\n    }\n    const uint8_t *tail = (const uint8_t*)(data + nblocks*4);\n    uint32_t k1 = 0;\n    switch (keylen & 3) {\n    case 3: k1 ^= tail[2] << 16;\n    case 2: k1 ^= tail[1] << 8;\n    case 1: k1 ^= tail[0];\n            k1 *= c1; k1 = (k1 << 15) | (k1 >> 17); k1 *= c2; h1 ^= k1;\n    }\n    h1 ^= keylen;\n    h1 ^= h1 >> 16; h1 *= 0x85ebca6b; h1 ^= h1 >> 13; h1 *= 0xc2b2ae35; h1 ^= h1 >> 16;\n    return h1;\n}\n\nstatic inline void _uthash_init(UT_hash_table *tbl)\n{\n    tbl->num_buckets = 16;\n    tbl->log2_num_buckets = 4;\n    tbl->buckets = (UT_hash_bucket*)uthash_malloc(tbl->num_buckets * sizeof(UT_hash_bucket));\n    memset(tbl->buckets, 0, tbl->num_buckets * sizeof(UT_hash_bucket));\n    tbl->num_items = 0;\n    tbl->ideal_chain_maxlen = 8;\n    tbl->upper_bound = tbl->num_buckets * 10;\n    tbl->tail = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic inline void _uthash_expand(UT_hash_table *tbl);\n\n/* Add to hash */\nstatic inline void _uthash_add(UT_hash_table *tbl, UT_hash_handle *hh, const void *key, unsigned keylen)\n{\n    hh->key = key;\n    hh->keylen = keylen;\n    hh->hashv = _uthash_murmur(key, keylen);\n    unsigned bucket = hh->hashv & (tbl->num_buckets - 1);\n    hh->hh_next = tbl->buckets[bucket].hh_head;\n    if (hh->hh_next) hh->hh_next->hh_prev = hh;\n    tbl->buckets[bucket].hh_head = hh;\n    hh->hh_prev = NULL;\n    tbl->buckets[bucket].count++;\n    if (!tbl->tail) {\n        tbl->tbl = tbl;\n        tbl->tail = hh;\n        hh->prev = hh->next = NULL;\n    } else {\n        hh->prev = tbl->tail;\n        tbl->tail->next = hh;\n        hh->next = NULL;\n        tbl->tail = hh;\n    }\n    tbl->num_items++;\n    if (tbl->num_items > tbl->upper_bound) _uthash_expand(tbl);\n}\n\nstatic inline void _uthash_delete(UT_hash_table *tbl, UT_hash_handle *hh)\n{\n    unsigned bucket = hh->hashv & (tbl->num_buckets - 1);\n    if (hh->hh_prev) hh->hh_prev->hh_next = hh->hh_next;\n    else tbl->buckets[bucket].hh_head = hh->hh_next;\n    if (hh->hh_next) hh->hh_next->hh_prev = hh->hh_prev;\n    tbl->buckets[bucket].count--;\n    if (hh->prev) hh->prev->next = hh->next;\n    if (hh->next) hh->next->prev = hh->prev;\n    if (tbl->tail == hh) tbl->tail = hh->prev;\n    tbl->num_items--;\n}\n\nstatic inline void _uthash_expand(UT_hash_table *tbl)\n{\n    unsigned new_buckets = tbl->num_buckets * 2;\n    UT_hash_bucket *new_tbl = (UT_hash_bucket*)uthash_malloc(new_buckets * sizeof(UT_hash_bucket));\n    memset(new_tbl, 0, new_buckets * sizeof(UT_hash_bucket));\n\n    UT_hash_handle *hh, *tmp;\n    for (hh = tbl->tail; hh; hh = hh->prev) {\n        unsigned new_bucket = hh->hashv & (new_buckets - 1);\n        hh->hh_next = new_tbl[new_bucket].hh_head;\n        if (hh->hh_next) hh->hh_next->hh_prev = hh;\n        new_tbl[new_bucket].hh_head = hh;\n    }\n    uthash_free(tbl->buckets, tbl->num_buckets * sizeof(UT_hash_bucket));\n    tbl->buckets = new_tbl;\n    tbl->num_buckets = new_buckets;\n    tbl->log2_num_buckets++;\n    tbl->upper_bound = tbl->num_buckets * 10;\n}\n\n#define HASH_FIND_STR(head,findstr,out)                                  \\\ndo {                                                                     \\\n    unsigned _hf_hashv = _uthash_murmur(findstr, (unsigned)strlen(findstr)); \\\n    unsigned _hf_bucket = _hf_hashv & ((head)?((head)->tbl->num_buckets-1):0); \\\n    out=NULL;                                                            \\\n    if (head) {                                                          \\\n        UT_hash_handle *_hf_hh = (head)->tbl->buckets[_hf_bucket].hh_head; \\\n        while (_hf_hh) {                                                 \\\n            if ((_hf_hh->keylen == strlen(findstr)) &&                   \\\n                (memcmp(_hf_hh->key, findstr, _hf_hh->keylen) == 0)) {    \\\n                out = (void*)((char*)_hf_hh - ((char*)(&(((typeof(out))0)->hh)) - (char*)0)); \\\n                break;                                                   \\\n            }                                                            \\\n            _hf_hh = _hf_hh->hh_next;                                    \\\n        }                                                                \\\n    }                                                                    \\\n} while(0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hhname,keyptr,keylen_in,out) _uthash_add(hhname,keyptr,keylen_in,out)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(head,delptr) _uthash_delete((head)->tbl,&((delptr)->hh))\n/*  End uthash.h (condensed) */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* =========================================================================\n *                        Rate-Limiter Data Structures\n * ========================================================================= */\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket_s {\n    char               *key;          /* caller identifier (e.g., API key)   */\n    double              tokens;       /* current tokens                      */\n    double              max_tokens;   /* bucket capacity                     */\n    double              refill_rate;  /* tokens per second                   */\n    struct timespec     last_refill;  /* last refill timestamp               */\n\n    pthread_mutex_t     mtx;          /* bucket-level lock                   */\n    UT_hash_handle      hh;           /* uthash handle                       */\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\ntypedef struct rate_limiter_s {\n    rl_bucket_t        *buckets;      /* hash map                            */\n    pthread_rwlock_t    map_lock;     /* protects buckets table modifications*/\n    size_t              max_buckets;  /* guard memory usage                  */\n} rate_limiter_t;\n\n/* =========================================================================\n *                          Utility Helpers\n * ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Return seconds since epoch with nanosecond resolution as double */\nstatic inline double wallclock_now_sec(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    return (double)ts.tv_sec + ts.tv_nsec / 1e9;\n}\n\n/* Fill timespec with now */\nstatic inline void now_timespec(struct timespec *ts)\n{\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, ts);\n}\n\n/* Compute time diff (a - b) in seconds */\nstatic inline double timespec_diff_sec(const struct timespec *a,\n                                       const struct timespec *b)\n{\n    return (double)(a->tv_sec - b->tv_sec)\n         + (a->tv_nsec - b->tv_nsec) / 1e9;\n}\n\n/* =========================================================================\n *                         Bucket Helper Functions\n * ========================================================================= */\n\n/* Refill tokens in the bucket based on elapsed time */\nstatic void rl_bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *bucket, const struct timespec *now)\n{\n    double elapsed = timespec_diff_sec(now, &bucket->last_refill);\n    if (elapsed <= 0) return;\n\n    double new_tokens = bucket->tokens + elapsed * bucket->refill_rate;\n    if (new_tokens > bucket->max_tokens)\n        new_tokens = bucket->max_tokens;\n\n    bucket->tokens = new_tokens;\n    bucket->last_refill = *now;\n}\n\n/* Destroy bucket and free memory */\nstatic void rl_bucket_destroy(rl_bucket_t *bucket)\n{\n    if (!bucket) return;\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&bucket->mtx);\n    free(bucket->key);\n    free(bucket);\n}\n\n/* =========================================================================\n *                           Public API Functions\n * ========================================================================= */\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_create()\n * ---------------------\n *   Allocate and initialize a new rate limiter.\n *\n *   max_buckets: Upper bound of distinct callers before rejecting new ones.\n *                Prevents unbounded memory usage under keyed DoS.\n *\n *   Returns: Pointer to rate_limiter_t on success, NULL on allocation failure.\n */\nrate_limiter_t *rate_limiter_create(size_t max_buckets)\n{\n    rate_limiter_t *rl = calloc(1, sizeof(*rl));\n    if (!rl) {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"rate_limiter_create: Out of memory\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    _uthash_init((UT_hash_table *)((char *)&rl->buckets + offsetof(rl_bucket_t, hh) - offsetof(UT_hash_handle, hh_next)));\n    pthread_rwlock_init(&rl->map_lock, NULL);\n    rl->max_buckets = max_buckets;\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Rate-Limiter initialized (max_buckets=%zu)\", max_buckets);\n    return rl;\n}\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_destroy()\n * ----------------------\n *   Free all buckets and the rate limiter object.\n */\nvoid rate_limiter_destroy(rate_limiter_t *rl)\n{\n    if (!rl) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket, *tmp;\n    for (bucket = rl->buckets; bucket; ) {\n        tmp = (rl_bucket_t*)bucket->hh.next;\n        HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, bucket);\n        rl_bucket_destroy(bucket);\n        bucket = tmp;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&rl->map_lock);\n    free(rl);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Rate-Limiter destroyed\");\n}\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_update_defaults()\n * ------------------------------\n *   Dynamically update default capacity and refill rate for *new* buckets.\n *   Existing buckets remain unchanged (call update_bucket if you need that).\n *\n *   For simplicity, new default values are stored as atomic globals.\n */\nstatic atomic_double g_default_max_tokens = 100.0;\nstatic atomic_double g_default_refill_rate = 25.0; /* per second */\n\nvoid rate_limiter_set_defaults(double max_tokens, double refill_rate)\n{\n    atomic_store(&g_default_max_tokens, max_tokens);\n    atomic_store(&g_default_refill_rate, refill_rate);\n    LOG_INFO(\"Rate-Limiter defaults updated (capacity=%.1f, refill=%.1f/s)\",\n             max_tokens, refill_rate);\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_get_or_create_bucket()\n * -------------------------\n *   Internal helper to fetch (or lazily create) a bucket for key.\n *\n *   Caller must hold at least read-lock on map_lock; may upgrade to write.\n */\nstatic rl_bucket_t *rl_get_or_create_bucket(rate_limiter_t *rl,\n                                            const char      *key)\n{\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket = NULL;\n\n    /* Fast path: look-up under read lock */\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n    if (bucket) return bucket;\n\n    /* Not found: need to create (write lock) */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    /* Check again in case another writer inserted */\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n    if (bucket) {\n        /* Downgrade lock */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        return bucket;\n    }\n\n    /* Enforce max bucket limit */\n    if (rl->max_buckets && rl->buckets\n        && ((UT_hash_table*)((rl->buckets)->hh.tbl))->num_items >= rl->max_buckets) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock); /* restore state for caller */\n        LOG_WARN(\"Rate-Limiter bucket limit reached (%zu)\", rl->max_buckets);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    /* Allocate new bucket */\n    bucket = calloc(1, sizeof(*bucket));\n    if (!bucket) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        LOG_ERROR(\"Out of memory while creating bucket\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    bucket->key         = strdup(key);\n    bucket->max_tokens  = atomic_load(&g_default_max_tokens);\n    bucket->refill_rate = atomic_load(&g_default_refill_rate);\n    bucket->tokens      = bucket->max_tokens; /* start full */\n    now_timespec(&bucket->last_refill);\n    pthread_mutex_init(&bucket->mtx, NULL);\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh.tbl, &bucket->hh, bucket->key, strlen(bucket->key), &rl->buckets);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Created rate-limit bucket for '%s' (cap=%.0f, refill=%.1f/s)\",\n             key, bucket->max_tokens, bucket->refill_rate);\n\n    /* Downgrade to read lock */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    return bucket;\n}\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_allow()\n * --------------------\n *   Attempt to consume `tokens` from caller's bucket.\n *\n *   Returns: true  -> request is allowed\n *            false -> throttled\n */\nbool rate_limiter_allow(rate_limiter_t *rl,\n                        const char     *caller_key,\n                        double          tokens)\n{\n    if (!rl || !caller_key) return false;\n\n    /* Shared lock for map read; will upgrade lazily on bucket miss */\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket = rl_get_or_create_bucket(rl, caller_key);\n    if (!bucket) { /* bucket limit reached or allocation failure */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        metrics_inc(\"ratelimit_dropped_no_bucket\");\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    /* At this point we still hold map read-lock; bucket is stable. */\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bucket->mtx);\n\n    struct timespec now;\n    now_timespec(&now);\n    rl_bucket_refill(bucket, &now);\n\n    bool allowed = false;\n    if (bucket->tokens >= tokens) {\n        bucket->tokens -= tokens;\n        allowed = true;\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bucket->mtx);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    if (!allowed) {\n        metrics_inc(\"ratelimit_denied\");\n        LOG_WARN(\"Rate-Limit exceeded for '%s' (need %.1f, have %.1f)\",\n                 caller_key, tokens, bucket->tokens);\n    }\n\n    return allowed;\n}\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_update_bucket()\n * ----------------------------\n *   Adjust a bucket's capacity/refill rate at runtime.\n *   Returns 0 on success, ENOENT if bucket not found.\n */\nint rate_limiter_update_bucket(rate_limiter_t *rl,\n                               const char     *caller_key,\n                               double          new_max_tokens,\n                               double          new_refill_rate)\n{\n    if (!rl || !caller_key) return EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&rl->map_lock);\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, caller_key, bucket);\n    if (!bucket) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bucket->mtx);\n    bucket->max_tokens  = new_max_tokens;\n    bucket->refill_rate = new_refill_rate;\n    if (bucket->tokens > bucket->max_tokens)\n        bucket->tokens = bucket->max_tokens;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bucket->mtx);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Rate-Limiter bucket updated '%s' (cap=%.0f, refill=%.1f/s)\",\n             caller_key, new_max_tokens, new_refill_rate);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rate_limiter_clear()\n * --------------------\n *   Flush all buckets (e.g., after tenant deletion).\n *   Thread-safe, but may briefly block allowance checks.\n */\nvoid rate_limiter_clear(rate_limiter_t *rl)\n{\n    if (!rl) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&rl->map_lock);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket, *tmp;\n    for (bucket = rl->buckets; bucket; ) {\n        tmp = (rl_bucket_t*)bucket->hh.next;\n        HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, bucket);\n        rl_bucket_destroy(bucket);\n        bucket = tmp;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&rl->map_lock);\n    LOG_INFO(\"Rate-Limiter cleared all buckets\");\n}\n\n/* =========================================================================\n *                              Self-Test (optional)\n * ========================================================================= */\n#ifdef RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    rate_limiter_t *rl = rate_limiter_create(1024);\n    assert(rl);\n\n    const char *client = \"demo-client\";\n\n    /* Consume within limits */\n    for (int i = 0; i < 100; ++i) {\n        assert(rate_limiter_allow(rl, client, 1));\n    }\n\n    /* Should now reject */\n    assert(!rate_limiter_allow(rl, client, 1));\n\n    /* Wait for refill */\n    sleep(1);\n    assert(rate_limiter_allow(rl, client, 1));\n\n    rate_limiter_destroy(rl);\n    puts(\"Rate-Limiter self-test passed\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```c\n/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module: response_cache\n *\n * File path: src/module_64.txt   (compiled as C source)\n *\n * This module provides an in-memory, TTL-aware, thread-safe LRU cache that is\n * used by the REST/GraphQL Gateway layer to short-circuit expensive service\n * calls when identical requests repeat frequently.  The cache is namespace\n * aware (e.g. \u201corders.v1\u201d), exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics, and uses\n *        uthash (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n * for O(1) look-ups while maintaining an LRU eviction list.\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* single-header hash table */\n#include \"core/mm_log.h\"           /* project logging macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_metrics.h\"       /* project metrics macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_time.h\"          /* monotonic & wall-clock   */\n\n/* -------- build-time fallbacks for out-of-tree compilation ---------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\n#   define MM_LOG_H\n#   define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_METRICS_H\n#   define MM_METRICS_H\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_inc(const char *name)   { (void)name; }\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_set_gauge(const char *name, double v) { (void)name; (void)v; }\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_TIME_H\n#   define MM_TIME_H\nstatic inline uint64_t mm_time_now_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + (uint64_t)ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n#endif\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* ------------------------------ constants -------------------------------- */\n#define MM_CACHE_VERSION           \"1.0.0\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_HITS       \"response_cache_hits_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_MISSES     \"response_cache_misses_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_EVICTIONS  \"response_cache_evictions_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_SIZE       \"response_cache_size\"\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_64.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
