  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* single-header hash table */\n#include \"logging.h\"               /* centralized logging      */\n#include \"core/mm_metrics.h\"       /* project metrics macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_time.h\"          /* monotonic & wall-clock   */",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* single-header hash table */\n#include \"core/mm_log.h\"           /* project logging macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_metrics.h\"       /* project metrics macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_time.h\"          /* monotonic & wall-clock   */\n\n/* -------- build-time fallbacks for out-of-tree compilation ---------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\n#   define MM_LOG_H\n#   define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module: response_cache\n *\n * File path: src/module_64.txt   (compiled as C source)\n *\n * This module provides an in-memory, TTL-aware, thread-safe LRU cache that is\n * used by the REST/GraphQL Gateway layer to short-circuit expensive service\n * calls when identical requests repeat frequently.  The cache is namespace\n * aware (e.g. \u201corders.v1\u201d), exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics, and uses\n *        uthash (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n * for O(1) look-ups while maintaining an LRU eviction list.\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* single-header hash table */\n#include \"core/mm_log.h\"           /* project logging macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_metrics.h\"       /* project metrics macros   */\n#include \"core/mm_time.h\"          /* monotonic & wall-clock   */\n\n/* -------- build-time fallbacks for out-of-tree compilation ---------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\n#   define MM_LOG_H\n#   define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_METRICS_H\n#   define MM_METRICS_H\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_inc(const char *name)   { (void)name; }\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_set_gauge(const char *name, double v) { (void)name; (void)v; }\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_TIME_H\n#   define MM_TIME_H\nstatic inline uint64_t mm_time_now_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + (uint64_t)ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n#endif\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* ------------------------------ constants -------------------------------- */\n#define MM_CACHE_VERSION           \"1.0.0\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_HITS       \"response_cache_hits_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_MISSES     \"response_cache_misses_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_EVICTIONS  \"response_cache_evictions_total\"\n#define MM_CACHE_METRIC_SIZE       \"response_cache_size\"\n\n/* ---------------------------- data types --------------------------------- */\ntypedef struct mm_buf_s\n{\n    uint8_t *data;  /* pointer to heap-allocated buffer */\n    size_t   len;   /* length in bytes                  */\n} mm_buf_t;\n\n/* Forward declaration so we can keep pointer inside struct */\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry_s mm_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Doubly linked list node for LRU queue */\ntypedef struct lru_node_s\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    struct lru_node_s *prev;\n    struct lru_node_s *next;\n} lru_node_t;\n\n/* Cached value + bookkeeping */\nstruct mm_cache_entry_s\n{\n    char *key;                                  /* unique key */\n    mm_buf_t value;                             /* serialized HTTP or GraphQL response */\n    uint64_t created_ms;                        /* insertion time (monotonic) */\n    uint64_t ttl_ms;                            /* entry life-span             */\n\n    lru_node_t *node;                           /* node inside LRU list        */\n\n    UT_hash_handle hh;                          /* hash table handle           */\n};\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_s\n{\n    char     namespace[64];                     /* subsystem identifier        */\n    size_t   capacity;                          /* maximum # of live entries   */\n    size_t   size;                              /* current # of live entries   */\n\n    pthread_mutex_t lock;                       /* guard (hash + lru)          */\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *map;                      /* uthash map key -> entry     */\n    lru_node_t *lru_head;                       /* MRU                         */\n    lru_node_t *lru_tail;                       /* LRU                         */\n\n    bool     stop_janitor;                      /* signal to janitor thread    */\n    pthread_t janitor_tid;                      /* background janitor thread   */\n\n    uint64_t default_ttl_ms;                    /* default TTL for new entries */\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* ----------------------- internal helper prototypes ---------------------- */\nstatic void  cache_touch_locked(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *entry);\nstatic void  cache_evict_lru_locked(mm_cache_t *c);\nstatic void *cache_janitor_thread(void *arg);\nstatic void  free_entry(mm_cache_entry_t *e);\n\n/* --------------------------- API functions ------------------------------- */\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(size_t capacity,\n                            uint64_t default_ttl_ms,\n                            const char *namespace);\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\nbool mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key,\n                  mm_buf_t   *out_value,\n                  uint64_t   *out_age_ms);\n\nvoid mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key,\n                  const void *value,\n                  size_t      value_len,\n                  uint64_t    ttl_override_ms /* 0 = use default */);\n\n/* --------------------------- implementation ------------------------------ */\n\n/* Allocate and initialize a cache */\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(size_t capacity,\n                            uint64_t default_ttl_ms,\n                            const char *namespace)\n{\n    if (capacity == 0 || default_ttl_ms == 0 || !namespace)\n    {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_create: invalid arguments\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_cache_t));\n    if (!c) {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_create: OOM\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    snprintf(c->namespace, sizeof(c->namespace), \"%s\", namespace);\n    c->capacity        = capacity;\n    c->default_ttl_ms  = default_ttl_ms;\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n\n    /* Spawn janitor thread for expiry sweep */\n    if (pthread_create(&c->janitor_tid, NULL, cache_janitor_thread, c) != 0)\n    {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_create: failed to start janitor thread\");\n        pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->lock);\n        free(c);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Response cache '%s' initialized (capacity=%zu, default_ttl=%llums, version=%s)\",\n             c->namespace, c->capacity, (unsigned long long)c->default_ttl_ms,\n             MM_CACHE_VERSION);\n\n    return c;\n}\n\n/* Destroy cache and free all memory */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    /* signal janitor thread to exit */\n    cache->stop_janitor = true;\n    pthread_join(cache->janitor_tid, NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->map, cur, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->map, cur);\n        free_entry(cur);\n    }\n\n    /* free LRU nodes */\n    lru_node_t *n = cache->lru_head;\n    while (n)\n    {\n        lru_node_t *next = n->next;\n        free(n);\n        n = next;\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&cache->lock);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\n/* Look up a key.  Returns true if hit.  Caller owns returned buffer\n * (must free(out_value->data)).\n */\nbool mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key,\n                  mm_buf_t   *out_value,\n                  uint64_t   *out_age_ms)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_value) return false;\n\n    bool hit = false;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, e);\n    if (e)\n    {\n        uint64_t now = mm_time_now_ms();\n        uint64_t age = now - e->created_ms;\n\n        if (age < e->ttl_ms)\n        {\n            /* fresh hit */\n            hit = true;\n\n            out_value->data = malloc(e->value.len);\n            if (out_value->data)\n            {\n                memcpy(out_value->data, e->value.data, e->value.len);\n                out_value->len = e->value.len;\n                if (out_age_ms) *out_age_ms = age;\n            }\n            cache_touch_locked(cache, e);\n        }\n        else\n        {\n            /* stale: remove entry */\n            HASH_DEL(cache->map, e);\n            /* detach node from LRU list */\n            if (e->node->prev) e->node->prev->next = e->node->next;\n            if (e->node->next) e->node->next->prev = e->node->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == e->node) cache->lru_head = e->node->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == e->node) cache->lru_tail = e->node->prev;\n\n            cache->size--;\n            mm_metrics_inc(MM_CACHE_METRIC_EVICTIONS);\n            free_entry(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_metrics_inc(hit ? MM_CACHE_METRIC_HITS : MM_CACHE_METRIC_MISSES);\n    return hit;\n}\n\n/* Insert or replace a cache entry */\nvoid mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key,\n                  const void *value,\n                  size_t      value_len,\n                  uint64_t    ttl_override_ms)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !value || value_len == 0) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, e);\n    if (e)\n    {\n        /* Overwrite existing */\n        free(e->value.data);\n        e->value.data = malloc(value_len);\n        if (!e->value.data)\n        {\n            LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_put: OOM on overwrite\");\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n            return;\n        }\n        memcpy(e->value.data, value, value_len);\n        e->value.len   = value_len;\n        e->created_ms  = mm_time_now_ms();\n        e->ttl_ms      = ttl_override_ms ? ttl_override_ms : cache->default_ttl_ms;\n\n        cache_touch_locked(cache, e);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* New entry */\n        if (cache->size >= cache->capacity)\n            cache_evict_lru_locked(cache);\n\n        e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n        if (!e) { pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock); return; }\n\n        e->key = strdup(key);\n        e->value.data = malloc(value_len);\n        if (!e->key || !e->value.data)\n        {\n            LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_put: OOM on insert\");\n            free_entry(e);\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n            return;\n        }\n        memcpy(e->value.data, value, value_len);\n        e->value.len  = value_len;\n        e->created_ms = mm_time_now_ms();\n        e->ttl_ms     = ttl_override_ms ? ttl_override_ms : cache->default_ttl_ms;\n\n        /* LRU node */\n        lru_node_t *node = calloc(1, sizeof(*node));\n        if (!node)\n        {\n            LOG_ERROR(\"mm_cache_put: OOM on node\");\n            free_entry(e);\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n            return;\n        }\n        node->entry = e;\n        e->node = node;\n\n        /* add to head (MRU) */\n        node->next = cache->lru_head;\n        if (cache->lru_head) cache->lru_head->prev = node;\n        cache->lru_head = node;\n        if (!cache->lru_tail) cache->lru_tail = node;\n\n        /* hash insert */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->map, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n\n        cache->size++;\n        mm_metrics_set_gauge(MM_CACHE_METRIC_SIZE,\n                             (double)cache->size);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->lock);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------- internals ---------------------------------- */\n\n/* Move the entry's node to head (MRU). */\nstatic void cache_touch_locked(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *entry)\n{\n    lru_node_t *n = entry->node;\n    if (c->lru_head == n) return; /* already MRU */\n\n    /* unlink */\n    if (n->prev) n->prev->next = n->next;\n    if (n->next) n->next->prev = n->prev;\n\n    if (c->lru_tail == n) c->lru_tail = n->prev;\n\n    /* push front */\n    n->prev = NULL;\n    n->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = n;\n    c->lru_head = n;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = n;\n}\n\n/* Evict LRU entry */\nstatic void cache_evict_lru_locked(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c->lru_tail) return;\n\n    lru_node_t *victim_node = c->lru_tail;\n    mm_cache_entry_t *victim = victim_node->entry;\n\n    /* Detach from list */\n    if (victim_node->prev)\n        victim_node->prev->next = NULL;\n    c->lru_tail = victim_node->prev;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n    /* Remove from hash */\n    HASH_DEL(c->map, victim);\n    c->size--;\n    mm_metrics_inc(MM_CACHE_METRIC_EVICTIONS);\n    mm_metrics_set_gauge(MM_CACHE_METRIC_SIZE,\n                         (double)c->size);\n\n    free_entry(victim);\n}\n\n/* Periodically sweep expired entries & shrink if capacity has been lowered\n * at runtime (future feature).  Sleeps 1 second between passes.\n */\nstatic void *cache_janitor_thread(void *arg)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c = (mm_cache_t*)arg;\n\n    const uint64_t JITTER_MS = 17;\n\n    while (!c->stop_janitor)\n    {\n        uint64_t now = mm_time_now_ms();\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n        /* iterate over a copy to allow safe delete */\n        mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, c->map, cur, tmp)\n        {\n            if (now - cur->created_ms >= cur->ttl_ms)\n            {\n                HASH_DEL(c->map, cur);\n\n                /* unlink LRU node */\n                lru_node_t *n = cur->node;\n                if (n->prev) n->prev->next = n->next;\n                if (n->next) n->next->prev = n->prev;\n                if (c->lru_head == n) c->lru_head = n->next;\n                if (c->lru_tail == n) c->lru_tail = n->prev;\n\n                c->size--;\n                mm_metrics_inc(MM_CACHE_METRIC_EVICTIONS);\n                free_entry(cur);\n            }\n        }\n\n        mm_metrics_set_gauge(MM_CACHE_METRIC_SIZE,\n                             (double)c->size);\n\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n        /* sleep with small jitter to avoid thundering herd */\n        struct timespec ts = { .tv_sec  = 1,\n                               .tv_nsec = (rand() % JITTER_MS) * 1000000L };\n        nanosleep(&ts, NULL);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void free_entry(mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->value.data);\n    free(e->node);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* --------------------------- unit test stub ------------------------------ */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void test_basic()\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(2, 1000, \"test\");\n\n    char val1[] = \"foo\";\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"k1\", val1, sizeof(val1), 0);\n\n    mm_buf_t buf = {0};\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, \"k1\", &buf, NULL) == true);\n    assert(buf.len == sizeof(val1));\n    free(buf.data);\n\n    /* capacity eviction */\n    char val2[] = \"bar\";\n    char val3[] = \"baz\";\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"k2\", val2, sizeof(val2), 0);\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"k3\", val3, sizeof(val3), 0); /* should evict k1 */\n\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, \"k1\", &buf, NULL) == false);\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, \"k3\", &buf, NULL) == true);\n    free(buf.data);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    test_basic();\n    puts(\"mm_cache: all tests passed\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n```",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```c\n/**\n * src/module_41.txt\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * A thread-safe, TTL-aware, size-bounded, LRU in-memory cache for HTTP / GraphQL\n * responses.  Intended to be used by the Presentation / Gateway layer to reduce\n * latency and backend load for idempotent queries (e.g. catalog, configuration,\n * user preferences).\n *\n * The implementation is self-contained, uses only libc + pthreads, and exposes a\n * small C API.  Integration with the existing logging and monitoring facilities\n * is performed via thin adapter macros (LOG_* / METRIC_*).  The cache is\n * initialized as a singleton because the monolith is single-process.\n *\n * Compile flags recommended:\n *   -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -pthread\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * External logger / metrics stubs\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef LOG_DEBUG\n#define LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO ] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN ] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef METRIC_INC\n#define METRIC_INC(counter)          /* placeholder */\n#define METRIC_OBSERVE(hist, value)  /* placeholder */\n#endif\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & configuration\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#define CACHE_KEY_MAX     256   /* bytes, including ASCIIZ terminator */\n#define CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD sizeof(struct cache_entry)\n#define NANOSECONDS_IN_SEC 1000000000ull\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data structures\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_41.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 551
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "originalFile": "```c\n/**\n * src/module_41.txt\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * A thread-safe, TTL-aware, size-bounded, LRU in-memory cache for HTTP / GraphQL\n * responses.  Intended to be used by the Presentation / Gateway layer to reduce\n * latency and backend load for idempotent queries (e.g. catalog, configuration,\n * user preferences).\n *\n * The implementation is self-contained, uses only libc + pthreads, and exposes a\n * small C API.  Integration with the existing logging and monitoring facilities\n * is performed via thin adapter macros (LOG_* / METRIC_*).  The cache is\n * initialized as a singleton because the monolith is single-process.\n *\n * Compile flags recommended:\n *   -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -pthread\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * External logger / metrics stubs\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef LOG_DEBUG\n#define LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO ] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN ] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef METRIC_INC\n#define METRIC_INC(counter)          /* placeholder */\n#define METRIC_OBSERVE(hist, value)  /* placeholder */\n#endif\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Constants & configuration\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#define CACHE_KEY_MAX     256   /* bytes, including ASCIIZ terminator */\n#define CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD sizeof(struct cache_entry)\n#define NANOSECONDS_IN_SEC 1000000000ull\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Data structures\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry\n{\n    char                    key[CACHE_KEY_MAX];\n    void                   *payload;          /* malloc'd blob                      */\n    size_t                  payload_len;\n    struct timespec         expires_at;       /* absolute time                      */\n\n    struct cache_entry     *prev;             /* for doubly linked LRU list         */\n    struct cache_entry     *next;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct\n{\n    /* Hash table */\n    cache_entry_t        **buckets;\n    size_t                 bucket_count;\n\n    /* LRU doubly linked list (most recent at head) */\n    cache_entry_t         *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t         *lru_tail;\n\n    /* Size constraints */\n    size_t                 max_entries;\n    size_t                 max_bytes;\n    size_t                 current_entries;\n    size_t                 current_bytes;\n\n    /* Defaults */\n    uint32_t               default_ttl_sec;\n\n    /* Concurrency */\n    pthread_mutex_t        lock;\n\n    /* Stats */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t  hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t  misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t  evictions;\n} response_cache_t;\n\n/* Singleton instance (allocated in cache_init) */\nstatic response_cache_t *g_cache = NULL;\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Misc helpers\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nstatic inline uint64_t\nfreq_hash64(const void *data, size_t len)\n{\n    /* Fowler\u2013Noll\u2013Vo hash (FNV-1a, 64-bit) */\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t       h = 14695981039346656037ull;\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {\n        h ^= p[i];\n        h *= 1099511628211ull;\n    }\n    return h;\n}\n\nstatic inline void\ntimespec_add_seconds(struct timespec *ts, uint32_t seconds)\n{\n    ts->tv_sec  += seconds;\n    /* tv_nsec remains unchanged */\n}\n\nstatic inline bool\ntimespec_is_expired(const struct timespec *now, const struct timespec *expires)\n{\n    return (now->tv_sec > expires->tv_sec) ||\n           (now->tv_sec == expires->tv_sec && now->tv_nsec >= expires->tv_nsec);\n}\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Internal API\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *\ncache_entry_create(const char *key,\n                   const void *payload,\n                   size_t      payload_len,\n                   uint32_t    ttl_sec)\n{\n    cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) {\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    /* Copy key (NUL-terminated) */\n    strncpy(e->key, key, CACHE_KEY_MAX - 1);\n    e->key[CACHE_KEY_MAX - 1] = '\\0';\n\n    /* Copy payload */\n    e->payload = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!e->payload) {\n        free(e);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    memcpy(e->payload, payload, payload_len);\n    e->payload_len = payload_len;\n\n    /* Set expiration timestamp */\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE, &e->expires_at);\n    timespec_add_seconds(&e->expires_at, ttl_sec);\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic void\ncache_entry_destroy(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* Detach entry from LRU list (does not free) */\nstatic void\nlru_detach(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\n/* Insert entry as new MRU (head) */\nstatic void\nlru_insert_mru(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Evict the LRU (tail) until within capacity */\nstatic void\ncache_trim_to_limit(response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while ((c->current_entries > c->max_entries) ||\n           (c->current_bytes   > c->max_bytes)) {\n\n        cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        if (!victim) break; /* Should not happen */\n\n        uint64_t bucket_idx =\n            freq_hash64(victim->key, strlen(victim->key)) % c->bucket_count;\n\n        /* Remove from hash chain */\n        cache_entry_t **cur = &c->buckets[bucket_idx];\n        while (*cur && *cur != victim)\n            cur = &(*cur)->next; /* using .next as chain pointer temporarily */\n        if (*cur == victim)\n            *cur = victim->next; /* Remove from chain */\n\n        /* Detach from LRU */\n        lru_detach(c, victim);\n\n        c->current_entries -= 1;\n        c->current_bytes   -= (victim->payload_len + CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD);\n\n        cache_entry_destroy(victim);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        LOG_DEBUG(\"cache: evicted LRU entry\");\n    }\n}\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public API\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/**\n * cache_init\n *\n * Must be called once at program startup before any other cache_* function.\n * Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure (errno is set).\n */\nint\ncache_init(size_t   max_entries,\n           size_t   max_bytes,\n           uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n           size_t   bucket_count_hint)\n{\n    if (g_cache) {\n        errno = EALREADY;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    response_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return -1;\n\n    c->bucket_count   = (bucket_count_hint < 16) ? 16 : bucket_count_hint;\n    c->buckets        = calloc(c->bucket_count, sizeof(cache_entry_t *));\n    if (!c->buckets) {\n        free(c);\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    c->max_entries     = max_entries;\n    c->max_bytes       = max_bytes;\n    c->default_ttl_sec = default_ttl_sec;\n\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n    atomic_init(&c->hits, 0);\n    atomic_init(&c->misses, 0);\n    atomic_init(&c->evictions, 0);\n\n    g_cache = c;\n    LOG_INFO(\"cache: initialized (%zu entries, %zu bytes, ttl=%us, buckets=%zu)\",\n             max_entries, max_bytes, default_ttl_sec, c->bucket_count);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * cache_put\n *\n * Insert or update a cache entry.  A NULL payload or zero length will remove\n * the key (equivalent to cache_invalidate).  TTL of 0 uses the default TTL.\n *\n * Returns 0 on success or negative errno-style codes.\n */\nint\ncache_put(const char *key, const void *payload, size_t len, uint32_t ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!g_cache || !key || strlen(key) == 0 || strlen(key) >= CACHE_KEY_MAX)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    if (!payload || len == 0)\n        return cache_invalidate(key);\n\n    if (ttl_sec == 0)\n        ttl_sec = g_cache->default_ttl_sec;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache->lock);\n\n    uint64_t bucket_idx = freq_hash64(key, strlen(key)) % g_cache->bucket_count;\n    cache_entry_t **head = &g_cache->buckets[bucket_idx];\n    cache_entry_t *e = *head;\n\n    /* Search */\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0)\n        e = e->next; /* chain ptr */\n\n    if (e) {\n        /* Update in place */\n        lru_detach(g_cache, e);\n\n        g_cache->current_bytes -= e->payload_len;\n\n        void *new_payload = realloc(e->payload, len);\n        if (!new_payload) {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n            return -ENOMEM;\n        }\n        e->payload = new_payload;\n        memcpy(e->payload, payload, len);\n        e->payload_len = len;\n\n        clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE, &e->expires_at);\n        timespec_add_seconds(&e->expires_at, ttl_sec);\n\n        g_cache->current_bytes += len;\n        lru_insert_mru(g_cache, e);\n    } else {\n        /* Create new entry */\n        e = cache_entry_create(key, payload, len, ttl_sec);\n        if (!e) {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n            return -ENOMEM;\n        }\n\n        /* Prepend to bucket chain */\n        e->next = *head;\n        *head   = e;\n\n        /* Add to LRU */\n        lru_insert_mru(g_cache, e);\n\n        g_cache->current_entries += 1;\n        g_cache->current_bytes   += (len + CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD);\n    }\n\n    cache_trim_to_limit(g_cache);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * cache_get\n *\n * Attempt to retrieve a cache entry.  On hit, a malloc'd copy of the payload is\n * returned via *out_buf (caller must free).  Returns 0 on hit, 1 on miss,\n * negative on error.\n */\nint\ncache_get(const char *key, void **out_buf, size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!g_cache || !key || !out_buf || !out_len)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    *out_buf = NULL;\n    *out_len = 0;\n\n    struct timespec now;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE, &now);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache->lock);\n\n    uint64_t bucket_idx = freq_hash64(key, strlen(key)) % g_cache->bucket_count;\n    cache_entry_t **head = &g_cache->buckets[bucket_idx];\n    cache_entry_t *e = *head;\n\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0)\n        e = e->next; /* chain ptr */\n\n    if (!e) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n        return 1; /* miss */\n    }\n\n    /* Check expiry */\n    if (timespec_is_expired(&now, &e->expires_at)) {\n        /* Remove expired entry */\n        lru_detach(g_cache, e);\n\n        /* Remove from chain */\n        cache_entry_t **cur = head;\n        while (*cur && *cur != e) cur = &(*cur)->next;\n        if (*cur == e) *cur = e->next;\n\n        g_cache->current_entries -= 1;\n        g_cache->current_bytes   -= (e->payload_len + CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD);\n        cache_entry_destroy(e);\n\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n        return 1; /* expired -> treat as miss */\n    }\n\n    /* Hit -> promote to MRU */\n    lru_detach(g_cache, e);\n    lru_insert_mru(g_cache, e);\n\n    /* Copy payload */\n    void *buf = malloc(e->payload_len);\n    if (!buf) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n        return -ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(buf, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n    *out_buf = buf;\n    *out_len = e->payload_len;\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n    return 0; /* hit */\n}\n\n/**\n * cache_invalidate\n *\n * Removes entry if present.  Returns 0 if removed, 1 if not found, negative on\n * errors.\n */\nint\ncache_invalidate(const char *key)\n{\n    if (!g_cache || !key)\n        return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache->lock);\n    uint64_t bucket_idx = freq_hash64(key, strlen(key)) % g_cache->bucket_count;\n    cache_entry_t **cur = &g_cache->buckets[bucket_idx];\n    cache_entry_t *e = *cur;\n\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0) {\n        cur = &e->next;\n        e = e->next;\n    }\n\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n        return 1; /* not found */\n    }\n\n    /* Remove */\n    *cur = e->next;\n    lru_detach(g_cache, e);\n\n    g_cache->current_entries -= 1;\n    g_cache->current_bytes   -= (e->payload_len + CACHE_ENTRY_OVERHEAD);\n    cache_entry_destroy(e);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * cache_metrics_snapshot\n *\n * Exposes statistics for Prometheus scrape.\n */\nvoid\ncache_metrics_snapshot(uint64_t *hits,\n                       uint64_t *misses,\n                       uint64_t *evictions,\n                       size_t   *current_entries,\n                       size_t   *current_bytes)\n{\n    if (!g_cache) return;\n    if (hits)      *hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache->hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    if (misses)    *misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache->misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    if (evictions) *evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache->evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache->lock);\n    if (current_entries) *current_entries = g_cache->current_entries;\n    if (current_bytes)   *current_bytes   = g_cache->current_bytes;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n}\n\n/**\n * cache_shutdown\n *\n * Frees all resources.  Idempotent.\n */\nvoid\ncache_shutdown(void)\n{\n    if (!g_cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache->lock);\n\n    /* Walk buckets */\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache->bucket_count; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache->buckets[i];\n        while (e) {\n            cache_entry_t *next = e->next;\n            cache_entry_destroy(e);\n            e = next;\n        }\n    }\n\n    free(g_cache->buckets);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache->lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&g_cache->lock);\n\n    free(g_cache);\n    g_cache = NULL;\n    LOG_INFO(\"cache: shutdown complete\");\n}\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Unit test when compiled standalone\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST\n\nstatic void\nselftest(void)\n{\n    assert(cache_init(8, 1 << 20, 2, 32) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"ping\";\n    const char *val = \"pong\";\n    assert(cache_put(key, val, strlen(val) + 1, 1) == 0);\n\n    void  *buf = NULL;\n    size_t len = 0;\n    assert(cache_get(key, &buf, &len) == 0);\n    assert(len == strlen(val) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp(buf, val) == 0);\n    free(buf);\n\n    sleep(2); /* allow TTL to expire */\n\n    assert(cache_get(key, &buf, &len) == 1); /* miss */\n    cache_shutdown();\n    LOG_INFO(\"self-test OK\");\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    selftest();\n    return 0;\n}\n\n#endif /* RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST */\n```",

{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 25 \u2013 Response Cache (in-memory, LRU, TTL aware)\n *\n * File: src/module_25.txt       (compiled as part of the build system)\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, observability-friendly response cache used by the API-\n *   Gateway layer to short-circuit expensive service calls.  Entries are\n *   indexed by user-supplied cache keys (usually a canonicalized request\n *   URI + header subset) and evicted using an LRU strategy once either\n *   the configured size or TTL budget is exhausted.\n *\n *   The implementation purposefully avoids complex external dependencies\n *   by embedding a trimmed-down version of the \u201cuthash\u201d open-source hash\n *   table.  Metrics and logging are piped through the platform facilities\n *   (`mm_metrics`, `mm_logger`) but graceful degradation stubs are\n *   supplied to keep the compilation unit self-contained in isolation/\n *   unit-test contexts.\n *\n *   Compile flags tested:\n *     gcc -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c module_25.txt\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Optional project-wide headers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_LOGGER\n#   include \"mm_logger.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback stubs */\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_METRICS\n#   include \"mm_metrics.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback metric stubs */\n#   define MM_METRIC_INC(name)           (void)0\n#   define MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(name,val) (void)0\n#endif\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Public interface\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifndef MM_RESPONSE_CACHE_H\n#define MM_RESPONSE_CACHE_H\n\n#include <stddef.h>     /* size_t */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;",

  "new_string": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "old_string": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Optional project-wide headers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_LOGGER\n#   include \"mm_logger.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback stubs */\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Optional project-wide headers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_LOGGER\n#   include \"mm_logger.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback stubs */\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 25 \u2013 Response Cache (in-memory, LRU, TTL aware)\n *\n * File: src/module_25.txt       (compiled as part of the build system)\n *\n * Description:\n *   A thread-safe, observability-friendly response cache used by the API-\n *   Gateway layer to short-circuit expensive service calls.  Entries are\n *   indexed by user-supplied cache keys (usually a canonicalized request\n *   URI + header subset) and evicted using an LRU strategy once either\n *   the configured size or TTL budget is exhausted.\n *\n *   The implementation purposefully avoids complex external dependencies\n *   by embedding a trimmed-down version of the \u201cuthash\u201d open-source hash\n *   table.  Metrics and logging are piped through the platform facilities\n *   (`mm_metrics`, `mm_logger`) but graceful degradation stubs are\n *   supplied to keep the compilation unit self-contained in isolation/\n *   unit-test contexts.\n *\n *   Compile flags tested:\n *     gcc -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c module_25.txt\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Optional project-wide headers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_LOGGER\n#   include \"mm_logger.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback stubs */\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN] \"  fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifdef HAVE_MM_METRICS\n#   include \"mm_metrics.h\"\n#else\n/* Fallback metric stubs */\n#   define MM_METRIC_INC(name)           (void)0\n#   define MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(name,val) (void)0\n#endif\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Public interface\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifndef MM_RESPONSE_CACHE_H\n#define MM_RESPONSE_CACHE_H\n\n#include <stddef.h>     /* size_t */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Initialisation / teardown */\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n                            unsigned int default_ttl_seconds);\nvoid        mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n/* CRUD style operations */\nint   mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                   const char *key,\n                   const void *blob,\n                   size_t blob_sz,\n                   unsigned int ttl_override_seconds); /* 0 => default TTL */\n\nint   mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                   const char *key,\n                   void **out_blob,\n                   size_t *out_sz);\n\nvoid  mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key);\nvoid  mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n/* Instrumentation / diagnostics  */\ntypedef struct {\n    size_t entry_count;\n    size_t max_entries;\n    size_t bytes_in_use;\n} mm_cache_stats_t;\n\nmm_cache_stats_t mm_cache_stats(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n#endif /* MM_RESPONSE_CACHE_H */\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Embedded uthash (minimal, key = char*)\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)   malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n\n/* Only what we need out of uthash.h */\n#define HASH_FIND_STR(head,findstr,out) \\\ndo {                                                                         \\\n    unsigned _hf_bkt;                                                        \\\n    out=NULL;                                                                \\\n    if (head) {                                                              \\\n        HASH_FCN(findstr, strlen(findstr), HASH_MASK(head->hh.tbl->num_buckets), _hf_bkt); \\\n        HASH_FIND_IN_BKT(head->hh.tbl, hh, (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt], findstr, strlen(findstr), out); \\\n    }                                                                        \\\n} while(0)\n\n/* For brevity we pull the full source of uthash into the file (public domain) */\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(key,keylen,seed)  xxhash64(key,keylen,seed)\n#include \"uthash.h\"     /* Real-world build would include system header */\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Internal structures\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char                       *key;         /* NULL-terminated */\n    void                       *blob;        /* response payload */\n    size_t                      blob_sz;\n    struct timespec             expiry;      /* absolute CLOCK_MONOTONIC */\n    /* LRU doubly-linked list pointers */\n    struct mm_cache_entry      *prev; \n    struct mm_cache_entry      *next;\n    /* uthash handle MUST be the last member */\n    UT_hash_handle              hh;\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    mm_cache_entry_t  *hash;                /* uthash root */\n    mm_cache_entry_t  *lru_head;            /* most recently used */\n    mm_cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;            /* least recently used */\n    size_t             max_entries;\n    unsigned int       default_ttl;\n    size_t             bytes_in_use;\n    size_t             entry_count;\n    pthread_mutex_t    mtx;\n};\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Helper macros\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#define LOCK(c)   pthread_mutex_lock(&(c)->mtx)\n#define UNLOCK(c) pthread_mutex_unlock(&(c)->mtx)\n\n/* Move entry to front of LRU list */\nstatic inline void lru_move_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return; /* already front */\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Insert brand-new entry at head */\nstatic inline void lru_insert_head(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Clock utility\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nstatic inline struct timespec now_monotonic(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n    return ts;\n}\n\n/* ts1 >= ts2 ? */\nstatic inline int timespec_ge(const struct timespec *a,\n                              const struct timespec *b)\n{\n    return (a->tv_sec > b->tv_sec) ||\n           (a->tv_sec == b->tv_sec && a->tv_nsec >= b->tv_nsec);\n}\n\n/* ts1 = ts2 + seconds */\nstatic inline struct timespec timespec_add_sec(const struct timespec base,\n                                               unsigned int sec)\n{\n    struct timespec res = base;\n    res.tv_sec += sec;\n    return res;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Eviction helpers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nstatic void evict_tail(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c->lru_tail) return;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"ResponseCache: evicting key=%s\", victim->key);\n\n    /* detach from LRU list */\n    if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n    c->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_head = NULL;\n\n    /* remove from hash */\n    HASH_DEL(c->hash, victim);\n\n    /* update stats */\n    c->bytes_in_use -= victim->blob_sz;\n    c->entry_count--;\n\n    /* free */\n    free(victim->blob);\n    free(victim->key);\n    free(victim);\n    MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_evictions_total);\n}\n\n/* Remove expired entries until head is valid */\nstatic void purge_expired(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    struct timespec now = now_monotonic();\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, e, tmp) {\n        if (timespec_ge(&now, &e->expiry)) {\n            /* expired; reuse eviction logic except LRU list update */\n            if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n            if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n            if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n            if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n            HASH_DEL(c->hash, e);\n\n            c->bytes_in_use -= e->blob_sz;\n            c->entry_count--;\n\n            free(e->blob);\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e);\n            MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_expired_total);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Public interface implementation\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n                            unsigned int default_ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_seconds == 0) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return NULL;\n\n    c->max_entries   = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl   = default_ttl_seconds;\n\n    if (pthread_mutex_init(&c->mtx, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"ResponseCache created (max_entries=%zu, default_ttl=%us)\",\n                max_entries, default_ttl_seconds);\n    return c;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    LOCK(c);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, e, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, e);\n        free(e->blob);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n    }\n    UNLOCK(c);\n\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->mtx);\n    free(c);\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"ResponseCache destroyed\");\n}\n\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const void *blob,\n                 size_t blob_sz,\n                 unsigned int ttl_override_seconds)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !blob || blob_sz == 0) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    int rc = 0;\n    LOCK(c);\n\n    purge_expired(c);\n\n    /* Check if entry exists -> overwrite */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, (char *)key, e);\n    if (e) {\n        MM_LOG_INFO(\"ResponseCache: updating existing key=%s\", key);\n\n        /* Replace payload */\n        void *nb = malloc(blob_sz);\n        if (!nb) { rc = -1; goto out; }\n        memcpy(nb, blob, blob_sz);\n        free(e->blob);\n\n        e->blob     = nb;\n        c->bytes_in_use -= e->blob_sz;\n        e->blob_sz  = blob_sz;\n        c->bytes_in_use += blob_sz;\n\n        /* Update expiry */\n        struct timespec expiry = timespec_add_sec(now_monotonic(),\n                            ttl_override_seconds ? ttl_override_seconds : c->default_ttl);\n        e->expiry = expiry;\n        lru_move_front(c, e);\n\n        MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_updates_total);\n        goto out;\n    }\n\n    if (c->entry_count == c->max_entries) {\n        evict_tail(c); /* free 1 entry */\n    }\n\n    e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) { rc = -1; goto out; }\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) { free(e); rc = -1; goto out; }\n\n    e->blob = malloc(blob_sz);\n    if (!e->blob) { free(e->key); free(e); rc = -1; goto out; }\n\n    memcpy(e->blob, blob, blob_sz);\n    e->blob_sz = blob_sz;\n    e->expiry  = timespec_add_sec(now_monotonic(),\n                 ttl_override_seconds ? ttl_override_seconds : c->default_ttl);\n\n    /* hash insert + LRU */\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->hash, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n    lru_insert_head(c, e);\n\n    c->entry_count++;\n    c->bytes_in_use += blob_sz;\n\n    MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_inserts_total);\n\nout:\n    if (rc != 0)\n        MM_LOG_ERROR(\"ResponseCache: put failed for key=%s (%s)\",\n                     key, strerror(errno));\n    UNLOCK(c);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 void **out_blob,\n                 size_t *out_sz)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !out_blob || !out_sz) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    int rc = -1;\n    LOCK(c);\n\n    purge_expired(c);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, (char *)key, e);\n    if (!e) {\n        MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_miss_total);\n        errno = ENOENT;\n        goto out;\n    }\n\n    struct timespec now = now_monotonic();\n    if (timespec_ge(&now, &e->expiry)) {\n        /* expired \u2014 treat as miss and delete */\n        MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_expired_total);\n        MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_miss_total);\n        MM_LOG_INFO(\"ResponseCache: key expired (%s)\", key);\n        mm_cache_invalidate(c, key);\n        errno = ENOENT;\n        goto out;\n    }\n\n    /* Hit */\n    void *copy = malloc(e->blob_sz);\n    if (!copy) goto out;\n\n    memcpy(copy, e->blob, e->blob_sz);\n\n    *out_blob = copy;\n    *out_sz   = e->blob_sz;\n\n    lru_move_front(c, e);\n\n    MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_hit_total);\n\n    rc = 0;\nout:\n    UNLOCK(c);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n\n    LOCK(c);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, (char *)key, e);\n    if (!e) {\n        UNLOCK(c);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* detach from LRU */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    HASH_DEL(c->hash, e);\n    c->bytes_in_use -= e->blob_sz;\n    c->entry_count--;\n\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n\n    MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_invalidations_total);\n    UNLOCK(c);\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    LOCK(c);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, e, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, e);\n        free(e->blob);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n    }\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n    c->bytes_in_use = c->entry_count = 0;\n    UNLOCK(c);\n\n    MM_METRIC_INC(response_cache_clears_total);\n}\n\nmm_cache_stats_t mm_cache_stats(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    mm_cache_stats_t s = {0,0,0};\n    if (!c) return s;\n\n    LOCK(c);\n    s.entry_count   = c->entry_count;\n    s.max_entries   = c->max_entries;\n    s.bytes_in_use  = c->bytes_in_use;\n    UNLOCK(c);\n    return s;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n *  Basic unit test (run with: `gcc ... -DMM_CACHE_TEST && ./a.out`)\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(4, 2);\n    assert(c);\n\n    const char *key = \"/v1/products?id=42\";\n    const char *payload = \"{ \\\"id\\\": 42 }\";\n\n    assert(mm_cache_put(c, key, payload, strlen(payload)+1, 0) == 0);\n\n    void *out=NULL; size_t out_sz=0;\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, key, &out, &out_sz) == 0);\n    assert(out_sz == strlen(payload)+1);\n    assert(strcmp(out, payload) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    sleep(3); /* TTL expire */\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, key, &out, &out_sz) == -1);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    puts(\"All tests passed\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n\n/* End of file \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

42. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_36.txt",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 36 \u2014 In-memory Response Cache (LRU + TTL)\n *\n * This module implements a thread-safe, size-bounded, in-memory cache intended\n * for HTTP / GraphQL response caching.  It exposes a small API that other\n * layers of the monolith can rely on while remaining agnostic of *how* the\n * caching is performed internally.\n *\n * Design choices:\n *   - LRU eviction strategy, O(1) for get/put.\n *   - TTL per entry.  Expired entries are lazily purged on access.\n *   - Hash table implemented via the public-domain \u201cuthash\u201d project.\n *   - Single pthread mutex per cache instance (coarse-grained, good enough for\n *     typical request rates; can be upgraded to sharded locks if contention\n *     arises).\n *   - Prometheus-style metrics hooks (NO-OP if monitoring subsystem disabled).\n *\n * Compile unit: src/module_36.txt   (named *.txt to avoid symbol collisions in\n *                                     generated demo; treat it as *.c)\n */\n\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Project-wide includes (may be stubbed if the corresponding component is\n * disabled during unit tests).\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"      /* central structured logger               */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n/* Fallback stubs so unit tests can compile this file in isolation. */\n#   define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[DBG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[INF] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[WRN] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[ERR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC\n#   define MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(name)          (void)0\n#   define MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(name, value)     (void)0\n#endif\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash \u2014 public domain hash map\n * Simplified single-file include for this compilation unit.\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define uthash_malloc(sz) malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n#define HASH_BLOOM 16\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_36.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 459
  },
  "type": "text"
}

LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "old_string": "#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Project-wide includes (may be stubbed if the corresponding component is\n * disabled during unit tests).\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"      /* central structured logger               */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n/* Fallback stubs so unit tests can compile this file in isolation. */\n#   define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[DBG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[INF] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[WRN] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[ERR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
