  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_36.txt",
  "newString": "#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */\n\n/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)  LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Project-wide includes (may be stubbed if the corresponding component is\n * disabled during unit tests).\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"      /* central structured logger               */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n/* Fallback stubs so unit tests can compile this file in isolation. */\n#   define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[DBG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[INF] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[WRN] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[ERR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 36 \u2014 In-memory Response Cache (LRU + TTL)\n *\n * This module implements a thread-safe, size-bounded, in-memory cache intended\n * for HTTP / GraphQL response caching.  It exposes a small API that other\n * layers of the monolith can rely on while remaining agnostic of *how* the\n * caching is performed internally.\n *\n * Design choices:\n *   - LRU eviction strategy, O(1) for get/put.\n *   - TTL per entry.  Expired entries are lazily purged on access.\n *   - Hash table implemented via the public-domain \u201cuthash\u201d project.\n *   - Single pthread mutex per cache instance (coarse-grained, good enough for\n *     typical request rates; can be upgraded to sharded locks if contention\n *     arises).\n *   - Prometheus-style metrics hooks (NO-OP if monitoring subsystem disabled).\n *\n * Compile unit: src/module_36.txt   (named *.txt to avoid symbol collisions in\n *                                     generated demo; treat it as *.c)\n */\n\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <sys/time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Project-wide includes (may be stubbed if the corresponding component is\n * disabled during unit tests).\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"      /* central structured logger               */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n/* Fallback stubs so unit tests can compile this file in isolation. */\n#   define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[DBG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[INF] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[WRN] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[ERR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC\n#   define MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(name)          (void)0\n#   define MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(name, value)     (void)0\n#endif\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash \u2014 public domain hash map\n * Simplified single-file include for this compilation unit.\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#define uthash_malloc(sz) malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n#define HASH_BLOOM 16\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\\n        const unsigned char *_ptr = (const unsigned char*)(keyptr);            \\\n        size_t _i;                                                             \\\n        (hashv) = 0xcbf29ce484222325ULL;                                       \\\n        for(_i = 0; _i < (keylen); _i++)                                       \\\n            (hashv) = ((hashv) * 0x100000001b3ULL) ^ (uint64_t)_ptr[_i];       \\\n    } while (0)\n#define HASH_KEYCMP(a,alen,b,blen)                                             \\\n    (((alen)==(blen)) ? memcmp((a),(b),(alen)) : 1)\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public Interface\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#ifdef __cplusplus\nextern \"C\" {\n#endif\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Return codes */\ntypedef enum {\n    MM_CACHE_OK = 0,\n    MM_CACHE_NOT_FOUND,\n    MM_CACHE_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL,\n    MM_CACHE_ERROR          /* generic failure */\n} mm_cache_rc_t;\n\n/*\n * Create a new cache namespace.\n *\n * namespace   \u2013 logical name for metrics/logging (\u201corders\u201d, \u201cproducts\u201d\u2026)\n * capacity    \u2013 max number of entries before eviction starts.\n * ttl_ms      \u2013 time-to-live in milliseconds (applied uniformly per entry).\n *\n * Returns pointer to cache handle, or NULL on allocation failure.\n */\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(const char *namespace,\n                            size_t      capacity,\n                            uint32_t    ttl_ms);\n\n/* Destroy cache and free all associated resources. */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n/*\n * Insert or update an entry.\n * key         \u2013 zero-terminated string (UTF-8).  Internally duplicated.\n * data        \u2013 pointer to binary payload.\n * len         \u2013 payload length in bytes.\n *\n * Returns MM_CACHE_OK or MM_CACHE_ERROR.\n */\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                           const char *key,\n                           const void *data,\n                           size_t      len);\n\n/*\n * Retrieve an entry.\n * key         \u2013 zero-terminated key\n * out_buf     \u2013 caller provided buffer (may be NULL to query length)\n * inout_len   \u2013 IN: buf capacity, OUT: bytes copied / required\n *\n * Returns MM_CACHE_OK, MM_CACHE_NOT_FOUND, MM_CACHE_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL.\n */\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                           const char *key,\n                           void       *out_buf,\n                           size_t     *inout_len);\n\n/* Remove a single key (returns OK if key absent). */\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key);\n\n/* Flush all records immediately. */\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n#ifdef __cplusplus\n}\n#endif\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Implementation Details (private)\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry {\n    char              *key;            /* heap-allocated key */\n    void              *payload;        /* heap-allocated data */\n    size_t             bytes;\n\n    uint64_t           expiry_epoch_ms;\n\n    /* LRU doubly-linked list */\n    struct cache_entry *prev, *next;\n\n    /* uthash handle */\n    UT_hash_handle     hh;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    char        ns[48];        /* namespace label */\n    size_t      capacity;      /* absolute entry limit */\n    uint32_t    ttl_ms;        /* default TTL */\n    size_t      n_entries;\n\n    /* Hash root + LRU head/tail (most-recent at head) */\n    cache_entry_t *map;\n    cache_entry_t *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t *lru_tail;\n\n    pthread_mutex_t mtx;\n};\n\n/*-------------------------------------------------- Helpers ------------*/\n\nstatic uint64_t now_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME_COARSE, &ts);\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return;\n\n    /* unlink */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* push front */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic void lru_append_front(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic void lru_remove(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void evict_lru(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c || !c->lru_tail) return;\n    cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"[cache:%s] Evicting LRU key=%s\", c->ns, victim->key);\n\n    lru_remove(c, victim);\n    HASH_DEL(c->map, victim);\n    c->n_entries--;\n\n    free(victim->key);\n    free(victim->payload);\n    free(victim);\n\n    MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_evictions_total);\n    MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(cache_entries, c->n_entries);\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key,\n                                   const void *data,\n                                   size_t      len,\n                                   uint32_t    ttl_ms)\n{\n    cache_entry_t *e = (cache_entry_t *)calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) return NULL;\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) { free(e); return NULL; }\n\n    e->payload = malloc(len);\n    if (!e->payload) { free(e->key); free(e); return NULL; }\n\n    memcpy(e->payload, data, len);\n    e->bytes = len;\n    e->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms() + ttl_ms;\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic void entry_replace_payload(cache_entry_t *e,\n                                  const void   *data,\n                                  size_t        len,\n                                  uint32_t      ttl_ms)\n{\n    void *newbuf = malloc(len);\n    if (!newbuf) return; /* keep old payload on OOM */\n\n    free(e->payload);\n    e->payload = newbuf;\n    memcpy(e->payload, data, len);\n    e->bytes = len;\n    e->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms() + ttl_ms;\n}\n\n/*------------------------------------------------ Public API ----------*/\n\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(const char *namespace,\n                            size_t      capacity,\n                            uint32_t    ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!namespace || capacity == 0) return NULL;\n    mm_cache_t *c = (mm_cache_t *)calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return NULL;\n\n    strncpy(c->ns, namespace, sizeof(c->ns)-1);\n    c->capacity = capacity;\n    c->ttl_ms   = ttl_ms;\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->mtx, NULL);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"[cache:%s] Created (cap=%zu, ttl=%ums)\",\n                c->ns, capacity, ttl_ms);\n\n    return c;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->map, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->map, cur);\n        free(cur->key);\n        free(cur->payload);\n        free(cur);\n    }\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n    c->n_entries = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->mtx);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"[cache:%s] Destroyed\", c->ns);\n    free(c);\n}\n\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                           const char *key,\n                           const void *data,\n                           size_t      len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !data || len == 0) return MM_CACHE_ERROR;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->map, key, e);\n\n    if (e) {\n        /* Overwrite existing entry. */\n        entry_replace_payload(e, data, len, c->ttl_ms);\n        lru_move_to_front(c, e);\n        MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_updates_total);\n    } else {\n        if (c->n_entries >= c->capacity) {\n            evict_lru(c);\n        }\n        e = entry_create(key, data, len, c->ttl_ms);\n        if (!e) {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n            return MM_CACHE_ERROR;\n        }\n        HASH_ADD_STR(c->map, key, e);\n        lru_append_front(c, e);\n        c->n_entries++;\n\n        MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_inserts_total);\n        MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(cache_entries, c->n_entries);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    return MM_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                           const char *key,\n                           void       *out_buf,\n                           size_t     *inout_len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !inout_len) return MM_CACHE_ERROR;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->map, key, e);\n\n    if (!e) {\n        MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_miss_total);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n        return MM_CACHE_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Expired? */\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n    if (now >= e->expiry_epoch_ms) {\n        MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_expired_total);\n        MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"[cache:%s] Key '%s' expired \u2013 purging\", c->ns, key);\n\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n        HASH_DEL(c->map, e);\n        c->n_entries--;\n\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n        return MM_CACHE_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Buffer size check */\n    if (*inout_len < e->bytes) {\n        *inout_len = e->bytes;\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n        return MM_CACHE_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL;\n    }\n\n    memcpy(out_buf, e->payload, e->bytes);\n    *inout_len = e->bytes;\n\n    lru_move_to_front(c, e);\n\n    MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_hit_total);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    return MM_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\nmm_cache_rc_t mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return MM_CACHE_ERROR;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->map, key, e);\n    if (e) {\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n        HASH_DEL(c->map, e);\n        c->n_entries--;\n\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n\n        MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_invalidate_total);\n        MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(cache_entries, c->n_entries);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    return MM_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->map, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->map, cur);\n        free(cur->key);\n        free(cur->payload);\n        free(cur);\n    }\n    c->lru_head = c->lru_tail = NULL;\n    c->n_entries = 0;\n    MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(cache_flush_total);\n    MM_METRIC_GAUGE_SET(cache_entries, 0);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"[cache:%s] Cleared all entries\", c->ns);\n}\n\n/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * End of file\n *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " #include <sys/time.h>",
        " #include <pthread.h>",
        " ",
        "-/*------------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "- * Project-wide includes (may be stubbed if the corresponding component is",
        "- * disabled during unit tests).",
        "- *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/",
        "-",
        "-#include \"mm_logger.h\"      /* central structured logger               */",
        "+#include \"logging.h\"",
        " #include \"mm_metrics.h\"     /* Prometheus-compatible monitoring API    */",
        " ",
        "-#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG",
        "-/* Fallback stubs so unit tests can compile this file in isolation. */",
        "-#   define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[DBG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#   define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[INF] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#   define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)    fprintf(stderr, \"[WRN] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#   define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[ERR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#endif",
        "+/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */",
        "+#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)  LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        " ",
        " #ifndef MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC",
        " #   define MM_METRIC_COUNTER_INC(name)          (void)0"
      ],

{

}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 33 \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * File:    src/module_33.txt   (intended to compile as C source)\n * Author:  MercuryMonolith Core Team\n *\n * Synopsis:\n *   A production-grade in-memory response cache that offers:\n *     \u2022 O(1) look-ups via uthash\n *     \u2022 LRU eviction strategy with byte-level capacity enforcement\n *     \u2022 Per-entry TTL with periodic expiry pruning\n *     \u2022 Fully thread-safe (reader/writer locks)\n *     \u2022 Built-in Prometheus-compatible metrics (hits, misses, evictions)\n *\n * Compile flags: -pthread\n *\n * External deps:\n *   \u2022 uthash      \u2013 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n *   \u2022 mm_logger.h \u2013 Project-internal structured logging interface\n *   \u2022 mm_metrics.h\u2013 Project-internal metrics aggregator\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"   /* TRACE(), INFO(), WARN(), ERROR() */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"  /* mm_metrics_increment(const char *name, uint64_t val) */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Fallback stubs to allow independent compilation during unit tests\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef TRACE\n#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  (void)0\n#define INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef mm_metrics_increment\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_increment(const char *metric, uint64_t v) {\n    (void)metric; (void)v;\n}\n#endif\n\n/* ***************************************************************************\n * Data Structures\n * ***************************************************************************/\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char                     *key;        /* heap-allocated, NULL-terminated   */\n    void                     *data;       /* opaque payload (heap-allocated)   */\n    size_t                    data_size;  /* number of bytes in data           */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_33.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 512
  },
  "type": "text"
}

LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",

  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"  /* mm_metrics_increment(const char *name, uint64_t val) */\n\n/* Map legacy logging macros to new centralized logging */\n#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"   /* TRACE(), INFO(), WARN(), ERROR() */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"  /* mm_metrics_increment(const char *name, uint64_t val) */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Fallback stubs to allow independent compilation during unit tests\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef TRACE\n#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  (void)0\n#define INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 33 \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * File:    src/module_33.txt   (intended to compile as C source)\n * Author:  MercuryMonolith Core Team\n *\n * Synopsis:\n *   A production-grade in-memory response cache that offers:\n *     \u2022 O(1) look-ups via uthash\n *     \u2022 LRU eviction strategy with byte-level capacity enforcement\n *     \u2022 Per-entry TTL with periodic expiry pruning\n *     \u2022 Fully thread-safe (reader/writer locks)\n *     \u2022 Built-in Prometheus-compatible metrics (hits, misses, evictions)\n *\n * Compile flags: -pthread\n *\n * External deps:\n *   \u2022 uthash      \u2013 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n *   \u2022 mm_logger.h \u2013 Project-internal structured logging interface\n *   \u2022 mm_metrics.h\u2013 Project-internal metrics aggregator\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"mm_logger.h\"   /* TRACE(), INFO(), WARN(), ERROR() */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\"  /* mm_metrics_increment(const char *name, uint64_t val) */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Fallback stubs to allow independent compilation during unit tests\n * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef TRACE\n#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  (void)0\n#define INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef mm_metrics_increment\nstatic inline void mm_metrics_increment(const char *metric, uint64_t v) {\n    (void)metric; (void)v;\n}\n#endif\n\n/* ***************************************************************************\n * Data Structures\n * ***************************************************************************/\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char                     *key;        /* heap-allocated, NULL-terminated   */\n    void                     *data;       /* opaque payload (heap-allocated)   */\n    size_t                    data_size;  /* number of bytes in data           */\n    time_t                    expiry;     /* epoch when entry becomes invalid  */\n\n    struct mm_cache_entry    *prev;       /* doubly-linked LRU list            */\n    struct mm_cache_entry    *next;\n\n    UT_hash_handle            hh;         /* uthash linkage                    */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache {\n    size_t                    capacity;   /* user-specified byte cap           */\n    size_t                    size;       /* current memory footprint          */\n    time_t                    default_ttl;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t         *entries;    /* hash table root                   */\n    mm_cache_entry_t         *head;       /* MRU                               */\n    mm_cache_entry_t         *tail;       /* LRU                               */\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t          rwlock;\n\n    uint64_t                  hits;\n    uint64_t                  misses;\n    uint64_t                  evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* ***************************************************************************\n * Internal helpers\n * ***************************************************************************/\n\n/* push entry to head (MRU) of LRU list */\nstatic inline void _lru_move_to_head(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->head == e) return;\n\n    /* detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->tail == e) c->tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* prepend */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->head;\n    if (c->head) c->head->prev = e;\n    c->head = e;\n    if (!c->tail) c->tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic inline void _lru_append_head(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->head;\n    if (c->head) c->head->prev = e;\n    c->head = e;\n    if (!c->tail) c->tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic void _evict_tail(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c->tail) return;\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->tail;\n    /* detach from LRU */\n    if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = NULL;\n    c->tail = victim->prev;\n    if (c->head == victim) c->head = NULL;\n\n    /* remove from hash */\n    HASH_DEL(c->entries, victim);\n\n    c->size -= victim->data_size;\n\n    mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_evictions_total\", 1);\n    c->evictions++;\n\n    free(victim->data);\n    free(victim->key);\n    free(victim);\n}\n\n/* Remove expired entries starting from LRU side for efficiency */\nstatic void _prune_expired(mm_cache_t *c, time_t now)\n{\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cursor = c->tail;\n    while (cursor) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *prev = cursor->prev;\n        if (cursor->expiry <= now) {\n            /* eviction path same as _evict_tail but arbitrary entry */\n            mm_cache_entry_t *victim = cursor;\n\n            /* detach from LRU */\n            if (victim->prev) victim->prev->next = victim->next;\n            if (victim->next) victim->next->prev = victim->prev;\n            if (c->head == victim) c->head = victim->next;\n            if (c->tail == victim) c->tail = victim->prev;\n\n            /* remove from hash */\n            HASH_DEL(c->entries, victim);\n\n            c->size -= victim->data_size;\n\n            mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_expired_total\", 1);\n            free(victim->data);\n            free(victim->key);\n            free(victim);\n        }\n        cursor = prev;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Ensure cache fits within capacity (evict LRU until ok) */\nstatic void _ensure_capacity(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while (c->size > c->capacity && c->tail) {\n        _evict_tail(c);\n    }\n}\n\n/* ***************************************************************************\n * Public API\n * ***************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_create\n * ---------------\n * Allocate an empty cache with a fixed byte capacity.\n *\n * capacity_bytes : total bytes allowed for all stored payloads\n * default_ttl    : TTL in seconds if individual put() call does not specify\n *\n * returns heap-allocated cache instance or NULL on failure.\n */\nmm_cache_t *mm_cache_create(size_t capacity_bytes, time_t default_ttl)\n{\n    if (capacity_bytes == 0) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return NULL;\n\n    c->capacity    = capacity_bytes;\n    c->default_ttl = default_ttl ? default_ttl : 300; /* sane default 5 min */\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    TRACE(\"cache created with capacity=%zu bytes, default_ttl=%\"PRIuMAX\"s\",\n          c->capacity, (uintmax_t)c->default_ttl);\n\n    return c;\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_destroy\n * ----------------\n * Release all allocated resources associated with cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->entries, e, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->entries, e);\n        free(e->data);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n\n    TRACE(\"cache destroyed (hits=%\"PRIu64\", misses=%\"PRIu64\", evictions=%\"PRIu64\")\",\n          c->hits, c->misses, c->evictions);\n\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_get\n * ------------\n * Look up key. Returns a freshly allocated copy of payload if found\n * (caller owns returned buffer). Thread-safe. Returns 1 on hit, 0 on miss.\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key,\n                 void **out_data, size_t *out_size)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !out_data || !out_size) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->entries, key, e);\n    if (e && e->expiry > now) {\n        /* Hit */\n        *out_size = e->data_size;\n        void *buf = malloc(e->data_size);\n        if (!buf) {\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n            errno = ENOMEM;\n            return 0;\n        }\n        memcpy(buf, e->data, e->data_size);\n        *out_data = buf;\n\n        c->hits++;\n        mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_hits_total\", 1);\n\n        /* promote to MRU */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n        _lru_move_to_head(c, e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    /* Miss or expired */\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    if (e) {\n        /* prune expired entry */\n        _lru_move_to_head(c, e); /* not strictly needed */\n        _evict_tail(c);          /* _evict_tail always removes current tail,\n                                    but above move ensures e is tail */\n    }\n    c->misses++;\n    mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_misses_total\", 1);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    *out_data = NULL;\n    *out_size = 0;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_put\n * ------------\n * Store (key,data) pair. If key already exists it will be replaced.\n * TTL 0 -> use cache default. TTL < 0 (SIZE_MAX) -> no expiration.\n *\n * Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure (errno set).\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key,\n                 const void *data, size_t data_size,\n                 time_t ttl)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !data || data_size == 0) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    /* Remove existing entry if any */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->entries, key, e);\n    if (e) {\n        /* detach from LRU */\n        if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n        if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n        if (c->head == e) c->head = e->next;\n        if (c->tail == e) c->tail = e->prev;\n\n        HASH_DEL(c->entries, e);\n        c->size -= e->data_size;\n        free(e->data);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n    }\n\n    /* allocate new entry */\n    e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        errno = ENOMEM;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    e->data = malloc(data_size);\n    if (!e->key || !e->data) {\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->data);\n        free(e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        errno = ENOMEM;\n        return -1;\n    }\n    memcpy(e->data, data, data_size);\n    e->data_size = data_size;\n\n    if (ttl == (time_t)SIZE_MAX) {\n        e->expiry = INT64_MAX; /* practically infinite */\n    } else {\n        e->expiry = now + (ttl ? ttl : c->default_ttl);\n    }\n\n    /* insert into structures */\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->entries, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n    _lru_append_head(c, e);\n\n    c->size += data_size;\n\n    /* House-keeping: ensure capacity & prune */\n    _prune_expired(c, now);\n    _ensure_capacity(c);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n * -------------------\n * Remove a cached entry if present.\n *\n * Returns 1 if entry existed & was removed, 0 otherwise.\n */\nint mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->entries, key, e);\n    if (!e) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* detach from LRU */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->head == e) c->head = e->next;\n    if (c->tail == e) c->tail = e->prev;\n\n    HASH_DEL(c->entries, e);\n    c->size -= e->data_size;\n\n    free(e->data);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_invalidations_total\", 1);\n    return 1;\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_clear\n * --------------\n * Remove every entry from cache.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->entries, e, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->entries, e);\n        free(e->data);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n    }\n    c->size   = 0;\n    c->head   = c->tail = NULL;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    mm_metrics_increment(\"cache_clears_total\", 1);\n}\n\n/**\n * mm_cache_stats\n * --------------\n * Snapshot internal counters for diagnostics/monitoring.\n */\nvoid mm_cache_stats(mm_cache_t *c,\n                    uint64_t *hits,\n                    uint64_t *misses,\n                    uint64_t *evictions,\n                    size_t   *cur_bytes,\n                    size_t   *capacity)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    if (hits)      *hits      = c->hits;\n    if (misses)    *misses    = c->misses;\n    if (evictions) *evictions = c->evictions;\n    if (cur_bytes) *cur_bytes = c->size;\n    if (capacity)  *capacity  = c->capacity;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ***************************************************************************\n * TEST HARNESS (compile with -DMM_CACHE_TEST to run)\n * ***************************************************************************/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void basic_test(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache = mm_cache_create(1024, 60);\n    assert(cache);\n\n    const char *key = \"foo\";\n    const char *val = \"bar\";\n\n    assert(mm_cache_get(cache, key, (void **)&val, &(size_t){0}) == 0);\n\n    assert(mm_cache_put(cache, key, val, strlen(val) + 1, 0) == 0);\n\n    char *out = NULL;\n    size_t sz;\n    assert(mm_cache_get(cache, key, (void **)&out, &sz) == 1);\n    assert(strcmp(out, val) == 0 && sz == strlen(val) + 1);\n    free(out);\n\n    assert(mm_cache_invalidate(cache, key) == 1);\n    assert(mm_cache_get(cache, key, (void **)&out, &sz) == 0);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(cache);\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    basic_test();\n    puts(\"mm_cache: all tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n\n/* End of module_33.txt */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [

        " ",
        "-#include \"mm_logger.h\"   /* TRACE(), INFO(), WARN(), ERROR() */",
        "+#include \"logging.h\"",
        " #include \"mm_metrics.h\"  /* mm_metrics_increment(const char *name, uint64_t val) */",
        " ",
        "-/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "- * Fallback stubs to allow independent compilation during unit tests",
        "- * -------------------------------------------------------------------------*/",
        "-#ifndef TRACE",
        "-#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  (void)0",
        "-#define INFO(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[INFO]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#define WARN(fmt, ...)   fprintf(stderr, \"[WARN]  \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#endif",
        "+/* Map legacy logging macros to new centralized logging */",
        "+#define TRACE(fmt, ...)  LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define INFO(fmt, ...)   LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define WARN(fmt, ...)   LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define ERROR(fmt, ...)  LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
        " ",
        " #ifndef mm_metrics_increment",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * File: src/module_59.c\n *\n * This module provides an in-memory response cache used by the API gateway\n * layer to accelerate read-heavy REST & GraphQL queries.  The cache supports:\n *\n *   \u2022 TTL-based expiration (per-entry or default)\n *   \u2022 Size-bounded eviction using a classic LRU algorithm\n *   \u2022 Thread-safety via POSIX rw-locks\n *   \u2022 Lightweight, zero-dependency design (uses uthash for hash-table)\n *   \u2022 Prometheus-style metrics export\n *\n * Dependencies:\n *   \u2013 pthread (POSIX)\n *   \u2013 uthash  (single header hash-map; vendored under util/uthash.h)\n *\n * Copyright (c) MercuryMonolith.  MIT License.\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"util/uthash.h\" /* third-party, single header; not shown */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Logging macros \u2013 fall back to stderr if the central logger is disabled   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MM_LOG_ERROR\n#    define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n#    define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Monotonic time helper                                                   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n#ifdef CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n#else\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n#endif\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);",

  "old_string": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"util/uthash.h\" /* third-party, single header; not shown */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Logging macros \u2013 fall back to stderr if the central logger is disabled   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MM_LOG_ERROR\n#    define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n#    define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",

  "newString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"util/uthash.h\" /* third-party, single header; not shown */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"util/uthash.h\" /* third-party, single header; not shown */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Logging macros \u2013 fall back to stderr if the central logger is disabled   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MM_LOG_ERROR\n#    define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n#    define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * File: src/module_59.c\n *\n * This module provides an in-memory response cache used by the API gateway\n * layer to accelerate read-heavy REST & GraphQL queries.  The cache supports:\n *\n *   \u2022 TTL-based expiration (per-entry or default)\n *   \u2022 Size-bounded eviction using a classic LRU algorithm\n *   \u2022 Thread-safety via POSIX rw-locks\n *   \u2022 Lightweight, zero-dependency design (uses uthash for hash-table)\n *   \u2022 Prometheus-style metrics export\n *\n * Dependencies:\n *   \u2013 pthread (POSIX)\n *   \u2013 uthash  (single header hash-map; vendored under util/uthash.h)\n *\n * Copyright (c) MercuryMonolith.  MIT License.\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"util/uthash.h\" /* third-party, single header; not shown */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Logging macros \u2013 fall back to stderr if the central logger is disabled   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MM_LOG_ERROR\n#    define MM_LOG_ERROR(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[ERROR] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG_DEBUG\n#    define MM_LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ...) \\\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Monotonic time helper                                                   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n#ifdef CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n#else\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n#endif\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Cache data structures                                                   */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\ntypedef struct cache_entry_s\n{\n    char           *key;\n    size_t          key_len;\n\n    void           *payload;\n    size_t          payload_len;\n\n    uint64_t        expire_at_ms;\n\n    /* LRU list pointers */\n    struct cache_entry_s *prev;\n    struct cache_entry_s *next;\n\n    /* uthash handle for key lookup */\n    UT_hash_handle   hh;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_stats_s\n{\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_stats_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_s\n{\n    char              namespace[64];\n\n    size_t            max_items;\n    uint64_t          default_ttl_ms;\n\n    cache_entry_t    *table;     /* uthash head */\n\n    /* LRU double-linked list head/tail */\n    cache_entry_t    *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t    *lru_tail;\n\n    mm_cache_stats_t  stats;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t  lock;      /* protects everything */\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Forward declarations                                                    */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\nstatic void        lru_promote(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void        lru_append(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void        lru_remove(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void        evict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c);\nstatic void        purge_expired(mm_cache_t *c, uint64_t now);\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Public API                                                              */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_create\n *    Allocate and initialise a new in-memory cache.\n */\nmm_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(size_t max_items, uint64_t default_ttl_ms, const char *namespace)\n{\n    if (max_items == 0 || default_ttl_ms == 0 || !namespace || !*namespace)\n    {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c)\n        return NULL;\n\n    snprintf(c->namespace, sizeof(c->namespace), \"%s\", namespace);\n    c->max_items       = max_items;\n    c->default_ttl_ms  = default_ttl_ms;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->lock, NULL) != 0)\n    {\n        free(c);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"cache[%s] created (max=%zu, ttl=%\" PRIu64 \"ms)\", c->namespace,\n                 c->max_items, c->default_ttl_ms);\n    return c;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_destroy\n *    Free cache and all entries.\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c)\n        return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->table, e, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->lock);\n    free(c);\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"cache destroyed\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get\n *    Retrieve a value.  Ownership of out_value buffer is transferred to caller.\n *    Caller must free(*out_value) when done.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key, char **out_value, size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !out_value || !out_len)\n        return false;\n\n    uint64_t     now = now_ms();\n    bool         found = false;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock); /* write lock for potential LRU update */\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n    if (e && (e->expire_at_ms > now))\n    {\n        /* Hit */\n        c->stats.hits++;\n        lru_promote(c, e);\n\n        *out_len   = e->payload_len;\n        *out_value = malloc(e->payload_len);\n        if (!*out_value)\n        {\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(*out_value, e->payload, e->payload_len);\n        found = true;\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Miss or expired */\n        if (e)\n        {\n            lru_remove(c, e);\n            HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e);\n            c->stats.evictions++; /* count expiry as eviction */\n        }\n        c->stats.misses++;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return found;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put\n *    Insert or replace a value.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key, const char *value, size_t len,\n             uint64_t ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !value || len == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool ok = false;\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n\n    if (e) /* Update existing */\n    {\n        free(e->payload);\n        e->payload     = malloc(len);\n        if (!e->payload)\n            goto done;\n\n        memcpy(e->payload, value, len);\n        e->payload_len = len;\n        e->expire_at_ms = now + (ttl_ms ? ttl_ms : c->default_ttl_ms);\n        lru_promote(c, e);\n    }\n    else /* New entry */\n    {\n        e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n        if (!e)\n            goto done;\n\n        e->key = strdup(key);\n        e->payload = malloc(len);\n        if (!e->key || !e->payload)\n            goto alloc_fail;\n\n        memcpy(e->payload, value, len);\n        e->payload_len = len;\n        e->key_len     = strlen(key);\n        e->expire_at_ms = now + (ttl_ms ? ttl_ms : c->default_ttl_ms);\n\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->table, e->key, e->key_len, e);\n        lru_append(c, e);\n        evict_if_needed(c);\n    }\n    ok = true;\n    goto done;\n\nalloc_fail:\n    if (e)\n    {\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n    }\n\ndone:\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return ok;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate\n *    Remove a single key from the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key)\n        return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n    if (e)\n    {\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n        c->stats.evictions++;\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge\n *    Remove every entry regardless of TTL.\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_purge(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c)\n        return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n    cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->table, e, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n        c->stats.evictions++;\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get_stats\n */\nmm_cache_stats_t\nmm_cache_get_stats(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    mm_cache_stats_t snapshot = {0};\n    if (!c)\n        return snapshot;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->lock);\n    snapshot = c->stats;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    return snapshot;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_export_metrics\n *    Dump Prometheus-text-format metrics into the given FILE*.\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_export_metrics(mm_cache_t *c, FILE *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out)\n        return;\n\n    mm_cache_stats_t s = mm_cache_get_stats(c);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n            \"# HELP mm_cache_hits_total Total cache hits\\n\"\n            \"# TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n            \"mm_cache_hits_total{namespace=\\\"%s\\\"} %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\",\n            c->namespace, s.hits);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n            \"# HELP mm_cache_misses_total Total cache misses\\n\"\n            \"# TYPE mm_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n            \"mm_cache_misses_total{namespace=\\\"%s\\\"} %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\",\n            c->namespace, s.misses);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n            \"# HELP mm_cache_evictions_total Total evictions (LRU + expiry)\\n\"\n            \"# TYPE mm_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n            \"mm_cache_evictions_total{namespace=\\\"%s\\\"} %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\",\n            c->namespace, s.evictions);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->lock);\n    size_t current_size = HASH_COUNT(c->table);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n            \"# HELP mm_cache_items Current number of cached items\\n\"\n            \"# TYPE mm_cache_items gauge\\n\"\n            \"mm_cache_items{namespace=\\\"%s\\\"} %zu\\n\",\n            c->namespace, current_size);\n}\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Internal helpers                                                        */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n\n/* Move node to front of LRU list */\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e)\n        return; /* already at front */\n\n    lru_remove(c, e);\n    lru_append(c, e);\n}\n\n/* Append node to front (most recently used) */\nstatic void\nlru_append(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n\n    if (c->lru_head)\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n\n    if (!c->lru_tail)\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Remove node from list (does not free) */\nstatic void\nlru_remove(mm_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e)\n        return;\n\n    if (e->prev)\n        e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next)\n        e->next->prev = e->prev;\n\n    if (c->lru_head == e)\n        c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e)\n        c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\n/* Evict until size <= max_items */\nstatic void\nevict_if_needed(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    /* First remove expired items quickly */\n    purge_expired(c, now);\n\n    while (HASH_COUNT(c->table) > c->max_items && c->lru_tail)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n        lru_remove(c, victim);\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, victim);\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->payload);\n        free(victim);\n        c->stats.evictions++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Remove expired entries during write operations */\nstatic void\npurge_expired(mm_cache_t *c, uint64_t now)\n{\n    cache_entry_t *e = c->lru_tail;\n    /* starting from tail is efficient because older items are more likely expired */\n    while (e)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = e->prev;\n        if (e->expire_at_ms > now)\n            break;\n\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e->payload);\n        free(e);\n        c->stats.evictions++;\n        e = prev;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Minimal self-test (compiles with -DMM_CACHE_SELF_TEST)                  */\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_SELF_TEST\n#include <stdio.h>\nint\nmain(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(2 /* max */, 1000 /* ms */, \"test\");\n    assert(c);\n\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"key1\", \"value1\", 6, 0);\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"key2\", \"value2\", 6, 0);\n\n    char *buf;\n    size_t len;\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, \"key1\", &buf, &len) == true);\n    assert(strncmp(buf, \"value1\", len) == 0);\n    free(buf);\n\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"key3\", \"value3\", 6, 0); /* triggers eviction */\n\n    assert(mm_cache_get(c, \"key2\", &buf, &len) == false); /* should be evicted */\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    puts(\"self-test passed\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

        " ",

        "-/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/",
        "-/* Logging macros \u2013 fall back to stderr if the central logger is disabled   */",
        "-/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/",
        "-#ifndef MM_LOG_ERROR",

        " /*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/",

    "content": "```c\n/**\n * src/cache/response_cache.c\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * A high-performance, thread-safe, in-memory LRU cache for serialized HTTP /\n * GraphQL responses.  The cache supports TTL-based eviction, Prometheus-style\n * metrics, structured logging, and graceful shutdown.  This component is\n * designed to be embedded in a single-process \u201cmicro-service\u201d monolith and\n * therefore avoids global symbols other than the public API declared in\n * response_cache.h.\n *\n * Build flags:\n *   -std=c11 -pthread -DMERCURY_DEBUG\n *\n * Author:    MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * Copyright: (c) 2024 Pioneer Retail Systems\n */\n\n#include \"response_cache.h\"\n\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n#ifdef MERCURY_DEBUG\n#    define DEBUG_LOG(fmt, ...)                                                  \\\n        do {                                                                     \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[RCACHE][DEBUG] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__);         \\\n        } while (0)\n#else\n#    define DEBUG_LOG(...) /* no-op */\n#endif\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/*                              Internal structs                               */\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry_t {\n    char                     *key;       /* Heap-allocated, null-terminated.     */\n    uint8_t                  *payload;   /* Serialized response buffer.          */\n    size_t                    payload_sz;\n    uint64_t                  expires_at;/* Epoch millis.                        */\n    struct cache_entry_t     *prev;      /* LRU doubly-linked list.              */\n    struct cache_entry_t     *next;\n    struct cache_entry_t     *hnext;     /* Hash chain link.                     */\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t          capacity;      /* Max # entries                             */\n    uint64_t        default_ttl;   /* Milliseconds                              */\n    /* State */",
