{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_69.txt",
  "newString": "    if (removed)\n        LOG_INFO(\"Invalidated key='%s'\", key);\n    else\n        LOG_DEBUG(\"Invalidate request for missing key='%s'\", key);",
  "oldString": "    if (removed)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Invalidated key='%s'\", key);\n    else\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"DEBUG\", \"Invalidate request for missing key='%s'\", key);",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 69  \u2013  Response Cache Manager\n *\n * Description:\n *   Thread-safe in-memory response cache with TTL, LRU eviction,\n *   structured logging, and Prometheus-compatible metrics export.\n *\n *   This component is used by the API-gateway layer to accelerate\n *   frequently requested REST / GraphQL queries while retaining\n *   fine-grained invalidation hooks for the service & repository\n *   layers.  It is deliberately implemented without third-party\n *   dependencies to keep the monolith self-contained.\n *\n * Author:  MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* =========================  Configuration  ========================= */\n\n/* Maximum number of cached objects (soft limit, eviction is best-effort). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_CAPACITY\n#  define MM_CACHE_CAPACITY 4096\n#endif\n\n/* Default TTL in seconds unless overridden by caller. */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL\n#  define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL 60\n#endif\n\n/* Hash map backend \u2014 simple open addressing; power-of-two buckets. */\n#define BUCKET_COUNT 8192u   /* Must be power-of-two for fast masking. */\n#define BUCKET_MASK  (BUCKET_COUNT - 1)\n\n/* =========================  Data Types  ========================= */\n\ntypedef struct _cache_entry {\n    /* Key & data */\n    char            *key;          /* NULL-terminated */\n    uint8_t         *blob;         /* Arbitrary opaque payload */\n    size_t           blob_len;\n    time_t           expiry;       /* Absolute UTC seconds */\n\n    /* LRU chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *prev;\n    struct _cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* Hash chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *hnext;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct\n{\n    cache_entry_t  *buckets[BUCKET_COUNT];\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;      /* Most recently used */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;      /* Least recently used */\n\n    size_t          size;          /* # of live entries */\n    pthread_mutex_t mtx;\n\n    /* Prometheus metrics (atomic for fast reads) */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* =========================  Utilities  ========================= */\n\nstatic inline uint32_t\nfnv1a_32(const char *data)\n{\n    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;\n    while (*data)\n    {\n        hash ^= (uint8_t)(*data++);\n        hash *= 16777619u;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Core Primitives  ========================= */\n\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    /* Already at head? */\n    if (cache->lru_head == ce) return;\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n    if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_tail == ce)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n    /* Insert at head */\n    ce->prev          = NULL;\n    ce->next          = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head   = ce;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_append(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    ce->prev = NULL;\n    ce->next = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head = ce;\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Public API  ========================= */\n\nmm_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_cache_t));\n    if (!cache)\n    {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"Failed to allocate cache\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_init(&cache->mtx, NULL);\n    return cache;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&cache->mtx);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_insert(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    ce->hnext = cache->buckets[h];\n    cache->buckets[h] = ce;\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *\nhash_lookup(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[h];\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        if (strcmp(cur->key, key) == 0)\n            return cur;\n        cur = cur->hnext;\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_remove(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t **cur = &cache->buckets[h];\n    while (*cur)\n    {\n        if (*cur == ce)\n        {\n            *cur = ce->hnext;\n            return;\n        }\n        cur = &(*cur)->hnext;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put:\n *   Stores (or overwrites) a payload under the specified key.\n *   `ttl_sec` of 0 means \"use default TTL\".\n *\n *   The payload is copied; the caller retains ownership of the input buffer.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             const uint8_t *payload,\n             size_t len,\n             uint32_t ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || len == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool replaced = false;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Replace */\n        free(ce->blob);\n        ce->blob     = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->blob)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n        replaced     = true;\n\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Make new entry */\n        ce = calloc(1, sizeof(cache_entry_t));\n        if (!ce)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        ce->key  = strdup(key);\n        ce->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->key || !ce->blob)\n        {\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n\n        hash_insert(cache, ce);\n        lru_append(cache, ce);\n        cache->size++;\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if over capacity */\n    while (cache->size > MM_CACHE_CAPACITY && cache->lru_tail)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n\n        if (victim->prev)\n            victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        cache->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == victim)\n            cache->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        hash_remove(cache, victim);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->blob);\n        free(victim);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"%s entry for key='%s' (ttl=%us)\",\n             replaced ? \"Updated\" : \"Cached\", key,\n             (unsigned)(ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL));\n    return true;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get:\n *   Returns a malloc'd copy of the cached payload (caller responsible\n *   for free()) or NULL if not found / expired.\n */\nuint8_t *\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return NULL;\n\n    uint8_t *copy = NULL;\n    time_t   now  = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n\n    if (ce && ce->expiry >= now)\n    {\n        /* Hit */\n        copy = malloc(ce->blob_len);\n        if (copy)\n        {\n            memcpy(copy, ce->blob, ce->blob_len);\n            if (out_len) *out_len = ce->blob_len;\n        }\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Miss or expired */\n        if (ce)\n        {\n            /* Remove expired entry */\n            if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n            if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n            hash_remove(cache, ce);\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            cache->size--;\n\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    return copy;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate:\n *   Removes a single cache entry if present.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return false;\n\n    bool removed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Detach from LRU */\n        if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n        if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n        if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n        /* Hash remove */\n        hash_remove(cache, ce);\n\n        free(ce->key);\n        free(ce->blob);\n        free(ce);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        removed = true;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (removed)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Invalidated key='%s'\", key);\n    else\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"DEBUG\", \"Invalidate request for missing key='%s'\", key);\n\n    return removed;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_flush_all:\n *   Invalidates ALL entries in the cache (use sparingly).\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_flush_all(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n        cache->buckets[i] = NULL;\n    }\n    cache->lru_head = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->size     = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Cache flushed\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_prometheus_export:\n *   Exports cache metrics as a newline-delimited Prometheus text\n *   exposition format block.  The caller must free() the returned\n *   string when done.\n *\n *   Example output:\n *     # TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\n *     mm_cache_hits_total 1024\n *     ...\n */\nchar *\nmm_cache_prometheus_export(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return NULL;\n\n    /* We know exactly how many lines we have \u2014 pre-size buffer. */\n    const char *tpl =\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_hits_total Total number of cache hits\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_hits_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_misses_total Total number of cache misses\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_misses_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_evictions_total Total number of evictions/expirations\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_evictions_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_entries Gauge of current cache entries\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_entries gauge\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_entries %zu\\n\";\n\n    size_t sz = snprintf(NULL, 0, tpl,\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n                         cache->size);\n\n    char *buf = malloc(sz + 1);\n    if (!buf) return NULL;\n\n    snprintf(buf, sz + 1, tpl,\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n             cache->size);\n\n    return buf;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Periodic Maintenance  ========================= */\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge_expired:\n *   Scans and removes expired entries (O(N) worst-case).  Intended to be\n *   triggered by a housekeeping thread every few seconds.\n */\nsize_t\nmm_cache_purge_expired(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return 0;\n\n    size_t purged = 0;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->lru_tail; /* Start from cold tail */\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = cur->prev;\n\n        if (cur->expiry < now)\n        {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (cur->prev) cur->prev->next = cur->next;\n            if (cur->next) cur->next->prev = cur->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == cur) cache->lru_head = cur->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == cur) cache->lru_tail = cur->prev;\n\n            /* Hash remove */\n            hash_remove(cache, cur);\n\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n\n            cache->size--;\n            purged++;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        cur = prev;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (purged)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);\n\n    return purged;\n}\n```",

  "originalFile": "```c\n/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 69  \u2013  Response Cache Manager\n *\n * Description:\n *   Thread-safe in-memory response cache with TTL, LRU eviction,\n *   structured logging, and Prometheus-compatible metrics export.\n *\n *   This component is used by the API-gateway layer to accelerate\n *   frequently requested REST / GraphQL queries while retaining\n *   fine-grained invalidation hooks for the service & repository\n *   layers.  It is deliberately implemented without third-party\n *   dependencies to keep the monolith self-contained.\n *\n * Author:  MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* =========================  Configuration  ========================= */\n\n/* Maximum number of cached objects (soft limit, eviction is best-effort). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_CAPACITY\n#  define MM_CACHE_CAPACITY 4096\n#endif\n\n/* Default TTL in seconds unless overridden by caller. */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL\n#  define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL 60\n#endif\n\n/* Hash map backend \u2014 simple open addressing; power-of-two buckets. */\n#define BUCKET_COUNT 8192u   /* Must be power-of-two for fast masking. */\n#define BUCKET_MASK  (BUCKET_COUNT - 1)\n\n/* =========================  Data Types  ========================= */\n\ntypedef struct _cache_entry {\n    /* Key & data */\n    char            *key;          /* NULL-terminated */\n    uint8_t         *blob;         /* Arbitrary opaque payload */\n    size_t           blob_len;\n    time_t           expiry;       /* Absolute UTC seconds */\n\n    /* LRU chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *prev;\n    struct _cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* Hash chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *hnext;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct\n{\n    cache_entry_t  *buckets[BUCKET_COUNT];\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;      /* Most recently used */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;      /* Least recently used */\n\n    size_t          size;          /* # of live entries */\n    pthread_mutex_t mtx;\n\n    /* Prometheus metrics (atomic for fast reads) */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* =========================  Utilities  ========================= */\n\nstatic inline uint32_t\nfnv1a_32(const char *data)\n{\n    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;\n    while (*data)\n    {\n        hash ^= (uint8_t)(*data++);\n        hash *= 16777619u;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Core Primitives  ========================= */\n\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    /* Already at head? */\n    if (cache->lru_head == ce) return;\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n    if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_tail == ce)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n    /* Insert at head */\n    ce->prev          = NULL;\n    ce->next          = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head   = ce;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_append(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    ce->prev = NULL;\n    ce->next = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head = ce;\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Public API  ========================= */\n\nmm_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_cache_t));\n    if (!cache)\n    {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"Failed to allocate cache\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_init(&cache->mtx, NULL);\n    return cache;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&cache->mtx);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_insert(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    ce->hnext = cache->buckets[h];\n    cache->buckets[h] = ce;\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *\nhash_lookup(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[h];\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        if (strcmp(cur->key, key) == 0)\n            return cur;\n        cur = cur->hnext;\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_remove(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t **cur = &cache->buckets[h];\n    while (*cur)\n    {\n        if (*cur == ce)\n        {\n            *cur = ce->hnext;\n            return;\n        }\n        cur = &(*cur)->hnext;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put:\n *   Stores (or overwrites) a payload under the specified key.\n *   `ttl_sec` of 0 means \"use default TTL\".\n *\n *   The payload is copied; the caller retains ownership of the input buffer.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             const uint8_t *payload,\n             size_t len,\n             uint32_t ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || len == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool replaced = false;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Replace */\n        free(ce->blob);\n        ce->blob     = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->blob)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n        replaced     = true;\n\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Make new entry */\n        ce = calloc(1, sizeof(cache_entry_t));\n        if (!ce)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        ce->key  = strdup(key);\n        ce->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->key || !ce->blob)\n        {\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n\n        hash_insert(cache, ce);\n        lru_append(cache, ce);\n        cache->size++;\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if over capacity */\n    while (cache->size > MM_CACHE_CAPACITY && cache->lru_tail)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n\n        if (victim->prev)\n            victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        cache->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == victim)\n            cache->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        hash_remove(cache, victim);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->blob);\n        free(victim);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"%s entry for key='%s' (ttl=%us)\",\n             replaced ? \"Updated\" : \"Cached\", key,\n             (unsigned)(ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL));\n    return true;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get:\n *   Returns a malloc'd copy of the cached payload (caller responsible\n *   for free()) or NULL if not found / expired.\n */\nuint8_t *\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return NULL;\n\n    uint8_t *copy = NULL;\n    time_t   now  = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n\n    if (ce && ce->expiry >= now)\n    {\n        /* Hit */\n        copy = malloc(ce->blob_len);\n        if (copy)\n        {\n            memcpy(copy, ce->blob, ce->blob_len);\n            if (out_len) *out_len = ce->blob_len;\n        }\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Miss or expired */\n        if (ce)\n        {\n            /* Remove expired entry */\n            if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n            if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n            hash_remove(cache, ce);\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            cache->size--;\n\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    return copy;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate:\n *   Removes a single cache entry if present.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return false;\n\n    bool removed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Detach from LRU */\n        if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n        if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n        if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n        /* Hash remove */\n        hash_remove(cache, ce);\n\n        free(ce->key);\n        free(ce->blob);\n        free(ce);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        removed = true;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (removed)\n        LOG_INFO(\"Invalidated key='%s'\", key);\n    else\n        LOG_DEBUG(\"Invalidate request for missing key='%s'\", key);\n\n    return removed;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_flush_all:\n *   Invalidates ALL entries in the cache (use sparingly).\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_flush_all(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n        cache->buckets[i] = NULL;\n    }\n    cache->lru_head = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->size     = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Cache flushed\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_prometheus_export:\n *   Exports cache metrics as a newline-delimited Prometheus text\n *   exposition format block.  The caller must free() the returned\n *   string when done.\n *\n *   Example output:\n *     # TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\n *     mm_cache_hits_total 1024\n *     ...\n */\nchar *\nmm_cache_prometheus_export(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return NULL;\n\n    /* We know exactly how many lines we have \u2014 pre-size buffer. */\n    const char *tpl =\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_hits_total Total number of cache hits\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_hits_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_misses_total Total number of cache misses\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_misses_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_evictions_total Total number of evictions/expirations\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_evictions_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_entries Gauge of current cache entries\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_entries gauge\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_entries %zu\\n\";\n\n    size_t sz = snprintf(NULL, 0, tpl,\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n                         cache->size);\n\n    char *buf = malloc(sz + 1);\n    if (!buf) return NULL;\n\n    snprintf(buf, sz + 1, tpl,\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n             cache->size);\n\n    return buf;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Periodic Maintenance  ========================= */\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge_expired:\n *   Scans and removes expired entries (O(N) worst-case).  Intended to be\n *   triggered by a housekeeping thread every few seconds.\n */\nsize_t\nmm_cache_purge_expired(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return 0;\n\n    size_t purged = 0;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->lru_tail; /* Start from cold tail */\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = cur->prev;\n\n        if (cur->expiry < now)\n        {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (cur->prev) cur->prev->next = cur->next;\n            if (cur->next) cur->next->prev = cur->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == cur) cache->lru_head = cur->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == cur) cache->lru_tail = cur->prev;\n\n            /* Hash remove */\n            hash_remove(cache, cur);\n\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n\n            cache->size--;\n            purged++;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        cur = prev;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (purged)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);\n\n    return purged;\n}\n```",
  "replaceAll": false,

{

  "oldString": "    if (purged)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 69  \u2013  Response Cache Manager\n *\n * Description:\n *   Thread-safe in-memory response cache with TTL, LRU eviction,\n *   structured logging, and Prometheus-compatible metrics export.\n *\n *   This component is used by the API-gateway layer to accelerate\n *   frequently requested REST / GraphQL queries while retaining\n *   fine-grained invalidation hooks for the service & repository\n *   layers.  It is deliberately implemented without third-party\n *   dependencies to keep the monolith self-contained.\n *\n * Author:  MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* =========================  Configuration  ========================= */\n\n/* Maximum number of cached objects (soft limit, eviction is best-effort). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_CAPACITY\n#  define MM_CACHE_CAPACITY 4096\n#endif\n\n/* Default TTL in seconds unless overridden by caller. */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL\n#  define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL 60\n#endif\n\n/* Hash map backend \u2014 simple open addressing; power-of-two buckets. */\n#define BUCKET_COUNT 8192u   /* Must be power-of-two for fast masking. */\n#define BUCKET_MASK  (BUCKET_COUNT - 1)\n\n/* =========================  Data Types  ========================= */\n\ntypedef struct _cache_entry {\n    /* Key & data */\n    char            *key;          /* NULL-terminated */\n    uint8_t         *blob;         /* Arbitrary opaque payload */\n    size_t           blob_len;\n    time_t           expiry;       /* Absolute UTC seconds */\n\n    /* LRU chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *prev;\n    struct _cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* Hash chain */\n    struct _cache_entry *hnext;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct\n{\n    cache_entry_t  *buckets[BUCKET_COUNT];\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;      /* Most recently used */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;      /* Least recently used */\n\n    size_t          size;          /* # of live entries */\n    pthread_mutex_t mtx;\n\n    /* Prometheus metrics (atomic for fast reads) */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* =========================  Utilities  ========================= */\n\nstatic inline uint32_t\nfnv1a_32(const char *data)\n{\n    uint32_t hash = 2166136261u;\n    while (*data)\n    {\n        hash ^= (uint8_t)(*data++);\n        hash *= 16777619u;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Core Primitives  ========================= */\n\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    /* Already at head? */\n    if (cache->lru_head == ce) return;\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n    if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_tail == ce)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n    /* Insert at head */\n    ce->prev          = NULL;\n    ce->next          = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head   = ce;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_append(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    ce->prev = NULL;\n    ce->next = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = ce;\n    cache->lru_head = ce;\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = ce;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Public API  ========================= */\n\nmm_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_cache_t));\n    if (!cache)\n    {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"Failed to allocate cache\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_init(&cache->mtx, NULL);\n    return cache;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&cache->mtx);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_insert(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    ce->hnext = cache->buckets[h];\n    cache->buckets[h] = ce;\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *\nhash_lookup(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[h];\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        if (strcmp(cur->key, key) == 0)\n            return cur;\n        cur = cur->hnext;\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_remove(mm_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *ce)\n{\n    uint32_t h = fnv1a_32(ce->key) & BUCKET_MASK;\n    cache_entry_t **cur = &cache->buckets[h];\n    while (*cur)\n    {\n        if (*cur == ce)\n        {\n            *cur = ce->hnext;\n            return;\n        }\n        cur = &(*cur)->hnext;\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_put:\n *   Stores (or overwrites) a payload under the specified key.\n *   `ttl_sec` of 0 means \"use default TTL\".\n *\n *   The payload is copied; the caller retains ownership of the input buffer.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             const uint8_t *payload,\n             size_t len,\n             uint32_t ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || len == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool replaced = false;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Replace */\n        free(ce->blob);\n        ce->blob     = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->blob)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n        replaced     = true;\n\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Make new entry */\n        ce = calloc(1, sizeof(cache_entry_t));\n        if (!ce)\n        {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        ce->key  = strdup(key);\n        ce->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!ce->key || !ce->blob)\n        {\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n            return false;\n        }\n        memcpy(ce->blob, payload, len);\n        ce->blob_len = len;\n        ce->expiry   = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL);\n\n        hash_insert(cache, ce);\n        lru_append(cache, ce);\n        cache->size++;\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if over capacity */\n    while (cache->size > MM_CACHE_CAPACITY && cache->lru_tail)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n\n        if (victim->prev)\n            victim->prev->next = NULL;\n        cache->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == victim)\n            cache->lru_head = NULL;\n\n        hash_remove(cache, victim);\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->blob);\n        free(victim);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"%s entry for key='%s' (ttl=%us)\",\n             replaced ? \"Updated\" : \"Cached\", key,\n             (unsigned)(ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL));\n    return true;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_get:\n *   Returns a malloc'd copy of the cached payload (caller responsible\n *   for free()) or NULL if not found / expired.\n */\nuint8_t *\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return NULL;\n\n    uint8_t *copy = NULL;\n    time_t   now  = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n\n    if (ce && ce->expiry >= now)\n    {\n        /* Hit */\n        copy = malloc(ce->blob_len);\n        if (copy)\n        {\n            memcpy(copy, ce->blob, ce->blob_len);\n            if (out_len) *out_len = ce->blob_len;\n        }\n        lru_promote(cache, ce);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    else\n    {\n        /* Miss or expired */\n        if (ce)\n        {\n            /* Remove expired entry */\n            if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n            if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n            hash_remove(cache, ce);\n            free(ce->key);\n            free(ce->blob);\n            free(ce);\n            cache->size--;\n\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    return copy;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_invalidate:\n *   Removes a single cache entry if present.\n */\nbool\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return false;\n\n    bool removed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *ce = hash_lookup(cache, key);\n    if (ce)\n    {\n        /* Detach from LRU */\n        if (ce->prev) ce->prev->next = ce->next;\n        if (ce->next) ce->next->prev = ce->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == ce) cache->lru_head = ce->next;\n        if (cache->lru_tail == ce) cache->lru_tail = ce->prev;\n\n        /* Hash remove */\n        hash_remove(cache, ce);\n\n        free(ce->key);\n        free(ce->blob);\n        free(ce);\n\n        cache->size--;\n        removed = true;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (removed)\n        LOG_INFO(\"Invalidated key='%s'\", key);\n    else\n        LOG_DEBUG(\"Invalidate request for missing key='%s'\", key);\n\n    return removed;\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_flush_all:\n *   Invalidates ALL entries in the cache (use sparingly).\n */\nvoid\nmm_cache_flush_all(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < BUCKET_COUNT; ++i)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *cur = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (cur)\n        {\n            cache_entry_t *nx = cur->hnext;\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n            cur = nx;\n        }\n        cache->buckets[i] = NULL;\n    }\n    cache->lru_head = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->size     = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    LOG_INFO(\"Cache flushed\");\n}\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_prometheus_export:\n *   Exports cache metrics as a newline-delimited Prometheus text\n *   exposition format block.  The caller must free() the returned\n *   string when done.\n *\n *   Example output:\n *     # TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\n *     mm_cache_hits_total 1024\n *     ...\n */\nchar *\nmm_cache_prometheus_export(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return NULL;\n\n    /* We know exactly how many lines we have \u2014 pre-size buffer. */\n    const char *tpl =\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_hits_total Total number of cache hits\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_hits_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_misses_total Total number of cache misses\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_misses_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_evictions_total Total number of evictions/expirations\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_evictions_total %lu\\n\"\n        \"# HELP mm_cache_entries Gauge of current cache entries\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_cache_entries gauge\\n\"\n        \"mm_cache_entries %zu\\n\";\n\n    size_t sz = snprintf(NULL, 0, tpl,\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n                         (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n                         cache->size);\n\n    char *buf = malloc(sz + 1);\n    if (!buf) return NULL;\n\n    snprintf(buf, sz + 1, tpl,\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->hits),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->misses),\n             (unsigned long)atomic_load(&cache->evictions),\n             cache->size);\n\n    return buf;\n}\n\n/* =========================  Periodic Maintenance  ========================= */\n\n/*\n * mm_cache_purge_expired:\n *   Scans and removes expired entries (O(N) worst-case).  Intended to be\n *   triggered by a housekeeping thread every few seconds.\n */\nsize_t\nmm_cache_purge_expired(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return 0;\n\n    size_t purged = 0;\n    time_t now    = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    cache_entry_t *cur = cache->lru_tail; /* Start from cold tail */\n    while (cur)\n    {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = cur->prev;\n\n        if (cur->expiry < now)\n        {\n            /* Detach from LRU */\n            if (cur->prev) cur->prev->next = cur->next;\n            if (cur->next) cur->next->prev = cur->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == cur) cache->lru_head = cur->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == cur) cache->lru_tail = cur->prev;\n\n            /* Hash remove */\n            hash_remove(cache, cur);\n\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur->blob);\n            free(cur);\n\n            cache->size--;\n            purged++;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        cur = prev;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    if (purged)\n        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);\n\n    return purged;\n}\n```",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);",
        " ",
        "     if (purged)",
        "-        MM_CACHE_LOG(\"INFO\", \"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);",
        "+        LOG_INFO(\"Purged %zu expired entries\", purged);",
        " ",
        "     return purged;",
        " }"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 523,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 523

  "userModified": false
}
67. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_9.txt",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * File: src/module_9.txt  (compiled as C source)\n *\n * A high-performance, thread-safe, TTL-aware LRU cache for HTTP/GraphQL\n * responses.  The cache implements an open-addressing hash-map for O(1)\n * look-ups, an intrusive doubly-linked list for O(1) LRU eviction,\n * fine-grained read/write locking, atomic Prometheus-compatible metrics,\n * and pluggable logging hooks.\n *\n * The module is intentionally self-contained (single compilation unit)\n * to ease static-linking & dead-code elimination when particular build\n * profiles disable response caching.\n *\n * Compile with:  -std=c11 -pthread\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging abstraction (resolves to the project-wide structured logger)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n#define MM_LOGGER_H\n\n/* Fallback logger for stand-alone compilation / unit-tests.                */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MM_LOG_LEVEL 3 /* 0=NONE 1=ERROR 2=WARN 3=INFO 4=DEBUG              */\n#endif\n\n#define MM_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                               \\\n        if ((level) <= MM_LOG_LEVEL) {                                                 \\\n            const char *_lvl = (level) == 1 ? \"ERROR\" :                                \\\n                               (level) == 2 ? \"WARN\"  :                                \\\n                               (level) == 3 ? \"INFO\"  : \"DEBUG\";                       \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", _lvl, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \\\n                    ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                    \\\n        }                                                                              \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(...) MM_LOG(1, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  MM_LOG(2, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  MM_LOG(3, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) MM_LOG(4, __VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Hash helpers (FNV-1a 64-bit)",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_9.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 500
  },
  "type": "text"
}

MM_LOG(1, __VA_ARGS__)\n52:#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  MM_LOG(2, __VA_ARGS__)\n53:#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  MM_LOG(3, __VA_ARGS__)\n54:#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) MM_LOG(4, __VA_ARGS__)\n150:    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized: capacity=%zu ttl=%us buckets=%zu\",\n176:    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Response cache destroyed\");\n449:    MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"LRU eviction performed\");\n491:    MM_LOG_INFO(\"All cache unit tests passed\");",

LOG_ERROR(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  LOG_WARN(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  LOG_INFO(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) LOG_DEBUG(__VA_ARGS__)",

LOG_ERROR(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  LOG_WARN(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  LOG_INFO(__VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) LOG_DEBUG(__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging abstraction (resolves to the project-wide structured logger)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n#define MM_LOGGER_H\n\n/* Fallback logger for stand-alone compilation / unit-tests.                */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MM_LOG_LEVEL 3 /* 0=NONE 1=ERROR 2=WARN 3=INFO 4=DEBUG              */\n#endif\n\n#define MM_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                               \\\n        if ((level) <= MM_LOG_LEVEL) {                                                 \\\n            const char *_lvl = (level) == 1 ? \"ERROR\" :                                \\\n                               (level) == 2 ? \"WARN\"  :                                \\\n                               (level) == 3 ? \"INFO\"  : \"DEBUG\";                       \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", _lvl, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \\\n                    ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                    \\\n        }                                                                              \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(...) MM_LOG(1, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  MM_LOG(2, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  MM_LOG(3, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) MM_LOG(4, __VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache\n *\n * File: src/module_9.txt  (compiled as C source)\n *\n * A high-performance, thread-safe, TTL-aware LRU cache for HTTP/GraphQL\n * responses.  The cache implements an open-addressing hash-map for O(1)\n * look-ups, an intrusive doubly-linked list for O(1) LRU eviction,\n * fine-grained read/write locking, atomic Prometheus-compatible metrics,\n * and pluggable logging hooks.\n *\n * The module is intentionally self-contained (single compilation unit)\n * to ease static-linking & dead-code elimination when particular build\n * profiles disable response caching.\n *\n * Compile with:  -std=c11 -pthread\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Logging abstraction (resolves to the project-wide structured logger)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H\n#define MM_LOGGER_H\n\n/* Fallback logger for stand-alone compilation / unit-tests.                */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MM_LOG_LEVEL 3 /* 0=NONE 1=ERROR 2=WARN 3=INFO 4=DEBUG              */\n#endif\n\n#define MM_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                               \\\n        if ((level) <= MM_LOG_LEVEL) {                                                 \\\n            const char *_lvl = (level) == 1 ? \"ERROR\" :                                \\\n                               (level) == 2 ? \"WARN\"  :                                \\\n                               (level) == 3 ? \"INFO\"  : \"DEBUG\";                       \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", _lvl, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \\\n                    ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                    \\\n        }                                                                              \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define MM_LOG_ERROR(...) MM_LOG(1, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  MM_LOG(2, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  MM_LOG(3, __VA_ARGS__)\n#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) MM_LOG(4, __VA_ARGS__)\n\n#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Hash helpers (FNV-1a 64-bit)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic inline uint64_t\nmm_fnv1a_hash(const char *data)\n{\n    uint64_t hash = 14695981039346656037ULL;\n    for (const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)data; *p; ++p) {\n        hash ^= *p;\n        hash *= 1099511628211ULL;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Cache structures\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry_s {\n    char                      *key;\n    uint8_t                   *blob;\n    size_t                     size;\n    uint64_t                   created_at_epoch; /* seconds */\n    struct mm_cache_entry_s   *prev;  /* MRU/LRU doubly-linked list */\n    struct mm_cache_entry_s   *next;\n    struct mm_cache_entry_s   *hnext; /* bucket chain */\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* configuration */\n    size_t                     capacity;   /* maximum # of entries          */\n    uint32_t                   ttl_sec;    /* time-to-live for each entry   */\n    size_t                     bucket_cnt; /* hash-map buckets (power of 2) */\n\n    /* data */\n    mm_cache_entry_t         **buckets;    /* bucket_cnt-sized array        */\n    mm_cache_entry_t          *mru;        /* list head (most recently used)*/\n    mm_cache_entry_t          *lru;        /* list tail (least recently used)*/\n    size_t                     size;       /* current # of entries          */\n\n    /* concurrency */\n    pthread_rwlock_t           lock;       /* global rw-lock                */\n\n    /* metrics */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t       hits;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t       misses;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t       evictions;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Forward declarations\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void      mm_cache_lru_move_to_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void      mm_cache_evict_lru(mm_cache_t *c);\nstatic void      mm_cache_unlink(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void      mm_cache_free_entry(mm_cache_entry_t *e);\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public API\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nmm_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(size_t capacity,\n                uint32_t ttl_sec,\n                size_t bucket_hint_power_of_two)\n{\n    if (capacity == 0) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(*cache));\n    if (!cache) {\n        return NULL; /* errno set by calloc */\n    }\n\n    cache->capacity   = capacity;\n    cache->ttl_sec    = ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : 60; /* default 1 min */\n    cache->bucket_cnt = 1ULL << bucket_hint_power_of_two;\n\n    cache->buckets = calloc(cache->bucket_cnt, sizeof(mm_cache_entry_t *));\n    if (!cache->buckets) {\n        free(cache);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&cache->lock, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(cache->buckets);\n        free(cache);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Response cache initialized: capacity=%zu ttl=%us buckets=%zu\",\n                cache->capacity, cache->ttl_sec, cache->bucket_cnt);\n    return cache;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < cache->bucket_cnt; ++i) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *e = cache->buckets[i];\n        while (e) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *next = e->hnext;\n            mm_cache_free_entry(e);\n            e = next;\n        }\n    }\n    free(cache->buckets);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&cache->lock);\n    free(cache);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Response cache destroyed\");\n}\n\nstatic inline time_t\nmm_now_sec(void)\n{\n    return time(NULL);\n}\n\n/*\n * Return NULL on miss / expired        \u2013 misses++ on both conditions\n * Return malloc()-ed blob on hit       \u2013 caller must free(*payload)\n *\n * out_size may be NULL if caller doesn't need blob length.\n */\nuint8_t *\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             size_t *out_size)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(key);\n    size_t bucket_idx = h & (cache->bucket_cnt - 1);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e;\n    for (e = cache->buckets[bucket_idx]; e; e = e->hnext) {\n        if (strcmp(e->key, key) == 0) break;\n    }\n\n    if (!e) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n        return NULL; /* miss */\n    }\n\n    /* Check TTL */\n    if ((uint64_t)(mm_now_sec() - e->created_at_epoch) > cache->ttl_sec) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock to evict */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->lock);\n        mm_cache_unlink(cache, e);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        mm_cache_free_entry(e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n        return NULL; /* expired */\n    }\n\n    /* Hit! Copy payload for safe return */\n    uint8_t *copy = malloc(e->size);\n    if (!copy) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n        return NULL; /* errno set */\n    }\n    memcpy(copy, e->blob, e->size);\n    if (out_size) *out_size = e->size;\n\n    /* Move to front (MRU) if not already */\n    if (cache->mru != e) {\n        /* Upgrade to write lock (lock upgrade pattern) */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->lock);\n        mm_cache_lru_move_to_front(cache, e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    } else {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    }\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    return copy;\n}\n\n/*\n * Insert or update an item (deep-copies blob).\n * Returns 0 on success, \u20111 on allocation error.\n */\nint\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n             const char *key,\n             const uint8_t *blob,\n             size_t size)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !blob || !size) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *new_e = NULL;\n\n    /* Allocate outside lock to minimize critical-section */\n    new_e = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_e));\n    if (!new_e) return -1;\n\n    new_e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!new_e->key) {\n        free(new_e);\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    new_e->blob = malloc(size);\n    if (!new_e->blob) {\n        free(new_e->key);\n        free(new_e);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    memcpy(new_e->blob, blob, size);\n    new_e->size              = size;\n    new_e->created_at_epoch  = mm_now_sec();\n\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(key);\n    size_t bucket_idx = h & (cache->bucket_cnt - 1);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    /* Replace existing? */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e;\n    for (e = cache->buckets[bucket_idx]; e; e = e->hnext) {\n        if (strcmp(e->key, key) == 0) break;\n    }\n    if (e) {\n        /* Update in place (retain list placement) */\n        free(e->blob);\n        free(e->key);\n\n        e->key              = new_e->key;\n        e->blob             = new_e->blob;\n        e->size             = new_e->size;\n        e->created_at_epoch = new_e->created_at_epoch;\n\n        free(new_e); /* only wrapper */\n        mm_cache_lru_move_to_front(cache, e);\n    } else {\n        /* Insert into bucket chain head */\n        new_e->hnext          = cache->buckets[bucket_idx];\n        cache->buckets[bucket_idx] = new_e;\n\n        /* Insert into MRU list front */\n        new_e->next = cache->mru;\n        if (cache->mru)\n            cache->mru->prev = new_e;\n        cache->mru = new_e;\n        if (!cache->lru)\n            cache->lru = new_e;\n\n        cache->size++;\n\n        /* Eviction if overflow */\n        if (cache->size > cache->capacity) {\n            mm_cache_evict_lru(cache);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_remove(mm_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) {\n        errno = EINVAL;\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(key);\n    size_t bucket_idx = h & (cache->bucket_cnt - 1);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t **pp = &cache->buckets[bucket_idx];\n    for (; *pp; pp = &(*pp)->hnext) {\n        if (strcmp((*pp)->key, key) == 0) {\n            mm_cache_entry_t *victim = *pp;\n            *pp = victim->hnext;\n\n            mm_cache_unlink(cache, victim);\n            mm_cache_free_entry(victim);\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n            return 0;\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n    return -1; /* not found */\n}\n\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    uint64_t current_size;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\nmm_cache_metrics_t\nmm_cache_metrics(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    mm_cache_metrics_t m = {0};\n    if (!cache) return m;\n\n    m.hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    m.misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    m.evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->lock);\n    m.current_size = cache->size;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->lock);\n\n    return m;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Internal helpers\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void\nmm_cache_lru_move_to_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->mru == e) return;\n\n    /* unlink e from current list position */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru  == e)          c->lru  = e->prev;\n\n    /* insert at front */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->mru;\n    if (c->mru) c->mru->prev = e;\n    c->mru = e;\n    if (!c->lru)\n        c->lru = e;\n}\n\nstatic void\nmm_cache_unlink(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    /* Remove from bucket chain will be done by caller */\n    /* unlink from LRU list */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->mru == e) c->mru = e->next;\n    if (c->lru == e) c->lru = e->prev;\n    c->size--;\n}\n\nstatic void\nmm_cache_evict_lru(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c->lru) return;\n    mm_cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru;\n\n    /* Remove from bucket chain */\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(victim->key);\n    size_t bucket_idx = h & (c->bucket_cnt - 1);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t **pp = &c->buckets[bucket_idx];\n    for (; *pp; pp = &(*pp)->hnext) {\n        if (*pp == victim) {\n            *pp = victim->hnext;\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_unlink(c, victim);\n    mm_cache_free_entry(victim);\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG(\"LRU eviction performed\");\n}\n\nstatic void\nmm_cache_free_entry(mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Self-test harness (compile with \u2011DMM_CACHE_TEST)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void\ntest_basic()\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(2, 1 /* TTL */, 4 /* 16 buckets */);\n    assert(c);\n\n    (void)mm_cache_put(c, \"a\", (const uint8_t *)\"apple\", 6);\n    (void)mm_cache_put(c, \"b\", (const uint8_t *)\"banana\", 7);\n\n    size_t n;\n    uint8_t *p = mm_cache_get(c, \"a\", &n);\n    assert(p && n == 6 && strcmp((char *)p, \"apple\") == 0);\n    free(p);\n\n    p = mm_cache_get(c, \"c\", NULL);\n    assert(!p);\n\n    /* force eviction */\n    (void)mm_cache_put(c, \"c\", (const uint8_t *)\"cherry\", 7);\n\n    p = mm_cache_get(c, \"b\", NULL); /* 'b' should be evicted (LRU) */\n    assert(!p);\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"All cache unit tests passed\");\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    test_basic();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " #include <errno.h>",
        " #include <pthread.h>",
        " ",
        "-/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "- * Logging abstraction (resolves to the project-wide structured logger)",
        "- * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */",
        "-#ifndef MM_LOGGER_H",
        "-#define MM_LOGGER_H",
        "+#include \"logging.h\"",
        " ",
        "-/* Fallback logger for stand-alone compilation / unit-tests.                */",
        "-#ifndef MM_LOG_LEVEL",
        "-#define MM_LOG_LEVEL 3 /* 0=NONE 1=ERROR 2=WARN 3=INFO 4=DEBUG              */",
        "-#endif",
        "+/* Map legacy MM_LOG_* macros to new centralized logging */",
        "+#define MM_LOG_ERROR(...) LOG_ERROR(__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  LOG_WARN(__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  LOG_INFO(__VA_ARGS__)",
        "+#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) LOG_DEBUG(__VA_ARGS__)",
        " ",
        "-#define MM_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\",
        "-    do {                                                                               \\",
        "-        if ((level) <= MM_LOG_LEVEL) {                                                 \\",
        "-            const char *_lvl = (level) == 1 ? \"ERROR\" :                                \\",
        "-                               (level) == 2 ? \"WARN\"  :                                \\",
        "-                               (level) == 3 ? \"INFO\"  : \"DEBUG\";                       \\",
        "-            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", _lvl, __FILE__, __LINE__,        \\",
        "-                    ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                    \\",
        "-        }                                                                              \\",
        "-    } while (0)",
        " ",
        "-#define MM_LOG_ERROR(...) MM_LOG(1, __VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#define MM_LOG_WARN(...)  MM_LOG(2, __VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#define MM_LOG_INFO(...)  MM_LOG(3, __VA_ARGS__)",
        "-#define MM_LOG_DEBUG(...) MM_LOG(4, __VA_ARGS__)",
        "-",
        "-#endif /* MM_LOGGER_H */",
        "-",
        "-",
        " /* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "  * Hash helpers (FNV-1a 64-bit)",
        "  * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */"
      ],
      "newLines": 15,
      "newStart": 26,
      "oldLines": 36,
      "oldStart": 26
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

    "content": "```c\n/****************************************************************************************\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 23  \u2013 Response Cache (in-memory, TTL aware, LRU, thread-safe)\n *\n * File Path : src/module_23.txt           (compiled as response_cache.c)\n * Language   : C99\n *\n * This module provides a small, fully in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache that is:\n *   \u2022 Thread-safe (read-mostly workloads optimized with RW-locks)\n *   \u2022 LRU-evicting, TTL-aware (seconds granularity)\n *   \u2022 Introspectable via Prometheus-style counters\n *   \u2022 Pluggable into Gateway / Service-Layer middleware chains\n *\n * NOTE: Only a minimal subset of the surrounding project\u2019s infrastructure (logging,\n *       metrics, event-bus) is mocked here so that this file remains self-contained.\n ****************************************************************************************/\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L    /* For clock_gettime / nanosleep */\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* Single-header hash table: https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500  Project-local lightweight shims  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MMCH_LOG_H\n#define MMCH_LOG_H\nenum { LOG_TRACE = 0, LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR, LOG_FATAL };\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = { \"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\", \"WARN\", \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\" };\n#define MMCH_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                                 \\\n        if ((level) >= LOG_INFO)                                                         \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", LOG_LEVEL_STR[level],              \\\n                    __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                  \\\n    } while (0)\n#endif /* MMCH_LOG_H */\n\n#ifndef MMCH_METRICS_H\n#define MMCH_METRICS_H\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    uint64_t expirations;\n} mmch_cache_metrics_t;\n#endif /* MMCH_METRICS_H */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500  Public API types / constants \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_entry_s {\n    char               *key;          /* Hash key (URI + query string) */\n    void               *blob;         /* Opaque response payload        */\n    size_t              blob_len;     /* Bytes                          */\n    time_t              expires_at;   /* Wall clock seconds             */\n",

  "new_string": "#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L    /* For clock_gettime / nanosleep */\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* Single-header hash table: https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500  Project-local lightweight shims  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n/* Map legacy log levels and MMCH_LOG macro to centralized logging */\nenum { LOG_TRACE_LEGACY = 0, LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY, LOG_INFO_LEGACY, LOG_WARN_LEGACY, LOG_ERROR_LEGACY, LOG_FATAL_LEGACY };\n#define MMCH_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                                 \\\n        if ((level) == LOG_ERROR_LEGACY || (level) == LOG_FATAL_LEGACY)                  \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                               \\\n        else if ((level) == LOG_WARN_LEGACY)                                             \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                \\\n        else if ((level) == LOG_INFO_LEGACY)                                             \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                \\\n        else                                                                             \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                               \\\n    } while (0)",
  "old_string": "#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L    /* For clock_gettime / nanosleep */\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include \"uthash.h\"                /* Single-header hash table: https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n/*\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500  Project-local lightweight shims  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500*/\n#ifndef MMCH_LOG_H\n#define MMCH_LOG_H\nenum { LOG_TRACE = 0, LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO, LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR, LOG_FATAL };\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = { \"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\", \"WARN\", \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\" };\n#define MMCH_LOG(level, fmt, ...)                                                        \\\n    do {                                                                                 \\\n        if ((level) >= LOG_INFO)                                                         \\\n            fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] (%s:%d) \" fmt \"\\n\", LOG_LEVEL_STR[level],              \\\n                    __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                  \\\n    } while (0)\n#endif /* MMCH_LOG_H */",
