Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub (api_microservice)\n * ------------------------------------------------\n * Module       : response_cache\n * File path    : src/module_13.txt\n * Language     : C99\n *\n * Description\n * -----------\n * Thread\u2013safe, TTL-aware, Least-Recently-Used response cache\n * used by the API-Gateway layer to short-circuit repeated\n * REST/GraphQL calls.  This module is entirely self-contained\n * (save for its dependency on the public-domain \u201cuthash.h\u201d file)\n * and can be wired into any service boundary that requires a\n * simple in-process key/value store with deterministic eviction.\n *\n * Key Features\n * ------------\n *  \u2022 O(1) lookup/update using uthash\n *  \u2022 Doubly-linked LRU queue for eviction\n *  \u2022 Per-entry TTL with lazy & capacity eviction\n *  \u2022 Thread-safe via POSIX read/write lock\n *  \u2022 Lightweight Prometheus-style metrics\n *\n * Build\n * -----\n *  gcc -std=c99 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c response_cache.c\n *\n * Copyright & License\n * -------------------\n * Copyright \u00a9 2023\u20132024 Helios Software\n * Licensed under the MIT License.  See LICENSE file for details.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"        /* https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Constants                         *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#   define RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  30U     /* fall-back TTL if caller passes 0 */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_KEY_LEN      1024U   /* sanity limit to avoid abuse      */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN    (1024U * 1024U * 4U) /* hard-cap at 4 MiB   */\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                  Diagnostic & Logging                   *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_LOG\n#   define RC_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[RC] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Structs                           *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct rc_entry {\n    /* --- hash key --- */\n    char         *key;         /* dynamically allocated, NUL-terminated   */\n    UT_hash_handle hh;         /* uthash handle                           */\n\n    /* --- payload --- */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_13.txt",
    "numLines": 80,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 495
  },
  "type": "text"
}

LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",

  "newString": "#include \"uthash.h\"        /* https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Constants                         *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#   define RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  30U     /* fall-back TTL if caller passes 0 */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_KEY_LEN      1024U   /* sanity limit to avoid abuse      */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN    (1024U * 1024U * 4U) /* hard-cap at 4 MiB   */\n#endif\n\n/* Map legacy RC_LOG to new centralized logging */\n#define RC_LOG(fmt, ...) LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#include \"uthash.h\"        /* https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Constants                         *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#   define RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  30U     /* fall-back TTL if caller passes 0 */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_KEY_LEN      1024U   /* sanity limit to avoid abuse      */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN    (1024U * 1024U * 4U) /* hard-cap at 4 MiB   */\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                  Diagnostic & Logging                   *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_LOG\n#   define RC_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[RC] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub (api_microservice)\n * ------------------------------------------------\n * Module       : response_cache\n * File path    : src/module_13.txt\n * Language     : C99\n *\n * Description\n * -----------\n * Thread\u2013safe, TTL-aware, Least-Recently-Used response cache\n * used by the API-Gateway layer to short-circuit repeated\n * REST/GraphQL calls.  This module is entirely self-contained\n * (save for its dependency on the public-domain \u201cuthash.h\u201d file)\n * and can be wired into any service boundary that requires a\n * simple in-process key/value store with deterministic eviction.\n *\n * Key Features\n * ------------\n *  \u2022 O(1) lookup/update using uthash\n *  \u2022 Doubly-linked LRU queue for eviction\n *  \u2022 Per-entry TTL with lazy & capacity eviction\n *  \u2022 Thread-safe via POSIX read/write lock\n *  \u2022 Lightweight Prometheus-style metrics\n *\n * Build\n * -----\n *  gcc -std=c99 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c response_cache.c\n *\n * Copyright & License\n * -------------------\n * Copyright \u00a9 2023\u20132024 Helios Software\n * Licensed under the MIT License.  See LICENSE file for details.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"        /* https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/ */\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Constants                         *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#   define RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  30U     /* fall-back TTL if caller passes 0 */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_KEY_LEN      1024U   /* sanity limit to avoid abuse      */\n#endif\n\n#ifndef RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN\n#   define RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN    (1024U * 1024U * 4U) /* hard-cap at 4 MiB   */\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                  Diagnostic & Logging                   *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifndef RC_LOG\n#   define RC_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[RC] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Structs                           *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\ntypedef struct rc_entry {\n    /* --- hash key --- */\n    char         *key;         /* dynamically allocated, NUL-terminated   */\n    UT_hash_handle hh;         /* uthash handle                           */\n\n    /* --- payload --- */\n    unsigned char *value;      /* opaque payload                          */\n    size_t         value_len;  /* payload length                          */\n    time_t         expires_at; /* epoch seconds                           */\n\n    /* --- LRU queue --- */\n    struct rc_entry *prev;\n    struct rc_entry *next;\n} rc_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t hits;       /* point-lookups that returned data  */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t misses;     /* lookups that did not find data    */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t stale;      /* lookups that found expired data   */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t inserts;    /* successful puts                   */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t evictions;  /* automatic (LRU/TTL) eviction      */\n} rc_metrics_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    rc_entry_t      *table;          /* uthash hash table anchor          */\n    rc_entry_t      *lru_head;       /* most-recently-used entry          */\n    rc_entry_t      *lru_tail;       /* least-recently-used entry         */\n\n    size_t           capacity;       /* max number of entries             */\n    size_t           size;           /* current entry count               */\n    unsigned int     default_ttl;    /* seconds                           */\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t rwlock;         /* read-mostly workload optimized    */\n    rc_metrics_t     metrics;\n} rc_cache_t;\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *              Forward-declarations (private)             *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic void       rc_lru_move_front(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e);\nstatic void       rc_lru_append_front(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e);\nstatic void       rc_lru_remove(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e);\nstatic void       rc_evict_tail(rc_cache_t *c);\nstatic void       rc_free_entry(rc_entry_t *e);\nstatic bool       rc_entry_is_expired(const rc_entry_t *e, time_t now);\nstatic void       rc_purge_expired(rc_cache_t *c);\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                    Public Interface                     *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*\n * rc_cache_init\n * -------------\n * Initialize an empty cache with a maximum of `capacity`\n * elements and the supplied default TTL (in seconds).  The\n * TTL applies only when rc_cache_put is called with ttl = 0.\n *\n * Returns 0 on success, or errno on failure.\n */\nint rc_cache_init(rc_cache_t *cache, size_t capacity, unsigned int default_ttl)\n{\n    if (!cache || capacity == 0) return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(cache, 0, sizeof(*cache));\n    cache->capacity    = capacity;\n    cache->default_ttl = default_ttl ? default_ttl : RC_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&cache->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        return errno ? errno : EAGAIN;\n    }\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_cache_destroy\n * ----------------\n * Release all resources held by the cache.\n */\nvoid rc_cache_destroy(rc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    rc_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, cur);\n        rc_free_entry(cur);\n    }\n    cache->table     = NULL;\n    cache->lru_head  = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->size      = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_cache_get\n * ------------\n * Retrieve a copy of the cached value associated with `key`.\n * The returned pointer is heap-allocated and must be freed by\n * the caller (using free(3)). On success, returns 0 and sets\n * out_value/out_len; otherwise, returns ENOENT/ETIMEDOUT.\n */\nint rc_cache_get(rc_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 unsigned char **out_value,\n                 size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_value || !out_len) return EINVAL;\n\n    *out_value = NULL;\n    *out_len   = 0;\n\n    const time_t now = time(NULL);\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    rc_entry_t *entry = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n    if (!entry) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n\n    if (rc_entry_is_expired(entry, now)) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.stale, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        /* We need a write lock to remove the stale entry */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n        /* Re-validate under write lock (double-check pattern) */\n        HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n        if (entry && rc_entry_is_expired(entry, now)) {\n            rc_lru_remove(cache, entry);\n            HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n            cache->size--;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n            rc_free_entry(entry);\n        }\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n        return ETIMEDOUT;\n    }\n\n    /* Hit \u2014 copy value */\n    unsigned char *dup = malloc(entry->value_len);\n    if (!dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(dup, entry->value, entry->value_len);\n    *out_value = dup;\n    *out_len   = entry->value_len;\n\n    /* Promote to MRU */\n    rc_lru_move_front(cache, entry);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_cache_put\n * ------------\n * Insert or update an entry.  If ttl_sec is 0, the cache\u2019s\n * default TTL is applied.  The cache acquires a write lock\n * during the operation.\n */\nint rc_cache_put(rc_cache_t        *cache,\n                 const char        *key,\n                 const unsigned char *value,\n                 size_t             value_len,\n                 unsigned int       ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !value || value_len == 0) return EINVAL;\n    if (strlen(key) > RC_MAX_KEY_LEN || value_len > RC_MAX_VALUE_LEN) return EFBIG;\n\n    const time_t now = time(NULL);\n    const time_t expire_ts = now + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : cache->default_ttl);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    rc_entry_t *entry = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Update existing entry in-place */\n        unsigned char *new_val = realloc(entry->value, value_len);\n        if (!new_val) {\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n            return ENOMEM;\n        }\n        memcpy(new_val, value, value_len);\n        entry->value     = new_val;\n        entry->value_len = value_len;\n        entry->expires_at = expire_ts;\n\n        rc_lru_move_front(cache, entry);\n    } else {\n        /* Add new entry */\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) {\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n            return ENOMEM;\n        }\n        entry->key = strdup(key);\n        entry->value = malloc(value_len);\n        if (!entry->key || !entry->value) {\n            rc_free_entry(entry);\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n            return ENOMEM;\n        }\n        memcpy(entry->value, value, value_len);\n        entry->value_len  = value_len;\n        entry->expires_at = expire_ts;\n\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->table, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n        rc_lru_append_front(cache, entry);\n        cache->size++;\n\n        /* Capacity eviction */\n        while (cache->size > cache->capacity) {\n            rc_evict_tail(cache);\n        }\n    }\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.inserts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_cache_clear\n * --------------\n * Remove every entry but keep cache structure intact.\n */\nvoid rc_cache_clear(rc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    rc_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, cur);\n        rc_free_entry(cur);\n    }\n    cache->table    = NULL;\n    cache->lru_head = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->size     = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_cache_metrics\n * ----------------\n * Snapshot cache metrics into caller-provided struct.\n */\nvoid rc_cache_metrics(rc_cache_t *cache, rc_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!cache || !out) return;\n    out->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->metrics.hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->metrics.misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->stale     = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->metrics.stale, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->inserts   = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->metrics.inserts, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->metrics.evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                 Internal Helper Functions               *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic inline bool rc_entry_is_expired(const rc_entry_t *e, time_t now)\n{\n    return (e->expires_at <= now);\n}\n\nstatic void rc_free_entry(rc_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->value);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/*---------------- LRU queue manipulation ----------------*/\nstatic void rc_lru_move_front(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return;       /* already MRU */\n\n    rc_lru_remove(c, e);\n    rc_lru_append_front(c, e);\n}\n\nstatic void rc_lru_append_front(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    e->prev = NULL;\n\n    if (c->lru_head)\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n\n    if (!c->lru_tail)\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic void rc_lru_remove(rc_cache_t *c, rc_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e->prev)\n        e->prev->next = e->next;\n    else\n        c->lru_head = e->next;\n\n    if (e->next)\n        e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    else\n        c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void rc_evict_tail(rc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    rc_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n    if (!victim) return;\n\n    rc_lru_remove(c, victim);\n    HASH_DEL(c->table, victim);\n    c->size--;\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    rc_free_entry(victim);\n}\n\n/*\n * rc_purge_expired\n * ----------------\n * Remove expired entries; called opportunistically.\n */\nstatic void rc_purge_expired(rc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    const time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    rc_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->table, cur, tmp) {\n        if (rc_entry_is_expired(cur, now)) {\n            rc_lru_remove(c, cur);\n            HASH_DEL(c->table, cur);\n            c->size--;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n            rc_free_entry(cur);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *            Optional Periodic Maintenance API            *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*\n * rc_cache_maintenance\n * --------------------\n * Convenience helper that a caller can run on a timer to\n * purge expired entries en masse. Safe to run concurrently\n * with regular Get/Put operations.\n */\nvoid rc_cache_maintenance(rc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    rc_purge_expired(cache);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Self-Test                         *\n *---------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef RC_SELFTEST\n\n#include <unistd.h>\n\nstatic void dump_metrics(const rc_metrics_t *m)\n{\n    printf(\"hits=%\" PRIuFAST64 \", misses=%\" PRIuFAST64\n           \", stale=%\" PRIuFAST64 \", inserts=%\" PRIuFAST64\n           \", evictions=%\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n           m->hits, m->misses, m->stale, m->inserts, m->evictions);\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    rc_cache_t cache;\n    int rc = rc_cache_init(&cache, 4, 2);\n    assert(rc == 0);\n\n    const char *k = \"/v1/products?sku=42\";\n    const char *v = \"{\\\"id\\\":42,\\\"name\\\":\\\"Milk\\\"}\";\n    rc_cache_put(&cache, k, (const unsigned char *)v, strlen(v) + 1, 0);\n\n    unsigned char *out;\n    size_t len;\n\n    rc = rc_cache_get(&cache, k, &out, &len);\n    assert(rc == 0);\n    printf(\"GET: %s\\n\", out);\n    free(out);\n\n    sleep(3); /* let entry expire */\n\n    rc = rc_cache_get(&cache, k, &out, &len);\n    assert(rc == ETIMEDOUT);\n\n    rc_metrics_t m;\n    rc_cache_metrics(&cache, &m);\n    dump_metrics(&m);\n\n    rc_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* RC_SELFTEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

      ],

        "-#   define RC_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[RC] \" fmt \"\\n\", ##__VA_ARGS__)",

82. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_1.txt",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * =========================================\n * Module: Response Cache (in-memory LRU)\n *\n * This module provides an in-process, thread-safe LRU cache that is used by the\n * API gateway layer to cache REST/GraphQL responses.  The cache is completely\n * self contained and requires no external runtime dependencies other than the\n * C standard library and pthreads.  The public interface is intentionally\n * minimal to make the module easy to embed inside high-performance request\n * paths without leaking implementation details.\n *\n * Compile:\n *   cc -std=c11 -O2 -pthread -DUSE_UTHASH -c response_cache.c\n *\n * Author: MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License: MIT\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#ifdef USE_UTHASH\n    /*  uthash is distributed under the Revised BSD License.\n     *  https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n     */\n    #include \"uthash.h\"\n#else\n    #error \"This file requires uthash. Define USE_UTHASH and ensure uthash.h is in your include path.\"\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Logging helpers                                                          */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG\n    #define MM_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[%s:%d] \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Type declarations                                                        */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry_t {\n    /* Hash key (path + query + relevant headers). */\n    char                    *key;           /* Null-terminated.                */\n    uint8_t                 *payload;       /* Owned copy of serialized resp.  */\n    size_t                   payload_len;   /* Number of bytes in payload.     */\n    time_t                   expires_at;    /* Epoch seconds.                  */\n\n    /* Intrusive LRU doubly-linked list. */\n    struct cache_entry_t    *prev;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_1.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 433
  },
  "type": "text"
}

LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",

Define USE_UTHASH and ensure uthash.h is in your include path.\"\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Logging helpers                                                          */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG\n    #define MM_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[%s:%d] \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",

  "newString": "#ifdef USE_UTHASH\n    /*  uthash is distributed under the Revised BSD License.\n     *  https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n     */\n    #include \"uthash.h\"\n#else\n    #error \"This file requires uthash. LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)",
  "oldString": "#ifdef USE_UTHASH\n    /*  uthash is distributed under the Revised BSD License.\n     *  https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n     */\n    #include \"uthash.h\"\n#else\n    #error \"This file requires uthash. Define USE_UTHASH and ensure uthash.h is in your include path.\"\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Logging helpers                                                          */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG\n    #define MM_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[%s:%d] \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * =========================================\n * Module: Response Cache (in-memory LRU)\n *\n * This module provides an in-process, thread-safe LRU cache that is used by the\n * API gateway layer to cache REST/GraphQL responses.  The cache is completely\n * self contained and requires no external runtime dependencies other than the\n * C standard library and pthreads.  The public interface is intentionally\n * minimal to make the module easy to embed inside high-performance request\n * paths without leaking implementation details.\n *\n * Compile:\n *   cc -std=c11 -O2 -pthread -DUSE_UTHASH -c response_cache.c\n *\n * Author: MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License: MIT\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#ifdef USE_UTHASH\n    /*  uthash is distributed under the Revised BSD License.\n     *  https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/\n     */\n    #include \"uthash.h\"\n#else\n    #error \"This file requires uthash. Define USE_UTHASH and ensure uthash.h is in your include path.\"\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Logging helpers                                                          */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n#ifndef MM_LOG\n    #define MM_LOG(fmt, ...) fprintf(stderr, \"[%s:%d] \" fmt \"\\n\", __FILE__, __LINE__, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#endif\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Type declarations                                                        */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry_t {\n    /* Hash key (path + query + relevant headers). */\n    char                    *key;           /* Null-terminated.                */\n    uint8_t                 *payload;       /* Owned copy of serialized resp.  */\n    size_t                   payload_len;   /* Number of bytes in payload.     */\n    time_t                   expires_at;    /* Epoch seconds.                  */\n\n    /* Intrusive LRU doubly-linked list. */\n    struct cache_entry_t    *prev;\n    struct cache_entry_t    *next;\n\n    /* uthash handle for O(1) lookups. */\n    UT_hash_handle           hh;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\n/* The cache object. */\ntypedef struct response_cache_t {\n    cache_entry_t           *table;         /* Hash table root.                */\n    cache_entry_t           *lru_head;      /* MRU end.                        */\n    cache_entry_t           *lru_tail;      /* LRU end.                        */\n    size_t                   capacity;      /* Max number of items.            */\n    size_t                   count;         /* Current number of items.        */\n\n    /* Metrics (must be atomic for lock-free collection). */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t     hits;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t     misses;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t     evictions;\n\n    /* Readers\u2013writers lock for concurrency control. */\n    pthread_rwlock_t         rwlock;\n} response_cache_t;\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Internal helpers                                                         */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Move `entry` to the MRU position (head of list). */\nstatic inline void\nrcache_move_to_front(response_cache_t *cache, cache_entry_t *entry)\n{\n    if (cache->lru_head == entry) return;   /* Already at front. */\n\n    /* Detach from current position. */\n    if (entry->prev) entry->prev->next = entry->next;\n    if (entry->next) entry->next->prev = entry->prev;\n    if (cache->lru_tail == entry) cache->lru_tail = entry->prev;\n\n    /* Insert at head. */\n    entry->prev = NULL;\n    entry->next = cache->lru_head;\n    if (cache->lru_head) cache->lru_head->prev = entry;\n    cache->lru_head = entry;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail) cache->lru_tail = entry; /* First element case. */\n}\n\n/* Remove the least-recently-used element (tail). */\nstatic void\nrcache_evict_lru(response_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache->lru_tail) return;\n\n    cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n\n    /* Unlink from LRU list. */\n    if (victim->prev)\n        victim->prev->next = NULL;\n    cache->lru_tail = victim->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_head == victim)\n        cache->lru_head = NULL;\n\n    /* Remove from hash table. */\n    HASH_DEL(cache->table, victim);\n\n    /* Free resources. */\n    free(victim->key);\n    free(victim->payload);\n    free(victim);\n\n    cache->count--;\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* Check if a cache entry is expired w.r.t. the current time. */\nstatic inline bool\nrcache_is_expired(const cache_entry_t *e, time_t now)\n{\n    return e->expires_at != 0 && e->expires_at <= now;\n}\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Public API                                                               */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * Initialize a response cache with `capacity` items.  Returns 0 on success or\n * an errno value on failure.\n */\nint\nrcache_init(response_cache_t *cache, size_t capacity)\n{\n    if (!cache || capacity == 0) return EINVAL;\n\n    memset(cache, 0, sizeof(*cache));\n    cache->capacity = capacity;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&cache->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        MM_LOG(\"pthread_rwlock_init failed\");\n        return errno;\n    }\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * Free all resources associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid\nrcache_destroy(response_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    /* Acquire exclusive lock to safely iterate/free. */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *curr, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, curr, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, curr);\n        free(curr->key);\n        free(curr->payload);\n        free(curr);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*\n * Insert or update an entry.  If the cache is full, the LRU entry is evicted.\n * A TTL of 0 means \"no expiration\".\n *\n * Returns 0 on success or ENOMEM.\n */\nint\nrcache_put(response_cache_t  *cache,\n           const char        *key,\n           const void        *payload,\n           size_t             payload_len,\n           uint32_t           ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    time_t expires = ttl_seconds ? now + ttl_seconds : 0;\n\n    /* Allocate copies outside write lock to reduce critical-section time. */\n    char *key_copy = strdup(key);\n    if (!key_copy) return ENOMEM;\n\n    uint8_t *payload_copy = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!payload_copy) { free(key_copy); return ENOMEM; }\n    memcpy(payload_copy, payload, payload_len);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    /* Check if the key already exists. */\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Update existing entry. */\n        free(entry->payload);\n        entry->payload     = payload_copy;\n        entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n        entry->expires_at  = expires;\n\n        free(key_copy); /* Key string unchanged. */\n\n        rcache_move_to_front(cache, entry);\n    } else {\n        /* Insert new entry. */\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) {\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n            free(key_copy);\n            free(payload_copy);\n            return ENOMEM;\n        }\n\n        entry->key         = key_copy;\n        entry->payload     = payload_copy;\n        entry->payload_len = payload_len;\n        entry->expires_at  = expires;\n\n        /* Add to hash table. */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->table, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n\n        /* Insert at MRU position. */\n        entry->next = cache->lru_head;\n        if (cache->lru_head) cache->lru_head->prev = entry;\n        cache->lru_head = entry;\n        if (!cache->lru_tail) cache->lru_tail = entry;\n\n        cache->count++;\n\n        /* Evict if over capacity. */\n        if (cache->count > cache->capacity) {\n            rcache_evict_lru(cache);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * Retrieve a cached item.  The caller receives an allocated buffer that must\n * be freed with `free()`.  *payload_len_out is set to the size in bytes.\n *\n * Returns 0 on HIT, ENOENT on MISS, or other errno for error.\n */\nint\nrcache_get(response_cache_t  *cache,\n           const char        *key,\n           uint8_t          **payload_out,\n           size_t            *payload_len_out)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload_out || !payload_len_out)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    if (!entry || rcache_is_expired(entry, now)) {\n        /* Miss or expired. */\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n        if (entry && rcache_is_expired(entry, now)) {\n            /* Upgrade to writer to remove expired entry. */\n            pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n            HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n            if (entry->prev) entry->prev->next = entry->next;\n            if (entry->next) entry->next->prev = entry->prev;\n            if (cache->lru_head == entry) cache->lru_head = entry->next;\n            if (cache->lru_tail == entry) cache->lru_tail = entry->prev;\n            cache->count--;\n            pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n            free(entry->key);\n            free(entry->payload);\n            free(entry);\n        }\n\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n\n    /* Cache HIT: make a copy for the caller. */\n    uint8_t *buf = malloc(entry->payload_len);\n    if (!buf) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(buf, entry->payload, entry->payload_len);\n    *payload_out     = buf;\n    *payload_len_out = entry->payload_len;\n\n    /* Promote to MRU position. */\n    rcache_move_to_front(cache, entry);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * Remove a single entry (if present).\n */\nvoid\nrcache_invalidate(response_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n\n        /* Unlink from LRU list. */\n        if (entry->prev) entry->prev->next = entry->next;\n        if (entry->next) entry->next->prev = entry->prev;\n        if (cache->lru_head == entry) cache->lru_head = entry->next;\n        if (cache->lru_tail == entry) cache->lru_tail = entry->prev;\n\n        cache->count--;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        free(entry->key);\n        free(entry->payload);\n        free(entry);\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * Render Prometheus style metrics into `buf`.  Returns the number of bytes\n * written (excluding trailing NUL) or a negative errno.\n */\nssize_t\nrcache_format_metrics(response_cache_t *cache, char *buf, size_t buf_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !buf || buf_len == 0) return -EINVAL;\n\n    uint64_t hits     = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    uint64_t misses   = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    uint64_t evicts   = atomic_load_explicit(&cache->evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    int n = snprintf(buf, buf_len,\n        \"# HELP rcache_hits_total Total number of cache hits\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE rcache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n        \"rcache_hits_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# HELP rcache_misses_total Total number of cache misses\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE rcache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n        \"rcache_misses_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# HELP rcache_evictions_total Total number of evicted entries\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE rcache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n        \"rcache_evictions_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# HELP rcache_entries Current number of cache entries\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE rcache_entries gauge\\n\"\n        \"rcache_entries %zu\\n\",\n        hits, misses, evicts, cache->count);\n\n    if (n < 0 || (size_t)n >= buf_len) return -ENOSPC;\n    return n;\n}\n\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*  Basic self-test (can be disabled in production)                          */\n/*---------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef RCACHE_SELFTEST\n\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void\nrun_selftest(void)\n{\n    response_cache_t cache;\n    assert(rcache_init(&cache, 4) == 0);\n\n    const char *key = \"/v1/hello\";\n    const char *body = \"{\\\"msg\\\":\\\"world\\\"}\";\n    assert(rcache_put(&cache, key, body, strlen(body), 1) == 0);\n\n    uint8_t *out = NULL;\n    size_t len = 0;\n    assert(rcache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len) == 0);\n    assert(len == strlen(body));\n    assert(memcmp(out, body, len) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    /* Wait for expiration. */\n    sleep(2);\n    assert(rcache_get(&cache, key, &out, &len) == ENOENT);\n\n    rcache_destroy(&cache);\n    MM_LOG(\"Self-test passed\");\n}\n\nint main(void) { run_selftest(); }\n\n#endif /* RCACHE_SELFTEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * -----------------------------------------\n * Module 15: In-Memory Event Bus\n *\n * This file provides a thread-safe, low-latency publish/subscribe message bus\n * that allows the various \u201cpseudo-microservices\u201d inside the monolith to\n * exchange domain events without leaving the process.\n *\n * Design goals:\n *   \u2022 lock contention kept to a minimum (per-subscription queues)\n *   \u2022 back-pressure handled via lossy overwrite or blocking wait (configurable)\n *   \u2022 observability hooks for Prometheus metrics\n *   \u2022 graceful shutdown & resource cleanup\n *\n * Authors:   MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License:   Apache-2.0\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Forward declarations for the monitoring layer. These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \u201cmonitoring/metrics.c\u201d, but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][error] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][warn] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n#ifdef MERCURY_DEBUG\n    fprintf(stdout, \"[event_bus][debug] %s\\n\", msg);\n#else\n    MM_UNUSED(msg);\n#endif\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Event definition                                                            */\n\n/* A single event message. Payload is an opaque blob owned by the bus. */\ntypedef struct mm_event {\n    char    *type;          /* Event type string / topic name.               */\n    void    *payload;       /* Opaque payload data.                           */\n    size_t   payload_size;  /* Bytes in payload.                              */\n    uint64_t timestamp_ns;  /* Monotonic clock time when event was published. */\n} mm_event_t;\n\n/* Construct a deep copy of an event so that each subscriber owns its memory. */\nstatic mm_event_t *mm_event_clone(const char *type,\n                                  const void *payload,\n                                  size_t payload_size)\n{\n    mm_event_t *ev = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_event_t));\n    if (!ev) {",

    "numLines": 80,
    "startLine": 1,

  },

}

These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \"monitoring/metrics.c\", but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_ERROR(\"%s\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_WARN(\"%s\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_DEBUG(\"%s\", msg);\n}",
  "old_string": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Forward declarations for the monitoring layer. These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \"monitoring/metrics.c\", but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][error] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][warn] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n#ifdef MERCURY_DEBUG\n    fprintf(stdout, \"[event_bus][debug] %s\\n\", msg);\n#else\n    MM_UNUSED(msg);\n#endif\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Forward declarations for the monitoring layer. These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \"monitoring/metrics.c\", but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_ERROR(\"%s\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_WARN(\"%s\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n    LOG_DEBUG(\"%s\", msg);\n}",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Forward declarations for the monitoring layer. These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \u201cmonitoring/metrics.c\u201d, but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][error] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][warn] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n#ifdef MERCURY_DEBUG\n    fprintf(stdout, \"[event_bus][debug] %s\\n\", msg);\n#else\n    MM_UNUSED(msg);\n#endif\n}",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * -----------------------------------------\n * Module 15: In-Memory Event Bus\n *\n * This file provides a thread-safe, low-latency publish/subscribe message bus\n * that allows the various \u201cpseudo-microservices\u201d inside the monolith to\n * exchange domain events without leaving the process.\n *\n * Design goals:\n *   \u2022 lock contention kept to a minimum (per-subscription queues)\n *   \u2022 back-pressure handled via lossy overwrite or blocking wait (configurable)\n *   \u2022 observability hooks for Prometheus metrics\n *   \u2022 graceful shutdown & resource cleanup\n *\n * Authors:   MercuryMonolith Core Team\n * License:   Apache-2.0\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Forward declarations for the monitoring layer. These are implemented in\n * the project-wide \u201cmonitoring/metrics.c\u201d, but we only need the prototypes\n * here to keep this module self-contained.\n */\nvoid mm_metrics_counter_inc(const char *name, uint64_t value);\nvoid mm_metrics_gauge_set(const char *name, double value);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Utility macros                                                              */\n#define MM_UNUSED(x)   ((void)(x))\n#define MM_ARRAY_GROW_CAP(cap)  ((cap) == 0 ? 4 : (cap) * 2)\n\n/* Log helpers (redirected to the unified structured logging subsystem). */\nstatic inline void mm_log_error(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][error] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_warn(const char *msg)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[event_bus][warn] %s\\n\", msg);\n}\n\nstatic inline void mm_log_debug(const char *msg)\n{\n#ifdef MERCURY_DEBUG\n    fprintf(stdout, \"[event_bus][debug] %s\\n\", msg);\n#else\n    MM_UNUSED(msg);\n#endif\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Event definition                                                            */\n\n/* A single event message. Payload is an opaque blob owned by the bus. */\ntypedef struct mm_event {\n    char    *type;          /* Event type string / topic name.               */\n    void    *payload;       /* Opaque payload data.                           */\n    size_t   payload_size;  /* Bytes in payload.                              */\n    uint64_t timestamp_ns;  /* Monotonic clock time when event was published. */\n} mm_event_t;\n\n/* Construct a deep copy of an event so that each subscriber owns its memory. */\nstatic mm_event_t *mm_event_clone(const char *type,\n                                  const void *payload,\n                                  size_t payload_size)\n{\n    mm_event_t *ev = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_event_t));\n    if (!ev) {\n        mm_log_error(\"Out of memory cloning event header\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    ev->type = strdup(type);\n    if (!ev->type) {\n        free(ev);\n        mm_log_error(\"Out of memory cloning event type\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    ev->payload = malloc(payload_size);\n    if (payload_size && !ev->payload) {\n        free(ev->type);\n        free(ev);\n        mm_log_error(\"Out of memory cloning event payload\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    if (payload_size) {\n        memcpy(ev->payload, payload, payload_size);\n    }\n\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n    ev->timestamp_ns = ((uint64_t) ts.tv_sec * 1000000000ull) + ts.tv_nsec;\n    ev->payload_size = payload_size;\n\n    return ev;\n}\n\nstatic void mm_event_free(mm_event_t *ev)\n{\n    if (!ev) return;\n    free(ev->type);\n    free(ev->payload);\n    free(ev);\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Subscription queue (single-producer, single-consumer ring buffer)           */\n\ntypedef enum {\n    MM_SUB_POLICY_BLOCK,     /* Wait until space is available (publisher)     */\n    MM_SUB_POLICY_OVERWRITE  /* Drop oldest message if queue is full          */\n} mm_sub_overflow_policy_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_sub_queue {\n    mm_event_t               **buf;      /* Ring buffer array                 */\n    size_t                     cap;      /* Capacity (# of pointers)          */\n    size_t                     head;     /* Next position to read             */\n    size_t                     tail;     /* Next position to write            */\n    pthread_mutex_t            mtx;      /* Protects head/tail & buf          */\n    pthread_cond_t             cv;       /* Signaled when new data arrives    */\n    bool                       closed;   /* Set by unsubscribe / shutdown     */\n    mm_sub_overflow_policy_t   policy;   /* Overflow behaviour                */\n} mm_sub_queue_t;\n\n/* Forward to avoid circular reference */\nstruct mm_subscription;\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Topic index: each topic owns a list of subscriptions                        */\n\ntypedef struct mm_topic {\n    char                   *name;\n    struct mm_subscription *subs;      /* Linked list head */\n    struct mm_topic        *next;      /* Next topic */\n} mm_topic_t;\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Subscription metadata                                                       */\n\ntypedef struct mm_subscription {\n    uint64_t            id;        /* Unique handle returned to caller */\n    mm_sub_queue_t      queue;     /* Per-subscriber ring buffer       */\n    struct mm_topic    *topic;     /* Back-pointer                     */\n    struct mm_subscription *next;  /* Next subscription in topic list  */\n} mm_subscription_t;\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Event bus                                                                   */\n\ntypedef struct mm_event_bus {\n    mm_topic_t           *topics;          /* Linked list of topics   */\n    pthread_mutex_t       mtx;             /* Protects topic index    */\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t  next_sub_id;     /* Monotonically increasing */\n    bool                  shutting_down;   /* Reject new work         */\n} mm_event_bus_t;\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Helper: allocate & initialise queue                                         */\nstatic int mm_queue_init(mm_sub_queue_t *q,\n                         size_t capacity,\n                         mm_sub_overflow_policy_t policy)\n{\n    memset(q, 0, sizeof(*q));\n    q->cap = capacity ? capacity : 1024;\n    q->policy = policy;\n    q->buf = calloc(q->cap, sizeof(mm_event_t *));\n    if (!q->buf) {\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_init(&q->mtx, NULL);\n    pthread_cond_init(&q->cv, NULL);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nstatic void mm_queue_free(mm_sub_queue_t *q)\n{\n    if (!q) return;\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < q->cap; ++i) {\n        mm_event_free(q->buf[i]);\n    }\n    free(q->buf);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&q->mtx);\n    pthread_cond_destroy(&q->cv);\n}\n\n/* Push an event into queue. Caller holds queue->mtx. */\nstatic int mm_queue_push_locked(mm_sub_queue_t *q, mm_event_t *ev)\n{\n    size_t next_tail = (q->tail + 1) % q->cap;\n    bool full = (next_tail == q->head);\n\n    if (full) {\n        if (q->policy == MM_SUB_POLICY_BLOCK) {\n            /* Wait until consumer reads at least one event */\n            do {\n                pthread_cond_wait(&q->cv, &q->mtx);\n                next_tail = (q->tail + 1) % q->cap;\n                full = (next_tail == q->head);\n            } while (full && !q->closed);\n            if (q->closed) return ECANCELED;\n        } else if (q->policy == MM_SUB_POLICY_OVERWRITE) {\n            /* Drop the oldest event */\n            mm_event_free(q->buf[q->head]);\n            q->head = (q->head + 1) % q->cap;\n        }\n    }\n\n    q->buf[q->tail] = ev;\n    q->tail = next_tail;\n    pthread_cond_signal(&q->cv);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Pop an event with optional timeout (ms). Returns NULL on timeout/closed.    */\nstatic mm_event_t *mm_queue_pop(mm_sub_queue_t *q, int timeout_ms)\n{\n    struct timespec ts_deadline;\n    bool use_timeout = timeout_ms >= 0;\n    if (use_timeout) {\n        struct timespec now;\n        clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &now);\n        ts_deadline.tv_sec  = now.tv_sec + timeout_ms / 1000;\n        ts_deadline.tv_nsec = now.tv_nsec + (timeout_ms % 1000) * 1000000;\n        if (ts_deadline.tv_nsec >= 1000000000) {\n            ts_deadline.tv_sec++;\n            ts_deadline.tv_nsec -= 1000000000;\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&q->mtx);\n    while (q->head == q->tail && !q->closed) {\n        int rc;\n        if (use_timeout) {\n            rc = pthread_cond_timedwait(&q->cv, &q->mtx, &ts_deadline);\n            if (rc == ETIMEDOUT) {\n                pthread_mutex_unlock(&q->mtx);\n                return NULL;\n            }\n        } else {\n            pthread_cond_wait(&q->cv, &q->mtx);\n        }\n    }\n\n    if (q->closed) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&q->mtx);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mm_event_t *ev = q->buf[q->head];\n    q->buf[q->head] = NULL; /* Prevent double free on shutdown. */\n    q->head = (q->head + 1) % q->cap;\n    pthread_cond_signal(&q->cv); /* Wake publisher if blocked. */\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&q->mtx);\n    return ev;\n}\n\n/* Close queue and wake up any blocked readers/writers. */\nstatic void mm_queue_close(mm_sub_queue_t *q)\n{\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&q->mtx);\n    q->closed = true;\n    pthread_cond_broadcast(&q->cv);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&q->mtx);\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Internal helpers for topic management                                       */\n\n/* Search or create topic. Caller must hold bus->mtx. */\nstatic mm_topic_t *mm_topic_get_or_create(mm_event_bus_t *bus,\n                                          const char *name,\n                                          bool create_if_missing)\n{\n    mm_topic_t *t = bus->topics;\n    while (t) {\n        if (strcmp(t->name, name) == 0) {\n            return t;\n        }\n        t = t->next;\n    }\n    if (!create_if_missing) return NULL;\n\n    /* Create new topic */\n    t = calloc(1, sizeof(*t));\n    if (!t) return NULL;\n    t->name = strdup(name);\n    if (!t->name) {\n        free(t);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    t->next = bus->topics;\n    bus->topics = t;\n    return t;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Public API                                                                  */\n\nmm_event_bus_t *mm_event_bus_create(void)\n{\n    mm_event_bus_t *bus = calloc(1, sizeof(mm_event_bus_t));\n    if (!bus) return NULL;\n    pthread_mutex_init(&bus->mtx, NULL);\n    atomic_init(&bus->next_sub_id, 1);\n    bus->shutting_down = false;\n    mm_metrics_gauge_set(\"mm_event_bus_topics\", 0.0);\n    mm_metrics_gauge_set(\"mm_event_bus_subscriptions\", 0.0);\n    return bus;\n}\n\nvoid mm_event_bus_destroy(mm_event_bus_t *bus)\n{\n    if (!bus) return;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n    bus->shutting_down = true;\n\n    mm_topic_t *topic = bus->topics;\n    while (topic) {\n        mm_subscription_t *sub = topic->subs;\n        while (sub) {\n            mm_queue_close(&sub->queue);\n            sub = sub->next;\n        }\n        topic = topic->next;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n\n    /* Give consumers time to drain and exit (optional). */\n    /* In production we would join known consumer threads; out of scope here. */\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n    topic = bus->topics;\n    while (topic) {\n        mm_subscription_t *sub = topic->subs;\n        while (sub) {\n            mm_subscription_t *next_sub = sub->next;\n            mm_queue_free(&sub->queue);\n            free(sub);\n            sub = next_sub;\n        }\n        mm_topic_t *next_topic = topic->next;\n        free(topic->name);\n        free(topic);\n        topic = next_topic;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&bus->mtx);\n    free(bus);\n    mm_metrics_gauge_set(\"mm_event_bus_topics\", 0.0);\n    mm_metrics_gauge_set(\"mm_event_bus_subscriptions\", 0.0);\n}\n\nuint64_t mm_event_bus_subscribe(mm_event_bus_t *bus,\n                                const char *topic_name,\n                                size_t queue_capacity,\n                                mm_sub_overflow_policy_t policy)\n{\n    if (!bus || !topic_name) return 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n    if (bus->shutting_down) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    mm_topic_t *topic = mm_topic_get_or_create(bus, topic_name, true);\n    if (!topic) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    mm_subscription_t *sub = calloc(1, sizeof(*sub));\n    if (!sub) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return 0;\n    }\n    sub->id = atomic_fetch_add(&bus->next_sub_id, 1);\n    sub->topic = topic;\n    int rc = mm_queue_init(&sub->queue, queue_capacity, policy);\n    if (rc != 0) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        free(sub);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Insert at list head */\n    sub->next = topic->subs;\n    topic->subs = sub;\n\n    mm_metrics_counter_inc(\"mm_event_bus_subscribe_total\", 1);\n    double current = (double) atomic_load(&bus->next_sub_id) - 1;\n    mm_metrics_gauge_set(\"mm_event_bus_subscriptions\", current);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n    mm_log_debug(\"new subscription registered\");\n    return sub->id;\n}\n\nint mm_event_bus_unsubscribe(mm_event_bus_t *bus, uint64_t sub_id)\n{\n    if (!bus || sub_id == 0) return EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n\n    mm_topic_t *topic = bus->topics;\n    while (topic) {\n        mm_subscription_t **prev = &topic->subs;\n        mm_subscription_t *sub = topic->subs;\n        while (sub) {\n            if (sub->id == sub_id) {\n                /* Remove from list */\n                *prev = sub->next;\n                mm_queue_close(&sub->queue);\n                pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n\n                mm_queue_free(&sub->queue);\n                free(sub);\n\n                mm_metrics_counter_inc(\"mm_event_bus_unsubscribe_total\", 1);\n                /* We don\u2019t know exact count without scanning, but keep rough metric. */\n                return 0;\n            }\n            prev = &sub->next;\n            sub = sub->next;\n        }\n        topic = topic->next;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n    return ENOENT;\n}\n\nint mm_event_bus_publish(mm_event_bus_t *bus,\n                         const char *topic_name,\n                         const void *payload,\n                         size_t payload_size)\n{\n    if (!bus || !topic_name) return EINVAL;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n    if (bus->shutting_down) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return ECANCELED;\n    }\n\n    mm_topic_t *topic = mm_topic_get_or_create(bus, topic_name, false);\n    if (!topic || !topic->subs) {\n        /* No subscribers \u2013 early exit. */\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Clone event once per subscriber to avoid contention. */\n    mm_subscription_t *sub = topic->subs;\n    int result = 0;\n    while (sub) {\n        mm_event_t *ev = mm_event_clone(topic_name, payload, payload_size);\n        if (!ev) {\n            result = ENOMEM;\n            break;\n        }\n\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&sub->queue.mtx);\n        int rc = mm_queue_push_locked(&sub->queue, ev);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&sub->queue.mtx);\n        if (rc != 0) {\n            mm_event_free(ev);\n            result = rc; /* Continue to push others, but remember first error */\n        }\n        sub = sub->next;\n    }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n    mm_metrics_counter_inc(\"mm_event_bus_publish_total\", 1);\n    return result;\n}\n\nint mm_event_bus_receive(mm_event_bus_t *bus,\n                         uint64_t sub_id,\n                         mm_event_t **out_event,\n                         int timeout_ms)\n{\n    if (!bus || !out_event) return EINVAL;\n    *out_event = NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&bus->mtx);\n    mm_topic_t *topic = bus->topics;\n    mm_subscription_t *target = NULL;\n    while (topic && !target) {\n        mm_subscription_t *sub = topic->subs;\n        while (sub) {\n            if (sub->id == sub_id) {\n                target = sub;\n                break;\n            }\n            sub = sub->next;\n        }\n        topic = topic->next;\n    }\n    if (!target) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n\n    /* Increase ref count of queue by taking pointer; safe after unlock. */\n    mm_sub_queue_t *queue = &target->queue;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&bus->mtx);\n\n    mm_event_t *ev = mm_queue_pop(queue, timeout_ms);\n    if (!ev) {\n        return ETIMEDOUT;\n    }\n    *out_event = ev;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Convenience wrapper */\nvoid mm_event_free_message(mm_event_t *ev)\n{\n    mm_event_free(ev);\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n/* Example usage (unit test skeleton)                                          */\n#ifdef MERCURY_EVENT_BUS_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void *producer_thread(void *arg)\n{\n    mm_event_bus_t *bus = arg;\n    for (int i = 0; i < 1000; ++i) {\n        char buf[32];\n        snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, \"hello-%d\", i);\n        mm_event_bus_publish(bus, \"demo.topic\", buf, strlen(buf) + 1);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void *consumer_thread(void *arg)\n{\n    uint64_t sub_id = *(uint64_t *) arg;\n    extern mm_event_bus_t *g_bus;\n\n    mm_event_t *ev;\n    int rc;\n    size_t cnt = 0;\n    while ((rc = mm_event_bus_receive(g_bus, sub_id, &ev, 100)) == 0) {\n        cnt++;\n        printf(\"recv: %s\\n\", (char *) ev->payload);\n        mm_event_free_message(ev);\n        if (cnt == 1000) break;\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nmm_event_bus_t *g_bus;\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    g_bus = mm_event_bus_create();\n    uint64_t sub_id = mm_event_bus_subscribe(g_bus,\n                                             \"demo.topic\",\n                                             256,\n                                             MM_SUB_POLICY_BLOCK);\n\n    pthread_t prod, cons;\n    pthread_create(&prod, NULL, producer_thread, g_bus);\n    pthread_create(&cons, NULL, consumer_thread, &sub_id);\n\n    pthread_join(prod, NULL);\n    pthread_join(cons, NULL);\n\n    mm_event_bus_unsubscribe(g_bus, sub_id);\n    mm_event_bus_destroy(g_bus);\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

        " /* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */",

88. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_26.txt",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * src/module_26.c\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------\n * Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with TTL expiration, LRU eviction,\n * and basic Prometheus-style instrumentation.\n *\n * This module is used across the REST/GraphQL gateway to avoid executing\n * expensive service code paths when a stable response can be served directly\n * from memory.  The cache is intentionally self-contained and does not depend\n * on any external daemon (e.g., Redis) so that deployments remain\n * single-binary and operationally simple.\n *\n * Public API (see comments below):\n *  - mmc_cache_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                  uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n *                                  mmc_logger_t *logger);\n *  - void mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_t *);\n *  - bool mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       uint8_t **out_buf, size_t *out_len);\n *  - void mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       const uint8_t *buf, size_t len,\n *                       uint32_t ttl_override_sec);\n *  - void mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key);\n *  - void mmc_cache_collect_metrics(const mmc_cache_t *,\n *                                   struct mmc_cache_metrics *);\n *\n * NOTE: This file deliberately has no header; it is intended to be compiled\n *       once into the final monolith and accessed through the forward\n *       declarations in the central \"mmc.h\" umbrella header.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L  /* clock_gettime */\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash-table implementation (public domain) */\n#include \"external/uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"mmc_logger.h\"   /* Project-wide structured logger abstraction */\n#include \"mmc_metrics.h\"  /* Prometheus exposition helpers */\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_26.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 483
  },
  "type": "text"
}

    "content": "                 uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n                 mmc_logger_t *logger)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[mmc_cache] max_entries must be > 0\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) {\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    c->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec ? default_ttl_sec : 60;\n    c->logger           = logger;",
