  "originalFile": "/*\n * src/module_26.c\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------\n * Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with TTL expiration, LRU eviction,\n * and basic Prometheus-style instrumentation.\n *\n * This module is used across the REST/GraphQL gateway to avoid executing\n * expensive service code paths when a stable response can be served directly\n * from memory.  The cache is intentionally self-contained and does not depend\n * on any external daemon (e.g., Redis) so that deployments remain\n * single-binary and operationally simple.\n *\n * Public API (see comments below):\n *  - mmc_cache_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                  uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n *                                  mmc_logger_t *logger);\n *  - void mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_t *);\n *  - bool mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       uint8_t **out_buf, size_t *out_len);\n *  - void mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       const uint8_t *buf, size_t len,\n *                       uint32_t ttl_override_sec);\n *  - void mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key);\n *  - void mmc_cache_collect_metrics(const mmc_cache_t *,\n *                                   struct mmc_cache_metrics *);\n *\n * NOTE: This file deliberately has no header; it is intended to be compiled\n *       once into the final monolith and accessed through the forward\n *       declarations in the central \"mmc.h\" umbrella header.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L  /* clock_gettime */\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash-table implementation (public domain) */\n#include \"external/uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"mmc_logger.h\"   /* Project-wide structured logger abstraction */\n#include \"mmc_metrics.h\"  /* Prometheus exposition helpers */\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Data Types                          *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Linked list node for LRU management */\ntypedef struct lru_node_s {\n    struct lru_node_s *prev;\n    struct lru_node_s *next;\n    char              *key; /* shallow copy; owned by entry */\n} lru_node_t;\n\n/* Cache entry stored in hash table                       */\ntypedef struct cache_entry_s {\n    char            *key;        /* key string (deep copy)          */\n    uint8_t         *blob;       /* payload (deep copy)             */\n    size_t           blob_len;\n    uint64_t         expiry_epoch_ms;\n    lru_node_t       lru;        /* embedded LRU node               */\n\n    UT_hash_handle   hh;         /* makes this structure hashable   */\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_s {\n    size_t            max_entries;\n    uint32_t          default_ttl_sec;\n    cache_entry_t    *hash;          /* uthash handle */\n    lru_node_t        lru_head;      /* sentinel head/tail for O(1)  */\n    pthread_rwlock_t  rwlock;        /* read/write lock */\n    mmc_logger_t     *logger;\n\n    /* Metrics (atomics for lock-free reads) */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t puts;\n} mmc_cache_t;\n\n/* Exposed metrics container (for snapshot) */\nstruct mmc_cache_metrics {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    uint64_t puts;\n    size_t   current_entries;\n};\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                 Internal Helper Functions                 *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_epoch_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n\n/* Initialize LRU doubly-linked list sentinel */\nstatic void\nlru_init(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    c->lru_head.prev = &c->lru_head;\n    c->lru_head.next = &c->lru_head;\n}\n\n/* Remove node from its current position */\nstatic inline void\nlru_detach(lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    node->prev->next = node->next;\n    node->next->prev = node->prev;\n}\n\n/* Insert node at front (MRU) */\nstatic inline void\nlru_insert_front(mmc_cache_t *c, lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    node->next = c->lru_head.next;\n    node->prev = &c->lru_head;\n    c->lru_head.next->prev = node;\n    c->lru_head.next      = node;\n}\n\n/* Move node to front (after hit) */\nstatic inline void\nlru_move_to_front(mmc_cache_t *c, lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    lru_detach(node);\n    lru_insert_front(c, node);\n}\n\n/* Evict LRU tail, return the removed entry (locked) */\nstatic cache_entry_t *\nevict_lru_tail(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head.prev == &c->lru_head) {\n        return NULL; /* empty */\n    }\n    lru_node_t *tail = c->lru_head.prev;\n    lru_detach(tail);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry = (cache_entry_t *)((char *)tail -\n                                    offsetof(cache_entry_t, lru));\n    HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    return entry;\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                     Public  API                           *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nmmc_cache_t *\nmmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n                 uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n                 mmc_logger_t *logger)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[mmc_cache] max_entries must be > 0\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) {\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    c->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec ? default_ttl_sec : 60;\n    c->logger           = logger;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL);\n    lru_init(c);\n\n    mmc_log_info(c->logger,\n                 \"response_cache.init\",\n                 \"Initialized cache with capacity=%zu ttl=%us\",\n                 c->max_entries, c->default_ttl_sec);\n    return c;\n}\n\nvoid\nmmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, cur);\n        free(cur->key);\n        free(cur->blob);\n        free(cur);\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/*\n * Retrieve value for `key`.\n *  - On hit: returns true, caller owns *out_buf (malloced).\n *  - On miss: returns false, *out_buf untouched.\n */\nbool\nmmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_t *c,\n              const char *key,\n              uint8_t **out_buf,\n              size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return false;\n\n    uint64_t now_ms = now_epoch_ms();\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock); /* upgradeable read would be nicer */\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (!entry || entry->expiry_epoch_ms < now_ms) {\n        if (entry) {\n            /* Expired entry\u2014remove it */\n            lru_detach(&entry->lru);\n            HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n            free(entry->key);\n            free(entry->blob);\n            free(entry);\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    /* Cache hit */\n    lru_move_to_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    /* Deep copy payload for caller */\n    uint8_t *copy = malloc(entry->blob_len);\n    if (!copy) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return false;\n    }\n    memcpy(copy, entry->blob, entry->blob_len);\n    *out_buf = copy;\n    *out_len = entry->blob_len;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return true;\n}\n\n/*\n * Store value for `key` with optional TTL override (seconds).\n * When capacity exceeded, evicts least-recently-used entry.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_t *c,\n              const char *key,\n              const uint8_t *buf,\n              size_t len,\n              uint32_t ttl_override_sec)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !buf || len == 0) return;\n\n    uint64_t now_ms  = now_epoch_ms();\n    uint64_t ttl_sec = ttl_override_sec ? ttl_override_sec : c->default_ttl_sec;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Replace existing entry in place */\n        free(entry->blob);\n        entry->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!entry->blob) goto unlock;\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, len);\n        entry->blob_len        = len;\n        entry->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms + ttl_sec * 1000ULL;\n\n        lru_move_to_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    } else {\n        /* Evict if over capacity */\n        if (HASH_COUNT(c->hash) >= c->max_entries) {\n            cache_entry_t *ev = evict_lru_tail(c);\n            if (ev) {\n                free(ev->key);\n                free(ev->blob);\n                free(ev);\n            }\n        }\n\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) goto unlock;\n\n        entry->key = strdup(key);\n        if (!entry->key) { free(entry); goto unlock; }\n\n        entry->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!entry->blob) { free(entry->key); free(entry); goto unlock; }\n\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, len);\n        entry->blob_len        = len;\n        entry->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms + ttl_sec * 1000ULL;\n\n        /* Insert into hash & LRU front */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->hash, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n        lru_insert_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    }\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->puts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\nunlock:\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* Explicitly remove key (e.g., after underlying data mutation) */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        lru_detach(&entry->lru);\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n        free(entry->key);\n        free(entry->blob);\n        free(entry);\n\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* Collect metrics snapshot (no lock required for counters) */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_collect_metrics(const mmc_cache_t *c,\n                          struct mmc_cache_metrics *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n\n    out->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&c->misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&c->evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->puts      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->puts, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n    out->current_entries = HASH_COUNT(c->hash);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *            Periodic Cleanup & Prometheus Export           *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * Lightweight cleanup thread that runs every N seconds to remove expired\n * entries. This avoids accumulations when keys are rarely accessed.\n */\nstruct cleanup_args {\n    mmc_cache_t *cache;\n    uint32_t     interval_sec;\n};\n\nstatic void *\ncleanup_thread_fn(void *arg)\n{\n    struct cleanup_args *ctx = arg;\n    mmc_cache_t *c = ctx->cache;\n    const uint32_t sleep_sec = ctx->interval_sec;\n\n    while (true) {\n        struct timespec ts = { .tv_sec = sleep_sec, .tv_nsec = 0 };\n        nanosleep(&ts, NULL);\n\n        uint64_t now_ms = now_epoch_ms();\n        size_t removed  = 0;\n\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n        cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, cur, tmp) {\n            if (cur->expiry_epoch_ms < now_ms) {\n                lru_detach(&cur->lru);\n                HASH_DEL(c->hash, cur);\n                free(cur->key);\n                free(cur->blob);\n                free(cur);\n                atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1,\n                                          memory_order_relaxed);\n                ++removed;\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        if (removed > 0) {\n            mmc_log_debug(c->logger,\n                          \"response_cache.cleanup\",\n                          \"Removed %zu expired entries\", removed);\n        }\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\n/*\n * Spawn background cleanup thread. Should be invoked once at service start.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_start_cleanup_daemon(mmc_cache_t *c, uint32_t interval_sec)\n{\n    if (!c || interval_sec == 0) return;\n\n    static pthread_t tid;\n    static bool started = false;\n    if (started) return;\n\n    struct cleanup_args *args = malloc(sizeof(*args));\n    args->cache        = c;\n    args->interval_sec = interval_sec;\n\n    int rc = pthread_create(&tid, NULL, cleanup_thread_fn, args);\n    if (rc == 0) {\n        pthread_detach(tid);\n        started = true;\n        mmc_log_info(c->logger,\n                     \"response_cache.daemon\",\n                     \"Started cleanup thread with interval=%us\",\n                     interval_sec);\n    } else {\n        mmc_log_error(c->logger,\n                      \"response_cache.daemon\",\n                      \"Failed to spawn cleanup thread (rc=%d)\", rc);\n        free(args);\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * Expose metrics in Prometheus-compatible text format.\n * Call from the HTTP /metrics endpoint handler.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_prometheus_dump(const mmc_cache_t *c, FILE *out)\n{\n    struct mmc_cache_metrics m = {0};\n    mmc_cache_collect_metrics(c, &m);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_hits_total Total cache hits\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_hits_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_hits_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.hits);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_misses_total Total cache misses\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_misses_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_misses_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.misses);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_evictions_total Total cache evictions\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_evictions_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.evictions);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_puts_total Total cache insertions\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_puts_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_puts_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.puts);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_entries Current number of entries\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_entries gauge\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_entries %zu\\n\", m.current_entries);\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       End of File                         *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n",

  "originalFile": "/*\n * src/module_26.c\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------\n * Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with TTL expiration, LRU eviction,\n * and basic Prometheus-style instrumentation.\n *\n * This module is used across the REST/GraphQL gateway to avoid executing\n * expensive service code paths when a stable response can be served directly\n * from memory.  The cache is intentionally self-contained and does not depend\n * on any external daemon (e.g., Redis) so that deployments remain\n * single-binary and operationally simple.\n *\n * Public API (see comments below):\n *  - mmc_cache_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                  uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n *                                  mmc_logger_t *logger);\n *  - void mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_t *);\n *  - bool mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       uint8_t **out_buf, size_t *out_len);\n *  - void mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key,\n *                       const uint8_t *buf, size_t len,\n *                       uint32_t ttl_override_sec);\n *  - void mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_t *, const char *key);\n *  - void mmc_cache_collect_metrics(const mmc_cache_t *,\n *                                   struct mmc_cache_metrics *);\n *\n * NOTE: This file deliberately has no header; it is intended to be compiled\n *       once into the final monolith and accessed through the forward\n *       declarations in the central \"mmc.h\" umbrella header.\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L  /* clock_gettime */\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash-table implementation (public domain) */\n#include \"external/uthash.h\"\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"mmc_metrics.h\"  /* Prometheus exposition helpers */\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       Data Types                          *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/* Linked list node for LRU management */\ntypedef struct lru_node_s {\n    struct lru_node_s *prev;\n    struct lru_node_s *next;\n    char              *key; /* shallow copy; owned by entry */\n} lru_node_t;\n\n/* Cache entry stored in hash table                       */\ntypedef struct cache_entry_s {\n    char            *key;        /* key string (deep copy)          */\n    uint8_t         *blob;       /* payload (deep copy)             */\n    size_t           blob_len;\n    uint64_t         expiry_epoch_ms;\n    lru_node_t       lru;        /* embedded LRU node               */\n\n    UT_hash_handle   hh;         /* makes this structure hashable   */\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_s {\n    size_t            max_entries;\n    uint32_t          default_ttl_sec;\n    cache_entry_t    *hash;          /* uthash handle */\n    lru_node_t        lru_head;      /* sentinel head/tail for O(1)  */\n    pthread_rwlock_t  rwlock;        /* read/write lock */\n    mmc_logger_t     *logger;\n\n    /* Metrics (atomics for lock-free reads) */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t puts;\n} mmc_cache_t;\n\n/* Exposed metrics container (for snapshot) */\nstruct mmc_cache_metrics {\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    uint64_t puts;\n    size_t   current_entries;\n};\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                 Internal Helper Functions                 *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_epoch_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n\n/* Initialize LRU doubly-linked list sentinel */\nstatic void\nlru_init(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    c->lru_head.prev = &c->lru_head;\n    c->lru_head.next = &c->lru_head;\n}\n\n/* Remove node from its current position */\nstatic inline void\nlru_detach(lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    node->prev->next = node->next;\n    node->next->prev = node->prev;\n}\n\n/* Insert node at front (MRU) */\nstatic inline void\nlru_insert_front(mmc_cache_t *c, lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    node->next = c->lru_head.next;\n    node->prev = &c->lru_head;\n    c->lru_head.next->prev = node;\n    c->lru_head.next      = node;\n}\n\n/* Move node to front (after hit) */\nstatic inline void\nlru_move_to_front(mmc_cache_t *c, lru_node_t *node)\n{\n    lru_detach(node);\n    lru_insert_front(c, node);\n}\n\n/* Evict LRU tail, return the removed entry (locked) */\nstatic cache_entry_t *\nevict_lru_tail(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head.prev == &c->lru_head) {\n        return NULL; /* empty */\n    }\n    lru_node_t *tail = c->lru_head.prev;\n    lru_detach(tail);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry = (cache_entry_t *)((char *)tail -\n                                    offsetof(cache_entry_t, lru));\n    HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    return entry;\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                     Public  API                           *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\nmmc_cache_t *\nmmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n                 uint32_t default_ttl_sec,\n                 mmc_logger_t *logger)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[mmc_cache] max_entries must be > 0\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) {\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    c->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec ? default_ttl_sec : 60;\n    c->logger           = logger;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL);\n    lru_init(c);\n\n    mmc_log_info(c->logger,\n                 \"response_cache.init\",\n                 \"Initialized cache with capacity=%zu ttl=%us\",\n                 c->max_entries, c->default_ttl_sec);\n    return c;\n}\n\nvoid\nmmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, cur);\n        free(cur->key);\n        free(cur->blob);\n        free(cur);\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/*\n * Retrieve value for `key`.\n *  - On hit: returns true, caller owns *out_buf (malloced).\n *  - On miss: returns false, *out_buf untouched.\n */\nbool\nmmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_t *c,\n              const char *key,\n              uint8_t **out_buf,\n              size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return false;\n\n    uint64_t now_ms = now_epoch_ms();\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock); /* upgradeable read would be nicer */\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (!entry || entry->expiry_epoch_ms < now_ms) {\n        if (entry) {\n            /* Expired entry\u2014remove it */\n            lru_detach(&entry->lru);\n            HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n            free(entry->key);\n            free(entry->blob);\n            free(entry);\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    /* Cache hit */\n    lru_move_to_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    /* Deep copy payload for caller */\n    uint8_t *copy = malloc(entry->blob_len);\n    if (!copy) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return false;\n    }\n    memcpy(copy, entry->blob, entry->blob_len);\n    *out_buf = copy;\n    *out_len = entry->blob_len;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return true;\n}\n\n/*\n * Store value for `key` with optional TTL override (seconds).\n * When capacity exceeded, evicts least-recently-used entry.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_t *c,\n              const char *key,\n              const uint8_t *buf,\n              size_t len,\n              uint32_t ttl_override_sec)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !buf || len == 0) return;\n\n    uint64_t now_ms  = now_epoch_ms();\n    uint64_t ttl_sec = ttl_override_sec ? ttl_override_sec : c->default_ttl_sec;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Replace existing entry in place */\n        free(entry->blob);\n        entry->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!entry->blob) goto unlock;\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, len);\n        entry->blob_len        = len;\n        entry->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms + ttl_sec * 1000ULL;\n\n        lru_move_to_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    } else {\n        /* Evict if over capacity */\n        if (HASH_COUNT(c->hash) >= c->max_entries) {\n            cache_entry_t *ev = evict_lru_tail(c);\n            if (ev) {\n                free(ev->key);\n                free(ev->blob);\n                free(ev);\n            }\n        }\n\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) goto unlock;\n\n        entry->key = strdup(key);\n        if (!entry->key) { free(entry); goto unlock; }\n\n        entry->blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!entry->blob) { free(entry->key); free(entry); goto unlock; }\n\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, len);\n        entry->blob_len        = len;\n        entry->expiry_epoch_ms = now_ms + ttl_sec * 1000ULL;\n\n        /* Insert into hash & LRU front */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->hash, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n        lru_insert_front(c, &entry->lru);\n    }\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->puts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\nunlock:\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* Explicitly remove key (e.g., after underlying data mutation) */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->hash, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        lru_detach(&entry->lru);\n        HASH_DEL(c->hash, entry);\n        free(entry->key);\n        free(entry->blob);\n        free(entry);\n\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* Collect metrics snapshot (no lock required for counters) */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_collect_metrics(const mmc_cache_t *c,\n                          struct mmc_cache_metrics *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n\n    out->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&c->misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&c->evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->puts      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->puts, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n    out->current_entries = HASH_COUNT(c->hash);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock((pthread_rwlock_t *)&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *            Periodic Cleanup & Prometheus Export           *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n\n/*\n * Lightweight cleanup thread that runs every N seconds to remove expired\n * entries. This avoids accumulations when keys are rarely accessed.\n */\nstruct cleanup_args {\n    mmc_cache_t *cache;\n    uint32_t     interval_sec;\n};\n\nstatic void *\ncleanup_thread_fn(void *arg)\n{\n    struct cleanup_args *ctx = arg;\n    mmc_cache_t *c = ctx->cache;\n    const uint32_t sleep_sec = ctx->interval_sec;\n\n    while (true) {\n        struct timespec ts = { .tv_sec = sleep_sec, .tv_nsec = 0 };\n        nanosleep(&ts, NULL);\n\n        uint64_t now_ms = now_epoch_ms();\n        size_t removed  = 0;\n\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n        cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, c->hash, cur, tmp) {\n            if (cur->expiry_epoch_ms < now_ms) {\n                lru_detach(&cur->lru);\n                HASH_DEL(c->hash, cur);\n                free(cur->key);\n                free(cur->blob);\n                free(cur);\n                atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1,\n                                          memory_order_relaxed);\n                ++removed;\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        if (removed > 0) {\n            mmc_log_debug(c->logger,\n                          \"response_cache.cleanup\",\n                          \"Removed %zu expired entries\", removed);\n        }\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\n/*\n * Spawn background cleanup thread. Should be invoked once at service start.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_start_cleanup_daemon(mmc_cache_t *c, uint32_t interval_sec)\n{\n    if (!c || interval_sec == 0) return;\n\n    static pthread_t tid;\n    static bool started = false;\n    if (started) return;\n\n    struct cleanup_args *args = malloc(sizeof(*args));\n    args->cache        = c;\n    args->interval_sec = interval_sec;\n\n    int rc = pthread_create(&tid, NULL, cleanup_thread_fn, args);\n    if (rc == 0) {\n        pthread_detach(tid);\n        started = true;\n        mmc_log_info(c->logger,\n                     \"response_cache.daemon\",\n                     \"Started cleanup thread with interval=%us\",\n                     interval_sec);\n    } else {\n        mmc_log_error(c->logger,\n                      \"response_cache.daemon\",\n                      \"Failed to spawn cleanup thread (rc=%d)\", rc);\n        free(args);\n    }\n}\n\n/*\n * Expose metrics in Prometheus-compatible text format.\n * Call from the HTTP /metrics endpoint handler.\n */\nvoid\nmmc_cache_prometheus_dump(const mmc_cache_t *c, FILE *out)\n{\n    struct mmc_cache_metrics m = {0};\n    mmc_cache_collect_metrics(c, &m);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_hits_total Total cache hits\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_hits_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_hits_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.hits);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_misses_total Total cache misses\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_misses_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_misses_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.misses);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_evictions_total Total cache evictions\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_evictions_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.evictions);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_puts_total Total cache insertions\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_puts_total counter\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_puts_total %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\", m.puts);\n\n    fprintf(out, \"# HELP mmc_cache_entries Current number of entries\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"# TYPE mmc_cache_entries gauge\\n\");\n    fprintf(out, \"mmc_cache_entries %zu\\n\", m.current_entries);\n}\n\n/*-----------------------------------------------------------*\n *                       End of File                         *\n *-----------------------------------------------------------*/\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                  mmc_logger_t *logger)",
        " {",
        "     if (max_entries == 0) {",
        "-        fprintf(stderr, \"[mmc_cache] max_entries must be > 0\\n\");",
        "+        LOG_ERROR(\"mmc_cache: max_entries must be > 0\");",
        "         return NULL;",
        "     }",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 166,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 166
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```c\n/**\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  ------------------------------------\n *  Module:   response_cache\n *  File:     src/module_40.c            (named *.txt to comply with prompt)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with LRU eviction and\n *      TTL (time-to-live) semantics.  Integrates basic metrics counters\n *      (hits/misses/evictions) to be scraped by the monitoring layer.\n *\n *  This cache is intended to sit in front of REST / GraphQL handlers to\n *  reduce latency and database load for idempotent, cache-friendly\n *  requests (e.g. catalog listings, public product pages, etc.).\n *\n *  Public API:\n *      mmch_cache_t *mmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                      uint32_t default_ttl_sec);\n *      void          mmch_cache_destroy(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_get(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          void        **out_buf,\n *                          size_t       *out_size);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_put(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          const void   *buf,\n *                          size_t        size,\n *                          uint32_t      ttl_sec);   // 0 == default\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_invalidate(mmch_cache_t *cache, const char *key);\n *      void mmch_cache_clear(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_snapshot_metrics(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                                       uint64_t *hits,\n *                                       uint64_t *misses,\n *                                       uint64_t *evictions);\n *\n *  Build dependencies:\n *      - pthread\n *      - uthash (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n *\n *  Compile example:\n *      cc -O2 -pthread -I./include -c src/module_40.c\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_40.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 453
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"    /* Public-domain single-header hash table. */\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                         Data Types                           *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/* A single cache entry. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_entry_s {\n    char                *key;        /* Hash key (heap allocated).                   */\n    void                *blob;       /* Cached payload (heap allocated).             */\n    size_t               blob_sz;    /* Size of payload in bytes.                    */\n    time_t               expiry_ts;  /* Epoch seconds after which entry is invalid.  */\n\n    /* Intrusive LRU doubly-linked list pointers. */\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *prev;\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *next;\n\n    UT_hash_handle       hh;         /* Makes this structure hashable by uthash.     */\n} mmch_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Cache handle exposed to callers. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_s {\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *table;            /* Hash table root (uthash).          */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_head;         /* Most recently used.                */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;         /* Least recently used.               */\n\n    size_t               entry_count;      /* Current number of items.           */\n    size_t               max_entries;      /* Hard cap on # of items.            */\n    uint32_t             default_ttl_sec;  /* TTL for entries with ttl==0.       */\n\n    /* Metrics counters (monotonic). */\n    uint64_t             hits;\n    uint64_t             misses;\n    uint64_t             evictions;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t     rwlock;           /* Readers-writer lock for concurrency.*/\n\n} mmch_cache_t;\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                   Internal Helper Prototypes                 *\n * ************************************************************ */\nstatic void   lru_promote(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   lru_remove(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   evict_if_needed(mmch_cache_t *cache);\nstatic void   free_entry(mmch_cache_entry_t *e);\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                     Public API \u2014 Create/Destory              *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_create\n *\n * Allocate and initialise a new cache handle.  Returns NULL on failure.\n */\nmmch_cache_t *\nmmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint32_t default_ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_sec == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"mmch_cache_create: invalid arguments\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmch_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(*cache));\n    if (!cache) {\n        perror(\"calloc\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    cache->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    cache->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec;",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_40.txt",
  "newString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"    /* Public-domain single-header hash table. */\n#include \"logging.h\"",
  "oldString": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"    /* Public-domain single-header hash table. */",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/**\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  ------------------------------------\n *  Module:   response_cache\n *  File:     src/module_40.c            (named *.txt to comply with prompt)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with LRU eviction and\n *      TTL (time-to-live) semantics.  Integrates basic metrics counters\n *      (hits/misses/evictions) to be scraped by the monitoring layer.\n *\n *  This cache is intended to sit in front of REST / GraphQL handlers to\n *  reduce latency and database load for idempotent, cache-friendly\n *  requests (e.g. catalog listings, public product pages, etc.).\n *\n *  Public API:\n *      mmch_cache_t *mmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                      uint32_t default_ttl_sec);\n *      void          mmch_cache_destroy(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_get(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          void        **out_buf,\n *                          size_t       *out_size);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_put(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          const void   *buf,\n *                          size_t        size,\n *                          uint32_t      ttl_sec);   // 0 == default\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_invalidate(mmch_cache_t *cache, const char *key);\n *      void mmch_cache_clear(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_snapshot_metrics(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                                       uint64_t *hits,\n *                                       uint64_t *misses,\n *                                       uint64_t *evictions);\n *\n *  Build dependencies:\n *      - pthread\n *      - uthash (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n *\n *  Compile example:\n *      cc -O2 -pthread -I./include -c src/module_40.c\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"    /* Public-domain single-header hash table. */\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                         Data Types                           *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/* A single cache entry. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_entry_s {\n    char                *key;        /* Hash key (heap allocated).                   */\n    void                *blob;       /* Cached payload (heap allocated).             */\n    size_t               blob_sz;    /* Size of payload in bytes.                    */\n    time_t               expiry_ts;  /* Epoch seconds after which entry is invalid.  */\n\n    /* Intrusive LRU doubly-linked list pointers. */\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *prev;\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *next;\n\n    UT_hash_handle       hh;         /* Makes this structure hashable by uthash.     */\n} mmch_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Cache handle exposed to callers. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_s {\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *table;            /* Hash table root (uthash).          */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_head;         /* Most recently used.                */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;         /* Least recently used.               */\n\n    size_t               entry_count;      /* Current number of items.           */\n    size_t               max_entries;      /* Hard cap on # of items.            */\n    uint32_t             default_ttl_sec;  /* TTL for entries with ttl==0.       */\n\n    /* Metrics counters (monotonic). */\n    uint64_t             hits;\n    uint64_t             misses;\n    uint64_t             evictions;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t     rwlock;           /* Readers-writer lock for concurrency.*/\n\n} mmch_cache_t;\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                   Internal Helper Prototypes                 *\n * ************************************************************ */\nstatic void   lru_promote(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   lru_remove(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   evict_if_needed(mmch_cache_t *cache);\nstatic void   free_entry(mmch_cache_entry_t *e);\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                     Public API \u2014 Create/Destory              *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_create\n *\n * Allocate and initialise a new cache handle.  Returns NULL on failure.\n */\nmmch_cache_t *\nmmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint32_t default_ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_sec == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"mmch_cache_create: invalid arguments\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmch_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(*cache));\n    if (!cache) {\n        perror(\"calloc\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    cache->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    cache->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&cache->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        perror(\"pthread_rwlock_init\");\n        free(cache);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    return cache;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_destroy\n *\n * Free all resources associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_destroy(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, entry, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&cache->rwlock);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                     Public API \u2014 Query                       *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_get\n *\n * Attempt to retrieve a cached blob by key.\n * Returns true if found and not expired.  Caller is responsible\n * for freeing *out_buf (allocated with malloc).\n */\nbool\nmmch_cache_get(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n               const char   *key,\n               void        **out_buf,\n               size_t       *out_size)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_buf || !out_size)\n        return false;\n\n    bool found = false;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock); /* Need write lock to promote in LRU. */\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        time_t now = time(NULL);\n        if (entry->expiry_ts < now) {\n            /* Expired \u2014 delete. */\n            lru_remove(cache, entry);\n            HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n            free_entry(entry);\n            cache->misses++;\n        } else {\n            /* Hit.  */\n            *out_buf  = malloc(entry->blob_sz);\n            if (*out_buf) {\n                memcpy(*out_buf, entry->blob, entry->blob_sz);\n                *out_size = entry->blob_sz;\n                found     = true;\n                cache->hits++;\n                lru_promote(cache, entry);\n            } else {\n                perror(\"malloc\");\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        cache->misses++;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return found;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_put\n *\n * Insert or update a cache entry.\n * Returns true on success.\n */\nbool\nmmch_cache_put(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n               const char   *key,\n               const void   *buf,\n               size_t        size,\n               uint32_t      ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !buf || size == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool ok = false;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    time_t expiry = time(NULL) + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : cache->default_ttl_sec);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Update existing entry. */\n        void *new_blob = realloc(entry->blob, size);\n        if (!new_blob) goto done;\n\n        memcpy(new_blob, buf, size);\n        entry->blob      = new_blob;\n        entry->blob_sz   = size;\n        entry->expiry_ts = expiry;\n\n        lru_promote(cache, entry);\n        ok = true;\n    } else {\n        /* Insert new entry. */\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) goto done;\n\n        entry->key = strdup(key);\n        if (!entry->key) { free(entry); goto done; }\n\n        entry->blob = malloc(size);\n        if (!entry->blob) { free(entry->key); free(entry); goto done; }\n\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, size);\n        entry->blob_sz   = size;\n        entry->expiry_ts = expiry;\n\n        /* Add to hash table and LRU list (front). */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->table, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n        lru_promote(cache, entry);\n\n        cache->entry_count++;\n        ok = true;\n\n        /* Evict if we're over capacity. */\n        evict_if_needed(cache);\n    }\n\ndone:\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_invalidate\n *\n * Remove a single entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_invalidate(mmch_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        lru_remove(cache, entry);\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n        cache->entry_count--;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_clear\n *\n * Remove all entries from the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_clear(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, entry, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n    }\n    cache->lru_head    = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->entry_count = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_snapshot_metrics\n *\n * Atomically snapshot the metrics counters.  May be invoked by\n * the Prometheus scrapper thread in the monitoring subsystem.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_snapshot_metrics(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n                            uint64_t *hits,\n                            uint64_t *misses,\n                            uint64_t *evictions)\n{\n    if (!cache || !hits || !misses || !evictions) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    *hits      = cache->hits;\n    *misses    = cache->misses;\n    *evictions = cache->evictions;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *               Internal Helpers \u2014 LRU management              *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/* Promote 'node' to LRU head (most recently used). */\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node)\n{\n    if (cache->lru_head == node) return; /* Already at front. */\n\n    lru_remove(cache, node);\n\n    node->prev = NULL;\n    node->next = cache->lru_head;\n\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = node;\n    cache->lru_head = node;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = node;\n}\n\n/* Remove 'node' from LRU list but do not free it. */\nstatic void\nlru_remove(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node)\n{\n    if (!node) return;\n\n    if (node->prev)\n        node->prev->next = node->next;\n    if (node->next)\n        node->next->prev = node->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_head == node)\n        cache->lru_head = node->next;\n    if (cache->lru_tail == node)\n        cache->lru_tail = node->prev;\n\n    node->prev = node->next = NULL;\n}\n\n/* Evict least recently used entries until within max_entries. */\nstatic void\nevict_if_needed(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    while (cache->entry_count > cache->max_entries && cache->lru_tail) {\n        mmch_cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n        lru_remove(cache, victim);\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, victim);\n        free_entry(victim);\n        cache->entry_count--;\n        cache->evictions++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Free an entry's resources. */\nstatic void\nfree_entry(mmch_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                    Unit Test (optional)                      *\n * ************************************************************ *\n *  To quickly validate the cache in isolation:\n *      gcc -DMMCH_CACHE_TEST -pthread src/module_40.c\n *      ./a.out\n */\n#ifdef MMCH_CACHE_TEST\n#include <assert.h>\nint\nmain(void)\n{\n    mmch_cache_t *cache = mmch_cache_create(4, 2); /* 4 entries max, 2s TTL */\n    assert(cache);\n\n    const char *payload = \"hello world\";\n    assert(mmch_cache_put(cache, \"greeting\", payload, strlen(payload) + 1, 0));\n\n    void *out = NULL;\n    size_t sz = 0;\n    assert(mmch_cache_get(cache, \"greeting\", &out, &sz));\n    assert(sz == strlen(payload) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp(out, payload) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    /* wait 3 sec to expire */\n    printf(\"Sleeping to test expiration...\\n\");\n    sleep(3);\n    assert(!mmch_cache_get(cache, \"greeting\", &out, &sz)); /* expired */\n\n    mmch_cache_destroy(cache);\n    puts(\"All tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n```",
  "replaceAll": false,

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_40.txt",
  "newString": "    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_sec == 0) {\n        LOG_ERROR(\"mmch_cache_create: invalid arguments\");\n        return NULL;\n    }",
  "oldString": "    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_sec == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"mmch_cache_create: invalid arguments\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/**\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  ------------------------------------\n *  Module:   response_cache\n *  File:     src/module_40.c            (named *.txt to comply with prompt)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe, in-memory response cache with LRU eviction and\n *      TTL (time-to-live) semantics.  Integrates basic metrics counters\n *      (hits/misses/evictions) to be scraped by the monitoring layer.\n *\n *  This cache is intended to sit in front of REST / GraphQL handlers to\n *  reduce latency and database load for idempotent, cache-friendly\n *  requests (e.g. catalog listings, public product pages, etc.).\n *\n *  Public API:\n *      mmch_cache_t *mmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries,\n *                                      uint32_t default_ttl_sec);\n *      void          mmch_cache_destroy(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_get(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          void        **out_buf,\n *                          size_t       *out_size);\n *\n *      bool mmch_cache_put(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                          const char   *key,\n *                          const void   *buf,\n *                          size_t        size,\n *                          uint32_t      ttl_sec);   // 0 == default\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_invalidate(mmch_cache_t *cache, const char *key);\n *      void mmch_cache_clear(mmch_cache_t *cache);\n *\n *      void mmch_cache_snapshot_metrics(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n *                                       uint64_t *hits,\n *                                       uint64_t *misses,\n *                                       uint64_t *evictions);\n *\n *  Build dependencies:\n *      - pthread\n *      - uthash (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n *\n *  Compile example:\n *      cc -O2 -pthread -I./include -c src/module_40.c\n */\n\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"    /* Public-domain single-header hash table. */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                         Data Types                           *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/* A single cache entry. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_entry_s {\n    char                *key;        /* Hash key (heap allocated).                   */\n    void                *blob;       /* Cached payload (heap allocated).             */\n    size_t               blob_sz;    /* Size of payload in bytes.                    */\n    time_t               expiry_ts;  /* Epoch seconds after which entry is invalid.  */\n\n    /* Intrusive LRU doubly-linked list pointers. */\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *prev;\n    struct mmch_cache_entry_s *next;\n\n    UT_hash_handle       hh;         /* Makes this structure hashable by uthash.     */\n} mmch_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Cache handle exposed to callers. */\ntypedef struct mmch_cache_s {\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *table;            /* Hash table root (uthash).          */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_head;         /* Most recently used.                */\n    mmch_cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;         /* Least recently used.               */\n\n    size_t               entry_count;      /* Current number of items.           */\n    size_t               max_entries;      /* Hard cap on # of items.            */\n    uint32_t             default_ttl_sec;  /* TTL for entries with ttl==0.       */\n\n    /* Metrics counters (monotonic). */\n    uint64_t             hits;\n    uint64_t             misses;\n    uint64_t             evictions;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t     rwlock;           /* Readers-writer lock for concurrency.*/\n\n} mmch_cache_t;\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                   Internal Helper Prototypes                 *\n * ************************************************************ */\nstatic void   lru_promote(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   lru_remove(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node);\nstatic void   evict_if_needed(mmch_cache_t *cache);\nstatic void   free_entry(mmch_cache_entry_t *e);\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                     Public API \u2014 Create/Destory              *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_create\n *\n * Allocate and initialise a new cache handle.  Returns NULL on failure.\n */\nmmch_cache_t *\nmmch_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint32_t default_ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_sec == 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"mmch_cache_create: invalid arguments\\n\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    mmch_cache_t *cache = calloc(1, sizeof(*cache));\n    if (!cache) {\n        perror(\"calloc\");\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    cache->max_entries      = max_entries;\n    cache->default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec;\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&cache->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        perror(\"pthread_rwlock_init\");\n        free(cache);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    return cache;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_destroy\n *\n * Free all resources associated with the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_destroy(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, entry, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&cache->rwlock);\n    free(cache);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                     Public API \u2014 Query                       *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_get\n *\n * Attempt to retrieve a cached blob by key.\n * Returns true if found and not expired.  Caller is responsible\n * for freeing *out_buf (allocated with malloc).\n */\nbool\nmmch_cache_get(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n               const char   *key,\n               void        **out_buf,\n               size_t       *out_size)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_buf || !out_size)\n        return false;\n\n    bool found = false;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock); /* Need write lock to promote in LRU. */\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        time_t now = time(NULL);\n        if (entry->expiry_ts < now) {\n            /* Expired \u2014 delete. */\n            lru_remove(cache, entry);\n            HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n            free_entry(entry);\n            cache->misses++;\n        } else {\n            /* Hit.  */\n            *out_buf  = malloc(entry->blob_sz);\n            if (*out_buf) {\n                memcpy(*out_buf, entry->blob, entry->blob_sz);\n                *out_size = entry->blob_sz;\n                found     = true;\n                cache->hits++;\n                lru_promote(cache, entry);\n            } else {\n                perror(\"malloc\");\n            }\n        }\n    } else {\n        cache->misses++;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return found;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_put\n *\n * Insert or update a cache entry.\n * Returns true on success.\n */\nbool\nmmch_cache_put(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n               const char   *key,\n               const void   *buf,\n               size_t        size,\n               uint32_t      ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !buf || size == 0)\n        return false;\n\n    bool ok = false;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n\n    time_t expiry = time(NULL) + (ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : cache->default_ttl_sec);\n\n    if (entry) {\n        /* Update existing entry. */\n        void *new_blob = realloc(entry->blob, size);\n        if (!new_blob) goto done;\n\n        memcpy(new_blob, buf, size);\n        entry->blob      = new_blob;\n        entry->blob_sz   = size;\n        entry->expiry_ts = expiry;\n\n        lru_promote(cache, entry);\n        ok = true;\n    } else {\n        /* Insert new entry. */\n        entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*entry));\n        if (!entry) goto done;\n\n        entry->key = strdup(key);\n        if (!entry->key) { free(entry); goto done; }\n\n        entry->blob = malloc(size);\n        if (!entry->blob) { free(entry->key); free(entry); goto done; }\n\n        memcpy(entry->blob, buf, size);\n        entry->blob_sz   = size;\n        entry->expiry_ts = expiry;\n\n        /* Add to hash table and LRU list (front). */\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->table, entry->key, strlen(entry->key), entry);\n        lru_promote(cache, entry);\n\n        cache->entry_count++;\n        ok = true;\n\n        /* Evict if we're over capacity. */\n        evict_if_needed(cache);\n    }\n\ndone:\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    return ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_invalidate\n *\n * Remove a single entry from the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_invalidate(mmch_cache_t *cache, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->table, key, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        lru_remove(cache, entry);\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n        cache->entry_count--;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_clear\n *\n * Remove all entries from the cache.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_clear(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&cache->rwlock);\n\n    mmch_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->table, entry, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, entry);\n        free_entry(entry);\n    }\n    cache->lru_head    = cache->lru_tail = NULL;\n    cache->entry_count = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/**\n * mmch_cache_snapshot_metrics\n *\n * Atomically snapshot the metrics counters.  May be invoked by\n * the Prometheus scrapper thread in the monitoring subsystem.\n */\nvoid\nmmch_cache_snapshot_metrics(mmch_cache_t *cache,\n                            uint64_t *hits,\n                            uint64_t *misses,\n                            uint64_t *evictions)\n{\n    if (!cache || !hits || !misses || !evictions) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&cache->rwlock);\n    *hits      = cache->hits;\n    *misses    = cache->misses;\n    *evictions = cache->evictions;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&cache->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *               Internal Helpers \u2014 LRU management              *\n * ************************************************************ */\n\n/* Promote 'node' to LRU head (most recently used). */\nstatic void\nlru_promote(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node)\n{\n    if (cache->lru_head == node) return; /* Already at front. */\n\n    lru_remove(cache, node);\n\n    node->prev = NULL;\n    node->next = cache->lru_head;\n\n    if (cache->lru_head)\n        cache->lru_head->prev = node;\n    cache->lru_head = node;\n\n    if (!cache->lru_tail)\n        cache->lru_tail = node;\n}\n\n/* Remove 'node' from LRU list but do not free it. */\nstatic void\nlru_remove(mmch_cache_t *cache, mmch_cache_entry_t *node)\n{\n    if (!node) return;\n\n    if (node->prev)\n        node->prev->next = node->next;\n    if (node->next)\n        node->next->prev = node->prev;\n\n    if (cache->lru_head == node)\n        cache->lru_head = node->next;\n    if (cache->lru_tail == node)\n        cache->lru_tail = node->prev;\n\n    node->prev = node->next = NULL;\n}\n\n/* Evict least recently used entries until within max_entries. */\nstatic void\nevict_if_needed(mmch_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    while (cache->entry_count > cache->max_entries && cache->lru_tail) {\n        mmch_cache_entry_t *victim = cache->lru_tail;\n        lru_remove(cache, victim);\n        HASH_DEL(cache->table, victim);\n        free_entry(victim);\n        cache->entry_count--;\n        cache->evictions++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Free an entry's resources. */\nstatic void\nfree_entry(mmch_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* ************************************************************ *\n *                    Unit Test (optional)                      *\n * ************************************************************ *\n *  To quickly validate the cache in isolation:\n *      gcc -DMMCH_CACHE_TEST -pthread src/module_40.c\n *      ./a.out\n */\n#ifdef MMCH_CACHE_TEST\n#include <assert.h>\nint\nmain(void)\n{\n    mmch_cache_t *cache = mmch_cache_create(4, 2); /* 4 entries max, 2s TTL */\n    assert(cache);\n\n    const char *payload = \"hello world\";\n    assert(mmch_cache_put(cache, \"greeting\", payload, strlen(payload) + 1, 0));\n\n    void *out = NULL;\n    size_t sz = 0;\n    assert(mmch_cache_get(cache, \"greeting\", &out, &sz));\n    assert(sz == strlen(payload) + 1);\n    assert(strcmp(out, payload) == 0);\n    free(out);\n\n    /* wait 3 sec to expire */\n    printf(\"Sleeping to test expiration...\\n\");\n    sleep(3);\n    assert(!mmch_cache_get(cache, \"greeting\", &out, &sz)); /* expired */\n\n    mmch_cache_destroy(cache);\n    puts(\"All tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n```",
  "replaceAll": false,

{

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "#ifdef RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST\n\n/* Simple self-test when compiled standalone */\nint main(void)\n{\n    if (mercury_cache_init(4, 2) != 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    const char *key = \"/v1/products?sku=123\";\n    const char *resp = \"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"widget\\\",\\\"price\\\":9.99}\";\n    mercury_cache_put(key, (const uint8_t *)resp, strlen(resp)+1, 200, 1);\n\n    uint8_t *payload;\n    size_t len;\n    uint16_t status;\n    bool hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 1: hit=%d, payload=%s\\n\", hit, payload);\n\n    sleep(2); /* Wait for expiry */\n\n    hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 2: hit=%d\\n\", hit);\n\n    mercury_cache_shutdown();\n    return 0;\n}\n\n#endif /* RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_19.txt",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 460,
    "totalLines": 490
  },
  "type": "text"
}
98. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_19.txt",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/**\n * src/module_19.txt\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache (LRU w/ TTL)\n *\n * This module implements an in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache that is\n * used by the API-Gateway layer to lower latency for idempotent, frequently\n * requested resources.  The implementation uses an open-addressed hash table\n * combined with a doubly-linked list to maintain an LRU eviction policy and\n * supports per-entry TTLs (time-to-live).\n *\n * Thread-safety is achieved through a single read/write lock that allows\n * multiple concurrent readers while serialising mutating operations.  For\n * typical read-heavy workloads this provides adequate scalability while\n * keeping the implementation simple.\n *\n * Metrics (Prometheus-compatible) and structured logging are integrated\n * via the internal metrics.h and logger.h facilities.\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logger.h\"   /* mercury_log(), LOG_LEVEL_*, etc.              */\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              CONFIGURATION                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS 8192U\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  60U\n#endif\n\n/* Internal Prometheus metric names */\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_HITS      = \"mercury_response_cache_hits_total\";",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_19.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 490
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "old_string": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logger.h\"   /* mercury_log(), LOG_LEVEL_*, etc.              */\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */",
