{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_19.txt",
  "newString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */",
  "oldString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logger.h\"   /* mercury_log(), LOG_LEVEL_*, etc.              */\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */",
  "originalFile": "/**\n * src/module_19.txt\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache (LRU w/ TTL)\n *\n * This module implements an in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache that is\n * used by the API-Gateway layer to lower latency for idempotent, frequently\n * requested resources.  The implementation uses an open-addressed hash table\n * combined with a doubly-linked list to maintain an LRU eviction policy and\n * supports per-entry TTLs (time-to-live).\n *\n * Thread-safety is achieved through a single read/write lock that allows\n * multiple concurrent readers while serialising mutating operations.  For\n * typical read-heavy workloads this provides adequate scalability while\n * keeping the implementation simple.\n *\n * Metrics (Prometheus-compatible) and structured logging are integrated\n * via the internal metrics.h and logger.h facilities.\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logger.h\"   /* mercury_log(), LOG_LEVEL_*, etc.              */\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              CONFIGURATION                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS 8192U\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  60U\n#endif\n\n/* Internal Prometheus metric names */\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_HITS      = \"mercury_response_cache_hits_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_MISSES    = \"mercury_response_cache_misses_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS = \"mercury_response_cache_evictions_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_SIZE      = \"mercury_response_cache_items\";\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                                 TYPES                                      */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry_t {\n    char           *key;          /* Normalised cache key                  */\n    uint8_t        *payload;      /* Response bytes (immutable)            */\n    size_t          payload_len;\n    uint16_t        status_code;  /* HTTP status / GraphQL envelope code   */\n    uint64_t        expires_at;   /* Epoch-millis when entry becomes stale */\n    uint64_t        hash;         /* Cached hash of key                    */\n\n    /* LRU doubly linked list pointers */\n    struct cache_entry_t *prev;\n    struct cache_entry_t *next;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Hash table buckets \u2013 resolved via linear probing */\n    cache_entry_t **buckets;\n    size_t          bucket_count; /* Must be power of two                 */\n    size_t          size;         /* Number of live entries               */\n    size_t          max_items;    /* Soft limit for eviction              */\n\n    /* LRU list head/tail (most/least recently used) */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;\n\n    /* Synchronisation primitive (RW lock) */\n    pthread_rwlock_t lock;\n\n    /* Default TTL fallback */\n    uint32_t        default_ttl_sec;\n\n    /* Stats */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n} response_cache_t;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           STATIC DECLARATIONS                              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t fnv1a_hash(const char *s);\nstatic cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key, const uint8_t *payload,\n                                   size_t len, uint16_t status, uint32_t ttl_sec,\n                                   uint64_t now_ms);\nstatic void entry_destroy(cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void lru_remove(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void cache_evict_if_needed(response_cache_t *c);\nstatic size_t next_pow2(size_t n);\nstatic uint64_t now_epoch_millis(void);\nstatic void metrics_update_size(response_cache_t *c);\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              SINGLETON CACHE                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic response_cache_t g_cache;\nstatic bool g_cache_initialised = false;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         PUBLIC API IMPLEMENTATION                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint mercury_cache_init(size_t max_items, uint32_t default_ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (g_cache_initialised) {\n        return 0; /* idempotent */\n    }\n\n    size_t buckets = next_pow2(max_items * 2); /* load factor 0.5 */\n    if (buckets < 16) {\n        buckets = 16;\n    }\n\n    memset(&g_cache, 0, sizeof(g_cache));\n    g_cache.bucket_count     = buckets;\n    g_cache.buckets          = calloc(buckets, sizeof(cache_entry_t *));\n    g_cache.max_items        = max_items ? max_items : MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS;\n    g_cache.default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec ? default_ttl_sec : MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC;\n\n    if (!g_cache.buckets) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, \"Response cache initialisation failed: %s\", strerror(errno));\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&g_cache.lock, NULL) != 0) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, \"pthread_rwlock_init failed: %s\", strerror(errno));\n        free(g_cache.buckets);\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    /* Register metrics */\n    mercury_gauge_set(METRIC_CACHE_SIZE, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_HITS, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_MISSES, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS, 0);\n\n    g_cache_initialised = true;\n    mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, \"Response cache initialised (capacity=%zu, buckets=%zu, TTL=%us)\",\n                g_cache.max_items, g_cache.bucket_count, g_cache.default_ttl_sec);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mercury_cache_shutdown(void)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache.bucket_count; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[i];\n        if (e) {\n            entry_destroy(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(g_cache.buckets);\n    g_cache.buckets = NULL;\n    g_cache.bucket_count = g_cache.size = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    g_cache_initialised = false;\n    mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, \"Response cache shutdown complete\");\n}\n\nbool mercury_cache_get(const char *key,\n                       uint8_t **out_payload, size_t *out_len,\n                       uint16_t *out_status_code)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised || !key) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    bool hit = false;\n    uint64_t hash = fnv1a_hash(key);\n    uint64_t now  = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    size_t idx = hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[idx];\n        if (!e) break; /* Not found */\n\n        if (e->hash == hash && strcmp(e->key, key) == 0) {\n            /* Found matching key; check expiry */\n            if (e->expires_at > now) {\n                *out_payload     = e->payload;\n                *out_len         = e->payload_len;\n                *out_status_code = e->status_code;\n                hit = true;\n            }\n            break;\n        }\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    if (hit) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_HITS, 1);\n        /* Need to update LRU ordering \u2013 do this in separate write lock */\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        size_t idx2 = hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n        for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n            cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[idx2];\n            if (!e) break;\n            if (e->hash == hash && strcmp(e->key, key) == 0 && e->expires_at > now) {\n                lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, e);\n                break;\n            }\n            idx2 = (idx2 + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n        }\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return true;\n    } else {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_MISSES, 1);\n        return false;\n    }\n}\n\nint mercury_cache_put(const char *key,\n                      const uint8_t *payload, size_t len,\n                      uint16_t status_code,\n                      uint32_t ttl_sec /* 0 for default */)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised || !key || !payload || len == 0) {\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    uint32_t ttl = ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : g_cache.default_ttl_sec;\n    uint64_t now = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    cache_entry_t *new_entry = entry_create(key, payload, len, status_code, ttl, now);\n    if (!new_entry) {\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    /* Insert or replace */\n    size_t idx = new_entry->hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n        cache_entry_t *existing = g_cache.buckets[idx];\n        if (!existing) {\n            /* Empty slot */\n            g_cache.buckets[idx] = new_entry;\n            ++g_cache.size;\n            lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, new_entry);\n            break;\n        } else if (existing->hash == new_entry->hash &&\n                   strcmp(existing->key, new_entry->key) == 0) {\n            /* Replace existing entry */\n            lru_remove(&g_cache, existing);\n            entry_destroy(existing);\n            g_cache.buckets[idx] = new_entry;\n            lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, new_entry);\n            break;\n        }\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    }\n\n    cache_evict_if_needed(&g_cache);\n    metrics_update_size(&g_cache);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mercury_cache_invalidate(const char *key_prefix)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    size_t prefix_len = key_prefix ? strlen(key_prefix) : 0;\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache.bucket_count; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[i];\n        if (!e) continue;\n\n        if (!key_prefix || strncmp(e->key, key_prefix, prefix_len) == 0) {\n            lru_remove(&g_cache, e);\n            entry_destroy(e);\n            g_cache.buckets[i] = NULL;\n            --g_cache.size;\n            mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", e->key);\n        }\n    }\n\n    metrics_update_size(&g_cache);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         INTERNAL HELPER FUNCTIONS                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t fnv1a_hash(const char *s)\n{\n    uint64_t hash = 14695981039346656037ULL;\n    while (*s) {\n        hash ^= (unsigned char)(*s++);\n        hash *= 1099511628211ULL;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key, const uint8_t *payload,\n                                   size_t len, uint16_t status, uint32_t ttl_sec,\n                                   uint64_t now_ms)\n{\n    cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) return NULL;\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) {\n        free(e);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    e->payload = malloc(len);\n    if (!e->payload) {\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    memcpy(e->payload, payload, len);\n\n    e->payload_len = len;\n    e->status_code = status;\n    e->expires_at  = now_ms + ((uint64_t)ttl_sec * 1000ULL);\n    e->hash        = fnv1a_hash(key);\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic void entry_destroy(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e);\n}\n\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) {\n        return; /* Already at front */\n    }\n\n    /* Remove from list if in list */\n    if (e->prev || e->next || c->lru_tail == e) {\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n    }\n\n    /* Insert at head */\n    e->next     = c->lru_head;\n    e->prev     = NULL;\n    if (c->lru_head) {\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    }\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) {\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void lru_remove(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n\n    if (e->prev) {\n        e->prev->next = e->next;\n    } else {\n        c->lru_head = e->next;\n    }\n    if (e->next) {\n        e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    } else {\n        c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n    }\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void cache_evict_if_needed(response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    uint64_t now = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    /* First reap expired entries */\n    cache_entry_t *iter = c->lru_tail;\n    while (iter) {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = iter->prev;\n        if (iter->expires_at <= now ||\n            c->size > c->max_items)  /* force eviction if over capacity */\n        {\n            /* Remove from hash table */\n            size_t idx = iter->hash & (c->bucket_count - 1);\n            for (size_t probe = 0; probe < c->bucket_count; ++probe) {\n                if (c->buckets[idx] == iter) {\n                    c->buckets[idx] = NULL;\n                    break;\n                }\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (c->bucket_count - 1);\n            }\n\n            lru_remove(c, iter);\n            entry_destroy(iter);\n            --c->size;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n            mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS, 1);\n        }\n        iter = prev;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic size_t next_pow2(size_t n)\n{\n    size_t v = 1;\n    while (v < n) v <<= 1;\n    return v;\n}\n\nstatic uint64_t now_epoch_millis(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n\nstatic void metrics_update_size(response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    mercury_gauge_set(METRIC_CACHE_SIZE, (double)c->size);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                                TEST HARNESS                                */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST\n\n/* Simple self-test when compiled standalone */\nint main(void)\n{\n    if (mercury_cache_init(4, 2) != 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    const char *key = \"/v1/products?sku=123\";\n    const char *resp = \"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"widget\\\",\\\"price\\\":9.99}\";\n    mercury_cache_put(key, (const uint8_t *)resp, strlen(resp)+1, 200, 1);\n\n    uint8_t *payload;\n    size_t len;\n    uint16_t status;\n    bool hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 1: hit=%d, payload=%s\\n\", hit, payload);\n\n    sleep(2); /* Wait for expiry */\n\n    hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 2: hit=%d\\n\", hit);\n\n    mercury_cache_shutdown();\n    return 0;\n}\n\n#endif /* RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST */\n",

  "originalFile": "/**\n * src/module_19.txt\n *\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub \u2013 Response Cache (LRU w/ TTL)\n *\n * This module implements an in-memory HTTP/GraphQL response cache that is\n * used by the API-Gateway layer to lower latency for idempotent, frequently\n * requested resources.  The implementation uses an open-addressed hash table\n * combined with a doubly-linked list to maintain an LRU eviction policy and\n * supports per-entry TTLs (time-to-live).\n *\n * Thread-safety is achieved through a single read/write lock that allows\n * multiple concurrent readers while serialising mutating operations.  For\n * typical read-heavy workloads this provides adequate scalability while\n * keeping the implementation simple.\n *\n * Metrics (Prometheus-compatible) and structured logging are integrated\n * via the internal metrics.h and logger.h facilities.\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"metrics.h\"  /* mercury_counter_inc(), mercury_gauge_set()    */\n#include \"response_cache.h\" /* Public interface for this module        */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              CONFIGURATION                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS 8192U\n#endif\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC\n#    define MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC  60U\n#endif\n\n/* Internal Prometheus metric names */\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_HITS      = \"mercury_response_cache_hits_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_MISSES    = \"mercury_response_cache_misses_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS = \"mercury_response_cache_evictions_total\";\nstatic const char *METRIC_CACHE_SIZE      = \"mercury_response_cache_items\";\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                                 TYPES                                      */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry_t {\n    char           *key;          /* Normalised cache key                  */\n    uint8_t        *payload;      /* Response bytes (immutable)            */\n    size_t          payload_len;\n    uint16_t        status_code;  /* HTTP status / GraphQL envelope code   */\n    uint64_t        expires_at;   /* Epoch-millis when entry becomes stale */\n    uint64_t        hash;         /* Cached hash of key                    */\n\n    /* LRU doubly linked list pointers */\n    struct cache_entry_t *prev;\n    struct cache_entry_t *next;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    /* Hash table buckets \u2013 resolved via linear probing */\n    cache_entry_t **buckets;\n    size_t          bucket_count; /* Must be power of two                 */\n    size_t          size;         /* Number of live entries               */\n    size_t          max_items;    /* Soft limit for eviction              */\n\n    /* LRU list head/tail (most/least recently used) */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;\n\n    /* Synchronisation primitive (RW lock) */\n    pthread_rwlock_t lock;\n\n    /* Default TTL fallback */\n    uint32_t        default_ttl_sec;\n\n    /* Stats */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n} response_cache_t;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                           STATIC DECLARATIONS                              */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t fnv1a_hash(const char *s);\nstatic cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key, const uint8_t *payload,\n                                   size_t len, uint16_t status, uint32_t ttl_sec,\n                                   uint64_t now_ms);\nstatic void entry_destroy(cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void lru_remove(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void cache_evict_if_needed(response_cache_t *c);\nstatic size_t next_pow2(size_t n);\nstatic uint64_t now_epoch_millis(void);\nstatic void metrics_update_size(response_cache_t *c);\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                              SINGLETON CACHE                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic response_cache_t g_cache;\nstatic bool g_cache_initialised = false;\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         PUBLIC API IMPLEMENTATION                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint mercury_cache_init(size_t max_items, uint32_t default_ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (g_cache_initialised) {\n        return 0; /* idempotent */\n    }\n\n    size_t buckets = next_pow2(max_items * 2); /* load factor 0.5 */\n    if (buckets < 16) {\n        buckets = 16;\n    }\n\n    memset(&g_cache, 0, sizeof(g_cache));\n    g_cache.bucket_count     = buckets;\n    g_cache.buckets          = calloc(buckets, sizeof(cache_entry_t *));\n    g_cache.max_items        = max_items ? max_items : MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_MAX_ITEMS;\n    g_cache.default_ttl_sec  = default_ttl_sec ? default_ttl_sec : MERCURY_CACHE_DEFAULT_TTL_SEC;\n\n    if (!g_cache.buckets) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, \"Response cache initialisation failed: %s\", strerror(errno));\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&g_cache.lock, NULL) != 0) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, \"pthread_rwlock_init failed: %s\", strerror(errno));\n        free(g_cache.buckets);\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    /* Register metrics */\n    mercury_gauge_set(METRIC_CACHE_SIZE, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_HITS, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_MISSES, 0);\n    mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS, 0);\n\n    g_cache_initialised = true;\n    mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, \"Response cache initialised (capacity=%zu, buckets=%zu, TTL=%us)\",\n                g_cache.max_items, g_cache.bucket_count, g_cache.default_ttl_sec);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mercury_cache_shutdown(void)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache.bucket_count; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[i];\n        if (e) {\n            entry_destroy(e);\n        }\n    }\n    free(g_cache.buckets);\n    g_cache.buckets = NULL;\n    g_cache.bucket_count = g_cache.size = 0;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    g_cache_initialised = false;\n    mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_INFO, \"Response cache shutdown complete\");\n}\n\nbool mercury_cache_get(const char *key,\n                       uint8_t **out_payload, size_t *out_len,\n                       uint16_t *out_status_code)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised || !key) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    bool hit = false;\n    uint64_t hash = fnv1a_hash(key);\n    uint64_t now  = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    size_t idx = hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[idx];\n        if (!e) break; /* Not found */\n\n        if (e->hash == hash && strcmp(e->key, key) == 0) {\n            /* Found matching key; check expiry */\n            if (e->expires_at > now) {\n                *out_payload     = e->payload;\n                *out_len         = e->payload_len;\n                *out_status_code = e->status_code;\n                hit = true;\n            }\n            break;\n        }\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    if (hit) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_HITS, 1);\n        /* Need to update LRU ordering \u2013 do this in separate write lock */\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        size_t idx2 = hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n        for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n            cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[idx2];\n            if (!e) break;\n            if (e->hash == hash && strcmp(e->key, key) == 0 && e->expires_at > now) {\n                lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, e);\n                break;\n            }\n            idx2 = (idx2 + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n        }\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return true;\n    } else {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_MISSES, 1);\n        return false;\n    }\n}\n\nint mercury_cache_put(const char *key,\n                      const uint8_t *payload, size_t len,\n                      uint16_t status_code,\n                      uint32_t ttl_sec /* 0 for default */)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised || !key || !payload || len == 0) {\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    uint32_t ttl = ttl_sec ? ttl_sec : g_cache.default_ttl_sec;\n    uint64_t now = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    cache_entry_t *new_entry = entry_create(key, payload, len, status_code, ttl, now);\n    if (!new_entry) {\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    /* Insert or replace */\n    size_t idx = new_entry->hash & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    for (size_t probe = 0; probe < g_cache.bucket_count; ++probe) {\n        cache_entry_t *existing = g_cache.buckets[idx];\n        if (!existing) {\n            /* Empty slot */\n            g_cache.buckets[idx] = new_entry;\n            ++g_cache.size;\n            lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, new_entry);\n            break;\n        } else if (existing->hash == new_entry->hash &&\n                   strcmp(existing->key, new_entry->key) == 0) {\n            /* Replace existing entry */\n            lru_remove(&g_cache, existing);\n            entry_destroy(existing);\n            g_cache.buckets[idx] = new_entry;\n            lru_move_to_front(&g_cache, new_entry);\n            break;\n        }\n        idx = (idx + 1) & (g_cache.bucket_count - 1);\n    }\n\n    cache_evict_if_needed(&g_cache);\n    metrics_update_size(&g_cache);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mercury_cache_invalidate(const char *key_prefix)\n{\n    if (!g_cache_initialised) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    size_t prefix_len = key_prefix ? strlen(key_prefix) : 0;\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < g_cache.bucket_count; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[i];\n        if (!e) continue;\n\n        if (!key_prefix || strncmp(e->key, key_prefix, prefix_len) == 0) {\n            lru_remove(&g_cache, e);\n            entry_destroy(e);\n            g_cache.buckets[i] = NULL;\n            --g_cache.size;\n            mercury_log(LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG, \"Cache invalidated: %s\", e->key);\n        }\n    }\n\n    metrics_update_size(&g_cache);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                         INTERNAL HELPER FUNCTIONS                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t fnv1a_hash(const char *s)\n{\n    uint64_t hash = 14695981039346656037ULL;\n    while (*s) {\n        hash ^= (unsigned char)(*s++);\n        hash *= 1099511628211ULL;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\nstatic cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key, const uint8_t *payload,\n                                   size_t len, uint16_t status, uint32_t ttl_sec,\n                                   uint64_t now_ms)\n{\n    cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) return NULL;\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) {\n        free(e);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n\n    e->payload = malloc(len);\n    if (!e->payload) {\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n        return NULL;\n    }\n    memcpy(e->payload, payload, len);\n\n    e->payload_len = len;\n    e->status_code = status;\n    e->expires_at  = now_ms + ((uint64_t)ttl_sec * 1000ULL);\n    e->hash        = fnv1a_hash(key);\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic void entry_destroy(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e);\n}\n\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) {\n        return; /* Already at front */\n    }\n\n    /* Remove from list if in list */\n    if (e->prev || e->next || c->lru_tail == e) {\n        lru_remove(c, e);\n    }\n\n    /* Insert at head */\n    e->next     = c->lru_head;\n    e->prev     = NULL;\n    if (c->lru_head) {\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    }\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) {\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void lru_remove(response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n\n    if (e->prev) {\n        e->prev->next = e->next;\n    } else {\n        c->lru_head = e->next;\n    }\n    if (e->next) {\n        e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    } else {\n        c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n    }\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void cache_evict_if_needed(response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    uint64_t now = now_epoch_millis();\n\n    /* First reap expired entries */\n    cache_entry_t *iter = c->lru_tail;\n    while (iter) {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = iter->prev;\n        if (iter->expires_at <= now ||\n            c->size > c->max_items)  /* force eviction if over capacity */\n        {\n            /* Remove from hash table */\n            size_t idx = iter->hash & (c->bucket_count - 1);\n            for (size_t probe = 0; probe < c->bucket_count; ++probe) {\n                if (c->buckets[idx] == iter) {\n                    c->buckets[idx] = NULL;\n                    break;\n                }\n                idx = (idx + 1) & (c->bucket_count - 1);\n            }\n\n            lru_remove(c, iter);\n            entry_destroy(iter);\n            --c->size;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n            mercury_counter_inc(METRIC_CACHE_EVICTIONS, 1);\n        }\n        iter = prev;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic size_t next_pow2(size_t n)\n{\n    size_t v = 1;\n    while (v < n) v <<= 1;\n    return v;\n}\n\nstatic uint64_t now_epoch_millis(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL;\n}\n\nstatic void metrics_update_size(response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    mercury_gauge_set(METRIC_CACHE_SIZE, (double)c->size);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*                                TEST HARNESS                                */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST\n\n/* Simple self-test when compiled standalone */\nint main(void)\n{\n    if (mercury_cache_init(4, 2) != 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    const char *key = \"/v1/products?sku=123\";\n    const char *resp = \"{\\\"name\\\":\\\"widget\\\",\\\"price\\\":9.99}\";\n    mercury_cache_put(key, (const uint8_t *)resp, strlen(resp)+1, 200, 1);\n\n    uint8_t *payload;\n    size_t len;\n    uint16_t status;\n    bool hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 1: hit=%d, payload=%s\\n\", hit, payload);\n\n    sleep(2); /* Wait for expiry */\n\n    hit = mercury_cache_get(key, &payload, &len, &status);\n    printf(\"GET 2: hit=%d\\n\", hit);\n\n    mercury_cache_shutdown();\n    return 0;\n}\n\n#endif /* RESPONSE_CACHE_SELFTEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ----------------------------------------\n * File:    src/module_30.txt         (compiled as C source)\n * Module:  Response Cache Sub-system\n *\n * Synopsis\n * --------\n * A thread-safe, in-memory LRU response cache with per-entry TTL that can be\n * enabled by individual HTTP/GraphQL handlers via declarative attributes.  The\n * implementation is generic and does not depend on the rest of the Mercury\n * routing stack; integration happens through a small adapter that converts a\n * `mm_request_t` + `mm_response_t` pair into a canonical cache key/value.\n *\n *  - Fully O(1) put/get with hash-map lookup and intrusive doubly linked list\n *    for eviction ordering (least-recently-used).\n *  - Fine-grained metrics counters (hits, misses, evictions, insert_errors).\n *  - Pluggable logger abstraction (see mm_log.h).\n *  - Hard upper bound on memory usage (`capacity_bytes`) with eviction of the\n *    oldest entry/entries before overflow.\n *\n * External deps:\n *   * uthash      \u2013 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash\n *   * pthreads    \u2013 POSIX threads\n *\n * Build flags (example):\n *   cc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -O2 -I./deps -c src/module_30.txt -o cache.o\n *\n * Copyright \u00a9 2023-2024 Mercury\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"mm_log.h\"     /* Structured logger (info, warn, error)          */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\" /* Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges           */\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Configuration knobs                                                   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_30.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 413
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_30.txt",
  "newString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\" /* Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges           */\n#include \"uthash.h\"",
  "oldString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"mm_log.h\"     /* Structured logger (info, warn, error)          */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\" /* Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges           */\n#include \"uthash.h\"",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ----------------------------------------\n * File:    src/module_30.txt         (compiled as C source)\n * Module:  Response Cache Sub-system\n *\n * Synopsis\n * --------\n * A thread-safe, in-memory LRU response cache with per-entry TTL that can be\n * enabled by individual HTTP/GraphQL handlers via declarative attributes.  The\n * implementation is generic and does not depend on the rest of the Mercury\n * routing stack; integration happens through a small adapter that converts a\n * `mm_request_t` + `mm_response_t` pair into a canonical cache key/value.\n *\n *  - Fully O(1) put/get with hash-map lookup and intrusive doubly linked list\n *    for eviction ordering (least-recently-used).\n *  - Fine-grained metrics counters (hits, misses, evictions, insert_errors).\n *  - Pluggable logger abstraction (see mm_log.h).\n *  - Hard upper bound on memory usage (`capacity_bytes`) with eviction of the\n *    oldest entry/entries before overflow.\n *\n * External deps:\n *   * uthash      \u2013 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash\n *   * pthreads    \u2013 POSIX threads\n *\n * Build flags (example):\n *   cc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -O2 -I./deps -c src/module_30.txt -o cache.o\n *\n * Copyright \u00a9 2023-2024 Mercury\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"mm_log.h\"     /* Structured logger (info, warn, error)          */\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\" /* Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges           */\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Configuration knobs                                                   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Default maximum in-memory cache size if not provided at init (16 MiB). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP (16 * 1024 * 1024UL)\n#endif\n\n/* Maximum key length we are willing to accept (for DoS protection). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN 2048\n#endif\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Public API                                                            */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Initialize a cache instance. Returns 0 on success or errno-style code. */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t **out_cache, size_t capacity_bytes);\n\n/* Gracefully release cache resources; afterwards *cache_ptr is set NULL. */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t **cache_ptr);\n\n/*\n * Attempt to look up an entry.\n *  - If present & not expired, allocates and returns a copy of the payload\n *    in *out_buf (caller must free) and writes length to *out_len.\n *  - Returns 0 on hit, ENOENT if no valid entry exists, or errno on error.\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 uint8_t **out_buf,\n                 size_t *out_len);\n\n/*\n * Insert value into cache with TTL (sec).  The payload is deep-copied.\n * Returns 0 on success or errno on failure (e.g., ENOMEM, EINVAL).\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const uint8_t *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 unsigned ttl_sec);\n\n/* Manually purge all entries (used by admin endpoints for cache-clear).  */\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Implementation details                                                */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char      *key;           /* NULL-terminated, unique               */\n    uint8_t   *blob;          /* response body                         */\n    size_t     blob_len;      /* bytes                                 */\n    time_t     expire_ts;     /* epoch seconds                         */\n\n    /* LRU list hooks */\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev;\n    struct mm_cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* uthash handle \u2013 key is `key` field                            */\n    UT_hash_handle hh;\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    size_t capacity;      /* Hard limit (bytes)                     */\n    size_t size;          /* Current resident size (bytes)          */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *map;/* uthash map                             */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;\n    pthread_mutex_t  mtx;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    mm_metric_t *m_hits;\n    mm_metric_t *m_misses;\n    mm_metric_t *m_evictions;\n    mm_metric_t *m_insert_err;\n};\n\n/* ------------- Helpers ------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Move node to front of LRU list (most-recent). */\nstatic inline void lru_move_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return;\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* Re-insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Insert new node at LRU front. */\nstatic inline void lru_insert_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Remove node completely from list & hash. */\nstatic void evict_entry(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    HASH_DEL(c->map, e);\n\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->size -= e->blob_len;\n    c->size -= strlen(e->key) + 1;\n\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n\n    mm_metric_inc(c->m_evictions, 1);\n}\n\n/* Purge expired entries starting from the tail to keep traversal bounded. */\nstatic void lru_purge_expired(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->lru_tail;\n\n    while (e) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *prev = e->prev;\n        if (e->expire_ts > now) break; /* Entries before this are newer */\n\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n        e = prev;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Evict until capacity is below limit. */\nstatic void lru_evict_overflow(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while (c->size > c->capacity && c->lru_tail) {\n        evict_entry(c, c->lru_tail);\n    }\n}\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Public interface implementations                                      */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t **out_cache, size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!out_cache) return EINVAL;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return ENOMEM;\n\n    c->capacity = capacity_bytes ? capacity_bytes : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP;\n\n    if (pthread_mutex_init(&c->mtx, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c);\n        return errno;\n    }\n\n    /* Register metrics */\n    c->m_hits       = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_hits\", \"Response cache hit count\");\n    c->m_misses     = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_misses\", \"Response cache miss count\");\n    c->m_evictions  = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_evictions\", \"Entries evicted from cache\");\n    c->m_insert_err = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_insert_errors\", \"Failed cache insertions\");\n\n    *out_cache = c;\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache initialised (capacity=%zu bytes)\", c->capacity);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t **cache_ptr)\n{\n    if (!cache_ptr || !*cache_ptr) return;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = *cache_ptr;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->map, cur, tmp) { evict_entry(c, cur); }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->mtx);\n\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_hits);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_misses);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_evictions);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_insert_err);\n\n    free(c);\n\n    *cache_ptr = NULL;\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache destroyed\");\n}\n\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 uint8_t **out_buf,\n                 size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_buf || !out_len) return EINVAL;\n\n    int rc = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    lru_purge_expired(cache);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, e);\n    if (!e) {\n        mm_metric_inc(cache->m_misses, 1);\n        rc = ENOENT;\n        goto done;\n    }\n\n    if (e->expire_ts <= time(NULL)) {\n        /* Stale entry */\n        evict_entry(cache, e);\n        mm_metric_inc(cache->m_misses, 1);\n        rc = ENOENT;\n        goto done;\n    }\n\n    /* Hit */\n    *out_len = e->blob_len;\n    *out_buf = malloc(e->blob_len);\n    if (!*out_buf) {\n        rc = ENOMEM;\n        goto done;\n    }\n    memcpy(*out_buf, e->blob, e->blob_len);\n\n    lru_move_front(cache, e);\n    mm_metric_inc(cache->m_hits, 1);\n\ndone:\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const uint8_t *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 unsigned ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0) return EINVAL;\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN) return EMSGSIZE;\n\n    int rc = 0;\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    lru_purge_expired(cache);\n\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Override in place */\n        cache->size -= existing->blob_len;\n        uint8_t *dup = realloc(existing->blob, payload_len);\n        if (!dup) {\n            rc = ENOMEM;\n            goto fail;\n        }\n        memcpy(dup, payload, payload_len);\n        existing->blob = dup;\n        existing->blob_len = payload_len;\n        existing->expire_ts = now + ttl_sec;\n        cache->size += payload_len;\n\n        lru_move_front(cache, existing);\n        goto success;\n    }\n\n    /* Fresh insert */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) { rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) { free(e); rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n\n    e->blob = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!e->blob) { free(e->key); free(e); rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n    memcpy(e->blob, payload, payload_len);\n    e->blob_len = payload_len;\n    e->expire_ts = now + ttl_sec;\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->map, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n    lru_insert_front(cache, e);\n\n    cache->size += payload_len;\n    cache->size += strlen(e->key) + 1;\n\nsuccess:\n    lru_evict_overflow(cache);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return 0;\n\nfail:\n    mm_metric_inc(cache->m_insert_err, 1);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->map, cur, tmp) { evict_entry(cache, cur); }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache cleared via admin command\");\n}\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Demo stub (only compiled in unit test builds)                         */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_STANDALONE_TEST\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache;\n    if (mm_cache_init(&cache, 1024 * 1024) != 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    const char key[] = \"GET:/api/v1/ping\";\n    const uint8_t payload[] = \"pong\";\n\n    mm_cache_put(cache, key, payload, sizeof(payload), 2);\n\n    uint8_t *out;\n    size_t   len;\n    if (mm_cache_get(cache, key, &out, &len) == 0) {\n        printf(\"Hit: %.*s\\n\", (int)len, out);\n        free(out);\n    } else {\n        puts(\"Miss (?)\");\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_30.txt",
  "newString": "        LOG_ERROR(\"Cache init failed\");",
  "oldString": "        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ----------------------------------------\n * File:    src/module_30.txt         (compiled as C source)\n * Module:  Response Cache Sub-system\n *\n * Synopsis\n * --------\n * A thread-safe, in-memory LRU response cache with per-entry TTL that can be\n * enabled by individual HTTP/GraphQL handlers via declarative attributes.  The\n * implementation is generic and does not depend on the rest of the Mercury\n * routing stack; integration happens through a small adapter that converts a\n * `mm_request_t` + `mm_response_t` pair into a canonical cache key/value.\n *\n *  - Fully O(1) put/get with hash-map lookup and intrusive doubly linked list\n *    for eviction ordering (least-recently-used).\n *  - Fine-grained metrics counters (hits, misses, evictions, insert_errors).\n *  - Pluggable logger abstraction (see mm_log.h).\n *  - Hard upper bound on memory usage (`capacity_bytes`) with eviction of the\n *    oldest entry/entries before overflow.\n *\n * External deps:\n *   * uthash      \u2013 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash\n *   * pthreads    \u2013 POSIX threads\n *\n * Build flags (example):\n *   cc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -O2 -I./deps -c src/module_30.txt -o cache.o\n *\n * Copyright \u00a9 2023-2024 Mercury\n */\n\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n#include \"mm_metrics.h\" /* Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges           */\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Configuration knobs                                                   */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Default maximum in-memory cache size if not provided at init (16 MiB). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP\n#define MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP (16 * 1024 * 1024UL)\n#endif\n\n/* Maximum key length we are willing to accept (for DoS protection). */\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN\n#define MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN 2048\n#endif\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Public API                                                            */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Initialize a cache instance. Returns 0 on success or errno-style code. */\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t **out_cache, size_t capacity_bytes);\n\n/* Gracefully release cache resources; afterwards *cache_ptr is set NULL. */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t **cache_ptr);\n\n/*\n * Attempt to look up an entry.\n *  - If present & not expired, allocates and returns a copy of the payload\n *    in *out_buf (caller must free) and writes length to *out_len.\n *  - Returns 0 on hit, ENOENT if no valid entry exists, or errno on error.\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 uint8_t **out_buf,\n                 size_t *out_len);\n\n/*\n * Insert value into cache with TTL (sec).  The payload is deep-copied.\n * Returns 0 on success or errno on failure (e.g., ENOMEM, EINVAL).\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const uint8_t *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 unsigned ttl_sec);\n\n/* Manually purge all entries (used by admin endpoints for cache-clear).  */\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache);\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Implementation details                                                */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_entry {\n    char      *key;           /* NULL-terminated, unique               */\n    uint8_t   *blob;          /* response body                         */\n    size_t     blob_len;      /* bytes                                 */\n    time_t     expire_ts;     /* epoch seconds                         */\n\n    /* LRU list hooks */\n    struct mm_cache_entry *prev;\n    struct mm_cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* uthash handle \u2013 key is `key` field                            */\n    UT_hash_handle hh;\n} mm_cache_entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    size_t capacity;      /* Hard limit (bytes)                     */\n    size_t size;          /* Current resident size (bytes)          */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *map;/* uthash map                             */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_head;\n    mm_cache_entry_t *lru_tail;\n    pthread_mutex_t  mtx;\n\n    /* Metrics */\n    mm_metric_t *m_hits;\n    mm_metric_t *m_misses;\n    mm_metric_t *m_evictions;\n    mm_metric_t *m_insert_err;\n};\n\n/* ------------- Helpers ------------------------------------------------ */\n\n/* Move node to front of LRU list (most-recent). */\nstatic inline void lru_move_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return;\n\n    /* Detach */\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    /* Re-insert at head */\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Insert new node at LRU front. */\nstatic inline void lru_insert_front(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Remove node completely from list & hash. */\nstatic void evict_entry(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    HASH_DEL(c->map, e);\n\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    c->size -= e->blob_len;\n    c->size -= strlen(e->key) + 1;\n\n    free(e->blob);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n\n    mm_metric_inc(c->m_evictions, 1);\n}\n\n/* Purge expired entries starting from the tail to keep traversal bounded. */\nstatic void lru_purge_expired(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = c->lru_tail;\n\n    while (e) {\n        mm_cache_entry_t *prev = e->prev;\n        if (e->expire_ts > now) break; /* Entries before this are newer */\n\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n        e = prev;\n    }\n}\n\n/* Evict until capacity is below limit. */\nstatic void lru_evict_overflow(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while (c->size > c->capacity && c->lru_tail) {\n        evict_entry(c, c->lru_tail);\n    }\n}\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Public interface implementations                                      */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint mm_cache_init(mm_cache_t **out_cache, size_t capacity_bytes)\n{\n    if (!out_cache) return EINVAL;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return ENOMEM;\n\n    c->capacity = capacity_bytes ? capacity_bytes : MM_CACHE_DEFAULT_CAP;\n\n    if (pthread_mutex_init(&c->mtx, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c);\n        return errno;\n    }\n\n    /* Register metrics */\n    c->m_hits       = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_hits\", \"Response cache hit count\");\n    c->m_misses     = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_misses\", \"Response cache miss count\");\n    c->m_evictions  = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_evictions\", \"Entries evicted from cache\");\n    c->m_insert_err = mm_metric_counter_create(\"cache_insert_errors\", \"Failed cache insertions\");\n\n    *out_cache = c;\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache initialised (capacity=%zu bytes)\", c->capacity);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t **cache_ptr)\n{\n    if (!cache_ptr || !*cache_ptr) return;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = *cache_ptr;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->mtx);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->map, cur, tmp) { evict_entry(c, cur); }\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->mtx);\n\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_hits);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_misses);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_evictions);\n    mm_metric_free(c->m_insert_err);\n\n    free(c);\n\n    *cache_ptr = NULL;\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache destroyed\");\n}\n\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 uint8_t **out_buf,\n                 size_t *out_len)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !out_buf || !out_len) return EINVAL;\n\n    int rc = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    lru_purge_expired(cache);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, e);\n    if (!e) {\n        mm_metric_inc(cache->m_misses, 1);\n        rc = ENOENT;\n        goto done;\n    }\n\n    if (e->expire_ts <= time(NULL)) {\n        /* Stale entry */\n        evict_entry(cache, e);\n        mm_metric_inc(cache->m_misses, 1);\n        rc = ENOENT;\n        goto done;\n    }\n\n    /* Hit */\n    *out_len = e->blob_len;\n    *out_buf = malloc(e->blob_len);\n    if (!*out_buf) {\n        rc = ENOMEM;\n        goto done;\n    }\n    memcpy(*out_buf, e->blob, e->blob_len);\n\n    lru_move_front(cache, e);\n    mm_metric_inc(cache->m_hits, 1);\n\ndone:\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *cache,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const uint8_t *payload,\n                 size_t payload_len,\n                 unsigned ttl_sec)\n{\n    if (!cache || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0) return EINVAL;\n    if (strlen(key) > MM_CACHE_MAX_KEY_LEN) return EMSGSIZE;\n\n    int rc = 0;\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n\n    mm_cache_entry_t *existing = NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    lru_purge_expired(cache);\n\n    HASH_FIND_STR(cache->map, key, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Override in place */\n        cache->size -= existing->blob_len;\n        uint8_t *dup = realloc(existing->blob, payload_len);\n        if (!dup) {\n            rc = ENOMEM;\n            goto fail;\n        }\n        memcpy(dup, payload, payload_len);\n        existing->blob = dup;\n        existing->blob_len = payload_len;\n        existing->expire_ts = now + ttl_sec;\n        cache->size += payload_len;\n\n        lru_move_front(cache, existing);\n        goto success;\n    }\n\n    /* Fresh insert */\n    mm_cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) { rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    if (!e->key) { free(e); rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n\n    e->blob = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!e->blob) { free(e->key); free(e); rc = ENOMEM; goto fail; }\n    memcpy(e->blob, payload, payload_len);\n    e->blob_len = payload_len;\n    e->expire_ts = now + ttl_sec;\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, cache->map, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n    lru_insert_front(cache, e);\n\n    cache->size += payload_len;\n    cache->size += strlen(e->key) + 1;\n\nsuccess:\n    lru_evict_overflow(cache);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return 0;\n\nfail:\n    mm_metric_inc(cache->m_insert_err, 1);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n    return rc;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_clear(mm_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&cache->mtx);\n    mm_cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, cache->map, cur, tmp) { evict_entry(cache, cur); }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&cache->mtx);\n\n    MM_LOG_INFO(\"Cache cleared via admin command\");\n}\n\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Demo stub (only compiled in unit test builds)                         */\n/* --------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_STANDALONE_TEST\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *cache;\n    if (mm_cache_init(&cache, 1024 * 1024) != 0) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Cache init failed\\n\");\n        return 1;\n    }\n\n    const char key[] = \"GET:/api/v1/ping\";\n    const uint8_t payload[] = \"pong\";\n\n    mm_cache_put(cache, key, payload, sizeof(payload), 2);\n\n    uint8_t *out;\n    size_t   len;\n    if (mm_cache_get(cache, key, &out, &len) == 0) {\n        printf(\"Hit: %.*s\\n\", (int)len, out);\n        free(out);\n    } else {\n        puts(\"Miss (?)\");\n    }\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(&cache);\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n",

{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\nstatic void\nassert_ok(int cond, const char *msg)\n{\n    if (!cond) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Assert failed: %s\\n\", msg);\n        abort();\n    }\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c;\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_create(&c, 4, 1 << 20, 1000) == 0, \"create\");",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_77.txt",
    "numLines": 15,
    "startLine": 425,
    "totalLines": 465
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module: Response Cache (in-memory LRU w/ TTL)\n * File:    src/module_77.txt   (compiled as part of objects/libcache.a)\n *\n * NOTE: This module implements a lightweight, thread-safe, TTL-aware LRU cache\n *       used by the API gateway layer for HTTP/GraphQL response caching.\n *       It exposes a minimal surface that the gateway can depend on while\n *       remaining independent of HTTP specifics.\n *\n * Copyright (c) 2023-2024, Megaverse Software\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n */\n\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Public Interface                                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_H\n#define MM_CACHE_H\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Opaque snapshot used for Prometheus / statsd scraping */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hit;\n    uint64_t miss;\n    uint64_t put;\n    uint64_t eviction;\n    uint64_t bytes_curr;\n    uint64_t items_curr;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* Initialize a cache instance. Returns 0 on success. */\nint mm_cache_create(mm_cache_t **out_cache,\n                    size_t        max_items,\n                    size_t        max_bytes,\n                    uint32_t      default_ttl_ms);\n\n/* Destroy and free all resources */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c);\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_77.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 465
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "newString": "#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"",
  "oldString": "#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdio.h>",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module: Response Cache (in-memory LRU w/ TTL)\n * File:    src/module_77.txt   (compiled as part of objects/libcache.a)\n *\n * NOTE: This module implements a lightweight, thread-safe, TTL-aware LRU cache\n *       used by the API gateway layer for HTTP/GraphQL response caching.\n *       It exposes a minimal surface that the gateway can depend on while\n *       remaining independent of HTTP specifics.\n *\n * Copyright (c) 2023-2024, Megaverse Software\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n */\n\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Public Interface                                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_H\n#define MM_CACHE_H\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Opaque snapshot used for Prometheus / statsd scraping */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hit;\n    uint64_t miss;\n    uint64_t put;\n    uint64_t eviction;\n    uint64_t bytes_curr;\n    uint64_t items_curr;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* Initialize a cache instance. Returns 0 on success. */\nint mm_cache_create(mm_cache_t **out_cache,\n                    size_t        max_items,\n                    size_t        max_bytes,\n                    uint32_t      default_ttl_ms);\n\n/* Destroy and free all resources */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c);\n\n/*\n * Fetch an item from cache.\n * On success (*payload != NULL), ownership of the returned buffer belongs to\n * the caller who must free() it.\n * Returns 0 (found) | ENOENT (not found) | ETIMEDOUT (expired) | other errno\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 char      **payload,\n                 size_t     *payload_len,\n                 uint32_t   *age_ms);\n\n/*\n * Put an item into the cache. If ttl_override_ms == 0, default_ttl is used.\n * The callee duplicates the payload buffer; caller retains ownership of input.\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const void *payload,\n                 size_t      payload_len,\n                 uint32_t    ttl_override_ms);\n\n/* Remove cached entry (if present). Non-existent keys are ignored. */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key);\n\n/* Atomically copy metrics into provided struct */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_metrics_t *out);\n\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_H */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Internal \u2013 implementation details                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#define FNV_OFFSET 14695981039346656037ULL\n#define FNV_PRIME  1099511628211ULL\nstatic inline uint64_t\nfnv1a(const char *key)\n{\n    uint64_t hash = FNV_OFFSET;\n    for (const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)key; *p; ++p) {\n        hash ^= (uint64_t)(*p);\n        hash *= FNV_PRIME;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\ntypedef struct entry {\n    struct entry *prev;\n    struct entry *next;\n    struct entry *h_next;      /* next in hash bucket */\n    char         *key;\n    char         *payload;\n    size_t        len;\n    size_t        mem_cost;    /* key + payload */\n    uint64_t      expiry_ms;\n    uint64_t      last_hit_ms; /* for age calculation */\n} entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    size_t               n_buckets;\n    entry_t            **buckets;\n\n    /* LRU doubly linked list head/tail */\n    entry_t             *lru_head;\n    entry_t             *lru_tail;\n\n    size_t               max_items;\n    size_t               max_bytes;\n    uint32_t             default_ttl;\n\n    size_t               curr_items;\n    size_t               curr_bytes;\n\n    mm_cache_metrics_t   metrics;\n    pthread_rwlock_t     rwlock;        /* reader/writer lock for concurrency */\n};\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Utility                                                                   */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n#ifdef CLOCK_MONOTONIC\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n#else\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n#endif\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_remove(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_push_front(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head)\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail)\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Remove item from hashtable + LRU and free memory */\nstatic void\nevict_entry(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    /* unlink from bucket */\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(e->key) % c->n_buckets;\n    entry_t **pp = &c->buckets[h];\n    while (*pp && *pp != e) pp = &(*pp)->h_next;\n    if (*pp == e) *pp = e->h_next;\n\n    /* unlink from LRU */\n    lru_remove(c, e);\n\n    c->curr_items--;\n    c->curr_bytes -= e->mem_cost;\n    c->metrics.eviction++;\n\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* Ensure memory & item count limits; evict oldest */\nstatic void\nenforce_limits(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while ((c->curr_items > c->max_items) ||\n           (c->curr_bytes > c->max_bytes)) {\n        if (!c->lru_tail) break;\n        evict_entry(c, c->lru_tail);\n    }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Public API implementation                                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint\nmm_cache_create(mm_cache_t **out_cache,\n                size_t        max_items,\n                size_t        max_bytes,\n                uint32_t      default_ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!out_cache || max_items == 0 || max_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return ENOMEM;\n\n    c->n_buckets   = 2048;       /* power-of-two for better distribution */\n    c->buckets     = calloc(c->n_buckets, sizeof(entry_t *));\n    if (!c->buckets) {\n        free(c);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    c->max_items   = max_items;\n    c->max_bytes   = max_bytes;\n    c->default_ttl = default_ttl_ms ? default_ttl_ms : 5000; /* default 5s */\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c->buckets);\n        free(c);\n        return errno;\n    }\n\n    *out_cache = c;\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->n_buckets; ++i) {\n        entry_t *e = c->buckets[i];\n        while (e) {\n            entry_t *next = e->h_next;\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e);\n            e = next;\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->buckets);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n             const char *key,\n             char      **payload,\n             size_t     *payload_len,\n             uint32_t   *age_ms)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload) return EINVAL;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    entry_t *e = c->buckets[h];\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0) e = e->h_next;\n\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    if (!e) {\n        c->metrics.miss++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n    if (e->expiry_ms <= now) {\n        /* expired -> treat as miss and evict lazily under write lock */\n        c->metrics.miss++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ETIMEDOUT;\n    }\n\n    /* hit */\n    c->metrics.hit++;\n    e->last_hit_ms = now;\n\n    /* duplicate payload for caller */\n    char *dup = malloc(e->len);\n    if (!dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(dup, e->payload, e->len);\n\n    lru_remove(c, e);\n    lru_push_front(c, e);\n\n    uint32_t age     = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ms - c->default_ttl));\n    *payload         = dup;\n    if (payload_len) *payload_len = e->len;\n    if (age_ms)      *age_ms = age;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n             const char *key,\n             const void *payload,\n             size_t      payload_len,\n             uint32_t    ttl_override_ms)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    /* allocate resources before locking to reduce contention */\n    char *key_copy = strdup(key);\n    if (!key_copy) return ENOMEM;\n\n    char *payload_copy = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!payload_copy) {\n        free(key_copy);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_copy, payload, payload_len);\n\n    entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry) {\n        free(key_copy);\n        free(payload_copy);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n\n    uint32_t ttl = ttl_override_ms ? ttl_override_ms : c->default_ttl;\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    new_entry->key        = key_copy;\n    new_entry->payload    = payload_copy;\n    new_entry->len        = payload_len;\n    new_entry->mem_cost   = strlen(key) + payload_len;\n    new_entry->expiry_ms  = now + ttl;\n    new_entry->last_hit_ms = now;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* if key exists -> replace */\n    entry_t **pp = &c->buckets[h];\n    while (*pp && strcmp((*pp)->key, key) != 0) pp = &(*pp)->h_next;\n\n    if (*pp) {\n        /* replace in place */\n        entry_t *old = *pp;\n        c->curr_bytes -= old->mem_cost;\n        c->curr_bytes += new_entry->mem_cost;\n\n        new_entry->h_next = old->h_next;\n        *pp = new_entry;\n\n        lru_remove(c, old);\n        lru_push_front(c, new_entry);\n\n        free(old->payload);\n        free(old->key);\n        free(old);\n    } else {\n        /* insert new */\n        new_entry->h_next = c->buckets[h];\n        c->buckets[h] = new_entry;\n        lru_push_front(c, new_entry);\n\n        c->curr_items++;\n        c->curr_bytes += new_entry->mem_cost;\n    }\n\n    c->metrics.put++;\n\n    enforce_limits(c);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    entry_t *e = c->buckets[h];\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0) e = e->h_next;\n    if (e)\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_metrics(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    *out = c->metrics;\n    out->bytes_curr = c->curr_bytes;\n    out->items_curr = c->curr_items;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Simple self-test when built with -DMM_CACHE_TEST                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\nstatic void\nassert_ok(int cond, const char *msg)\n{\n    if (!cond) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Assert failed: %s\\n\", msg);\n        abort();\n    }\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c;\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_create(&c, 4, 1 << 20, 1000) == 0, \"create\");\n\n    const char data[] = \"payload\";\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_put(c, \"/items?id=1\", data, sizeof(data), 0) == 0, \"put\");\n\n    char  *out = NULL;\n    size_t len = 0;\n    uint32_t age;\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_get(c, \"/items?id=1\", &out, &len, &age) == 0, \"get\");\n    assert_ok(len == sizeof(data), \"len\");\n    assert_ok(memcmp(out, data, len) == 0, \"payload\");\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_metrics_t m;\n    mm_cache_metrics(c, &m);\n    assert_ok(m.hit == 1 && m.put == 1 && m.miss == 0, \"metrics\");\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    puts(\"All tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  End of file                                                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
