  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module: Response Cache (in-memory LRU w/ TTL)\n * File:    src/module_77.txt   (compiled as part of objects/libcache.a)\n *\n * NOTE: This module implements a lightweight, thread-safe, TTL-aware LRU cache\n *       used by the API gateway layer for HTTP/GraphQL response caching.\n *       It exposes a minimal surface that the gateway can depend on while\n *       remaining independent of HTTP specifics.\n *\n * Copyright (c) 2023-2024, Megaverse Software\n * SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT\n */\n\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Public Interface                                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifndef MM_CACHE_H\n#define MM_CACHE_H\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Opaque snapshot used for Prometheus / statsd scraping */\ntypedef struct {\n    uint64_t hit;\n    uint64_t miss;\n    uint64_t put;\n    uint64_t eviction;\n    uint64_t bytes_curr;\n    uint64_t items_curr;\n} mm_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* Initialize a cache instance. Returns 0 on success. */\nint mm_cache_create(mm_cache_t **out_cache,\n                    size_t        max_items,\n                    size_t        max_bytes,\n                    uint32_t      default_ttl_ms);\n\n/* Destroy and free all resources */\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c);\n\n/*\n * Fetch an item from cache.\n * On success (*payload != NULL), ownership of the returned buffer belongs to\n * the caller who must free() it.\n * Returns 0 (found) | ENOENT (not found) | ETIMEDOUT (expired) | other errno\n */\nint mm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 char      **payload,\n                 size_t     *payload_len,\n                 uint32_t   *age_ms);\n\n/*\n * Put an item into the cache. If ttl_override_ms == 0, default_ttl is used.\n * The callee duplicates the payload buffer; caller retains ownership of input.\n */\nint mm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n                 const char *key,\n                 const void *payload,\n                 size_t      payload_len,\n                 uint32_t    ttl_override_ms);\n\n/* Remove cached entry (if present). Non-existent keys are ignored. */\nvoid mm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key);\n\n/* Atomically copy metrics into provided struct */\nvoid mm_cache_metrics(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_metrics_t *out);\n\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_H */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Internal \u2013 implementation details                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#define FNV_OFFSET 14695981039346656037ULL\n#define FNV_PRIME  1099511628211ULL\nstatic inline uint64_t\nfnv1a(const char *key)\n{\n    uint64_t hash = FNV_OFFSET;\n    for (const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)key; *p; ++p) {\n        hash ^= (uint64_t)(*p);\n        hash *= FNV_PRIME;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\ntypedef struct entry {\n    struct entry *prev;\n    struct entry *next;\n    struct entry *h_next;      /* next in hash bucket */\n    char         *key;\n    char         *payload;\n    size_t        len;\n    size_t        mem_cost;    /* key + payload */\n    uint64_t      expiry_ms;\n    uint64_t      last_hit_ms; /* for age calculation */\n} entry_t;\n\nstruct mm_cache {\n    size_t               n_buckets;\n    entry_t            **buckets;\n\n    /* LRU doubly linked list head/tail */\n    entry_t             *lru_head;\n    entry_t             *lru_tail;\n\n    size_t               max_items;\n    size_t               max_bytes;\n    uint32_t             default_ttl;\n\n    size_t               curr_items;\n    size_t               curr_bytes;\n\n    mm_cache_metrics_t   metrics;\n    pthread_rwlock_t     rwlock;        /* reader/writer lock for concurrency */\n};\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Utility                                                                   */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic uint64_t\nnow_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n#ifdef CLOCK_MONOTONIC\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);\n#else\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n#endif\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_remove(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_push_front(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    if (c->lru_head)\n        c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail)\n        c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\n/* Remove item from hashtable + LRU and free memory */\nstatic void\nevict_entry(mm_cache_t *c, entry_t *e)\n{\n    /* unlink from bucket */\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(e->key) % c->n_buckets;\n    entry_t **pp = &c->buckets[h];\n    while (*pp && *pp != e) pp = &(*pp)->h_next;\n    if (*pp == e) *pp = e->h_next;\n\n    /* unlink from LRU */\n    lru_remove(c, e);\n\n    c->curr_items--;\n    c->curr_bytes -= e->mem_cost;\n    c->metrics.eviction++;\n\n    free(e->payload);\n    free(e->key);\n    free(e);\n}\n\n/* Ensure memory & item count limits; evict oldest */\nstatic void\nenforce_limits(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    while ((c->curr_items > c->max_items) ||\n           (c->curr_bytes > c->max_bytes)) {\n        if (!c->lru_tail) break;\n        evict_entry(c, c->lru_tail);\n    }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Public API implementation                                                 */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint\nmm_cache_create(mm_cache_t **out_cache,\n                size_t        max_items,\n                size_t        max_bytes,\n                uint32_t      default_ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!out_cache || max_items == 0 || max_bytes == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    mm_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c) return ENOMEM;\n\n    c->n_buckets   = 2048;       /* power-of-two for better distribution */\n    c->buckets     = calloc(c->n_buckets, sizeof(entry_t *));\n    if (!c->buckets) {\n        free(c);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    c->max_items   = max_items;\n    c->max_bytes   = max_bytes;\n    c->default_ttl = default_ttl_ms ? default_ttl_ms : 5000; /* default 5s */\n\n    if (pthread_rwlock_init(&c->rwlock, NULL) != 0) {\n        free(c->buckets);\n        free(c);\n        return errno;\n    }\n\n    *out_cache = c;\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(mm_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < c->n_buckets; ++i) {\n        entry_t *e = c->buckets[i];\n        while (e) {\n            entry_t *next = e->h_next;\n            free(e->payload);\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e);\n            e = next;\n        }\n    }\n    free(c->buckets);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->rwlock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_get(mm_cache_t *c,\n             const char *key,\n             char      **payload,\n             size_t     *payload_len,\n             uint32_t   *age_ms)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload) return EINVAL;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    entry_t *e = c->buckets[h];\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0) e = e->h_next;\n\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    if (!e) {\n        c->metrics.miss++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ENOENT;\n    }\n    if (e->expiry_ms <= now) {\n        /* expired -> treat as miss and evict lazily under write lock */\n        c->metrics.miss++;\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ETIMEDOUT;\n    }\n\n    /* hit */\n    c->metrics.hit++;\n    e->last_hit_ms = now;\n\n    /* duplicate payload for caller */\n    char *dup = malloc(e->len);\n    if (!dup) {\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(dup, e->payload, e->len);\n\n    lru_remove(c, e);\n    lru_push_front(c, e);\n\n    uint32_t age     = (uint32_t)(now - (e->expiry_ms - c->default_ttl));\n    *payload         = dup;\n    if (payload_len) *payload_len = e->len;\n    if (age_ms)      *age_ms = age;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_put(mm_cache_t *c,\n             const char *key,\n             const void *payload,\n             size_t      payload_len,\n             uint32_t    ttl_override_ms)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || payload_len == 0)\n        return EINVAL;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    /* allocate resources before locking to reduce contention */\n    char *key_copy = strdup(key);\n    if (!key_copy) return ENOMEM;\n\n    char *payload_copy = malloc(payload_len);\n    if (!payload_copy) {\n        free(key_copy);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n    memcpy(payload_copy, payload, payload_len);\n\n    entry_t *new_entry = calloc(1, sizeof(*new_entry));\n    if (!new_entry) {\n        free(key_copy);\n        free(payload_copy);\n        return ENOMEM;\n    }\n\n    uint32_t ttl = ttl_override_ms ? ttl_override_ms : c->default_ttl;\n    uint64_t now = now_ms();\n\n    new_entry->key        = key_copy;\n    new_entry->payload    = payload_copy;\n    new_entry->len        = payload_len;\n    new_entry->mem_cost   = strlen(key) + payload_len;\n    new_entry->expiry_ms  = now + ttl;\n    new_entry->last_hit_ms = now;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n\n    /* if key exists -> replace */\n    entry_t **pp = &c->buckets[h];\n    while (*pp && strcmp((*pp)->key, key) != 0) pp = &(*pp)->h_next;\n\n    if (*pp) {\n        /* replace in place */\n        entry_t *old = *pp;\n        c->curr_bytes -= old->mem_cost;\n        c->curr_bytes += new_entry->mem_cost;\n\n        new_entry->h_next = old->h_next;\n        *pp = new_entry;\n\n        lru_remove(c, old);\n        lru_push_front(c, new_entry);\n\n        free(old->payload);\n        free(old->key);\n        free(old);\n    } else {\n        /* insert new */\n        new_entry->h_next = c->buckets[h];\n        c->buckets[h] = new_entry;\n        lru_push_front(c, new_entry);\n\n        c->curr_items++;\n        c->curr_bytes += new_entry->mem_cost;\n    }\n\n    c->metrics.put++;\n\n    enforce_limits(c);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_invalidate(mm_cache_t *c, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!c || !key) return;\n\n    uint64_t h = fnv1a(key) % c->n_buckets;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->rwlock);\n    entry_t *e = c->buckets[h];\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0) e = e->h_next;\n    if (e)\n        evict_entry(c, e);\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_metrics(mm_cache_t *c, mm_cache_metrics_t *out)\n{\n    if (!c || !out) return;\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->rwlock);\n    *out = c->metrics;\n    out->bytes_curr = c->curr_bytes;\n    out->items_curr = c->curr_items;\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->rwlock);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Simple self-test when built with -DMM_CACHE_TEST                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_TEST\nstatic void\nassert_ok(int cond, const char *msg)\n{\n    if (!cond) {\n        fprintf(stderr, \"Assert failed: %s\\n\", msg);\n        abort();\n    }\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_t *c;\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_create(&c, 4, 1 << 20, 1000) == 0, \"create\");\n\n    const char data[] = \"payload\";\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_put(c, \"/items?id=1\", data, sizeof(data), 0) == 0, \"put\");\n\n    char  *out = NULL;\n    size_t len = 0;\n    uint32_t age;\n    assert_ok(mm_cache_get(c, \"/items?id=1\", &out, &len, &age) == 0, \"get\");\n    assert_ok(len == sizeof(data), \"len\");\n    assert_ok(memcmp(out, data, len) == 0, \"payload\");\n    free(out);\n\n    mm_cache_metrics_t m;\n    mm_cache_metrics(c, &m);\n    assert_ok(m.hit == 1 && m.put == 1 && m.miss == 0, \"metrics\");\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    puts(\"All tests passed.\");\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_TEST */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  End of file                                                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " assert_ok(int cond, const char *msg)",
        " {",
        "     if (!cond) {",
        "-        fprintf(stderr, \"Assert failed: %s\\n\", msg);",
        "+        LOG_ERROR(\"Assert failed: %s\", msg);",
        "         abort();",
        "     }",
        " }"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 430,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 430
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

  "file": {
    "content": "static void busy_loop(const char *key, int n)\n{\n    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(key);\n        if (!ok) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"Rate limit hit @%d\\n\", i);\n            nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);\n        }\n    }\n}\nint main(void)\n{\n    rl_init(10, 10); /* 10 req/sec, burst 10 */\n    busy_loop(\"clientA\", 25);\n    FILE *f = fopen(\"/tmp/metrics.txt\", \"w\");",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_74.txt",
    "numLines": 15,
    "startLine": 350,
    "totalLines": 370

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ------------------------------------\n * Module: src/module_74.c   (Rate-Limiting Engine)\n *\n * Provides an in-process, thread-safe, token-bucket rate limiter that can be\n * used by any layer (HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, internal event bus, \u2026) to protect\n * endpoints against abusive traffic while guaranteeing deterministic latency.\n *\n *   \u2011 Per-key (API-Key, JWT subject, IP, Route) token buckets\n *   \u2011 Optional per-route overrides\n *   \u2011 Lock-free hot-path (single CAS) for maximum performance\n *   \u2011 Background janitor to purge idle buckets\n *   \u2011 Prometheus-compatible metric exposition\n *\n * Build:\n *   cc -std=c11 -O2 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -c src/module_74.c\n *\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n#define _GNU_SOURCE\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash \u2013 single-file open-source hash-map (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n * A cut-down copy is embedded here to avoid extra build deps.\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n/* Only what we need: HASH_ADD_KEYPTR, HASH_FIND, HASH_DEL */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz) malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh,head,keyptr,keylen,out)                                          \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        out = NULL;                                                                  \\\n        if (head) {                                                                  \\\n            unsigned _hf_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)keyptr)[0] & (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1; \\\n            struct UT_hash_handle *_hf_hh = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head; \\\n            while (_hf_hh) {                                                         \\",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_74.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 370
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "oldString": "#define _GNU_SOURCE\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ------------------------------------\n * Module: src/module_74.c   (Rate-Limiting Engine)\n *\n * Provides an in-process, thread-safe, token-bucket rate limiter that can be\n * used by any layer (HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, internal event bus, \u2026) to protect\n * endpoints against abusive traffic while guaranteeing deterministic latency.\n *\n *   \u2011 Per-key (API-Key, JWT subject, IP, Route) token buckets\n *   \u2011 Optional per-route overrides\n *   \u2011 Lock-free hot-path (single CAS) for maximum performance\n *   \u2011 Background janitor to purge idle buckets\n *   \u2011 Prometheus-compatible metric exposition\n *\n * Build:\n *   cc -std=c11 -O2 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -c src/module_74.c\n *\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n#define _GNU_SOURCE\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash \u2013 single-file open-source hash-map (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n * A cut-down copy is embedded here to avoid extra build deps.\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n/* Only what we need: HASH_ADD_KEYPTR, HASH_FIND, HASH_DEL */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz) malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh,head,keyptr,keylen,out)                                          \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        out = NULL;                                                                  \\\n        if (head) {                                                                  \\\n            unsigned _hf_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)keyptr)[0] & (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1; \\\n            struct UT_hash_handle *_hf_hh = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head; \\\n            while (_hf_hh) {                                                         \\\n                if ((_hf_hh->keylen == (unsigned)(keylen)) &&                        \\\n                    (memcmp(_hf_hh->key, keyptr, keylen) == 0)) {                    \\\n                    out = _hf_hh->prev; /* prev pointer is container */              \\\n                    break;                                                           \\\n                }                                                                    \\\n                _hf_hh = _hf_hh->hh_next;                                            \\\n            }                                                                        \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n    } while(0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh,head,keyptr,keylen_in,add)                                \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        unsigned _ha_bkt;                                                            \\\n        if (!(head)) {                                                               \\\n            (head)=add;                                                              \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl = (UT_hash_table*)uthash_malloc(sizeof(UT_hash_table));   \\\n            memset((head)->hh.tbl,0,sizeof(UT_hash_table));                          \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets = 512;                                       \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl->buckets =                                                \\\n              (UT_hash_bucket*)uthash_malloc(512*sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket));     \\\n            memset((head)->hh.tbl->buckets,0,512*sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket));     \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n        (add)->hh.key = (const void*)(keyptr);                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.keylen = (unsigned)(keylen_in);                                    \\\n        _ha_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)keyptr)[0] & ((head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1);\\\n        (add)->hh.hh_next = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head;                \\\n        (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head = &((add)->hh);                     \\\n        (add)->hh.prev = (add); /* back-pointer to struct (simplification) */        \\\n    } while(0)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(hh,head,delptr)                                                     \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        unsigned _hd_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)(delptr)->hh.key)[0] &             \\\n                           ((head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1);                          \\\n        struct UT_hash_handle *_hd_hh = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head;    \\\n        struct UT_hash_handle *_hd_prev = NULL;                                      \\\n        while (_hd_hh) {                                                             \\\n            if (_hd_hh == &((delptr)->hh)) {                                         \\\n                if (_hd_prev) _hd_prev->hh_next = _hd_hh->hh_next;                   \\\n                else head->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head = _hd_hh->hh_next;       \\\n                uthash_free(delptr, sizeof(*(delptr)));                              \\\n                break;                                                               \\\n            }                                                                        \\\n            _hd_prev = _hd_hh;                                                       \\\n            _hd_hh = _hd_hh->hh_next;                                                \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n    } while(0)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket *buckets;\n    unsigned num_buckets;\n} UT_hash_table;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    const void *key;\n    unsigned keylen;\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next;\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    /* custom stuff */\n    void *prev;          /* back-pointer to container struct (simplification) */\n} UT_hash_handle;\n#endif /* UTHASH_H */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#define RL_MAX_KEY      96u\n#define RL_DEFAULT_RPS  50u\n#define RL_DEFAULT_BURST (RL_DEFAULT_RPS * 2u)\n#define RL_IDLE_TTL_SEC  180u          /* remove buckets idle for >3min         */\n#define RL_JANITOR_INTERVAL_SEC 30u\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket {\n    char               key[RL_MAX_KEY];\n    uint32_t           rps;           /* tokens added per second    */\n    uint32_t           burst;         /* bucket capacity            */\n    _Atomic uint32_t   tokens;        /* current tokens (<=burst)   */\n    _Atomic uint64_t   last_ns;       /* last refill timestamp      */\n    _Atomic uint64_t   last_touch_ns; /* last time key was used     */\n    UT_hash_handle     hh;            /* uthash linkage             */\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\n/* Global state ----------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic rl_bucket_t *g_table = NULL;\nstatic pthread_mutex_t g_table_mtx = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER;\nstatic atomic_bool g_rl_initialized = false;\nstatic uint32_t g_default_rps   = RL_DEFAULT_RPS;\nstatic uint32_t g_default_burst = RL_DEFAULT_BURST;\nstatic atomic_bool g_stop_janitor = false;\nstatic pthread_t janitor_tid;\n\n/* Utility ---------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic inline uint64_t nsec_now(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000000000ull + (uint64_t)ts.tv_nsec;\n}\n\n/* Refill algorithm: lock-free, CAS on tokens & last_ns                        */\nstatic inline void bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *b, uint64_t now_ns)\n{\n    uint64_t last_ns = atomic_load_explicit(&b->last_ns, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    /* If another thread updated more recently, nothing to do.                 */\n    if (now_ns <= last_ns) return;\n\n    uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - last_ns;\n    uint32_t add_tokens = (uint32_t)((elapsed_ns * b->rps) / 1000000000ull);\n    if (add_tokens == 0) return;\n\n    /* Attempt to advance last_ns \u2013 if fails, another thread updated.          */\n    if (!atomic_compare_exchange_strong(&b->last_ns, &last_ns, now_ns))\n        return;\n\n    uint32_t cur = atomic_load_explicit(&b->tokens, memory_order_relaxed);\n    uint32_t new_val = cur + add_tokens;\n    if (new_val > b->burst) new_val = b->burst;\n    atomic_store_explicit(&b->tokens, new_val, memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* Find or create bucket \u2013 must hold g_table_mtx                               */\nstatic rl_bucket_t *bucket_get_locked(const char *key, size_t keylen)\n{\n    rl_bucket_t *b = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, key, (unsigned)keylen, b);\n    if (!b) {\n        b = calloc(1, sizeof(*b));\n        strncpy(b->key, key, RL_MAX_KEY - 1);\n        b->rps          = g_default_rps;\n        b->burst        = g_default_burst;\n        atomic_store(&b->tokens, b->burst);\n        uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n        atomic_store(&b->last_ns, now);\n        atomic_store(&b->last_touch_ns, now);\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, g_table, b->key, (unsigned)strlen(b->key), b);\n    }\n    return b;\n}\n\nstatic int rl_allocate_janitor(void);\n\n/* API ------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*\n * rl_init()\n *  Initialize subsystem. Safe to call multiple times;\n *  subsequent calls are no-ops and return 0.\n */\nint rl_init(uint32_t default_rps, uint32_t default_burst)\n{\n    bool expected = false;\n    if (!atomic_compare_exchange_strong(&g_rl_initialized, &expected, true))\n        return 0; /* already initialized */\n\n    if (default_rps > 0)   g_default_rps   = default_rps;\n    if (default_burst > 0) g_default_burst = default_burst;\n\n    if (pthread_mutex_init(&g_table_mtx, NULL) != 0)\n        return -1;\n\n    if (rl_allocate_janitor() != 0)\n        return -1;\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_set_override()\n *  Override RPS/Burst for a specific key (or route).\n */\nint rl_set_override(const char *key, uint32_t rps, uint32_t burst)\n{\n    if (!key) return -EINVAL;\n    size_t len = strnlen(key, RL_MAX_KEY);\n    if (len == 0 || len >= RL_MAX_KEY) return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *b = bucket_get_locked(key, len);\n    if (rps   > 0) b->rps   = rps;\n    if (burst > 0) b->burst = burst;\n    if (atomic_load(&b->tokens) > b->burst)\n        atomic_store(&b->tokens, b->burst);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_allow()\n *  Fast-path check \u2013 may be called on every request.\n *  Returns true if request should be allowed, false otherwise.\n */\nbool rl_allow(const char *key)\n{\n    if (!key) return true; /* no key => unlimited */\n\n    size_t len = strnlen(key, RL_MAX_KEY);\n    if (len == 0) return true;\n\n    rl_bucket_t *b;\n    /* First try read-only lookup. We accept small risk of race creating twice. */\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, key, (unsigned)len, b);\n    if (!b) {\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n        b = bucket_get_locked(key, len);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    }\n\n    uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n    bucket_refill(b, now);\n    atomic_store_explicit(&b->last_touch_ns, now, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    uint32_t cur = atomic_load_explicit(&b->tokens, memory_order_relaxed);\n    if (cur == 0) return false;\n\n    /* Attempt to consume one token. */\n    while (cur > 0) {\n        if (atomic_compare_exchange_weak(&b->tokens, &cur, cur - 1))\n            return true;\n    }\n    return false; /* contention lost, bucket exhausted */\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_metrics_prometheus()\n *  Emit text format metrics into supplied FILE* (already opened).\n */\nvoid rl_metrics_prometheus(FILE *out)\n{\n    fprintf(out,\n        \"# HELP mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available Current tokens per key\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available gauge\\n\");\n\n    rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, \"\", 0, b); /* noop to silence macro warning */\n    HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n        uint32_t tok = atomic_load(&b->tokens);\n        fprintf(out,\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available{key=\\\"%s\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\",\n            b->key, tok);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n        \"# HELP mm_rate_limiter_config Configured RPS and Burst\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_rate_limiter_config gauge\\n\");\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n        fprintf(out,\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_config{key=\\\"%s\\\",type=\\\"rps\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\"\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_config{key=\\\"%s\\\",type=\\\"burst\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\",\n            b->key, b->rps, b->key, b->burst);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n}\n\n/* Background janitor ---------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic void *janitor_thread(void *arg)\n{\n    (void)arg;\n    while (!atomic_load(&g_stop_janitor)) {\n        sleep(RL_JANITOR_INTERVAL_SEC);\n        uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n        rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n        HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n            uint64_t last_touch = atomic_load(&b->last_touch_ns);\n            if ((now - last_touch) > (uint64_t)RL_IDLE_TTL_SEC * 1000000000ull) {\n                HASH_DEL(hh, g_table, b);\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic int rl_allocate_janitor(void)\n{\n    if (pthread_create(&janitor_tid, NULL, janitor_thread, NULL) != 0)\n        return -1;\n    pthread_detach(janitor_tid);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_shutdown()\n *  Optional graceful shutdown (main process exit will reclaim memory anyway).\n */\nvoid rl_shutdown(void)\n{\n    atomic_store(&g_stop_janitor, true);\n}\n\n/* Self-test (debug builds) ---------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef RL_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\nstatic void busy_loop(const char *key, int n)\n{\n    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(key);\n        if (!ok) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"Rate limit hit @%d\\n\", i);\n            nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);\n        }\n    }\n}\nint main(void)\n{\n    rl_init(10, 10); /* 10 req/sec, burst 10 */\n    busy_loop(\"clientA\", 25);\n    FILE *f = fopen(\"/tmp/metrics.txt\", \"w\");\n    rl_metrics_prometheus(f);\n    fclose(f);\n    rl_shutdown();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* RL_SELFTEST */",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " #include <errno.h>",
        " #include <inttypes.h>",
        " ",
        "+#include \"logging.h\"",
        "+",
        " /* -------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "  * uthash \u2013 single-file open-source hash-map (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)",
        "  * A cut-down copy is embedded here to avoid extra build deps."
      ],

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_74.txt",
  "newString": "        if (!ok) {\n            LOG_WARN(\"Rate limit hit @%d\", i);\n            nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);\n        }",
  "oldString": "        if (!ok) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"Rate limit hit @%d\\n\", i);\n            nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);\n        }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ------------------------------------\n * Module: src/module_74.c   (Rate-Limiting Engine)\n *\n * Provides an in-process, thread-safe, token-bucket rate limiter that can be\n * used by any layer (HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, internal event bus, \u2026) to protect\n * endpoints against abusive traffic while guaranteeing deterministic latency.\n *\n *   \u2011 Per-key (API-Key, JWT subject, IP, Route) token buckets\n *   \u2011 Optional per-route overrides\n *   \u2011 Lock-free hot-path (single CAS) for maximum performance\n *   \u2011 Background janitor to purge idle buckets\n *   \u2011 Prometheus-compatible metric exposition\n *\n * Build:\n *   cc -std=c11 -O2 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -c src/module_74.c\n *\n * License: Apache-2.0\n */\n#define _GNU_SOURCE\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * uthash \u2013 single-file open-source hash-map (https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n * A cut-down copy is embedded here to avoid extra build deps.\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n/* Only what we need: HASH_ADD_KEYPTR, HASH_FIND, HASH_DEL */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz) malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz) free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh,head,keyptr,keylen,out)                                          \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        out = NULL;                                                                  \\\n        if (head) {                                                                  \\\n            unsigned _hf_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)keyptr)[0] & (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1; \\\n            struct UT_hash_handle *_hf_hh = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head; \\\n            while (_hf_hh) {                                                         \\\n                if ((_hf_hh->keylen == (unsigned)(keylen)) &&                        \\\n                    (memcmp(_hf_hh->key, keyptr, keylen) == 0)) {                    \\\n                    out = _hf_hh->prev; /* prev pointer is container */              \\\n                    break;                                                           \\\n                }                                                                    \\\n                _hf_hh = _hf_hh->hh_next;                                            \\\n            }                                                                        \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n    } while(0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh,head,keyptr,keylen_in,add)                                \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        unsigned _ha_bkt;                                                            \\\n        if (!(head)) {                                                               \\\n            (head)=add;                                                              \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl = (UT_hash_table*)uthash_malloc(sizeof(UT_hash_table));   \\\n            memset((head)->hh.tbl,0,sizeof(UT_hash_table));                          \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets = 512;                                       \\\n            (head)->hh.tbl->buckets =                                                \\\n              (UT_hash_bucket*)uthash_malloc(512*sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket));     \\\n            memset((head)->hh.tbl->buckets,0,512*sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket));     \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n        (add)->hh.key = (const void*)(keyptr);                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.keylen = (unsigned)(keylen_in);                                    \\\n        _ha_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)keyptr)[0] & ((head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1);\\\n        (add)->hh.hh_next = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head;                \\\n        (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head = &((add)->hh);                     \\\n        (add)->hh.prev = (add); /* back-pointer to struct (simplification) */        \\\n    } while(0)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(hh,head,delptr)                                                     \\\n    do {                                                                             \\\n        unsigned _hd_bkt = ((const unsigned char*)(delptr)->hh.key)[0] &             \\\n                           ((head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets-1);                          \\\n        struct UT_hash_handle *_hd_hh = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head;    \\\n        struct UT_hash_handle *_hd_prev = NULL;                                      \\\n        while (_hd_hh) {                                                             \\\n            if (_hd_hh == &((delptr)->hh)) {                                         \\\n                if (_hd_prev) _hd_prev->hh_next = _hd_hh->hh_next;                   \\\n                else head->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head = _hd_hh->hh_next;       \\\n                uthash_free(delptr, sizeof(*(delptr)));                              \\\n                break;                                                               \\\n            }                                                                        \\\n            _hd_prev = _hd_hh;                                                       \\\n            _hd_hh = _hd_hh->hh_next;                                                \\\n        }                                                                            \\\n    } while(0)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket *buckets;\n    unsigned num_buckets;\n} UT_hash_table;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    const void *key;\n    unsigned keylen;\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next;\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    /* custom stuff */\n    void *prev;          /* back-pointer to container struct (simplification) */\n} UT_hash_handle;\n#endif /* UTHASH_H */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#define RL_MAX_KEY      96u\n#define RL_DEFAULT_RPS  50u\n#define RL_DEFAULT_BURST (RL_DEFAULT_RPS * 2u)\n#define RL_IDLE_TTL_SEC  180u          /* remove buckets idle for >3min         */\n#define RL_JANITOR_INTERVAL_SEC 30u\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket {\n    char               key[RL_MAX_KEY];\n    uint32_t           rps;           /* tokens added per second    */\n    uint32_t           burst;         /* bucket capacity            */\n    _Atomic uint32_t   tokens;        /* current tokens (<=burst)   */\n    _Atomic uint64_t   last_ns;       /* last refill timestamp      */\n    _Atomic uint64_t   last_touch_ns; /* last time key was used     */\n    UT_hash_handle     hh;            /* uthash linkage             */\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\n/* Global state ----------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic rl_bucket_t *g_table = NULL;\nstatic pthread_mutex_t g_table_mtx = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER;\nstatic atomic_bool g_rl_initialized = false;\nstatic uint32_t g_default_rps   = RL_DEFAULT_RPS;\nstatic uint32_t g_default_burst = RL_DEFAULT_BURST;\nstatic atomic_bool g_stop_janitor = false;\nstatic pthread_t janitor_tid;\n\n/* Utility ---------------------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic inline uint64_t nsec_now(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return (uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000000000ull + (uint64_t)ts.tv_nsec;\n}\n\n/* Refill algorithm: lock-free, CAS on tokens & last_ns                        */\nstatic inline void bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *b, uint64_t now_ns)\n{\n    uint64_t last_ns = atomic_load_explicit(&b->last_ns, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    /* If another thread updated more recently, nothing to do.                 */\n    if (now_ns <= last_ns) return;\n\n    uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - last_ns;\n    uint32_t add_tokens = (uint32_t)((elapsed_ns * b->rps) / 1000000000ull);\n    if (add_tokens == 0) return;\n\n    /* Attempt to advance last_ns \u2013 if fails, another thread updated.          */\n    if (!atomic_compare_exchange_strong(&b->last_ns, &last_ns, now_ns))\n        return;\n\n    uint32_t cur = atomic_load_explicit(&b->tokens, memory_order_relaxed);\n    uint32_t new_val = cur + add_tokens;\n    if (new_val > b->burst) new_val = b->burst;\n    atomic_store_explicit(&b->tokens, new_val, memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* Find or create bucket \u2013 must hold g_table_mtx                               */\nstatic rl_bucket_t *bucket_get_locked(const char *key, size_t keylen)\n{\n    rl_bucket_t *b = NULL;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, key, (unsigned)keylen, b);\n    if (!b) {\n        b = calloc(1, sizeof(*b));\n        strncpy(b->key, key, RL_MAX_KEY - 1);\n        b->rps          = g_default_rps;\n        b->burst        = g_default_burst;\n        atomic_store(&b->tokens, b->burst);\n        uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n        atomic_store(&b->last_ns, now);\n        atomic_store(&b->last_touch_ns, now);\n        HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, g_table, b->key, (unsigned)strlen(b->key), b);\n    }\n    return b;\n}\n\nstatic int rl_allocate_janitor(void);\n\n/* API ------------------------------------------------------------------------*/\n/*\n * rl_init()\n *  Initialize subsystem. Safe to call multiple times;\n *  subsequent calls are no-ops and return 0.\n */\nint rl_init(uint32_t default_rps, uint32_t default_burst)\n{\n    bool expected = false;\n    if (!atomic_compare_exchange_strong(&g_rl_initialized, &expected, true))\n        return 0; /* already initialized */\n\n    if (default_rps > 0)   g_default_rps   = default_rps;\n    if (default_burst > 0) g_default_burst = default_burst;\n\n    if (pthread_mutex_init(&g_table_mtx, NULL) != 0)\n        return -1;\n\n    if (rl_allocate_janitor() != 0)\n        return -1;\n\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_set_override()\n *  Override RPS/Burst for a specific key (or route).\n */\nint rl_set_override(const char *key, uint32_t rps, uint32_t burst)\n{\n    if (!key) return -EINVAL;\n    size_t len = strnlen(key, RL_MAX_KEY);\n    if (len == 0 || len >= RL_MAX_KEY) return -EINVAL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *b = bucket_get_locked(key, len);\n    if (rps   > 0) b->rps   = rps;\n    if (burst > 0) b->burst = burst;\n    if (atomic_load(&b->tokens) > b->burst)\n        atomic_store(&b->tokens, b->burst);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_allow()\n *  Fast-path check \u2013 may be called on every request.\n *  Returns true if request should be allowed, false otherwise.\n */\nbool rl_allow(const char *key)\n{\n    if (!key) return true; /* no key => unlimited */\n\n    size_t len = strnlen(key, RL_MAX_KEY);\n    if (len == 0) return true;\n\n    rl_bucket_t *b;\n    /* First try read-only lookup. We accept small risk of race creating twice. */\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, key, (unsigned)len, b);\n    if (!b) {\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n        b = bucket_get_locked(key, len);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    }\n\n    uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n    bucket_refill(b, now);\n    atomic_store_explicit(&b->last_touch_ns, now, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    uint32_t cur = atomic_load_explicit(&b->tokens, memory_order_relaxed);\n    if (cur == 0) return false;\n\n    /* Attempt to consume one token. */\n    while (cur > 0) {\n        if (atomic_compare_exchange_weak(&b->tokens, &cur, cur - 1))\n            return true;\n    }\n    return false; /* contention lost, bucket exhausted */\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_metrics_prometheus()\n *  Emit text format metrics into supplied FILE* (already opened).\n */\nvoid rl_metrics_prometheus(FILE *out)\n{\n    fprintf(out,\n        \"# HELP mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available Current tokens per key\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available gauge\\n\");\n\n    rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    HASH_FIND(hh, g_table, \"\", 0, b); /* noop to silence macro warning */\n    HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n        uint32_t tok = atomic_load(&b->tokens);\n        fprintf(out,\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_tokens_available{key=\\\"%s\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\",\n            b->key, tok);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n\n    fprintf(out,\n        \"# HELP mm_rate_limiter_config Configured RPS and Burst\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE mm_rate_limiter_config gauge\\n\");\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n    HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n        fprintf(out,\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_config{key=\\\"%s\\\",type=\\\"rps\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\"\n            \"mm_rate_limiter_config{key=\\\"%s\\\",type=\\\"burst\\\"} %\" PRIu32 \"\\n\",\n            b->key, b->rps, b->key, b->burst);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n}\n\n/* Background janitor ---------------------------------------------------------*/\nstatic void *janitor_thread(void *arg)\n{\n    (void)arg;\n    while (!atomic_load(&g_stop_janitor)) {\n        sleep(RL_JANITOR_INTERVAL_SEC);\n        uint64_t now = nsec_now();\n        rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&g_table_mtx);\n        HASH_ITER(hh, g_table, b, tmp) {\n            uint64_t last_touch = atomic_load(&b->last_touch_ns);\n            if ((now - last_touch) > (uint64_t)RL_IDLE_TTL_SEC * 1000000000ull) {\n                HASH_DEL(hh, g_table, b);\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_table_mtx);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nstatic int rl_allocate_janitor(void)\n{\n    if (pthread_create(&janitor_tid, NULL, janitor_thread, NULL) != 0)\n        return -1;\n    pthread_detach(janitor_tid);\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/*\n * rl_shutdown()\n *  Optional graceful shutdown (main process exit will reclaim memory anyway).\n */\nvoid rl_shutdown(void)\n{\n    atomic_store(&g_stop_janitor, true);\n}\n\n/* Self-test (debug builds) ---------------------------------------------------*/\n#ifdef RL_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\nstatic void busy_loop(const char *key, int n)\n{\n    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(key);\n        if (!ok) {\n            fprintf(stderr, \"Rate limit hit @%d\\n\", i);\n            nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);\n        }\n    }\n}\nint main(void)\n{\n    rl_init(10, 10); /* 10 req/sec, burst 10 */\n    busy_loop(\"clientA\", 25);\n    FILE *f = fopen(\"/tmp/metrics.txt\", \"w\");\n    rl_metrics_prometheus(f);\n    fclose(f);\n    rl_shutdown();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* RL_SELFTEST */",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {",
        "         bool ok = rl_allow(key);",
        "         if (!ok) {",

        "+            LOG_WARN(\"Rate limit hit @%d\", i);",
        "             nanosleep(&(struct timespec){.tv_sec = 0, .tv_nsec = 10000000}, NULL);",
        "         }",
        "     }"
      ],

    }

113. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_20.txt",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```c\n/******************************************************************************************\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  Module: Rate Limiter (src/module_20.c)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe token-bucket rate limiter with per-key quotas, automatic window rollover,\n *      periodic cleanup of idle buckets, and Prometheus-compatible metrics hooks.\n *\n *  Notes:\n *      \u2013 Designed for high-QPS internal use (shared in-process across HTTP and GraphQL\n *        gateways).  \n *      \u2013 Uses uthash (single header BSD-licensed hash-map implementation).\n *\n *  Build flags:\n *      gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c src/module_20.c\n ******************************************************************************************/\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* uthash (condensed)                                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)          malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz)        free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)   /* silent */\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)     /* silent */\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\\n        const unsigned _hf_keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);        \\\n        unsigned _hf_i, _hf_hash = 5381U;                                      \\\n        for (_hf_i = 0; _hf_i < _hf_keylen; _hf_i++)                           \\\n            _hf_hash = (_hf_hash * 33U) ^ _hf_key[_hf_i];                      \\\n        (hashv) = _hf_hash;                                                    \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG,\n    LOG_INFO,\n    LOG_WARN,\n    LOG_ERROR,\n    LOG_FATAL\n} log_level_t;\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL LOG_INFO\n#endif\n\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = {\"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\",\n                                      \"WARN\",  \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\"};\n\nstatic inline void mercury_log(log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    if (lvl < MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL) return;\n\n    char tsbuf[32];\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    gmtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    strftime(tsbuf, sizeof tsbuf, \"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout,\n            \"{\\\"ts\\\":\\\"%s.%03ldZ\\\",\\\"lvl\\\":\\\"%s\\\",\\\"msg\\\":\\\"\",\n            tsbuf, ts.tv_nsec / 1000000L, LOG_LEVEL_STR[lvl]);\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    fputs(\"\\\"}\\n\", (lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n    fflush((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Prometheus metric callbacks stubs                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef void (*metric_inc_fn)(const char *name, const char *key_label);\ntypedef void (*metric_gauge_fn)(const char *name, double value);\n\nstatic void noop_inc(const char *name, const char *key_label)   { (void)name; (void)key_label; }",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_20.txt",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 311
  },
  "type": "text"
}

115. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_20.txt",
  "new_string": "#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers - mapped to centralized logging          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE_LEGACY = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY,\n    LOG_INFO_LEGACY,\n    LOG_WARN_LEGACY,\n    LOG_ERROR_LEGACY,\n    LOG_FATAL_LEGACY\n} log_level_t;\n\n#define mercury_log(lvl, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if ((lvl) == LOG_ERROR_LEGACY || (lvl) == LOG_FATAL_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_WARN_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_INFO_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)",
  "old_string": "#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG,\n    LOG_INFO,\n    LOG_WARN,\n    LOG_ERROR,\n    LOG_FATAL\n} log_level_t;\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL LOG_INFO\n#endif\n\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = {\"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\",\n                                      \"WARN\",  \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\"};\n\nstatic inline void mercury_log(log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    if (lvl < MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL) return;\n\n    char tsbuf[32];\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    gmtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    strftime(tsbuf, sizeof tsbuf, \"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout,\n            \"{\\\"ts\\\":\\\"%s.%03ldZ\\\",\\\"lvl\\\":\\\"%s\\\",\\\"msg\\\":\\\"\",\n            tsbuf, ts.tv_nsec / 1000000L, LOG_LEVEL_STR[lvl]);\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    fputs(\"\\\"}\\n\", (lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n    fflush((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_20.txt",
  "newString": "#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers - mapped to centralized logging          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE_LEGACY = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY,\n    LOG_INFO_LEGACY,\n    LOG_WARN_LEGACY,\n    LOG_ERROR_LEGACY,\n    LOG_FATAL_LEGACY\n} log_level_t;\n\n#define mercury_log(lvl, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if ((lvl) == LOG_ERROR_LEGACY || (lvl) == LOG_FATAL_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_WARN_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_INFO_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)",
  "oldString": "#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG,\n    LOG_INFO,\n    LOG_WARN,\n    LOG_ERROR,\n    LOG_FATAL\n} log_level_t;\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL LOG_INFO\n#endif\n\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = {\"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\",\n                                      \"WARN\",  \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\"};\n\nstatic inline void mercury_log(log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    if (lvl < MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL) return;\n\n    char tsbuf[32];\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    gmtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    strftime(tsbuf, sizeof tsbuf, \"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout,\n            \"{\\\"ts\\\":\\\"%s.%03ldZ\\\",\\\"lvl\\\":\\\"%s\\\",\\\"msg\\\":\\\"\",\n            tsbuf, ts.tv_nsec / 1000000L, LOG_LEVEL_STR[lvl]);\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    fputs(\"\\\"}\\n\", (lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n    fflush((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n}",
  "originalFile": "```c\n/******************************************************************************************\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  Module: Rate Limiter (src/module_20.c)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe token-bucket rate limiter with per-key quotas, automatic window rollover,\n *      periodic cleanup of idle buckets, and Prometheus-compatible metrics hooks.\n *\n *  Notes:\n *      \u2013 Designed for high-QPS internal use (shared in-process across HTTP and GraphQL\n *        gateways).  \n *      \u2013 Uses uthash (single header BSD-licensed hash-map implementation).\n *\n *  Build flags:\n *      gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c src/module_20.c\n ******************************************************************************************/\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* uthash (condensed)                                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)          malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz)        free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)   /* silent */\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)     /* silent */\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\\n        const unsigned _hf_keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);        \\\n        unsigned _hf_i, _hf_hash = 5381U;                                      \\\n        for (_hf_i = 0; _hf_i < _hf_keylen; _hf_i++)                           \\\n            _hf_hash = (_hf_hash * 33U) ^ _hf_key[_hf_i];                      \\\n        (hashv) = _hf_hash;                                                    \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG,\n    LOG_INFO,\n    LOG_WARN,\n    LOG_ERROR,\n    LOG_FATAL\n} log_level_t;\n\n#ifndef MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL\n#define MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL LOG_INFO\n#endif\n\nstatic const char *LOG_LEVEL_STR[] = {\"TRACE\", \"DEBUG\", \"INFO\",\n                                      \"WARN\",  \"ERROR\", \"FATAL\"};\n\nstatic inline void mercury_log(log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    if (lvl < MERCURY_LOG_LEVEL) return;\n\n    char tsbuf[32];\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    gmtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    strftime(tsbuf, sizeof tsbuf, \"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout,\n            \"{\\\"ts\\\":\\\"%s.%03ldZ\\\",\\\"lvl\\\":\\\"%s\\\",\\\"msg\\\":\\\"\",\n            tsbuf, ts.tv_nsec / 1000000L, LOG_LEVEL_STR[lvl]);\n\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout, fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n\n    fputs(\"\\\"}\\n\", (lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n    fflush((lvl >= LOG_ERROR) ? stderr : stdout);\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Prometheus metric callbacks stubs                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef void (*metric_inc_fn)(const char *name, const char *key_label);\ntypedef void (*metric_gauge_fn)(const char *name, double value);\n\nstatic void noop_inc(const char *name, const char *key_label)   { (void)name; (void)key_label; }\nstatic void noop_gauge(const char *name, double value)          { (void)name; (void)value; }\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Rate Limiter                                                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct {\n    uint32_t                capacity;           /* tokens per window            */\n    uint32_t                refill_amount;      /* tokens to add per window     */\n    uint32_t                window_sec;         /* window length (seconds)      */\n    uint32_t                idle_ttl_sec;       /* remove bucket after ttl      */\n    metric_inc_fn           m_inc;              /* increment counter hook       */\n    metric_gauge_fn         m_gauge;            /* gauge hook                   */\n} rl_config_t;\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket_s {\n    char                    key[64];            /* client identifier            */\n    uint32_t                tokens;             /* remaining tokens             */\n    time_t                  window_start_epoch; /* start of current window      */\n    UT_hash_handle          hh;\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    rl_config_t             cfg;\n    rl_bucket_t            *buckets;\n    pthread_mutex_t         mtx;\n    pthread_t               reaper_thread;\n    atomic_bool             stop_reaper;\n} rate_limiter_t;\n\n/* --------------------------- internal helpers ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void rl_bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *b, time_t now, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    /* Determine how many whole windows have passed and refill accordingly */\n    uint32_t elapsed_windows = (uint32_t)((now - b->window_start_epoch) / cfg->window_sec);\n    if (elapsed_windows > 0) {\n        uint64_t new_tokens = (uint64_t)b->tokens +\n                              (uint64_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->refill_amount;\n        if (new_tokens > cfg->capacity) new_tokens = cfg->capacity;\n        b->tokens = (uint32_t)new_tokens;\n        b->window_start_epoch += (time_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->window_sec;\n    }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------- public interface ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void *rl_reaper_main(void *arg)\n{\n    rate_limiter_t *rl = arg;\n    const uint32_t sleep_sec = rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec ? rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec : 30;\n\n    while (!atomic_load(&rl->stop_reaper)) {\n        sleep(sleep_sec);\n\n        time_t now = time(NULL);\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n        rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n        {\n            if ((now - b->window_start_epoch) > rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec) {\n                HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n                mercury_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"GC bucket key=%s\", b->key);\n                rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n                free(b);\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nint rl_init(rate_limiter_t *rl, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    if (!rl || !cfg) return EINVAL;\n\n    *rl = (rate_limiter_t){\n        .cfg       = *cfg,\n        .buckets   = NULL,\n        .mtx       = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER,\n        .stop_reaper = ATOMIC_VAR_INIT(false)\n    };\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_inc)   rl->cfg.m_inc   = noop_inc;\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_gauge) rl->cfg.m_gauge = noop_gauge;\n\n    /* Spawn background reaper */\n    int rc = pthread_create(&rl->reaper_thread, NULL, rl_reaper_main, rl);\n    if (rc != 0) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"Failed to spawn rate-limiter reaper: %s\", strerror(rc));\n        return rc;\n    }\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid rl_destroy(rate_limiter_t *rl)\n{\n    if (!rl) return;\n\n    atomic_store(&rl->stop_reaper, true);\n    pthread_join(rl->reaper_thread, NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n        free(b);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&rl->mtx);\n}\n\nbool rl_allow(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return false;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    bool allowed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n\n    if (!bucket) {\n        bucket = calloc(1, sizeof *bucket);\n        if (!bucket) {\n            mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"rate limiter OOM\");\n            goto out;\n        }\n        strncpy(bucket->key, key, sizeof(bucket->key) - 1);\n        bucket->tokens = rl->cfg.capacity;\n        bucket->window_start_epoch = now;\n        HASH_ADD_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n        rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n    }\n\n    rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n\n    if (bucket->tokens > 0) {\n        bucket->tokens--;\n        allowed = true;\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_allow_total\", key);\n    } else {\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_deny_total\", key);\n    }\n\nout:\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return allowed;\n}\n\nuint32_t rl_remaining(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return 0;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    uint32_t remaining = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n    if (bucket) {\n        rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n        remaining = bucket->tokens;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return remaining;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Example usage (unit-test style)                                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifdef RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST\n#include <signal.h>\n\nstatic volatile bool quit = false;\nstatic void on_sig(int _) { (void)_; quit = true; }\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    signal(SIGINT, on_sig);\n\n    rl_config_t cfg = {\n        .capacity       = 5,\n        .refill_amount  = 5,\n        .window_sec     = 10,\n        .idle_ttl_sec   = 30\n    };\n    rate_limiter_t rl;\n    if (rl_init(&rl, &cfg) != 0) {\n        fputs(\"Failed to init rate limiter\\n\", stderr);\n        return EXIT_FAILURE;\n    }\n\n    const char *client = \"client-123\";\n\n    while (!quit) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(&rl, client);\n        printf(\"[%ld] Request %s (remaining=%u)\\n\",\n               time(NULL), ok ? \"ALLOWED\" : \"DENIED\",\n               rl_remaining(&rl, client));\n        sleep(1);\n    }\n\n    rl_destroy(&rl);\n    return EXIT_SUCCESS;\n}\n#endif /* RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST */\n```",
  "replaceAll": false,

  "originalFile": "```c\n/******************************************************************************************\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  Module: Rate Limiter (src/module_20.c)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe token-bucket rate limiter with per-key quotas, automatic window rollover,\n *      periodic cleanup of idle buckets, and Prometheus-compatible metrics hooks.\n *\n *  Notes:\n *      \u2013 Designed for high-QPS internal use (shared in-process across HTTP and GraphQL\n *        gateways).  \n *      \u2013 Uses uthash (single header BSD-licensed hash-map implementation).\n *\n *  Build flags:\n *      gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c src/module_20.c\n ******************************************************************************************/\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* uthash (condensed)                                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)          malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz)        free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)   /* silent */\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)     /* silent */\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\\n        const unsigned _hf_keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);        \\\n        unsigned _hf_i, _hf_hash = 5381U;                                      \\\n        for (_hf_i = 0; _hf_i < _hf_keylen; _hf_i++)                           \\\n            _hf_hash = (_hf_hash * 33U) ^ _hf_key[_hf_i];                      \\\n        (hashv) = _hf_hash;                                                    \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers - mapped to centralized logging          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE_LEGACY = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY,\n    LOG_INFO_LEGACY,\n    LOG_WARN_LEGACY,\n    LOG_ERROR_LEGACY,\n    LOG_FATAL_LEGACY\n} log_level_t;\n\n#define mercury_log(lvl, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if ((lvl) == LOG_ERROR_LEGACY || (lvl) == LOG_FATAL_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_WARN_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_INFO_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Prometheus metric callbacks stubs                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef void (*metric_inc_fn)(const char *name, const char *key_label);\ntypedef void (*metric_gauge_fn)(const char *name, double value);\n\nstatic void noop_inc(const char *name, const char *key_label)   { (void)name; (void)key_label; }\nstatic void noop_gauge(const char *name, double value)          { (void)name; (void)value; }\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Rate Limiter                                                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct {\n    uint32_t                capacity;           /* tokens per window            */\n    uint32_t                refill_amount;      /* tokens to add per window     */\n    uint32_t                window_sec;         /* window length (seconds)      */\n    uint32_t                idle_ttl_sec;       /* remove bucket after ttl      */\n    metric_inc_fn           m_inc;              /* increment counter hook       */\n    metric_gauge_fn         m_gauge;            /* gauge hook                   */\n} rl_config_t;\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket_s {\n    char                    key[64];            /* client identifier            */\n    uint32_t                tokens;             /* remaining tokens             */\n    time_t                  window_start_epoch; /* start of current window      */\n    UT_hash_handle          hh;\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    rl_config_t             cfg;\n    rl_bucket_t            *buckets;\n    pthread_mutex_t         mtx;\n    pthread_t               reaper_thread;\n    atomic_bool             stop_reaper;\n} rate_limiter_t;\n\n/* --------------------------- internal helpers ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void rl_bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *b, time_t now, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    /* Determine how many whole windows have passed and refill accordingly */\n    uint32_t elapsed_windows = (uint32_t)((now - b->window_start_epoch) / cfg->window_sec);\n    if (elapsed_windows > 0) {\n        uint64_t new_tokens = (uint64_t)b->tokens +\n                              (uint64_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->refill_amount;\n        if (new_tokens > cfg->capacity) new_tokens = cfg->capacity;\n        b->tokens = (uint32_t)new_tokens;\n        b->window_start_epoch += (time_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->window_sec;\n    }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------- public interface ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void *rl_reaper_main(void *arg)\n{\n    rate_limiter_t *rl = arg;\n    const uint32_t sleep_sec = rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec ? rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec : 30;\n\n    while (!atomic_load(&rl->stop_reaper)) {\n        sleep(sleep_sec);\n\n        time_t now = time(NULL);\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n        rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n        {\n            if ((now - b->window_start_epoch) > rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec) {\n                HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n                mercury_log(LOG_DEBUG, \"GC bucket key=%s\", b->key);\n                rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n                free(b);\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nint rl_init(rate_limiter_t *rl, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    if (!rl || !cfg) return EINVAL;\n\n    *rl = (rate_limiter_t){\n        .cfg       = *cfg,\n        .buckets   = NULL,\n        .mtx       = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER,\n        .stop_reaper = ATOMIC_VAR_INIT(false)\n    };\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_inc)   rl->cfg.m_inc   = noop_inc;\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_gauge) rl->cfg.m_gauge = noop_gauge;\n\n    /* Spawn background reaper */\n    int rc = pthread_create(&rl->reaper_thread, NULL, rl_reaper_main, rl);\n    if (rc != 0) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"Failed to spawn rate-limiter reaper: %s\", strerror(rc));\n        return rc;\n    }\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid rl_destroy(rate_limiter_t *rl)\n{\n    if (!rl) return;\n\n    atomic_store(&rl->stop_reaper, true);\n    pthread_join(rl->reaper_thread, NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n        free(b);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&rl->mtx);\n}\n\nbool rl_allow(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return false;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    bool allowed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n\n    if (!bucket) {\n        bucket = calloc(1, sizeof *bucket);\n        if (!bucket) {\n            mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"rate limiter OOM\");\n            goto out;\n        }\n        strncpy(bucket->key, key, sizeof(bucket->key) - 1);\n        bucket->tokens = rl->cfg.capacity;\n        bucket->window_start_epoch = now;\n        HASH_ADD_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n        rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n    }\n\n    rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n\n    if (bucket->tokens > 0) {\n        bucket->tokens--;\n        allowed = true;\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_allow_total\", key);\n    } else {\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_deny_total\", key);\n    }\n\nout:\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return allowed;\n}\n\nuint32_t rl_remaining(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return 0;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    uint32_t remaining = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n    if (bucket) {\n        rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n        remaining = bucket->tokens;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return remaining;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Example usage (unit-test style)                                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifdef RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST\n#include <signal.h>\n\nstatic volatile bool quit = false;\nstatic void on_sig(int _) { (void)_; quit = true; }\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    signal(SIGINT, on_sig);\n\n    rl_config_t cfg = {\n        .capacity       = 5,\n        .refill_amount  = 5,\n        .window_sec     = 10,\n        .idle_ttl_sec   = 30\n    };\n    rate_limiter_t rl;\n    if (rl_init(&rl, &cfg) != 0) {\n        fputs(\"Failed to init rate limiter\\n\", stderr);\n        return EXIT_FAILURE;\n    }\n\n    const char *client = \"client-123\";\n\n    while (!quit) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(&rl, client);\n        printf(\"[%ld] Request %s (remaining=%u)\\n\",\n               time(NULL), ok ? \"ALLOWED\" : \"DENIED\",\n               rl_remaining(&rl, client));\n        sleep(1);\n    }\n\n    rl_destroy(&rl);\n    return EXIT_SUCCESS;\n}\n#endif /* RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST */\n```",
  "replaceAll": true,
