  "originalFile": "```c\n/******************************************************************************************\n *  MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n *  Module: Rate Limiter (src/module_20.c)\n *\n *  Description:\n *      Thread-safe token-bucket rate limiter with per-key quotas, automatic window rollover,\n *      periodic cleanup of idle buckets, and Prometheus-compatible metrics hooks.\n *\n *  Notes:\n *      \u2013 Designed for high-QPS internal use (shared in-process across HTTP and GraphQL\n *        gateways).  \n *      \u2013 Uses uthash (single header BSD-licensed hash-map implementation).\n *\n *  Build flags:\n *      gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -pthread -c src/module_20.c\n ******************************************************************************************/\n#define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n#include <unistd.h>\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* uthash (condensed)                                                         */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define uthash_malloc(sz)          malloc(sz)\n#define uthash_free(ptr,sz)        free(ptr)\n#define uthash_noexpand_fyi(tbl)   /* silent */\n#define uthash_expand_fyi(tbl)     /* silent */\n#define HASH_FUNCTION(keyptr, keylen, hashv)                                   \\\n    do {                                                                       \\\n        const unsigned _hf_keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);        \\\n        unsigned _hf_i, _hf_hash = 5381U;                                      \\\n        for (_hf_i = 0; _hf_i < _hf_keylen; _hf_i++)                           \\\n            _hf_hash = (_hf_hash * 33U) ^ _hf_key[_hf_i];                      \\\n        (hashv) = _hf_hash;                                                    \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#include \"uthash.h\"  /* Expect this header to be on include path */\n#include \"logging.h\"\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Simple structured logging helpers - mapped to centralized logging          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef enum {\n    LOG_TRACE_LEGACY = 0,\n    LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY,\n    LOG_INFO_LEGACY,\n    LOG_WARN_LEGACY,\n    LOG_ERROR_LEGACY,\n    LOG_FATAL_LEGACY\n} log_level_t;\n\n#define mercury_log(lvl, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if ((lvl) == LOG_ERROR_LEGACY || (lvl) == LOG_FATAL_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_WARN_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if ((lvl) == LOG_INFO_LEGACY) \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Prometheus metric callbacks stubs                                          */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\ntypedef void (*metric_inc_fn)(const char *name, const char *key_label);\ntypedef void (*metric_gauge_fn)(const char *name, double value);\n\nstatic void noop_inc(const char *name, const char *key_label)   { (void)name; (void)key_label; }\nstatic void noop_gauge(const char *name, double value)          { (void)name; (void)value; }\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Rate Limiter                                                               */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\ntypedef struct {\n    uint32_t                capacity;           /* tokens per window            */\n    uint32_t                refill_amount;      /* tokens to add per window     */\n    uint32_t                window_sec;         /* window length (seconds)      */\n    uint32_t                idle_ttl_sec;       /* remove bucket after ttl      */\n    metric_inc_fn           m_inc;              /* increment counter hook       */\n    metric_gauge_fn         m_gauge;            /* gauge hook                   */\n} rl_config_t;\n\ntypedef struct rl_bucket_s {\n    char                    key[64];            /* client identifier            */\n    uint32_t                tokens;             /* remaining tokens             */\n    time_t                  window_start_epoch; /* start of current window      */\n    UT_hash_handle          hh;\n} rl_bucket_t;\n\ntypedef struct {\n    rl_config_t             cfg;\n    rl_bucket_t            *buckets;\n    pthread_mutex_t         mtx;\n    pthread_t               reaper_thread;\n    atomic_bool             stop_reaper;\n} rate_limiter_t;\n\n/* --------------------------- internal helpers ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void rl_bucket_refill(rl_bucket_t *b, time_t now, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    /* Determine how many whole windows have passed and refill accordingly */\n    uint32_t elapsed_windows = (uint32_t)((now - b->window_start_epoch) / cfg->window_sec);\n    if (elapsed_windows > 0) {\n        uint64_t new_tokens = (uint64_t)b->tokens +\n                              (uint64_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->refill_amount;\n        if (new_tokens > cfg->capacity) new_tokens = cfg->capacity;\n        b->tokens = (uint32_t)new_tokens;\n        b->window_start_epoch += (time_t)elapsed_windows * cfg->window_sec;\n    }\n}\n\n/* -------------------------- public interface ---------------------------- */\n\nstatic void *rl_reaper_main(void *arg)\n{\n    rate_limiter_t *rl = arg;\n    const uint32_t sleep_sec = rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec ? rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec : 30;\n\n    while (!atomic_load(&rl->stop_reaper)) {\n        sleep(sleep_sec);\n\n        time_t now = time(NULL);\n        pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n        rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n        HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n        {\n            if ((now - b->window_start_epoch) > rl->cfg.idle_ttl_sec) {\n                HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n                mercury_log(LOG_DEBUG_LEGACY, \"GC bucket key=%s\", b->key);\n                rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n                free(b);\n            }\n        }\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    }\n    return NULL;\n}\n\nint rl_init(rate_limiter_t *rl, const rl_config_t *cfg)\n{\n    if (!rl || !cfg) return EINVAL;\n\n    *rl = (rate_limiter_t){\n        .cfg       = *cfg,\n        .buckets   = NULL,\n        .mtx       = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER,\n        .stop_reaper = ATOMIC_VAR_INIT(false)\n    };\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_inc)   rl->cfg.m_inc   = noop_inc;\n    if (!rl->cfg.m_gauge) rl->cfg.m_gauge = noop_gauge;\n\n    /* Spawn background reaper */\n    int rc = pthread_create(&rl->reaper_thread, NULL, rl_reaper_main, rl);\n    if (rc != 0) {\n        mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"Failed to spawn rate-limiter reaper: %s\", strerror(rc));\n        return rc;\n    }\n    return 0;\n}\n\nvoid rl_destroy(rate_limiter_t *rl)\n{\n    if (!rl) return;\n\n    atomic_store(&rl->stop_reaper, true);\n    pthread_join(rl->reaper_thread, NULL);\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *b, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, rl->buckets, b, tmp)\n    {\n        HASH_DEL(rl->buckets, b);\n        free(b);\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&rl->mtx);\n}\n\nbool rl_allow(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return false;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    bool allowed = false;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n\n    if (!bucket) {\n        bucket = calloc(1, sizeof *bucket);\n        if (!bucket) {\n            mercury_log(LOG_ERROR, \"rate limiter OOM\");\n            goto out;\n        }\n        strncpy(bucket->key, key, sizeof(bucket->key) - 1);\n        bucket->tokens = rl->cfg.capacity;\n        bucket->window_start_epoch = now;\n        HASH_ADD_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n        rl->cfg.m_gauge(\"mercury_rl_buckets\", (double)HASH_COUNT(rl->buckets));\n    }\n\n    rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n\n    if (bucket->tokens > 0) {\n        bucket->tokens--;\n        allowed = true;\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_allow_total\", key);\n    } else {\n        rl->cfg.m_inc(\"mercury_rl_deny_total\", key);\n    }\n\nout:\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return allowed;\n}\n\nuint32_t rl_remaining(rate_limiter_t *rl, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!rl || !key) return 0;\n\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    uint32_t remaining = 0;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&rl->mtx);\n    rl_bucket_t *bucket;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(rl->buckets, key, bucket);\n    if (bucket) {\n        rl_bucket_refill(bucket, now, &rl->cfg);\n        remaining = bucket->tokens;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&rl->mtx);\n    return remaining;\n}\n\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Example usage (unit-test style)                                            */\n/* -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#ifdef RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST\n#include <signal.h>\n\nstatic volatile bool quit = false;\nstatic void on_sig(int _) { (void)_; quit = true; }\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    signal(SIGINT, on_sig);\n\n    rl_config_t cfg = {\n        .capacity       = 5,\n        .refill_amount  = 5,\n        .window_sec     = 10,\n        .idle_ttl_sec   = 30\n    };\n    rate_limiter_t rl;\n    if (rl_init(&rl, &cfg) != 0) {\n        fputs(\"Failed to init rate limiter\\n\", stderr);\n        return EXIT_FAILURE;\n    }\n\n    const char *client = \"client-123\";\n\n    while (!quit) {\n        bool ok = rl_allow(&rl, client);\n        printf(\"[%ld] Request %s (remaining=%u)\\n\",\n               time(NULL), ok ? \"ALLOWED\" : \"DENIED\",\n               rl_remaining(&rl, client));\n        sleep(1);\n    }\n\n    rl_destroy(&rl);\n    return EXIT_SUCCESS;\n}\n#endif /* RATE_LIMITER_SELFTEST */\n```",
  "replaceAll": true,

118. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_35.txt",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------------------\n * Module 35: Response Cache (in-memory LRU with TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread-safe, instrumented response cache that\n * can be shared by all presentation-layer handlers (REST, GraphQL, gRPC).\n *\n * Features\n *  - O(1) look-ups via hash table (uthash)\n *  - Probabilistic LRU eviction (clock-hand algorithm)\n *  - Per-entry TTL with monotonic clock checks\n *  - Metrics hooks for Prometheus exporter\n *  - Structured logging with severity levels\n *  - Zero external allocations on hot path (small-buffer optimization)\n *\n * NOTE: This file purposefully does not expose HTTP specifics.  Serialisation\n * and ownership of payload memory remain responsibilities of callers.  The\n * cache merely stores opaque byte buffers.\n */\n\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Compile-time tuning knobs                                                 *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Size at which we stop using the small-buffer optimisation and malloc. */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD\n#define MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD 256U\n#endif\n\n/* How aggressive the eviction clock moves on every set (0-100%). */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE\n#define MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE 10U\n#endif\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);\nstatic void           log_debug(const char *fmt, ...);\nstatic void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * uthash                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_35.txt",
    "numLines": 60,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 632
  },
  "type": "text"
}
119. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_35.txt",
  "limit": 40,
  "offset": 200
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "    MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG,\n} mmc_cache_status_t;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Helpers                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Returns the current monotonic time in milliseconds */\nstatic uint64_t monotonic_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\n/* Trivial logger (placeholder for full logging framework) */\n#include <stdarg.h>\nstatic void vlog(const char *lvl, const char *fmt, va_list ap)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"%s [%s] \", lvl, MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n}\n\nstatic void log_debug(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n#ifndef NDEBUG\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"DEBUG\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void log_error(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"ERROR\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_35.txt",
    "numLines": 40,
    "startLine": 200,
    "totalLines": 632
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_35.txt",
  "newString": "#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"",
  "oldString": "#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------------------\n * Module 35: Response Cache (in-memory LRU with TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread-safe, instrumented response cache that\n * can be shared by all presentation-layer handlers (REST, GraphQL, gRPC).\n *\n * Features\n *  - O(1) look-ups via hash table (uthash)\n *  - Probabilistic LRU eviction (clock-hand algorithm)\n *  - Per-entry TTL with monotonic clock checks\n *  - Metrics hooks for Prometheus exporter\n *  - Structured logging with severity levels\n *  - Zero external allocations on hot path (small-buffer optimization)\n *\n * NOTE: This file purposefully does not expose HTTP specifics.  Serialisation\n * and ownership of payload memory remain responsibilities of callers.  The\n * cache merely stores opaque byte buffers.\n */\n\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Compile-time tuning knobs                                                 *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Size at which we stop using the small-buffer optimisation and malloc. */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD\n#define MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD 256U\n#endif\n\n/* How aggressive the eviction clock moves on every set (0-100%). */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE\n#define MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE 10U\n#endif\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);\nstatic void           log_debug(const char *fmt, ...);\nstatic void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * uthash                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*\n * We vendor a cut-down copy of uthash 2.3.0 to avoid an external dependency.\n * Only the macros strictly required for this file have been retained.\n */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n\n#define uthash_fatal(msg) exit(-1)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, out)                                \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hf_bkt;                                                       \\\n        out = NULL;                                                             \\\n        if (head) {                                                             \\\n            HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hf_bkt);     \\\n            out = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head;                     \\\n            while (out) {                                                       \\\n                if ((out)->hh.keylen == keylen &&                               \\\n                    memcmp(out->hh.key, keyptr, keylen) == 0) {                 \\\n                    break;                                                      \\\n                }                                                               \\\n                out = (out)->hh_next;                                           \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, add)                          \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        uint32_t _ha_bkt;                                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.key = (char *)(keyptr);                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                                  \\\n        if (!(head)) {                                                          \\\n            (head) = (add);                                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl = &((struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)))[0]; \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->num_buckets = 1;                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->buckets = (struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)); \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _ha_bkt);         \\\n        (add)->hh_next = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head;              \\\n        (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head = (UT_hash_handle *)(add);     \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(hh, head, delptr)                                              \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hd_bkt;                                                       \\\n        if ((head)) {                                                           \\\n            HASH_FCN((delptr)->hh.key, (delptr)->hh.keylen,                     \\\n                      (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hd_bkt);                    \\\n            if ((head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head == (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) { \\\n                (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head = (delptr)->hh_next;   \\\n            } else {                                                            \\\n                UT_hash_handle *_tmp = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head;\\\n                while (_tmp->hh_next && _tmp->hh_next != (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) \\\n                    _tmp = _tmp->hh_next;                                       \\\n                if (_tmp->hh_next)                                              \\\n                    _tmp->hh_next = (delptr)->hh_next;                          \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    void                 *prev;   /* previous element in app order      */\n    void                 *next;   /* next element in app order          */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_prev; /* previous element in bucket order */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next; /* next element in bucket order     */\n    const void           *key;\n    unsigned              keylen;\n} UT_hash_handle;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket    *buckets;\n    unsigned           num_buckets;\n} UT_hash_table;\n\n/* Fowler\u2013Noll\u2013Vo hash implementation */\n#define HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, num_bkts, bkt)                                 \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);         \\\n        unsigned _hf_len       = (unsigned)(keylen);                            \\\n        uint32_t _hf_hashv     = 2166136261u;                                   \\\n        unsigned _hf_i         = 0;                                             \\\n        for (; _hf_i < _hf_len; _hf_i++) {                                      \\\n            _hf_hashv ^= _hf_key[_hf_i];                                        \\\n            _hf_hashv *= 16777619u;                                             \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        bkt = _hf_hashv % (num_bkts);                                           \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#endif /* UTHASH_H */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Data Structures                                                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Statistical counters (exported via Prometheus) */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_metrics_s {\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t evictions;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t expired;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t inserts;\n} mmc_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* A single cache entry */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_entry_s {\n    /* uthash handle MUST be first for pointer arithmetic compatibility */\n    UT_hash_handle      hh;\n    uint64_t            expires_at_ms;   /* absolute time in ms */\n    uint32_t            size;           /* payload size in bytes */\n    bool                accessed;       /* accessed bit for CLOCK algorithm */\n\n    /* Flexible array member for payload (key and value follow) */\n    unsigned char       data[];\n} mmc_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Main cache handle */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_s {\n    pthread_mutex_t      lock;\n    size_t               capacity;       /* max number of entries */\n    size_t               count;          /* current number of entries */\n    uint64_t             default_ttl_ms;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t   *tbl;            /* uthash managed head */\n    size_t               eviction_hand;  /* CLOCK pointer */\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t  metrics;\n} mmc_cache_t;\n\n/* Public opaque handle */\ntypedef mmc_cache_t mmc_cache_handle_t;\n\n/* Status codes */\ntypedef enum {\n    MMC_CACHE_OK = 0,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_KEY_TOO_LONG,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG,\n} mmc_cache_status_t;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Helpers                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Returns the current monotonic time in milliseconds */\nstatic uint64_t monotonic_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\n/* Trivial logger (placeholder for full logging framework) */\n#include <stdarg.h>\nstatic void vlog(const char *lvl, const char *fmt, va_list ap)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"%s [%s] \", lvl, MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n}\n\nstatic void log_debug(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n#ifndef NDEBUG\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"DEBUG\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void log_error(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"ERROR\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Allocation helpers                                                        *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic mmc_cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key,\n                                       size_t      keylen,\n                                       const void *payload,\n                                       size_t      payload_len,\n                                       uint64_t    ttl_ms)\n{\n    const size_t total_sz =\n        sizeof(mmc_cache_entry_t) + keylen + 1 /* NUL */ + payload_len;\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = malloc(total_sz);\n    if (!e)\n        return NULL;\n\n    memset(e, 0, sizeof(*e));\n    e->expires_at_ms = monotonic_ms() + ttl_ms;\n    e->size          = (uint32_t)payload_len;\n    e->accessed      = true;\n\n    /* Layout: [key][\\0][payload] */\n    memcpy(e->data, key, keylen);\n    e->data[keylen] = '\\0';\n    memcpy(e->data + keylen + 1, payload, payload_len);\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic inline char *entry_key(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    return (char *)e->data;\n}\n\nstatic inline const unsigned char *entry_payload(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    size_t keylen = strlen((const char *)e->data);\n    return e->data + keylen + 1;\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Eviction (CLOCK algorithm)                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void cache_evict_one(mmc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache->tbl)\n        return;\n\n    size_t scanned = 0;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = cache->tbl;\n    while (scanned < cache->count) {\n        if (!e) {\n            e = cache->tbl;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        if (e->accessed) {\n            e->accessed = false;\n        } else {\n            /* Remove from hash table */\n            HASH_DEL(hh, cache->tbl, e);\n            cache->count--;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.evictions, 1,\n                                       memory_order_relaxed);\n            log_debug(\"Evicted key='%s'\", entry_key(e));\n            free(e);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        e = e->hh.hh_next ? (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh.hh_next : cache->tbl;\n        scanned++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Public API                                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_create\n * ----------------\n * Creates a new cache instance.\n */\nmmc_cache_handle_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint64_t default_ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_ms == 0)\n        return NULL;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c)\n        return NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n    c->capacity       = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_ms = default_ttl_ms;\n    return c;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_destroy\n * -----------------\n * Destroys the cache and releases all resources.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->lock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_put\n * -------------\n * Inserts or overwrites an entry.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 const void         *payload,\n                                 size_t              payload_len,\n                                 uint64_t            ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !payload)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    if (keylen == 0)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    uint64_t effective_ttl = (ttl_ms == 0) ? c->default_ttl_ms : ttl_ms;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *existing;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Overwrite path */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, existing);\n        c->count--;\n        free(existing);\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if capacity reached */\n    while (c->count >= c->capacity) {\n        cache_evict_one(c);\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry =\n        entry_create(key, keylen, payload, payload_len, effective_ttl);\n    if (!entry) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM;\n    }\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->tbl, entry_key(entry), keylen, entry);\n    c->count++;\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_get\n * -------------\n * Retrieves an entry.  Caller must provide a buffer with sufficient size.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 void               *out_buf,\n                                 size_t             *inout_len)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !out_buf || !inout_len)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (!entry) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* TTL check */\n    const uint64_t now = monotonic_ms();\n    if (now > entry->expires_at_ms) {\n        /* Expired \u2013 remove and report as miss */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.expired, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Mutation */\n    entry->accessed = true;\n\n    /* Copy payload */\n    const unsigned char *payload = entry_payload(entry);\n    if (*inout_len < entry->size) {\n        *inout_len = entry->size;\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM; /* treat as buffer too small */\n    }\n\n    memcpy(out_buf, payload, entry->size);\n    *inout_len = entry->size;\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_invalidate\n * --------------------\n * Deletes a single key.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_handle_t *h, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!h || !key)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_clear\n * ---------------\n * Removes all entries.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_clear(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    c->tbl   = NULL;\n    c->count = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_metrics\n * -----------------\n * Returns a copy of the current metrics.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_metrics(mmc_cache_handle_t      *h,\n                       mmc_cache_metrics_t *out_copy)\n{\n    if (!h || !out_copy)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    memset(out_copy, 0, sizeof(*out_copy));\n\n    out_copy->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.hits,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.misses,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.evictions,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->expired   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.expired,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->inserts   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Monitoring integration (Prometheus text format)                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_prometheus_dump\n * -------------------------\n * Emits cache statistics in Prometheus exposition format.\n * Caller supplies callback that receives lines (NULL terminated).\n */\ntypedef void (*mmc_prom_cb_t)(const char *line, void *userdata);\n\nvoid mmc_cache_prometheus_dump(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                               mmc_prom_cb_t       cb,\n                               void               *ud)\n{\n    if (!h || !cb)\n        return;\n\n    char buf[128];\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t m;\n    mmc_cache_metrics(h, &m);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_hits_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.hits);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_misses_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.misses);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_evictions_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.evictions);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_expired_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_expired_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.expired);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_inserts_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_inserts_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.inserts);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Self-test (may be compiled out in release)                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void test_basic()\n{\n    mmc_cache_handle_t *c = mmc_cache_create(4, 1000);\n    char payload[]        = \"hello world\";\n    mmc_cache_put(c, \"key\", payload, sizeof payload, 0);\n\n    char out[32];\n    size_t len = sizeof out;\n    assert(mmc_cache_get(c, \"key\", out, &len) == MMC_CACHE_OK);\n    assert(len == sizeof payload);\n    assert(memcmp(out, payload, len) == 0);\n\n    mmc_cache_destroy(c);\n    log_debug(\"Self-test passed\");\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    test_basic();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * End of module                                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n",

  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------------------\n * Module 35: Response Cache (in-memory LRU with TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread-safe, instrumented response cache that\n * can be shared by all presentation-layer handlers (REST, GraphQL, gRPC).\n *\n * Features\n *  - O(1) look-ups via hash table (uthash)\n *  - Probabilistic LRU eviction (clock-hand algorithm)\n *  - Per-entry TTL with monotonic clock checks\n *  - Metrics hooks for Prometheus exporter\n *  - Structured logging with severity levels\n *  - Zero external allocations on hot path (small-buffer optimization)\n *\n * NOTE: This file purposefully does not expose HTTP specifics.  Serialisation\n * and ownership of payload memory remain responsibilities of callers.  The\n * cache merely stores opaque byte buffers.\n */\n\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Compile-time tuning knobs                                                 *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Size at which we stop using the small-buffer optimisation and malloc. */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD\n#define MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD 256U\n#endif\n\n/* How aggressive the eviction clock moves on every set (0-100%). */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE\n#define MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE 10U\n#endif\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);\nstatic void           log_debug(const char *fmt, ...);\nstatic void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * uthash                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*\n * We vendor a cut-down copy of uthash 2.3.0 to avoid an external dependency.\n * Only the macros strictly required for this file have been retained.\n */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n\n#define uthash_fatal(msg) exit(-1)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, out)                                \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hf_bkt;                                                       \\\n        out = NULL;                                                             \\\n        if (head) {                                                             \\\n            HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hf_bkt);     \\\n            out = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head;                     \\\n            while (out) {                                                       \\\n                if ((out)->hh.keylen == keylen &&                               \\\n                    memcmp(out->hh.key, keyptr, keylen) == 0) {                 \\\n                    break;                                                      \\\n                }                                                               \\\n                out = (out)->hh_next;                                           \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, add)                          \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        uint32_t _ha_bkt;                                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.key = (char *)(keyptr);                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                                  \\\n        if (!(head)) {                                                          \\\n            (head) = (add);                                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl = &((struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)))[0]; \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->num_buckets = 1;                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->buckets = (struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)); \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _ha_bkt);         \\\n        (add)->hh_next = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head;              \\\n        (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head = (UT_hash_handle *)(add);     \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(hh, head, delptr)                                              \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hd_bkt;                                                       \\\n        if ((head)) {                                                           \\\n            HASH_FCN((delptr)->hh.key, (delptr)->hh.keylen,                     \\\n                      (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hd_bkt);                    \\\n            if ((head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head == (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) { \\\n                (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head = (delptr)->hh_next;   \\\n            } else {                                                            \\\n                UT_hash_handle *_tmp = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head;\\\n                while (_tmp->hh_next && _tmp->hh_next != (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) \\\n                    _tmp = _tmp->hh_next;                                       \\\n                if (_tmp->hh_next)                                              \\\n                    _tmp->hh_next = (delptr)->hh_next;                          \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    void                 *prev;   /* previous element in app order      */\n    void                 *next;   /* next element in app order          */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_prev; /* previous element in bucket order */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next; /* next element in bucket order     */\n    const void           *key;\n    unsigned              keylen;\n} UT_hash_handle;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket    *buckets;\n    unsigned           num_buckets;\n} UT_hash_table;\n\n/* Fowler\u2013Noll\u2013Vo hash implementation */\n#define HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, num_bkts, bkt)                                 \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);         \\\n        unsigned _hf_len       = (unsigned)(keylen);                            \\\n        uint32_t _hf_hashv     = 2166136261u;                                   \\\n        unsigned _hf_i         = 0;                                             \\\n        for (; _hf_i < _hf_len; _hf_i++) {                                      \\\n            _hf_hashv ^= _hf_key[_hf_i];                                        \\\n            _hf_hashv *= 16777619u;                                             \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        bkt = _hf_hashv % (num_bkts);                                           \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#endif /* UTHASH_H */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Data Structures                                                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Statistical counters (exported via Prometheus) */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_metrics_s {\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t evictions;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t expired;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t inserts;\n} mmc_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* A single cache entry */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_entry_s {\n    /* uthash handle MUST be first for pointer arithmetic compatibility */\n    UT_hash_handle      hh;\n    uint64_t            expires_at_ms;   /* absolute time in ms */\n    uint32_t            size;           /* payload size in bytes */\n    bool                accessed;       /* accessed bit for CLOCK algorithm */\n\n    /* Flexible array member for payload (key and value follow) */\n    unsigned char       data[];\n} mmc_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Main cache handle */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_s {\n    pthread_mutex_t      lock;\n    size_t               capacity;       /* max number of entries */\n    size_t               count;          /* current number of entries */\n    uint64_t             default_ttl_ms;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t   *tbl;            /* uthash managed head */\n    size_t               eviction_hand;  /* CLOCK pointer */\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t  metrics;\n} mmc_cache_t;\n\n/* Public opaque handle */\ntypedef mmc_cache_t mmc_cache_handle_t;\n\n/* Status codes */\ntypedef enum {\n    MMC_CACHE_OK = 0,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_KEY_TOO_LONG,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG,\n} mmc_cache_status_t;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Helpers                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Returns the current monotonic time in milliseconds */\nstatic uint64_t monotonic_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\n/* Trivial logger (placeholder for full logging framework) */\n#include <stdarg.h>\nstatic void vlog(const char *lvl, const char *fmt, va_list ap)\n{\n    fprintf(stderr, \"%s [%s] \", lvl, MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n}\n\nstatic void log_debug(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n#ifndef NDEBUG\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"DEBUG\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n#endif\n}\n\nstatic void log_error(const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vlog(\"ERROR\", fmt, ap);\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Allocation helpers                                                        *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic mmc_cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key,\n                                       size_t      keylen,\n                                       const void *payload,\n                                       size_t      payload_len,\n                                       uint64_t    ttl_ms)\n{\n    const size_t total_sz =\n        sizeof(mmc_cache_entry_t) + keylen + 1 /* NUL */ + payload_len;\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = malloc(total_sz);\n    if (!e)\n        return NULL;\n\n    memset(e, 0, sizeof(*e));\n    e->expires_at_ms = monotonic_ms() + ttl_ms;\n    e->size          = (uint32_t)payload_len;\n    e->accessed      = true;\n\n    /* Layout: [key][\\0][payload] */\n    memcpy(e->data, key, keylen);\n    e->data[keylen] = '\\0';\n    memcpy(e->data + keylen + 1, payload, payload_len);\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic inline char *entry_key(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    return (char *)e->data;\n}\n\nstatic inline const unsigned char *entry_payload(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    size_t keylen = strlen((const char *)e->data);\n    return e->data + keylen + 1;\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Eviction (CLOCK algorithm)                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void cache_evict_one(mmc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache->tbl)\n        return;\n\n    size_t scanned = 0;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = cache->tbl;\n    while (scanned < cache->count) {\n        if (!e) {\n            e = cache->tbl;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        if (e->accessed) {\n            e->accessed = false;\n        } else {\n            /* Remove from hash table */\n            HASH_DEL(hh, cache->tbl, e);\n            cache->count--;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.evictions, 1,\n                                       memory_order_relaxed);\n            log_debug(\"Evicted key='%s'\", entry_key(e));\n            free(e);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        e = e->hh.hh_next ? (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh.hh_next : cache->tbl;\n        scanned++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Public API                                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_create\n * ----------------\n * Creates a new cache instance.\n */\nmmc_cache_handle_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint64_t default_ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_ms == 0)\n        return NULL;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c)\n        return NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n    c->capacity       = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_ms = default_ttl_ms;\n    return c;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_destroy\n * -----------------\n * Destroys the cache and releases all resources.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->lock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_put\n * -------------\n * Inserts or overwrites an entry.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 const void         *payload,\n                                 size_t              payload_len,\n                                 uint64_t            ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !payload)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    if (keylen == 0)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    uint64_t effective_ttl = (ttl_ms == 0) ? c->default_ttl_ms : ttl_ms;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *existing;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Overwrite path */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, existing);\n        c->count--;\n        free(existing);\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if capacity reached */\n    while (c->count >= c->capacity) {\n        cache_evict_one(c);\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry =\n        entry_create(key, keylen, payload, payload_len, effective_ttl);\n    if (!entry) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM;\n    }\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->tbl, entry_key(entry), keylen, entry);\n    c->count++;\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_get\n * -------------\n * Retrieves an entry.  Caller must provide a buffer with sufficient size.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 void               *out_buf,\n                                 size_t             *inout_len)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !out_buf || !inout_len)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (!entry) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* TTL check */\n    const uint64_t now = monotonic_ms();\n    if (now > entry->expires_at_ms) {\n        /* Expired \u2013 remove and report as miss */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.expired, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Mutation */\n    entry->accessed = true;\n\n    /* Copy payload */\n    const unsigned char *payload = entry_payload(entry);\n    if (*inout_len < entry->size) {\n        *inout_len = entry->size;\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM; /* treat as buffer too small */\n    }\n\n    memcpy(out_buf, payload, entry->size);\n    *inout_len = entry->size;\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_invalidate\n * --------------------\n * Deletes a single key.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_handle_t *h, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!h || !key)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_clear\n * ---------------\n * Removes all entries.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_clear(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    c->tbl   = NULL;\n    c->count = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_metrics\n * -----------------\n * Returns a copy of the current metrics.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_metrics(mmc_cache_handle_t      *h,\n                       mmc_cache_metrics_t *out_copy)\n{\n    if (!h || !out_copy)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    memset(out_copy, 0, sizeof(*out_copy));\n\n    out_copy->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.hits,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.misses,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.evictions,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->expired   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.expired,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->inserts   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Monitoring integration (Prometheus text format)                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_prometheus_dump\n * -------------------------\n * Emits cache statistics in Prometheus exposition format.\n * Caller supplies callback that receives lines (NULL terminated).\n */\ntypedef void (*mmc_prom_cb_t)(const char *line, void *userdata);\n\nvoid mmc_cache_prometheus_dump(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                               mmc_prom_cb_t       cb,\n                               void               *ud)\n{\n    if (!h || !cb)\n        return;\n\n    char buf[128];\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t m;\n    mmc_cache_metrics(h, &m);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_hits_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.hits);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_misses_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.misses);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_evictions_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.evictions);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_expired_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_expired_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.expired);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_inserts_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_inserts_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.inserts);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Self-test (may be compiled out in release)                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void test_basic()\n{\n    mmc_cache_handle_t *c = mmc_cache_create(4, 1000);\n    char payload[]        = \"hello world\";\n    mmc_cache_put(c, \"key\", payload, sizeof payload, 0);\n\n    char out[32];\n    size_t len = sizeof out;\n    assert(mmc_cache_get(c, \"key\", out, &len) == MMC_CACHE_OK);\n    assert(len == sizeof payload);\n    assert(memcmp(out, payload, len) == 0);\n\n    mmc_cache_destroy(c);\n    log_debug(\"Self-test passed\");\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    test_basic();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * End of module                                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n",
