  "newString": "/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);",
  "oldString": "/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);\nstatic void           log_debug(const char *fmt, ...);\nstatic void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * ---------------------------------------------\n * Module 35: Response Cache (in-memory LRU with TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread-safe, instrumented response cache that\n * can be shared by all presentation-layer handlers (REST, GraphQL, gRPC).\n *\n * Features\n *  - O(1) look-ups via hash table (uthash)\n *  - Probabilistic LRU eviction (clock-hand algorithm)\n *  - Per-entry TTL with monotonic clock checks\n *  - Metrics hooks for Prometheus exporter\n *  - Structured logging with severity levels\n *  - Zero external allocations on hot path (small-buffer optimization)\n *\n * NOTE: This file purposefully does not expose HTTP specifics.  Serialisation\n * and ownership of payload memory remain responsibilities of callers.  The\n * cache merely stores opaque byte buffers.\n */\n\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n#define MMC_CACHE_LOG_TAG \"response_cache\"\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Compile-time tuning knobs                                                 *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Size at which we stop using the small-buffer optimisation and malloc. */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD\n#define MMC_CACHE_SBO_THRESHOLD 256U\n#endif\n\n/* How aggressive the eviction clock moves on every set (0-100%). */\n#ifndef MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE\n#define MMC_CACHE_EVICTION_PACE 10U\n#endif\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Forward declarations of internal helpers                                  *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);\nstatic void           eviction_run(void *ctx);\nstatic void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);\nstatic void           log_debug(const char *fmt, ...);\nstatic void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * uthash                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*\n * We vendor a cut-down copy of uthash 2.3.0 to avoid an external dependency.\n * Only the macros strictly required for this file have been retained.\n */\n#ifndef UTHASH_H\n#define UTHASH_H\n\n#define uthash_fatal(msg) exit(-1)\n#define HASH_FIND(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, out)                                \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hf_bkt;                                                       \\\n        out = NULL;                                                             \\\n        if (head) {                                                             \\\n            HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hf_bkt);     \\\n            out = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hf_bkt].hh_head;                     \\\n            while (out) {                                                       \\\n                if ((out)->hh.keylen == keylen &&                               \\\n                    memcmp(out->hh.key, keyptr, keylen) == 0) {                 \\\n                    break;                                                      \\\n                }                                                               \\\n                out = (out)->hh_next;                                           \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, head, keyptr, keylen, add)                          \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        uint32_t _ha_bkt;                                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.key = (char *)(keyptr);                                       \\\n        (add)->hh.keylen = (unsigned)(keylen);                                  \\\n        if (!(head)) {                                                          \\\n            (head) = (add);                                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl = &((struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)))[0]; \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->num_buckets = 1;                                     \\\n            (add)->hh.tbl->buckets = (struct UT_hash_bucket *)calloc(1, sizeof(struct UT_hash_bucket)); \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _ha_bkt);         \\\n        (add)->hh_next = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head;              \\\n        (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_ha_bkt].hh_head = (UT_hash_handle *)(add);     \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#define HASH_DEL(hh, head, delptr)                                              \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        unsigned _hd_bkt;                                                       \\\n        if ((head)) {                                                           \\\n            HASH_FCN((delptr)->hh.key, (delptr)->hh.keylen,                     \\\n                      (head)->hh.tbl->num_buckets, _hd_bkt);                    \\\n            if ((head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head == (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) { \\\n                (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head = (delptr)->hh_next;   \\\n            } else {                                                            \\\n                UT_hash_handle *_tmp = (head)->hh.tbl->buckets[_hd_bkt].hh_head;\\\n                while (_tmp->hh_next && _tmp->hh_next != (UT_hash_handle *)(delptr)) \\\n                    _tmp = _tmp->hh_next;                                       \\\n                if (_tmp->hh_next)                                              \\\n                    _tmp->hh_next = (delptr)->hh_next;                          \\\n            }                                                                   \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n    } while (0)\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_handle {\n    struct UT_hash_table *tbl;\n    void                 *prev;   /* previous element in app order      */\n    void                 *next;   /* next element in app order          */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_prev; /* previous element in bucket order */\n    struct UT_hash_handle *hh_next; /* next element in bucket order     */\n    const void           *key;\n    unsigned              keylen;\n} UT_hash_handle;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_bucket {\n    UT_hash_handle *hh_head;\n} UT_hash_bucket;\n\ntypedef struct UT_hash_table {\n    UT_hash_bucket    *buckets;\n    unsigned           num_buckets;\n} UT_hash_table;\n\n/* Fowler\u2013Noll\u2013Vo hash implementation */\n#define HASH_FCN(keyptr, keylen, num_bkts, bkt)                                 \\\n    do {                                                                        \\\n        const unsigned char *_hf_key = (const unsigned char *)(keyptr);         \\\n        unsigned _hf_len       = (unsigned)(keylen);                            \\\n        uint32_t _hf_hashv     = 2166136261u;                                   \\\n        unsigned _hf_i         = 0;                                             \\\n        for (; _hf_i < _hf_len; _hf_i++) {                                      \\\n            _hf_hashv ^= _hf_key[_hf_i];                                        \\\n            _hf_hashv *= 16777619u;                                             \\\n        }                                                                       \\\n        bkt = _hf_hashv % (num_bkts);                                           \\\n    } while (0)\n\n#endif /* UTHASH_H */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Data Structures                                                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Statistical counters (exported via Prometheus) */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_metrics_s {\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t evictions;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t expired;\n    atomic_uint_fast64_t inserts;\n} mmc_cache_metrics_t;\n\n/* A single cache entry */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_entry_s {\n    /* uthash handle MUST be first for pointer arithmetic compatibility */\n    UT_hash_handle      hh;\n    uint64_t            expires_at_ms;   /* absolute time in ms */\n    uint32_t            size;           /* payload size in bytes */\n    bool                accessed;       /* accessed bit for CLOCK algorithm */\n\n    /* Flexible array member for payload (key and value follow) */\n    unsigned char       data[];\n} mmc_cache_entry_t;\n\n/* Main cache handle */\ntypedef struct mmc_cache_s {\n    pthread_mutex_t      lock;\n    size_t               capacity;       /* max number of entries */\n    size_t               count;          /* current number of entries */\n    uint64_t             default_ttl_ms;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t   *tbl;            /* uthash managed head */\n    size_t               eviction_hand;  /* CLOCK pointer */\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t  metrics;\n} mmc_cache_t;\n\n/* Public opaque handle */\ntypedef mmc_cache_t mmc_cache_handle_t;\n\n/* Status codes */\ntypedef enum {\n    MMC_CACHE_OK = 0,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_KEY_TOO_LONG,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND,\n    MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG,\n} mmc_cache_status_t;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Helpers                                                                   *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/* Returns the current monotonic time in milliseconds */\nstatic uint64_t monotonic_ms(void)\n{\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, &ts);\n    return ((uint64_t)ts.tv_sec * 1000ULL) + (ts.tv_nsec / 1000000ULL);\n}\n\n/* Wrapper macros for centralized logging */\n#define log_debug(fmt, ...) LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n#define log_error(fmt, ...) LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__)\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Allocation helpers                                                        *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic mmc_cache_entry_t *entry_create(const char *key,\n                                       size_t      keylen,\n                                       const void *payload,\n                                       size_t      payload_len,\n                                       uint64_t    ttl_ms)\n{\n    const size_t total_sz =\n        sizeof(mmc_cache_entry_t) + keylen + 1 /* NUL */ + payload_len;\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = malloc(total_sz);\n    if (!e)\n        return NULL;\n\n    memset(e, 0, sizeof(*e));\n    e->expires_at_ms = monotonic_ms() + ttl_ms;\n    e->size          = (uint32_t)payload_len;\n    e->accessed      = true;\n\n    /* Layout: [key][\\0][payload] */\n    memcpy(e->data, key, keylen);\n    e->data[keylen] = '\\0';\n    memcpy(e->data + keylen + 1, payload, payload_len);\n\n    return e;\n}\n\nstatic inline char *entry_key(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    return (char *)e->data;\n}\n\nstatic inline const unsigned char *entry_payload(const mmc_cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    size_t keylen = strlen((const char *)e->data);\n    return e->data + keylen + 1;\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Eviction (CLOCK algorithm)                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic void cache_evict_one(mmc_cache_t *cache)\n{\n    if (!cache->tbl)\n        return;\n\n    size_t scanned = 0;\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e = cache->tbl;\n    while (scanned < cache->count) {\n        if (!e) {\n            e = cache->tbl;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        if (e->accessed) {\n            e->accessed = false;\n        } else {\n            /* Remove from hash table */\n            HASH_DEL(hh, cache->tbl, e);\n            cache->count--;\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&cache->metrics.evictions, 1,\n                                       memory_order_relaxed);\n            log_debug(\"Evicted key='%s'\", entry_key(e));\n            free(e);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        e = e->hh.hh_next ? (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh.hh_next : cache->tbl;\n        scanned++;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Public API                                                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_create\n * ----------------\n * Creates a new cache instance.\n */\nmmc_cache_handle_t *mmc_cache_create(size_t max_entries, uint64_t default_ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (max_entries == 0 || default_ttl_ms == 0)\n        return NULL;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof(*c));\n    if (!c)\n        return NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n    c->capacity       = max_entries;\n    c->default_ttl_ms = default_ttl_ms;\n    return c;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_destroy\n * -----------------\n * Destroys the cache and releases all resources.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_destroy(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&c->lock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_put\n * -------------\n * Inserts or overwrites an entry.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_put(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 const void         *payload,\n                                 size_t              payload_len,\n                                 uint64_t            ttl_ms)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !payload)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    if (keylen == 0)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    uint64_t effective_ttl = (ttl_ms == 0) ? c->default_ttl_ms : ttl_ms;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *existing;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Overwrite path */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, existing);\n        c->count--;\n        free(existing);\n    }\n\n    /* Evict if capacity reached */\n    while (c->count >= c->capacity) {\n        cache_evict_one(c);\n    }\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry =\n        entry_create(key, keylen, payload, payload_len, effective_ttl);\n    if (!entry) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM;\n    }\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->tbl, entry_key(entry), keylen, entry);\n    c->count++;\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_get\n * -------------\n * Retrieves an entry.  Caller must provide a buffer with sufficient size.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_get(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                                 const char         *key,\n                                 void               *out_buf,\n                                 size_t             *inout_len)\n{\n    if (!h || !key || !out_buf || !inout_len)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (!entry) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* TTL check */\n    const uint64_t now = monotonic_ms();\n    if (now > entry->expires_at_ms) {\n        /* Expired \u2013 remove and report as miss */\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.expired, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Mutation */\n    entry->accessed = true;\n\n    /* Copy payload */\n    const unsigned char *payload = entry_payload(entry);\n    if (*inout_len < entry->size) {\n        *inout_len = entry->size;\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_OOM; /* treat as buffer too small */\n    }\n\n    memcpy(out_buf, payload, entry->size);\n    *inout_len = entry->size;\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&c->metrics.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_invalidate\n * --------------------\n * Deletes a single key.\n */\nmmc_cache_status_t mmc_cache_invalidate(mmc_cache_handle_t *h, const char *key)\n{\n    if (!h || !key)\n        return MMC_CACHE_ERR_INV_ARG;\n\n    const size_t keylen = strlen(key);\n    mmc_cache_t *c      = h;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *entry;\n    HASH_FIND(hh, c->tbl, key, keylen, entry);\n    if (entry) {\n        HASH_DEL(hh, c->tbl, entry);\n        free(entry);\n        c->count--;\n    }\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n    return MMC_CACHE_OK;\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_clear\n * ---------------\n * Removes all entries.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_clear(mmc_cache_handle_t *h)\n{\n    if (!h)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&c->lock);\n    mmc_cache_entry_t *e, *tmp;\n    for (e = c->tbl; e != NULL;) {\n        tmp = (mmc_cache_entry_t *)e->hh_next;\n        free(e);\n        e = tmp;\n    }\n    c->tbl   = NULL;\n    c->count = 0;\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&c->lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_metrics\n * -----------------\n * Returns a copy of the current metrics.\n */\nvoid mmc_cache_metrics(mmc_cache_handle_t      *h,\n                       mmc_cache_metrics_t *out_copy)\n{\n    if (!h || !out_copy)\n        return;\n\n    mmc_cache_t *c = h;\n    memset(out_copy, 0, sizeof(*out_copy));\n\n    out_copy->hits      = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.hits,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->misses    = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.misses,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->evictions = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.evictions,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->expired   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.expired,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n    out_copy->inserts   = atomic_load_explicit(&c->metrics.inserts,\n                                               memory_order_relaxed);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Monitoring integration (Prometheus text format)                           *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n/*\n * mmc_cache_prometheus_dump\n * -------------------------\n * Emits cache statistics in Prometheus exposition format.\n * Caller supplies callback that receives lines (NULL terminated).\n */\ntypedef void (*mmc_prom_cb_t)(const char *line, void *userdata);\n\nvoid mmc_cache_prometheus_dump(mmc_cache_handle_t *h,\n                               mmc_prom_cb_t       cb,\n                               void               *ud)\n{\n    if (!h || !cb)\n        return;\n\n    char buf[128];\n    mmc_cache_metrics_t m;\n    mmc_cache_metrics(h, &m);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_hits_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_hits_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.hits);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_misses_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_misses_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.misses);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_evictions_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_evictions_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.evictions);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_expired_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_expired_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.expired);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n\n    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),\n             \"# TYPE mmc_cache_inserts_total counter\\n\"\n             \"mmc_cache_inserts_total %\" PRIuFAST64 \"\\n\",\n             (uint_fast64_t)m.inserts);\n    cb(buf, ud);\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * Self-test (may be compiled out in release)                                *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST\n#include <assert.h>\n\nstatic void test_basic()\n{\n    mmc_cache_handle_t *c = mmc_cache_create(4, 1000);\n    char payload[]        = \"hello world\";\n    mmc_cache_put(c, \"key\", payload, sizeof payload, 0);\n\n    char out[32];\n    size_t len = sizeof out;\n    assert(mmc_cache_get(c, \"key\", out, &len) == MMC_CACHE_OK);\n    assert(len == sizeof payload);\n    assert(memcmp(out, payload, len) == 0);\n\n    mmc_cache_destroy(c);\n    log_debug(\"Self-test passed\");\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    test_basic();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MMC_CACHE_ENABLE_SELFTEST */\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- *\n * End of module                                                             *\n * ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " static uint64_t       monotonic_ms(void);",
        " static void           eviction_run(void *ctx);",
        " static void           metrics_emit_snapshot(void *ctx);",

        "-static void           log_error(const char *fmt, ...);",

124. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_44.txt",
  "limit": 70
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 44 \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * This module implements an in-memory, size-bounded, TTL-aware LRU cache\n * designed for caching JSON/GraphQL HTTP responses.  It is intended to be\n * shared across all REST / GraphQL endpoints and therefore must be:\n *   1. Fast (O(1) get/put)\n *   2. Thread-safe\n *   3. Introspection-friendly (Prometheus metrics)\n *\n * Public API:\n *   mm_cache_init()\n *   mm_cache_destroy()\n *   mm_cache_get()\n *   mm_cache_put()\n *   mm_cache_stats()\n *   mm_cache_prometheus_snapshot()\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Utilities & logging\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 2, 3)));\n\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    localtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    char timebuf[32];\n    strftime(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] [%s] \", timebuf, level);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#else\n#include \"mm_log.h\"\n#endif /* MM_LOG_H */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public types / constants\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define MM_CACHE_SUCCESS           0\n#define MM_CACHE_KEY_NOT_FOUND     1\n#define MM_CACHE_ERR              -1\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_stats\n{\n    uint64_t hits;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_44.txt",
    "numLines": 70,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 535
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "new_string": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* Map legacy mm_log to new centralized logging */\n#define mm_log(level, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if (strcmp(level, \"ERROR\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if (strcmp(level, \"WARN\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if (strcmp(level, \"DEBUG\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)",
  "old_string": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Utilities & logging\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 2, 3)));\n\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    localtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    char timebuf[32];\n    strftime(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] [%s] \", timebuf, level);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#else\n#include \"mm_log.h\"\n#endif /* MM_LOG_H */",

}

  "newString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n#include \"logging.h\"\n\n/* Map legacy mm_log to new centralized logging */\n#define mm_log(level, fmt, ...) \\\n    do { \\\n        if (strcmp(level, \"ERROR\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if (strcmp(level, \"WARN\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else if (strcmp(level, \"DEBUG\") == 0) \\\n            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n        else \\\n            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\\n    } while (0)",
  "oldString": "#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Utilities & logging\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 2, 3)));\n\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    localtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    char timebuf[32];\n    strftime(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] [%s] \", timebuf, level);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#else\n#include \"mm_log.h\"\n#endif /* MM_LOG_H */",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * Module 44 \u2013 Response Cache (LRU, TTL-aware, thread-safe)\n *\n * This module implements an in-memory, size-bounded, TTL-aware LRU cache\n * designed for caching JSON/GraphQL HTTP responses.  It is intended to be\n * shared across all REST / GraphQL endpoints and therefore must be:\n *   1. Fast (O(1) get/put)\n *   2. Thread-safe\n *   3. Introspection-friendly (Prometheus metrics)\n *\n * Public API:\n *   mm_cache_init()\n *   mm_cache_destroy()\n *   mm_cache_get()\n *   mm_cache_put()\n *   mm_cache_stats()\n *   mm_cache_prometheus_snapshot()\n */\n\n#include <assert.h>\n#include <errno.h>\n#include <inttypes.h>\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdatomic.h>\n#include <stdarg.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Utilities & logging\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifndef MM_LOG_H\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 2, 3)));\n\nstatic void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    va_list ap;\n    va_start(ap, fmt);\n\n    struct timespec ts;\n    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);\n    struct tm tm;\n    localtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);\n    char timebuf[32];\n    strftime(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\", &tm);\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] [%s] \", timebuf, level);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fputc('\\n', stderr);\n\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n#else\n#include \"mm_log.h\"\n#endif /* MM_LOG_H */\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public types / constants\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#define MM_CACHE_SUCCESS           0\n#define MM_CACHE_KEY_NOT_FOUND     1\n#define MM_CACHE_ERR              -1\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache_stats\n{\n    uint64_t hits;\n    uint64_t misses;\n    uint64_t evictions;\n    uint64_t current_entries;\n    uint64_t current_bytes;\n} mm_cache_stats_t;\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Internal data structures\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\n#define DEFAULT_HASH_POWER 12 /* 2^12 = 4096 buckets */\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry\n{\n    char               *key;\n    uint8_t            *blob;\n    size_t              blob_len;\n    time_t              expire_at;\n\n    /* LRU doubly-linked list */\n    struct cache_entry *prev;\n    struct cache_entry *next;\n\n    /* Hash bucket singly-linked list */\n    struct cache_entry *hnext;\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_cache\n{\n    /* Configuration */\n    size_t              max_entries;\n    size_t              max_bytes;\n\n    /* Runtime counters  */\n    atomic_uint_least64_t hits;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t misses;\n    atomic_uint_least64_t evictions;\n\n    size_t              current_entries;\n    size_t              current_bytes;\n\n    /* Core containers */\n    cache_entry_t     **buckets;\n    size_t              hash_mask;\n\n    /* LRU root pointers (most-recent = head) */\n    cache_entry_t      *lru_head;\n    cache_entry_t      *lru_tail;\n\n    /* Synchronisation */\n    pthread_mutex_t     lock;\n\n    /* Init flag */\n    int                 initialized;\n} mm_cache_t;\n\n/* Singleton */\nstatic mm_cache_t g_cache = {\n    .initialized = 0\n};\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * FNV-1a 64-bit hash\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic inline uint64_t\nmm_fnv1a_hash(const char *data)\n{\n    const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)data;\n    uint64_t hash = 14695981039346656037ULL;\n\n    for (; *p; ++p) {\n        hash ^= (uint64_t)(*p);\n        hash *= 1099511628211ULL;\n    }\n    return hash;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Internal helpers (assumes g_cache.lock is held where noted)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nstatic void\nlru_remove_unlocked(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (!e) return;\n\n    if (e->prev)\n        e->prev->next = e->next;\n    else\n        g_cache.lru_head = e->next; /* was head */\n\n    if (e->next)\n        e->next->prev = e->prev;\n    else\n        g_cache.lru_tail = e->prev; /* was tail */\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void\nlru_insert_front_unlocked(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    e->next = g_cache.lru_head;\n    if (g_cache.lru_head)\n        g_cache.lru_head->prev = e;\n    g_cache.lru_head = e;\n    if (!g_cache.lru_tail)\n        g_cache.lru_tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic int\nhash_insert_unlocked(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key) & g_cache.hash_mask;\n    e->hnext = g_cache.buckets[h];\n    g_cache.buckets[h] = e;\n    return 0;\n}\n\nstatic void\nhash_remove_unlocked(cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(e->key) & g_cache.hash_mask;\n    cache_entry_t *cur = g_cache.buckets[h];\n    cache_entry_t *prev = NULL;\n\n    while (cur) {\n        if (cur == e) {\n            if (prev)\n                prev->hnext = cur->hnext;\n            else\n                g_cache.buckets[h] = cur->hnext;\n            break;\n        }\n        prev = cur;\n        cur = cur->hnext;\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void\nevict_if_needed_unlocked(size_t incoming_bytes)\n{\n    while ((g_cache.current_entries >= g_cache.max_entries) ||\n           (g_cache.current_bytes + incoming_bytes > g_cache.max_bytes)) {\n        cache_entry_t *victim = g_cache.lru_tail;\n        if (!victim)\n            return; /* Shouldn't happen */\n\n        mm_log(\"DEBUG\", \"Evicting key='%s' (%zu B)\", victim->key, victim->blob_len);\n\n        lru_remove_unlocked(victim);\n        hash_remove_unlocked(victim);\n\n        g_cache.current_entries--;\n        g_cache.current_bytes -= victim->blob_len;\n\n        free(victim->key);\n        free(victim->blob);\n        free(victim);\n\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n    }\n}\n\nstatic void\npurge_expired_entries_unlocked(void)\n{\n    time_t now = time(NULL);\n    cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.lru_tail;\n    while (e) {\n        cache_entry_t *prev = e->prev;\n        if (e->expire_at <= now) {\n            mm_log(\"DEBUG\", \"Purging expired key='%s'\", e->key);\n            lru_remove_unlocked(e);\n            hash_remove_unlocked(e);\n\n            g_cache.current_entries--;\n            g_cache.current_bytes -= e->blob_len;\n\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->blob);\n            free(e);\n\n            atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.evictions, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        }\n        e = prev;\n    }\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Public API implementation\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n\nint\nmm_cache_init(size_t max_entries, size_t max_bytes)\n{\n    if (g_cache.initialized)\n        return MM_CACHE_SUCCESS;\n\n    if (max_entries == 0 || max_bytes == 0)\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n\n    memset(&g_cache, 0, sizeof(g_cache));\n\n    size_t bucket_count = 1ULL << DEFAULT_HASH_POWER;\n    g_cache.buckets = calloc(bucket_count, sizeof(cache_entry_t *));\n    if (!g_cache.buckets)\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n\n    g_cache.hash_mask = bucket_count - 1;\n    g_cache.max_entries = max_entries;\n    g_cache.max_bytes  = max_bytes;\n\n    pthread_mutex_init(&g_cache.lock, NULL);\n\n    g_cache.initialized = 1;\n    mm_log(\"INFO\", \"Response cache initialised: capacity=%zu entries, %zu bytes\",\n           max_entries, max_bytes);\n    return MM_CACHE_SUCCESS;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_destroy(void)\n{\n    if (!g_cache.initialized)\n        return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i <= g_cache.hash_mask; ++i) {\n        cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[i];\n        while (e) {\n            cache_entry_t *next = e->hnext;\n            free(e->key);\n            free(e->blob);\n            free(e);\n            e = next;\n        }\n    }\n    free(g_cache.buckets);\n    g_cache.buckets = NULL;\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    pthread_mutex_destroy(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    g_cache.initialized = 0;\n\n    mm_log(\"INFO\", \"Response cache shut down.\");\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_get(const char *key, uint8_t **payload_out, size_t *len_out)\n{\n    if (!g_cache.initialized || !key || !payload_out || !len_out)\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    purge_expired_entries_unlocked();\n\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(key) & g_cache.hash_mask;\n    cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[h];\n\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0)\n        e = e->hnext;\n\n    if (!e) {\n        atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.misses, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return MM_CACHE_KEY_NOT_FOUND;\n    }\n\n    /* Move to front (MRU) */\n    lru_remove_unlocked(e);\n    lru_insert_front_unlocked(e);\n\n    /* Clone payload for caller */\n    uint8_t *dup = malloc(e->blob_len);\n    if (!dup) {\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n    }\n    memcpy(dup, e->blob, e->blob_len);\n\n    *payload_out = dup;\n    *len_out = e->blob_len;\n\n    atomic_fetch_add_explicit(&g_cache.hits, 1, memory_order_relaxed);\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    return MM_CACHE_SUCCESS;\n}\n\nint\nmm_cache_put(const char *key, const uint8_t *payload, size_t len, unsigned ttl_seconds)\n{\n    if (!g_cache.initialized || !key || !payload || len == 0 || ttl_seconds == 0)\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    purge_expired_entries_unlocked();\n\n    /* If key already exists replace it */\n    uint64_t h = mm_fnv1a_hash(key) & g_cache.hash_mask;\n    cache_entry_t *e = g_cache.buckets[h];\n\n    while (e && strcmp(e->key, key) != 0)\n        e = e->hnext;\n\n    if (e) {\n        /* Update in-place */\n        g_cache.current_bytes -= e->blob_len;\n\n        uint8_t *new_blob = malloc(len);\n        if (!new_blob) {\n            pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n            return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n        }\n        memcpy(new_blob, payload, len);\n        free(e->blob);\n\n        e->blob = new_blob;\n        e->blob_len = len;\n        e->expire_at = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n\n        lru_remove_unlocked(e);\n        lru_insert_front_unlocked(e);\n\n        g_cache.current_bytes += len;\n\n        evict_if_needed_unlocked(0);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return MM_CACHE_SUCCESS;\n    }\n\n    /* New entry */\n    char *key_dup = strdup(key);\n    uint8_t *blob_dup = malloc(len);\n    if (!key_dup || !blob_dup) {\n        free(key_dup);\n        free(blob_dup);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n    }\n    memcpy(blob_dup, payload, len);\n\n    e = calloc(1, sizeof(*e));\n    if (!e) {\n        free(key_dup);\n        free(blob_dup);\n        pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n    }\n    e->key       = key_dup;\n    e->blob      = blob_dup;\n    e->blob_len  = len;\n    e->expire_at = time(NULL) + ttl_seconds;\n\n    /* Evict to make room */\n    evict_if_needed_unlocked(len);\n\n    /* Insert */\n    lru_insert_front_unlocked(e);\n    hash_insert_unlocked(e);\n\n    g_cache.current_entries++;\n    g_cache.current_bytes += len;\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n    return MM_CACHE_SUCCESS;\n}\n\nvoid\nmm_cache_stats(mm_cache_stats_t *out)\n{\n    if (!out) return;\n\n    pthread_mutex_lock(&g_cache.lock);\n\n    out->hits            = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache.hits, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->misses          = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache.misses, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->evictions       = atomic_load_explicit(&g_cache.evictions, memory_order_relaxed);\n    out->current_entries = g_cache.current_entries;\n    out->current_bytes   = g_cache.current_bytes;\n\n    pthread_mutex_unlock(&g_cache.lock);\n}\n\n/*\n * Render Prometheus-compatible metrics into the supplied buffer.\n * The function returns the number of bytes written (excluding NUL terminator)\n * or a negative error code.\n */\nint\nmm_cache_prometheus_snapshot(char *buf, size_t buf_len)\n{\n    if (!buf || buf_len == 0) return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n\n    mm_cache_stats_t s;\n    mm_cache_stats(&s);\n\n    int written = snprintf(\n        buf, buf_len,\n        \"# TYPE cache_hits counter\\n\"\n        \"cache_hits %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE cache_misses counter\\n\"\n        \"cache_misses %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE cache_evictions counter\\n\"\n        \"cache_evictions %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE cache_current_entries gauge\\n\"\n        \"cache_current_entries %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\"\n        \"# TYPE cache_current_bytes gauge\\n\"\n        \"cache_current_bytes %\" PRIu64 \"\\n\",\n        s.hits,\n        s.misses,\n        s.evictions,\n        s.current_entries,\n        s.current_bytes\n    );\n\n    if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= buf_len)\n        return MM_CACHE_ERR;\n    return written;\n}\n\n/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n * Self-test (can be compiled with -DMM_CACHE_SELFTEST)\n * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_SELFTEST\nstatic void\nprint_stats(void)\n{\n    mm_cache_stats_t s;\n    mm_cache_stats(&s);\n    printf(\"hits=%\" PRIu64 \" misses=%\" PRIu64 \" evictions=%\" PRIu64\n           \" entries=%\" PRIu64 \" bytes=%\" PRIu64 \"\\n\",\n           s.hits, s.misses, s.evictions, s.current_entries, s.current_bytes);\n}\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    const char payload[] = \"{\\\"hello\\\":\\\"world\\\"}\";\n    const size_t payload_len = sizeof(payload) - 1;\n\n    assert(mm_cache_init(4, 1024) == 0);\n\n    assert(mm_cache_put(\"/foo\", (const uint8_t *)payload, payload_len, 10) == 0);\n    assert(mm_cache_put(\"/bar\", (const uint8_t *)payload, payload_len, 10) == 0);\n    assert(mm_cache_put(\"/baz\", (const uint8_t *)payload, payload_len, 10) == 0);\n\n    uint8_t *out;\n    size_t out_len;\n    assert(mm_cache_get(\"/foo\", &out, &out_len) == 0);\n    assert(out_len == payload_len);\n    free(out);\n\n    print_stats(); /* Observe hits/misses */\n\n    mm_cache_destroy();\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif /* MM_CACHE_SELFTEST */\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " #include <string.h>",
        " #include <time.h>",
        " ",
        "-/* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "- * Utilities & logging",
        "- * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */",
        "-#ifndef MM_LOG_H",
        "-static void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf, 2, 3)));",
        "+#include \"logging.h\"",
        " ",
        "-static void mm_log(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...)",
        "-{",
        "-    va_list ap;",
        "-    va_start(ap, fmt);",
        "+/* Map legacy mm_log to new centralized logging */",
        "+#define mm_log(level, fmt, ...) \\",
        "+    do { \\",
        "+        if (strcmp(level, \"ERROR\") == 0) \\",
        "+            LOG_ERROR(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\",
        "+        else if (strcmp(level, \"WARN\") == 0) \\",
        "+            LOG_WARN(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\",
        "+        else if (strcmp(level, \"DEBUG\") == 0) \\",
        "+            LOG_DEBUG(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\",
        "+        else \\",
        "+            LOG_INFO(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__); \\",
        "+    } while (0)",
        " ",
        "-    struct timespec ts;",
        "-    clock_gettime(CLOCK_REALTIME, &ts);",
        "-    struct tm tm;",
        "-    localtime_r(&ts.tv_sec, &tm);",
        "-    char timebuf[32];",
        "-    strftime(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\", &tm);",
        "-",
        "-    fprintf(stderr, \"[%s] [%s] \", timebuf, level);",
        "-    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);",
        "-    fputc('\\n', stderr);",
        "-",
        "-    va_end(ap);",
        "-}",
        "-#else",
        "-#include \"mm_log.h\"",
        "-#endif /* MM_LOG_H */",
        "-",
        " /* ---------------------------------------------------------------------------",
        "  * Public types / constants",
        "  * --------------------------------------------------------------------------- */"
      ],
      "newLines": 21,
      "newStart": 30,
      "oldLines": 34,
      "oldStart": 30
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": " * Self-test (compile with -DMM_CACHE_UNIT_TEST)\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_UNIT_TEST\nstatic void dummy_log(mm_logger_t *self, mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    (void)self;(void)lvl;\n    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n\nstatic void dummy_inc(mm_metrics_t *self, const char *name)\n{ (void)self; fprintf(stderr, \"[metric] %s++\\n\", name); }\n\nstatic void dummy_obs(mm_metrics_t *self, const char *name, double v)\n{ (void)self; fprintf(stderr, \"[metric] %s=%f\\n\", name, v); }\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_logger_t logger = { .log = dummy_log };\n    mm_metrics_t metrics = { .inc_counter = dummy_inc,\n                             .observe_histogram = dummy_obs };\n\n    mm_response_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(4, 2, &logger, &metrics);\n\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"/ping\", \"pong\", 4);\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"/hello\", \"world\", 5);\n\n    mm_buffer_t *buf;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_17.txt",
    "numLines": 30,
    "startLine": 380,
    "totalLines": 423
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/src/module_17.txt",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * --------------------------------\n * src/module_17.txt  (pretend .c)\n *\n * Response Cache (LRU + TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread\u2013safe, in-memory response cache that is used by the\n * API gateway layer to accelerate frequently-requested resources (e.g., catalog\n * look-ups, configuration, and low-volatility reference data).  It exposes a small\n * surface area that can be consumed by higher-level middleware without leaking\n * implementation details.\n *\n * Dependencies:\n *      - pthread (native)\n *      - uthash  (single-header hash table \u2014 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n *\n * The module is completely self-contained; external project types (mm_logger_t,\n * mm_metrics_t, mm_buffer_t) are forward-declared with minimal interfaces so that\n * this file can be dropped into the larger code-base without modification.\n */\n\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash table */\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n * Forward declarations to avoid tight coupling with the rest of the code-base.\n * In the real project these come from <mm_logging.h>, <mm_metrics.h>,\n * <mm_buffer.h>, etc.\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\ntypedef enum {\n    MM_LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG,\n    MM_LOG_INFO,\n    MM_LOG_WARN,\n    MM_LOG_ERROR,\n    MM_LOG_FATAL\n} mm_log_level_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_logger {\n    void (*log)(struct mm_logger *self, mm_log_level_t lvl,\n                const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf,3,4)));",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/src/module_17.txt",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 423
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "oldString": "#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash table */\n#include \"uthash.h\"",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * MercuryMonolith Commerce Hub\n * --------------------------------\n * src/module_17.txt  (pretend .c)\n *\n * Response Cache (LRU + TTL)\n *\n * This module provides a thread\u2013safe, in-memory response cache that is used by the\n * API gateway layer to accelerate frequently-requested resources (e.g., catalog\n * look-ups, configuration, and low-volatility reference data).  It exposes a small\n * surface area that can be consumed by higher-level middleware without leaking\n * implementation details.\n *\n * Dependencies:\n *      - pthread (native)\n *      - uthash  (single-header hash table \u2014 https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/)\n *\n * The module is completely self-contained; external project types (mm_logger_t,\n * mm_metrics_t, mm_buffer_t) are forward-declared with minimal interfaces so that\n * this file can be dropped into the larger code-base without modification.\n */\n\n#include <pthread.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n#include <stdlib.h>\n#include <string.h>\n#include <stdbool.h>\n#include <stdio.h>\n#include <time.h>\n\n/* 3rd-party single-header hash table */\n#include \"uthash.h\"\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n * Forward declarations to avoid tight coupling with the rest of the code-base.\n * In the real project these come from <mm_logging.h>, <mm_metrics.h>,\n * <mm_buffer.h>, etc.\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\ntypedef enum {\n    MM_LOG_TRACE = 0,\n    MM_LOG_DEBUG,\n    MM_LOG_INFO,\n    MM_LOG_WARN,\n    MM_LOG_ERROR,\n    MM_LOG_FATAL\n} mm_log_level_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_logger {\n    void (*log)(struct mm_logger *self, mm_log_level_t lvl,\n                const char *fmt, ...) __attribute__((format(printf,3,4)));\n} mm_logger_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_metrics {\n    void (*inc_counter)(struct mm_metrics *self, const char *name);\n    void (*observe_histogram)(struct mm_metrics *self, const char *name,\n                              double value);\n} mm_metrics_t;\n\n/* Simple, ref-counted byte buffer used throughout the monolith. */\ntypedef struct mm_buffer {\n    uint8_t *data;\n    size_t   len;\n    uint32_t refcnt;\n} mm_buffer_t;\n\n/* Buffer helpers */\nstatic mm_buffer_t *mm_buffer_new(const void *src, size_t len)\n{\n    mm_buffer_t *b = malloc(sizeof *b);\n    if (!b) return NULL;\n    b->data = malloc(len);\n    if (!b->data) { free(b); return NULL; }\n    memcpy(b->data, src, len);\n    b->len = len;\n    b->refcnt = 1;\n    return b;\n}\n\nstatic mm_buffer_t *mm_buffer_clone(mm_buffer_t *orig)\n{\n    if (orig) __sync_add_and_fetch(&orig->refcnt, 1);\n    return orig;\n}\n\nstatic void mm_buffer_release(mm_buffer_t *buf)\n{\n    if (!buf) return;\n    if (__sync_sub_and_fetch(&buf->refcnt, 1) == 0) {\n        free(buf->data);\n        free(buf);\n    }\n}\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\ntypedef struct cache_entry {\n    char             *key;         /* UTF-8 route + query string */\n    mm_buffer_t      *value;       /* Cached HTTP/GraphQL payload */\n    time_t            created_at;  /* Epoch seconds (monotonic not req.) */\n\n    struct cache_entry *prev;      /* LRU doubly-linked list pointers */\n    struct cache_entry *next;\n\n    UT_hash_handle     hh;         /* uthash linkage */\n} cache_entry_t;\n\ntypedef struct mm_response_cache {\n    cache_entry_t  *table;         /* Hash table (key \u2192 cache_entry)     */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_head;      /* Most recently used                 */\n    cache_entry_t  *lru_tail;      /* Least recently used                */\n\n    size_t          count;         /* # of live entries                  */\n    size_t          capacity;      /* Hard limit                         */\n    uint32_t        ttl;           /* Seconds until expiry               */\n\n    pthread_rwlock_t lock;         /* Reader\u2013writer lock                 */\n\n    mm_logger_t    *logger;        /* External structured logger         */\n    mm_metrics_t   *metrics;       /* Metrics collector (Prometheus)     */\n} mm_response_cache_t;\n\n/* Public API \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nmm_response_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(size_t capacity, uint32_t ttl_seconds,\n                mm_logger_t *logger, mm_metrics_t *metrics);\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_response_cache_t *cache);\n\nbool mm_cache_get(mm_response_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key, mm_buffer_t **out_buf);\n\nbool mm_cache_put(mm_response_cache_t *cache,\n                  const char *key, const void *payload, size_t len);\n\nvoid mm_cache_prune(mm_response_cache_t *cache); /* manual house-keeping */\n\n/* Internal helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nstatic void     lru_move_to_front(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     lru_evict_tail(mm_response_cache_t *c);\nstatic void     detach_node(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic void     attach_front(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e);\nstatic time_t   now_sec(void);\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n * IMPLEMENTATION\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nmm_response_cache_t *\nmm_cache_create(size_t capacity, uint32_t ttl_seconds,\n                mm_logger_t *logger, mm_metrics_t *metrics)\n{\n    mm_response_cache_t *c = calloc(1, sizeof *c);\n    if (!c) return NULL;\n\n    c->capacity = capacity ? capacity : 1024; /* default if 0 */\n    c->ttl      = ttl_seconds ? ttl_seconds : 60;\n    c->logger   = logger;\n    c->metrics  = metrics;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_init(&c->lock, NULL);\n\n    if (logger && logger->log)\n        logger->log(logger, MM_LOG_INFO,\n                    \"[cache] Initialized (capacity=%zu, ttl=%u)\",\n                    c->capacity, c->ttl);\n    return c;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_destroy(mm_response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->table, cur, tmp) {\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, cur);\n        mm_buffer_release(cur->value);\n        free(cur->key);\n        free(cur);\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n    pthread_rwlock_destroy(&c->lock);\n    free(c);\n}\n\nbool mm_cache_get(mm_response_cache_t *c,\n                  const char *key, mm_buffer_t **out_buf)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !out_buf) return false;\n\n    bool hit = false;\n\n    /* Acquire read lock first (optimistic) */\n    pthread_rwlock_rdlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *e;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n    if (e && (now_sec() - e->created_at <= c->ttl)) {\n        *out_buf = mm_buffer_clone(e->value);\n        hit = true;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    /* If hit, we still need to move the node to front (requires write lock) */\n    if (hit) {\n        pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n        HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n        if (e) lru_move_to_front(c, e);\n        pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n        if (c->metrics && c->metrics->inc_counter)\n            c->metrics->inc_counter(c->metrics, \"cache_hits\");\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /* Cache miss or expired entry (handle expire under write lock) */\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, e);\n    if (e && (now_sec() - e->created_at > c->ttl)) {\n        /* Expired \u2014 remove */\n        if (c->logger && c->logger->log)\n            c->logger->log(c->logger, MM_LOG_DEBUG,\n                            \"[cache] Expired entry '%s'\", key);\n\n        detach_node(c, e);\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, e);\n        mm_buffer_release(e->value);\n        free(e->key);\n        free(e);\n        c->count--;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    if (c->metrics && c->metrics->inc_counter)\n        c->metrics->inc_counter(c->metrics, \"cache_misses\");\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nbool mm_cache_put(mm_response_cache_t *c,\n                  const char *key, const void *payload, size_t len)\n{\n    if (!c || !key || !payload || !len) return false;\n\n    /* Allocate entry before locking to keep lock duration minimal */\n    cache_entry_t *e = calloc(1, sizeof *e);\n    if (!e) return false;\n\n    e->key = strdup(key);\n    e->value = mm_buffer_new(payload, len);\n    e->created_at = now_sec();\n    if (!e->key || !e->value) {\n        free(e->key);\n        mm_buffer_release(e->value);\n        free(e);\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *existing;\n    HASH_FIND_STR(c->table, key, existing);\n    if (existing) {\n        /* Replace */\n        detach_node(c, existing);\n        HASH_DEL(c->table, existing);\n        mm_buffer_release(existing->value);\n        free(existing->key);\n        free(existing);\n        c->count--;\n    }\n\n    HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, c->table, e->key, strlen(e->key), e);\n    attach_front(c, e);\n    c->count++;\n\n    if (c->count > c->capacity)\n        lru_evict_tail(c);\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    if (c->metrics && c->metrics->inc_counter)\n        c->metrics->inc_counter(c->metrics, \"cache_insert\");\n\n    return true;\n}\n\nvoid mm_cache_prune(mm_response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c) return;\n\n    pthread_rwlock_wrlock(&c->lock);\n\n    cache_entry_t *cur, *tmp;\n    time_t now = now_sec();\n    size_t removed = 0;\n\n    HASH_ITER(hh, c->table, cur, tmp) {\n        if ((now - cur->created_at) > c->ttl) {\n            detach_node(c, cur);\n            HASH_DEL(c->table, cur);\n            mm_buffer_release(cur->value);\n            free(cur->key);\n            free(cur);\n            c->count--;\n            removed++;\n        }\n    }\n\n    pthread_rwlock_unlock(&c->lock);\n\n    if (removed && c->logger && c->logger->log)\n        c->logger->log(c->logger, MM_LOG_INFO,\n                        \"[cache] Pruned %zu expired entries\", removed);\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n * Internal helpers\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nstatic inline time_t now_sec(void)\n{\n    return time(NULL);\n}\n\nstatic void detach_node(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (e->prev) e->prev->next = e->next;\n    if (e->next) e->next->prev = e->prev;\n\n    if (c->lru_head == e) c->lru_head = e->next;\n    if (c->lru_tail == e) c->lru_tail = e->prev;\n\n    e->prev = e->next = NULL;\n}\n\nstatic void attach_front(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    e->next = c->lru_head;\n    e->prev = NULL;\n    if (c->lru_head) c->lru_head->prev = e;\n    c->lru_head = e;\n    if (!c->lru_tail) c->lru_tail = e;\n}\n\nstatic void lru_move_to_front(mm_response_cache_t *c, cache_entry_t *e)\n{\n    if (c->lru_head == e) return; /* already front */\n    detach_node(c, e);\n    attach_front(c, e);\n}\n\nstatic void lru_evict_tail(mm_response_cache_t *c)\n{\n    if (!c->lru_tail) return;\n\n    cache_entry_t *victim = c->lru_tail;\n\n    if (c->logger && c->logger->log)\n        c->logger->log(c->logger, MM_LOG_DEBUG,\n                        \"[cache] Evicting LRU '%s'\", victim->key);\n\n    detach_node(c, victim);\n    HASH_DEL(c->table, victim);\n    mm_buffer_release(victim->value);\n    free(victim->key);\n    free(victim);\n    c->count--;\n\n    if (c->metrics && c->metrics->inc_counter)\n        c->metrics->inc_counter(c->metrics, \"cache_evictions\");\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n * Self-test (compile with -DMM_CACHE_UNIT_TEST)\n * \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n#ifdef MM_CACHE_UNIT_TEST\nstatic void dummy_log(mm_logger_t *self, mm_log_level_t lvl, const char *fmt, ...)\n{\n    (void)self;(void)lvl;\n    va_list ap; va_start(ap, fmt);\n    vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);\n    fprintf(stderr, \"\\n\");\n    va_end(ap);\n}\n\nstatic void dummy_inc(mm_metrics_t *self, const char *name)\n{ (void)self; fprintf(stderr, \"[metric] %s++\\n\", name); }\n\nstatic void dummy_obs(mm_metrics_t *self, const char *name, double v)\n{ (void)self; fprintf(stderr, \"[metric] %s=%f\\n\", name, v); }\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    mm_logger_t logger = { .log = dummy_log };\n    mm_metrics_t metrics = { .inc_counter = dummy_inc,\n                             .observe_histogram = dummy_obs };\n\n    mm_response_cache_t *c = mm_cache_create(4, 2, &logger, &metrics);\n\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"/ping\", \"pong\", 4);\n    mm_cache_put(c, \"/hello\", \"world\", 5);\n\n    mm_buffer_t *buf;\n    if (mm_cache_get(c, \"/ping\", &buf)) {\n        fwrite(buf->data, 1, buf->len, stdout);\n        putchar('\\n');\n        mm_buffer_release(buf);\n    }\n\n    sleep(3);\n    mm_cache_get(c, \"/ping\", &buf); /* should miss (expired) */\n\n    mm_cache_destroy(c);\n    return 0;\n}\n#endif\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
